# Thread: Wired Headphones EMF Warning

### Paul 22 — 2025-08-24T19:34:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-358684>

I’ve heard Uncle Jack talk about Apple ear pods and bluetooth headphones being very bad and so I abandoned them last year. But thinking I was smart, I bought some very expensive WIRED headphones to use instead.These eventually brought on a lot of strange symptoms including dizziness which I did not attribute to the headphones right away.

When I finally tested them with an EMF meter I was shocked to find that, even though they have no power or batteries, they put out peak EMF up to 36 mG every 30 - 60 seconds. So yeah, super bad right into the ears and brain.

I tried buying some “reduced EMF” wired earbuds that deliver sound though air tubes. And yes, they had no EMF, but they had huge spikes of RF.

So, posting this warning for anyone naively using headphones like i was.

### hacklose — 2025-08-25T23:21:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-358729>

> [Paul 22 said:](/goto/post?id=358720)
>
> Hi Anthony. Yes, I'm sure the RF was coming from the phone, but it was reading very high at the ear bud ends also. Can't run anymore tests, as those air tubes are long gone to the trash.
>
> No EMF though as advertised, LOL. Just a ton of RF. I mean anything connected to a cell phone has gotta be bad.

Your phone is what is generating the RF. The wired ear buds are close to the phone, so the RF you measured at the ear buds was probably just coming from the phone being in close proximity.

Cell phones are most people's largest RF exposure. They are terrible and should only be used for emergencies if at all possible. Use a wired ethernet connection for internet access/email/messaging and either a VOIP ethernet connected phone or copper landline for calls. We all need to limit our wireless tech use to the bare minimum.

### greentrees — 2025-09-02T07:04:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-359031>

Ultrasone in Germany is the only headphone brand I've seen that has a focus on shielding magnetic fields for their headphones. They use mu metal shielding the magnetic fields from the user. The headphones do have high electric fields if connecting to an average power grid, afaik there's no electric field mitigation. Mine measure low in mG but high in E-, I really need to figure out the grid here. If I connect them to a battery powered music player the levels across the board are low

I still think Jack's advice of not wearing is better and defer to that, especially if someone is doing a disease reversal. The Ultrasones are a step towards EMF safe headphones but still have to be used smartly. I'm guessing if you're wearing a pair and standing under a fluorescent light with a high electric field it probably doesn't matter if you're using a battery powered music player, your head now has a giant antenna on top drawing the electric fields (just a hunch)

### hacklose — 2025-09-16T18:45:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-359606>

> [Todd Schulz said:](/goto/post?id=359554)
>
> I just purchased the TriField EMF Model TF2 for $186. Can't spend $1k+ for a meter. It is on Amazon & at the company sight- https://trifield.com/products/trifield®-emf-meter-model-tf2
>
> ### [AlphaLab, Inc.](https://www.alphalabinc.com/)
>
> AlphaLab, Inc. designs and manufactures gaussmeters, air ion counters, electrostatic voltmeters and other instruments for scientific and industrial applications.
>
>  www.alphalabinc.com
>
> Says USA made.

The Trifield TF2 works pretty well for measuring magnetic fields, which is AlpaLabs specialty.
It isn't accurate enough for measuring electric fields and is very inaccurate for RF.
Unfortunately USA made doesn't necessarily mean accurate.

You'll definitely want to spend some more money in the future if you want anything close to accurate readings for all parts of the field.
In terms of low cost meters that are more accurate in other areas, the Cornet ED98, S&S Pro II, and S&S EM3 are my favorites.
I also don't recommend using the Cornet ED98 for it's electric field reading, but the other measurements it does seem to be quite accurate.

I've been lucky enough to make a friend that has the highest end Gigahertz Solutions meters that are very accurate and the meters I've listed above get close for a lot less money.

The old sayings, "You get what you pay for" and "Buy once, cry once" apply here.

# Thread: WiFi news......from Rusty
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 53
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-25T16:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-358703>

You use WiFi. Almost everyone does. It typically operates on 2 different bands: 2.4 GHz & 5 GHz. 2.4 GHz = Longer range, slower speeds. 5 GHz = Shorter range, faster speeds. When you set up your WiFi, both bands are TURNED ON by default. That's why you see 2 WiFi names, one with the name & the other one with 5G (This 5G has nothing to do with the wireless 5G on your phone). Both WiFi bands are on = MORE RF radiation. You don’t need both. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Turn one off.

When you set up your WiFi, both bands are TURNED ON by default. That's why you see 2 WiFi names, one with the name & the other one with 5G (This 5G has nothing to do with the wireless 5G on your phone). Both WiFi bands are on = MORE RF radiation. You don’t need both.[View attachment 31856](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/31856/) Turn one off.

I say turn off all wifi for good.. it is not needed. Convenience comes with a cost.. Don't really like this post as it implies it is ok to still cook yourself with 1 network instead of 2.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-26T10:08:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-358751>

[@Rusty](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26197/) - how did you confirm that disabling the SSID even in the admin settings actually stops RF transmission in that particular band?? As from what I have found (which I have posted about previously) that on most routers this only disables the broadcast of SSID and still transmits as a "hidden network". Some have an option to turn off the 2.4 or 5 outside the SSID, but found some still transmitted a "hidden network".

### Rusty — 2025-08-26T12:45:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-358758>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=358751)
>
> [@Rusty](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26197/) - how did you confirm that disabling the SSID even in the admin settings actually stops RF transmission in that particular band?? As from what I have found (which I have posted about previously) that on most routers this only disables the broadcast of SSID and still transmits as a "hidden network". Some have an option to turn off the 2.4 or 5 outside the SSID, but found some still transmitted a "hidden network".

You're right Mat. Some ISP provided routers are notorious for this stuff.

You can use an open source app like WiFi Analyzer to scan for nearby networks. hidden networks will still show up even without SSIDs.

Ref : [https://www.acrylicwifi.com/en/blog/hidden-ssid-wifi-how-to-know-name-of-network-without-ssid](https://www.acrylicwifi.com/en/blog/hidden-ssid-wifi-how-to-know-name-of-network-without-ssid/)

### Mat.I — 2025-08-26T12:58:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-358760>

> [Rusty said:](/goto/post?id=358758)
>
> You're right Mat. Some ISP provided routers are notorious for this stuff.
>
> You can use an open source app like WiFi Analyzer to scan for nearby networks. hidden networks will still show up even without SSIDs.

Yes, I use the analysers (including some advanced tools) and found the MACs (aka BSSIDs) always tied back to the router transmitting the hidden networks..I was yet to come accross a standard (non re-flashed one or open source firmware) that would actually disable the radios. Even with the toggle switches on the router on many all it would do is not broadcast the SSID..

IMO - best to avoid any wifi and go hardwired.. yes you minimise carrier wave exposure with one less network, however you are still getting pulsed.. Doesn't really change much in the grand scheme of things.

### Rusty — 2025-09-03T13:22:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-359076>

WiFi extenders?

Worst invention ever.

A WiFi extender/repeater isn’t just “1 more router”

It literally mirrors the radios of your main router.

Your main WiFi router usually has 2 radios.

Add an extender. It also has 2 radios (2.4 + 5 GHz)

So now instead of 2 transmitters in your space,

You’ve got 4 separate RF transmitters blasting.

And it doesn’t stop there.

The latest WiFi 7 standard unlocks the 6 GHz band too.

So now 1 router can run 3 radios at once

\- 2.4 GHz
\- 5 GHz
\- 6 GHz

Imagine adding an extender to that.

Now you don’t just have 3 radios,

You have 6x transmitters blasting all the time.

That’s way more artificial signals than your body was ever built to handle.

and this is just the Routers & Extenders..

Add in your Cellphones, AirPods, Smart watches

More & more Intracellular dehydration

More & more activation of VGCCs in the cells

More & more weakening of Blood-Brain-Barrier, blood-ocular/placenta/testis/CFS/skin/barrier & influx of toxins

MORE Sickness.

MORE Chronic health problems that no one can explain.

This is all compounding faster than the speed of light.

Meanwhile, Big Tech keeps pushing SMART upgrades you don’t actually need.

And the truth is,

you’ll probably keep buying

because the same blue-flickering screen you’re staring at right now was designed to make you.

### warriorT — 2025-09-16T08:18:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-359581>

If you are moving through rental properties would it be possible to carry a portable hardwiring kit to always be able to use Ethernet or does it depend on how each propertys wifi router is set up? What would a generic hard wiring kit look like?

### hacklose — 2025-09-16T19:17:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-359609>

> [warriorT said:](/goto/post?id=359581)
>
> If you are moving through rental properties would it be possible to carry a portable hardwiring kit to always be able to use Ethernet or does it depend on how each propertys wifi router is set up? What would a generic hard wiring kit look like?

Each property will be setup differently, so it will depend.
It will depend if the property uses a modem/router combo unit or a separate modem and router.

Most routers will enable you to turn off the wifi at the software level in the router settings, but I have seen some that do not allow it.
You should want to confirm with an RF meter that when you turn off the wifi bands in the router's settings, they actually turn off.

I prefer using routers with no wifi hardware inside. Mikrotik has a few units like this, but there are other options.

### rtd — 2025-09-16T22:32:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-359615>

> [warriorT said:](/goto/post?id=359581)
>
> If you are moving through rental properties would it be possible to carry a portable hardwiring kit to always be able to use Ethernet or does it depend on how each propertys wifi router is set up? What would a generic hard wiring kit look like?

1) A long Ethernet Cable. Recommend at least Cat5e shielded. A 50 foot should coil nicely for travel.
2) Small Network switch to add ethernet connections.
3) A few small Ethernet patch cables, at least 6ft
4) Velcro ties that secure to the cables. These help to stay tiddy
5) Some travel bag to store all this stuff. EagleCreek travel stuff comes to my mind. Maybe check out a marina/boat shop for waterproof travel bags.
6) A few RJ-45 to USB-C adapters. USB-C is popular on current devices. Laptops, cell phones etc may not have a physical RJ-45 port

At the location, check the router/cable modem. If there is a physical RJ-45 port (Ethernet) on the router/modem, you should be able to plug in an Ethernet cable and get a link light.

Turning off the WIFI broadcast, they can vary. For starters, maybe you do not have administrator privilege or the password to access the router or modem to make software changes, like disabling broadcast. Or maybe you are in luck and the modem has an external button to toggle the WIFI on/off.

### Rusty — 2025-09-25T06:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-360027>

If your house already has coaxial (TV/cable/DTH) wiring in the walls, you can plug MoCA (Multimedia over Coax Alliance) adapters at each end (router side + device side).

Many homes (especially in the US & parts of Europe) have coax jacks in multiple rooms so this adapter basically lets you repurpose the old TV cables in your home into a high-speed wired backbone

Also, MoCA is very different from PLC (Power Line Communication)

PLC forces data through your electrical wiring creating dirty electricity & turning your whole grid into a noisy antenna.

MoCA runs over shielded coax lines that were built to carry high-frequency signals.

That means:

\- No dirty electricity riding on your 50/60 Hz lines.
\- Less interference
\- Faster speeds

In fact, the latest MoCA 2.5 standard can deliver up to 2.5 Gbps across your coax network.

If you’ve ever wished for WIRED connections in every room without tearing open walls, MoCA is the hidden upgrade sitting in your house right now.

Good tip.. I have seen this done a few years back. One suggestion to add is to bond the coax main distribution splitter to earth that way the sheild on the coax will not float and become a noisy antenna.

### Mat.I — 2025-09-25T11:01:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-360032>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=360030)
>
> Good tip.. I have seen this done a few years back. One suggestion to add is to bond the coax main distribution splitter to earth that way the sheild on the coax will not float and become a noisy antenna.

Also from memory, subject to the coax config (e.g. star with multiple branches via splitter) some mods will be required at the branch points to manage signal attenuation + any splitters must be rated for the op band of Moca. Margin is likely ~ 50 to 60dB so tou have to manage the attenuation budget by the configuration..

> Hardwired is.the solution. No need for wifi at all period..

Would the following setup work? Any comments/input much appreciated:
\- Ethernet cable from wifi disabled router into a 10 Gtek gigabit Ethernet media converter multimode module powered by wall socket
\-SFP optical cable from that 50m to garden into another 10 Gtek (same as above) powered by a power Bank
\-Connected by a short 1 metre cat 6a UTP Ethernet cable into a 4 port Ethernet splitter switch also powered by power Bank
\-1 metre cat6a UTP cables to devices from the switch port

### Anthony Smith — 2025-10-11T21:01:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-360893>

A hard wired ethernet set up takes care of RF - WiFi/bluetooth - IF - you turn those features off on ALL devices.
SOME networks can pick up a 60Hz (50Hz) electric field - you need to measure - and if so the network needs to be grounded.

### warriorT — 2025-10-11T22:18:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-360897>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=360893)
>
> A hard wired ethernet set up takes care of RF - WiFi/bluetooth - IF - you turn those features off on ALL devices.
> SOME networks can pick up a 60Hz (50Hz) electric field - you need to measure - and if so the network needs to be grounded.

Ah okay thank you. Should I be using shielded Ethernet cables with this as it is?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-10-11T23:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-360901>

> [warriorT said:](/goto/post?id=360897)
>
> Ah okay thank you. Should I be using shielded Ethernet cables with this as it is?

NOT Cat5 (all plastic plug/connector) . . . Shielded Cat6A, 7 or 8 (metal in the plug)

### Mat.I — 2025-10-13T11:05:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-360965>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=360893)
>
> A hard wired ethernet set up takes care of RF - WiFi/bluetooth - IF - you turn those features off on ALL devices.
> SOME networks can pick up a 60Hz (50Hz) electric field - you need to measure - and if so the network needs to be grounded.

Or drain the noise at the source (0V) barrel on the DC side of the AC to DC power supply for router.. Or just bond and drain to a earth (portable stake in ground) anywhere else in the network. E.g. metal case + terminal network switch or at user device or via an alligator clip on sheilded and bonded ethernet metal jack etc.. many options to ground it.

### Mat.I — 2025-10-13T11:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-360966>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=360965)
>
> Or drain the noise at the source (0V) barrel on the DC side of the AC to DC power supply for router.. Or just bond and drain to a earth (portable stake in ground) anywhere else in the network. E.g. metal case + terminal network switch or at user device or via an alligator clip on sheilded and bonded ethernet metal jack etc.. many options to ground it.

Also, I have had decent network setups on stock cat5e cable that had no induced voltages or EMI due to very neat and segregated cable runs (comms separate from LV). If you want to bulletproof it just go all sheilded with metal Jack's and bond to an earth.. Just bare in mine you may loose the bond at some equipment that does not have (metal outer jack casings) so you have to earth strategically in these cases.. I will find the thread and link it re: metal vs plastic jack casings..

### amocchino1 — 2025-10-29T00:21:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-361789>

Hey guys looking at all of this and trying to narrow down what I'll need exactly to hard wire Ethernet through my house. I do have coaxial setup that my spectrum modem is connected to. There's another coaxial upstairs in my room and also the guest room that appears to have a phone adapter on the jack. My builder put an On-Q panel in the closet in the master bedroom and has cat5e cables running to these jacks from here. They originally designed it for the fiber box to be in the garage and run up the wall into the On-Q panel in the closet. The box was overheating in the summer so switched to regular coaxial from spectrum, no more ATT fiber. I'm assuming the coaxial is still running through that panel in the closet. From my understanding i might need a different cable anyways for better shielding? The modem/router live downstairs connected to one of the coaxial jacks. Im prepared to run wiring through to all the rooms including the garage. Maybe use the MoCA Rusty posted? Let me know yalls thoughts on best way to do this. THX!

### Rusty — 2025-10-29T09:51:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-361811>

Fiber optic cables transmit light, not electrical signals.

That means:

Zero electric fields (EF)
Zero magnetic fields (MF)
Zero dirty electricity (DE)
Zero conducted or radiated RF (EMI)

The only places where EMF returns is the endpoints, the media converters/switches that turn light back into electrical Ethernet signals.

**Q : Can you use Fiber Optic instead of Ethernet?**
A: Yes, you can absolutely use optical fiber instead of Ethernet cables.

You’ll need media converters (or fiber capable switches) at each end to turn the light signal (fiber) into an electrical signal (Ethernet)

Router → Media Converter → Fiber Cable → Media Converter → Device (via short Ethernet)

If you’re wiring multiple rooms, you’d only add more converters + fiber runs.

Total cost should be roughly $150-$200

**Q: How does this compare to normal shielded ethernet cables?**
A: Fiber Optic cable is completely different league.

It is completely immune to dirty electricity, induction or ground loops. The only EMF source is at the media converter boxes, and even that’s tiny and localized.

The shielded ethernet cable?

Even though it’s shielded, remember.. it still carries electrical signals.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### amocchino1 — 2025-10-30T07:33:12+0200

### TheCircleBastiat — 2025-10-30T21:52:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-361907>

I imagine I can use multiples of these on the device side, i.e., so multiple rooms will have wired connections in this way?

Also, on my modem/router combo, if I turn off both the 2.4g and 5g bands (5g already off thanks to Rusty's instructions on X), is it then effectively an RF-free modem?

> [Rusty said:](/goto/post?id=360027)
>
> If your house already has coaxial (TV/cable/DTH) wiring in the walls, you can plug MoCA (Multimedia over Coax Alliance) adapters at each end (router side + device side).
>
> Many homes (especially in the US & parts of Europe) have coax jacks in multiple rooms so this adapter basically lets you repurpose the old TV cables in your home into a high-speed wired backbone
>
> Also, MoCA is very different from PLC (Power Line Communication)
>
> PLC forces data through your electrical wiring creating dirty electricity & turning your whole grid into a noisy antenna.
>
> MoCA runs over shielded coax lines that were built to carry high-frequency signals.
>
> That means:
>
> \- No dirty electricity riding on your 50/60 Hz lines.
> \- Less interference
> \- Faster speeds
>
> In fact, the latest MoCA 2.5 standard can deliver up to 2.5 Gbps across your coax network.
>
> If you’ve ever wished for WIRED connections in every room without tearing open walls, MoCA is the hidden upgrade sitting in your house right now.
>

### Mat.I — 2025-10-31T00:15:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-361916>

> [TheCircleBastiat said:](/goto/post?id=361907)
>
> Also, on my modem/router combo, if I turn off both the 2.4g and 5g bands (5g already off thanks to Rusty's instructions on X), is it then effectively an RF-free modem?

No you must measure or use the free network analyser to pick up any hidden SSID's. As typically when you disable in settings (even advanced) it transmits as a "hidden" network. If you have one of these modem/routers (which is most of them based on my experience) the only option if you wish to retain the modem/router - is to reflash the firmware with a a free open source one (quite straight forward) then when you disable radios they actually disable all radios...

### Mat.I — 2025-10-31T00:18:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-news-from-rusty.31547/post-361917>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=361916)
>
> No you must measure or use the free network analyser to pick up any hidden SSID's. As typically when you disable in settings (even advanced) it transmits as a "hidden" network. If you have one of these modem/routers (which is most of them based on my experience) the only option if you wish to retain the modem/router - is to reflash the firmware with a a free open source one (quite straight forward) then when you disable radios they actually disable all radios...

I have had ones that you can only pick up via RF meter. so I would say in all cases for absolute certainty a meter is required. As one did not transmit any "identified network" .. It is like a "hidden" beacon layer. So the only definitive test is with an RF meter.

# Thread: Chainsaw EMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### barrypicker — 2025-08-25T16:24:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358704>

Gas vs Electric

Which is worse?

EMFs that emanate from the magneto (20,000 V to the spark plug, 12000 RPM) on a gas powered chainsaw

Or

EMF from a 60V electric chainsaw that only spins when making a cut

Has anyone metered this?

### Mat.I — 2025-08-26T09:38:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358749>

> [barrypicker said:](/goto/post?id=358704)
>
> Gas vs Electric
>
> Which is worse?
>
> EMFs that emanate from the magneto (20,000 V to the spark plug, 12000 RPM) on a gas powered chainsaw
>
> Or
>
> EMF from a 60V electric chainsaw that only spins when making a cut
>
> Has anyone metered this?

What would be your frequency of use?

### barrypicker — 2025-08-27T00:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358784>

During the winter probably average a few hours per week

### Mat.I — 2025-08-27T11:29:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358790>

> [barrypicker said:](/goto/post?id=358784)
>
> During the winter probably average a few hours per week

Quite low use. If it was me I wouldn't really be fussed over this and just go the 2 stroke standard one.. All new petrol saws should have in built suppression (e.g. resistor spark plug & suppressor caps etc..) Check this in the (user manual) specs prior to purchasing. I believe there was a international Reg that got adopted ~ 90's or 2000's to address this (EMC/EMI) even with small IC engines. I did a quick search of a Stihl manual which confirms this in specs & parts diagrams.. That is they come with the resistor plug, suppressor lead + cap.. If ordered just confirm the OHMs with a multimeter if unsure. You may find you get ~ 10k Ohms which is decent dampening.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-27T11:39:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358791>

Plus the cordless (batt option you posted) would use PWM for the motor which in my opinion based on theory (not measured) would be a shit show for EMF.. As the brushless motors require 3ph AC wave form so the DC is getting chopped into pulses with DE range harmonics

### barrypicker — 2025-08-27T15:36:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358800>

You always have great info on these forums, appreciate you!

### Mat.I — 2025-08-28T07:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358833>

> [barrypicker said:](/goto/post?id=358800)
>
> You always have great info on these forums, appreciate you!

No worries.. Happy to add my 2 cents .

### hacklose — 2025-08-28T08:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358836>

I agree with Mat. Without measuring, I would go for the 2 stroke gasoline option.

# Thread: Jack Kruse-inspired Costa Rican Retreat

### Beth Greer — 2025-08-25T21:35:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-inspired-costa-rican-retreat.31551/post-358728>

Two years ago, I had a very strong calling to move to Costa Rica. I am a New Yorker, then 30 years in N. California, then 2 years in S. Florida, thinking that was a better choice during the lockdowns. In 2023 a random visit to Coast Rica -- everything about it was a huge yes, but I didn’t know why at the time. It wasn’t until I discovered Jack’s work that I realized the reason: this land, this latitude, and this light were exactly what I needed to heal on the deepest level.

Now I’m creating an experiential retreat here so others can feel what it’s like to live fully immersed in these protocols. For 7 days (May 28-June 4, 2026) we’ll rise with equatorial sunlight at 9° latitude, ground in jungle rivers and waterfalls, experience cold thermogenesis, and nourish ourselves with organic animal- and wild seafood-based meals. All of it in a pristine rainforest sanctuary designed for true circadian alignment and mitochondrial repair.

This is a chance to practice quantum biology where it works best—under pure sun, surrounded by water and wild nature. If your mitochondria are craving a reset, I’d love for you to join me. Here's a link for more information. [https://supernaturalmom.com/retreat/](https://supernaturalmom.com/retreat/)

Jack, if you're reading this, please come as my guest (or stay at Shanti's home which is nearby the Retreat Center).

My adult daughter was sick in dec, we went to El Salvador all of jan tel great, she's been sick 3 times since being home with a cough she can't shake, head congestion. She's worked outside a lot during warm enough days. I've had the crud too, tho not as bad. I've wondered if it's the stuff from the sky keeping us sick but not dead yet. A nosebleed sounds next level. What are you going to do About your problem?

Could be varying factors impacting you.. For our n=1 environment our nn-emf is optimal, we are low pop density and the only issue I'm able to find/notice is in the skies for us.. No brain fog or any other issues or sickness etc.. Just notice when the sky is like this I can feel the irritants then I sometimes get a nose bleed... No other reasonable explanation I can find other then that.. BP all good and not stressed or anything like that, no intense exercise... air is not overly dry... It is a bit of a frustration to say the least. We are trying to get in the tropics (up north in OZ) in the next month we just need to decide on a short term rental.. I guess that will be a good test.

I can get the two missing frequencies from the same company I got the two UVB modules prior [https://www.alibaba.com/product-det...pm=a27aq.27095423.1978240560.1.78372277bVMaPj](https://www.alibaba.com/product-detail/25CM-365nm-UVA-Led-bar-Lamp_1601525872460.html?spm=a27aq.27095423.1978240560.1.78372277bVMaPj)
Aside from that I did modify the panel some and installed combination inductive/capacitive powerline filters on the power supplies internally feeding the LEDs and controller circuitry, cleaned up the wiring to lower the magnetic field propagation too ( twisted all stray pairs ). I don't get any DE jump on the stetzer or greenwave meter anymore. And no voltage/m reading now that the chassis is properly grounded. Power cable is also shielded. That's one thing about these panels from any manufacturer, seems most are shoddy QC where it concerns EMF emissions.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-03-08T16:32:54+0200

I have a feeling that panel story that Jack talks about with his father in law was a lot more about restoring water production at CCO in an acute setting (injury) rather than as a replacement / supplement for the sun. Polarized light (panels) = D-aminos = nothing works properly.

### bulkbiceps — 2026-03-08T19:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-panels.31556/post-368392>

> [Mr.AJ said:](/goto/post?id=368386)
>
> I have a feeling that panel story that Jack talks about with his father in law was a lot more about restoring water production at CCO in an acute setting (injury) rather than as a replacement / supplement for the sun. Polarized light (panels) = D-aminos = nothing works properly.

Probably, trying to wrap my head around the polarization description I keep seeing. Guessing this definition fits it best?

**"Artificial light** from sources like LEDs or fluorescent bulbs can appear **polarized** because the emission process often involves directional or structured interactions, such as electron transitions in a specific orientation or reflection from surfaces. In quantum terms, this means the photons are emitted in a **non-random mixture of polarization states**, sometimes even in a coherent superposition, leading to measurable polarization.

**Sunlight**, on the other hand, is **unpolarized** because it originates from the Sun’s photosphere, a vast region where countless independent atoms emit light in random directions and phases. Each emission event is uncorrelated, and the resulting light is a **classical statistical mixture** of all possible polarization states. This mixture cannot be described as a pure quantum superposition—instead, it’s a **mixed state** with no preferred polarization direction. As a result, the quantum state of sunlight is not a coherent superposition of polarization modes, but rather a probabilistic distribution, which is why it is considered unpolarized."

Best I can see from search engine queries...

If that's the case, could it not be improved by forcing the light through a depolarizer? Like a matrix of ground glass powder or smaller diffuser with nanoparticle glass beads?

### Mr.AJ — 2026-03-08T19:52:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-panels.31556/post-368394>

Plenty about it written on the forums. Just search.

### Anthony Smith — 2026-03-08T20:14:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-panels.31556/post-368396>

> [bulkbiceps said:](/goto/post?id=368392)
>
> Probably, trying to wrap my head around the polarization description I keep seeing. Guessing this definition fits it best?
>
> **"Artificial light** from sources like LEDs or fluorescent bulbs can appear **polarized** because the emission process often involves directional or structured interactions, such as electron transitions in a specific orientation or reflection from surfaces. In quantum terms, this means the photons are emitted in a **non-random mixture of polarization states**, sometimes even in a coherent superposition, leading to measurable polarization.
>
> **Sunlight**, on the other hand, is **unpolarized** because it originates from the Sun’s photosphere, a vast region where countless independent atoms emit light in random directions and phases. Each emission event is uncorrelated, and the resulting light is a **classical statistical mixture** of all possible polarization states. This mixture cannot be described as a pure quantum superposition—instead, it’s a **mixed state** with no preferred polarization direction. As a result, the quantum state of sunlight is not a coherent superposition of polarization modes, but rather a probabilistic distribution, which is why it is considered unpolarized."
>
> Best I can see from search engine queries...
>
> If that's the case, could it not be improved by forcing the light through a depolarizer? Like a matrix of ground glass powder or smaller diffuser with nanoparticle glass beads?

There appears to be some confusion between electrical polarization and light polarization . . .

**Electrical polarization:**
Unlike ordinary electronic components such as resistors and capacitors, LEDs are polarized, which means they can only emit light normally when powered in one direction. If the LED is connected in reverse, not only will it not light up, but it may also burn out sometimes. Therefore, it is very important to figure out the LED polarity, the LED symbol and connect the LED correctly.

**Light polarization:**
Many common light sources such as sunlight, halogen lighting, LED spotlights, and incandescent bulbs produce unpolarized light. If the direction of the electric field of light is well defined, it is called polarized light. The most common source of polarized light is a laser. [https://www.edmundoptics.com/knowledge-center/application-notes/optics/introduction-to-polarization/](https://www.edmundoptics.com/knowledge-center/application-notes/optics/introduction-to-polarization/)

While most LED’s are electricity polarized they do not necessarily put out polarized light (some LED’s do). Reflected light off water, glass etc - from any source, natural or artificial - becomes polarized.

Back in the day, as a professional photographer, if I was photographing a shinny object under incandescent studio lights I would have to put a polarizing filter on the lights **to polarize them** and use another polarizing filter on the lens so I could control the glare.

Also see from UC Davis:
\- In fact “natural” light from light bulbs and the sun is “unpolarized,” which comes about because each of the individual light sources (atoms) are aligned in random orientations, and all send out random, unaligned light waves.

### [2.6: Polarization](https://phys.libretexts.org/Courses/University_of_California_Davis/UCD%3A_Physics_9HC__Introduction_to_Waves_Physical_Optics_and_Quantum_Theory/2%3A_Physical_Optics/2.6%3A_Polarization)

Transverse waves in three dimensions like light have a degree of freedom related to the plane into which the transverse "displacement" occurs. For light in particular this leads to …

 phys.libretexts.org

### Manan — 2025-08-27T06:21:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-degree-burn-suggestions.31557/post-358789>

Context:

I live in Himachal Pradesh, India (**Latitude:** 32°). About 12 hours before this writing I had a superficial burn caused by steam through a gas stove, it affected my forehead, eyebrows and skin right under eyes. After 15 seconds I had water pouring over my head( 3 minutes), it felt hot and when I moved my felt very hot. Then I put my head in ice water for about 2 minutes. Then I went to ER with frozen corn reducing my burning sensation(no ice pack at home).

Followed SOP of BP and O2 level at hospital and had a teatnus injection. Thirty minutes later I was applied silver sulfadiazine( white paste/liquid) for reducing murning sensation from minimal to none even though it was not needed. Got my eyes checked after an hour, they were good with little dryness. Now 12 hours later I feel good and have few dark spots. While writing this I touched some of it to see that my skin peeled off and is healed with white skin(non tan).

Follow up:
I plan to sit under sun for Blue and UVA light to help skin regenerate faster with NO and POMC for skin pigmentation. Is there anything else I could do or anything I did wrong?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-27T13:56:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-degree-burn-suggestions.31557/post-358795>

Read the photorepair blog. All one needs.

# Thread: NIRA Lighting
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nira-lighting.31560/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rusty — 2025-08-27T21:31:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nira-lighting.31560/post-358813>

**NIRA Lighting by Scott Zimmerman**

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

They claim to emit NIR which is true

But the _amount_ is the problem.

1x 60W incandescent gives you **~7,873 mW/m² IR**

1x NIRA A19 gives you : **540 mW/m²**

1x NIRA BR30 gives you : **292 mW/m²**

Do the math:

You’d need

15x A19s ($450)

or

27x BR30s ($810)

to match 1x $5 Incandescent IR output.

_Why are people falling for this?_

### riksa — 2025-08-27T22:20:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nira-lighting.31560/post-358817>

Same reason they are buying supplements stacks and elaborate courses.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-28T07:17:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nira-lighting.31560/post-358831>

From his view point I take it - he believes he is at the edge of all "new" research and frequently underplays research that was already done in the 80's.

I understand his angle of attack on the centralised lighting standards with respect to the "energy" efficiency curve requirements, which beats the standard blue rich fittings, but ultimately, his solution is just weak artificial polarised light intended to fit in the centralised box of high lumen/W range.

He is pushing for a change to get his bulbs in (based on the IR component being considered).. As I understand the DOE (like many other authorities) considers the IR component to be only wasted energy, thus not considered in the lm/W calcs.. He wants the NIR to be considered as useful output (i.e. not waste). Which is a step in the right direction, however, it will never match the incandescent. Better than standard blue rich lighting, yes, however, my choice would be the incandescent for obvious reasons failing that the halogen would be next best in line.. Would his output be therapeutic - no, however at that low output could it reduce cumulative retinal stress - likely.. If that is all he is able to achieve in the current centralised system, then some improvement is better than none on a large scale policy level...

Would I buy his bulbs - no..

Hello Uncle Jack, I really loved your recent post in your Journal entitled Echoes of the Shadow: A Journey of the Seeker;
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-journey-2019-and-beyond.23037/post-358892](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-journey-2019-and-beyond.23037/post-358892)

1\. What you wrote is what I aspire to, learning to see the inner darkness, to try to understand it, to extract light from that dark which diminishes that darkness and grows the light of inner wisdom, empathy, unity etc, the proof of your inner work having embraced the suck/chaos of any moment that triggers a negative response. Trouble is that negative response however small or big can sometimes be lightning fast then the remorse of that comes a second later. Its not easy to consistently respond without any negativity as your first response, especially when tired. Yet, I think circadian rhythm health helps to acquire a base level of equanimity from which to be able to explore these inner workings and to work upon them. An inner war to get to and maintain inner peace.

There are things in nature which point to this process metaphorically or as a living symbol of the human condition and potential, particularly the yearly cycle of Solstices and Equinoxes with their increasing light or increasing darkness. Various spiritual texts from many traditions alude to this process also.

I tend to think this process is the primary reason why creation exists (if it exists by design), that a person might choose to work through this process of a journey of enlightenment, evolving higher consciousness, overcoming inner darkness. Your wounds create your wisdom, moreso if you are actively seeking to learn from life as it happens, so you can hold that wisdom as an imprint on your soul and apply it in any given moment, and pass tests in the school of life, to one day graduate...

Does the above resonate Jack, what do you think?

2\. Do you see the sun as being something more than just a physical fireball in space which programs our biology, but also as something metaphysical or inter-dimensional with which a person can commune?

3\. Is there something the ancient peoples knew, who venerated the sun, which modern humanity does not or has forgotten?

4\. do you think reincarnation exists? There are stories of kids who remember their last life and then go meet the people who used to be in their life and can verify their memories are true.

5\. what do you think of out of body experiences or so called near death experiences where it seems the consciousness can exist outside of the physical body? People can relate what happened in the operating room when it was known they were clinically brain dead at that time.

6\. do you think astrology has any truth to it, that the planets really can exert a psychological effect on us or influence our personalities? What about physically effect us as well?

7\. I don't normally follow astrology but I have become aware there is a rare triangle alignment happening at the moment with Uranus, Neptune and Pluto which is said to bring about a cultural, scientific, spiritual renaissance, so it is very interesting you made such a spirutally seeking post at this time. Are you feeling that?

# Thread: Is the 5G antenna actually turned off? (Guide)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 56
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rusty — 2025-08-30T21:05:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-358913>

Here’s a quick hack to check if 5G is truly off on your phone

**For iPhone users:**

→ Go to your keypad

→ Type **\*3001#12345#\***

→ If it says LTE

You’re on 4G, the 5G radio isn’t being used.

→ If it says NR (New Radio) or NR-SA / NR-NSA.

That’s true 5G.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**For most Androids:**

Type: \*#\*#4636#\*#\*

Go to Phone Information, scroll down

You’ll see which network type is active (LTE, NR etc)

**For most Samsung’s:**

Dial \*#0011#

If none of the above works,

Download **Network Signal Info/NetMonster** from Play Store.

### Julz — 2025-09-16T10:51:49+0300

### Rusty — 2025-09-16T22:51:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-359616>

> [S Hudson said:](/goto/post?id=359508)
>
> what is the benefit turning off the phones ability to use 5G with the signal being pumped all around us?

5G uses beamforming technology where antennas focus signals directly toward active devices rather than radiating equally in all directions

That’s why it radiates much less than existing 2G, 3G, 4G technologies in all directions

but..

MUCH MORE in the direction of the 5G equipped user.

What does it mean?

It means that if you do not have a 5G device in your home or your hand, there will be only minimal radiation from the 5G base station (including satellites)

It also means that, if you use a 5G device, the power density radiated in your direction from the base station will depend significantly on the amount & speed of data you require.

If you are standing with a friend & he is watching a 4K movie on a 5G smartphone..then you both are radiated a hell of a lot more than while your friend is doing a phone call or texting. Most people do not realize that the power density needed to stream 4K video is thousands of times higher than what’s needed for a phone call.

That’s why

1\. Stay away from packed areas where everyone and their dog is glued to their 5G devices
2\. Turn off the 5G antenna on your cellphone

### Mat.I — 2025-09-21T01:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-359773>

> Mat I super value your input, are you able to comment on the biological impact of carrying a 5G capable handset (the 5G cannot be disabled due to grapheneOS) however it would not actually be using 5G in El Salvador as they have not rolled out there (yet) so I'd expect it would not be receiving focused signals directly with the beam forming technology used for 5G as no 5G towers would be sending signals - would this handset therefore be considered inactive 5G wise, or would it still be trying to actively connect to a 5G signal from somewhere, therefore incurring 5G damage to the person despite not using it?

There are standards that govern this like building codes / regulations but for mobile devices aka user equipment. My understanding is if there are no 5G networks the phone would only sniff for them at the firmware intervals. But the sniffing should not transmit (should.only be listen/receive only). That is it should only transmit when it detects the network (with uplink established) this is standard telecommunications protocols.

I do not have experience withGraphene OS phone (and personally would not buy one) but I find it strange how you cannot select your network preferences as this is a stock standard setting... If you cannot access via setting surely there would be a "hidden" menu access code?? Maybe search for this online.

Obviously, if you wanted absolute concrete certainty you need to log the frequencies with a spectrum analyzer fitted with the appropriate antenna that suits the target frequencies (inclusive of a signal decoder software to see SSB structure)..

If there is no 5G where you are, there should be no transmission other than intermittent sniffing (listening only).

Am curious why you would not use GrapheneOS?

Thank you!

### Rusty — 2025-09-21T12:05:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-359791>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=359677)
>
> Are you referring to the duty cycle (i.e. not actually the power density)? As the PD would only slightly be elevated. How was this determined? Or is it just an opinion?

You are right. I was really talking about the duty cycle not the instantaneous power density.

The beam stays “on” far more often, so over time you absorb way more energy. My point was about the load on the link & how it scales exposure.

### Rusty — 2025-09-21T12:08:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-359792>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=359667)
>
> Rusty, your thoughts on if a much shorter exposure to a 5G down load (a few seconds for a movie) is better or worse than a much longer 4G download (multiple minuets for the same movie)?
> Do they even stream phone calls on a 5G signal or do they drop down to 3G/4G?

From a biological signaling perspective, the sharp 5G spikes may be worse. Our cells respond more to pulsed, high peak bursts than to steady lower levels.

### Mat.I — 2025-09-22T02:35:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-359809>

> [warriorT said:](/goto/post?id=359789)
>
> That is super helpful thank you. Would the antenna try to connect to 5G satellites in low earth orbit in the absence of 5G towers - could an uplink be established that way?

I thought this was only experimental and trial phases. The front ends & antenna of the devices would only be tuned to terrestrial 3GPP bands. If you are referring to the 5G-NTN (this is also only in trials phases as I understand). Whilst some phones may have the modem hardware, carriers would have to support this + it would need it to also have firmware to support.. I know they have standardised the 5G-NTN in recent 3GPP specs (based on what I have read on this), however it is in very early trial stages. All this means is the standard exists and R&D is being done.. True 5G - NTN may happen on a commercial level in some countries over the next few years... For this to be active you need the hardware + firmware + carrier provisioning + sat infrastructure.. I do not expect this to be an issue for you at this stage.

> [warriorT said:](/goto/post?id=359789)
>
> There is a way to disable 5G and use 4G only, however I understand much of El Salvador uses 3G and 2G - there does not seem to be any way to have just 2/3/4G only with a model that has 5G capability.

Are you in ES now? If you want to experiment, grab a ES sim and you may find you have other options based on the sim carrier profile e.g. 2G/3G/4G.. You will likely not get the option if you are in a place &/or with a carrier where certain bands have been shut down etc..

> [warriorT said:](/goto/post?id=359789)
>
> Am curious why you would not use GrapheneOS?

Does not make any sense to me.. Just on a very basic level it is software only (i.e. band aid) over shitty hardware. Every so called benefit/layered control is on a software level only.. Maybe I'm being to critical.. But it just does not really make sense to me that's all if your goal is max sovereignty & hardware level control when using the device.. Depends on why you "need" the phone and "what" is used for.. If you go minimalist with the above goal in mind then there will be trade offs for "convenience" with virtually no or minimal apps on the phone.. This decision is very individual - like BTC security & privacy, some will go all out and some will do nothing at all or kid themselves saying privacy does not matter...

### Rusty — 2025-10-08T02:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-360633>

Type **\*3001#12345#\*** on your iPhone Keypad

You’ll find the Field Test Meter.

Of all the numbers there,

**RSRP** is the most important.

**RSRP = Reference Signal Received Power**

It tells you the Cellular signal quality.

and is a direct reflection of how hard your phone has to work & how much it radiates.

The weaker this number gets,

The louder your phone shouts back at the tower.

**above -90 dBm →** Strong, healthy signal

**\-90 to -100 dBm →** Your phone starts working harder

**Below -100 dBm →** Phone ramps up transmit power increasing radiation

**\-110 dBm or lower →** Max transmit power, Max radiation

If RSRP _is below -100 dBm_, that means your phone might be transmitting upto _**1000x more RF Radiation**_

### Mat.I — 2025-10-08T03:11:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-360636>

> [Rusty said:](/goto/post?id=360633)
>
> The weaker this number gets,
>
> The louder your phone shouts back at the tower.
>
> **above -90 dBm →** Strong, healthy signal
>
> **\-90 to -100 dBm →** Your phone starts working harder
>
> **Below -100 dBm →** Phone ramps up transmit power increasing radiation
>
> **\-110 dBm or lower →** Max transmit power, Max radiation
>
> If RSRP _is below -100 dBm_, that means your phone might be transmitting upto _**1000x more RF Radiation**_

People often miss this basic operation principle.. Good to see you mentioning this.
Re: 1000 x more RF based on transmit (locally at device this should be caped at ~ 200mW) however, where this becomes a problem is the duty cycle.. E.g. if you assumed an RSRP of - 80dBm @ tx ~ 10mW say duty cycle 0.05% vs -110 dBm @ ~ 200mW duty cycle 10% could result in ~ 4000 x more. 10mW x 0.0005 = 0.005mW vs 200mW x 0.10 = 20mW. Now 20mW/0.005mW = 4000 x

### Rusty — 2025-11-05T14:03:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-362194>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=360957)
>
> Never took notice of the duty cycle on the cornet, but I can now confirm it is there.. I doubt it would be absolutely accurate though. Likely be an ratio average of peak signals over a time duration. I guess could be a good proxy for activity density.. e.g. choppy vs continuous but this is fairly easy to distinguish by sound alone e.g. cell tower vs wifi and so on..

Got a response from one of the members from the RD-100 development team

`"I think that the Duty Cycle is essential for the analysis. It is not 100% accurate since there are usually many signals in the air...for a better picture of the signal, use the RF Modulation mode, both in 5mSec resolution and 1mSec resolution. I predict that if there is 2 signals on the air, one of 5% with 10mW/m2 peaks, and the second one of 5% with 5mW/m2, the RD-100 will show 10% on the duty cycle."`

### Anthony Smith — 2026-01-05T20:04:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-5g-antenna-actually-turned-off-guide.31573/post-364794>

ANY phone is traceable/trackable and gives off nnEMF when in use.
ANY phone is not traceable/trackable when turned off AND in a Faraday bag.
You may invest in a phone that you can disconnect the battery BUT the second you turn it on “they” have you, so what’s the point?

Also keep in mind most countries have cameras everywhere. They can/are tracking your car AND many have digital face recognition.

AND . . . IF you use credit/debit cards they know where you are AND what you bought.

I do not have one. Of course, when you reconnect the battery it will ping. I have a satellite phone for back up that gives me 150 min. a month that cumulate and don't expire. I don't keep it on. I guess the best would be to buy throw aways (burner phone). Use and toss. Most ppl don't pay attention to the Stingray antenna's law enforcement use at intersections. Pretty controversial, invasion of privacy. We already live in a controlled censored society.

### ColoPE — 2025-08-31T01:45:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proper-use-of-emr-tek-lighting.31574/post-358927>

Hey all, I have a Krypton UV / IR light from EMR-TEK. Their guide talks in terms of under 10 minutes use for certain organs, but it doesn't even differentiate between UV and IR. I have searched here and in the blogs for some guidance on best use. This would be for adding to the sun we get daily - up to 4 hours. It is especially for use once it gets really cold here to sit out (CO). We are looking for therapeutic guidance, not maintenance. I'm guessing one should use red only in the early parts of the day and UV only midday, but I have no idea how much is too much.

Also, in light of the 80 year old that used Jack's light and cured cataracts, is it a bad idea not to use the covering goggles that came with it? Guidance appreciated.

# Thread: Carris’ Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carris%E2%80%99-optimal-journal.31576/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Misscc76 — 2025-08-31T06:50:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carris%E2%80%99-optimal-journal.31576/post-358938>

Hello,
I am 48 yrs old residing in Chicago, Il.In recent years I’ve struggled with constipation, bloating, and losing weight
( want to lose 40-60 lbs$I also have trouble staying sleep and have eczema on my fingers and one toe. Several factors contribute to these issues but I’m not sure how to tackle them:
I work midnights ( police)
I have teen twins with special needs one on the autism spectrum the other with ADHD both have their challenges .
My gallbladder was removed in 2012 due to my ignorance and painful stones.
I have silver amalgam fillings since childhood and got root canals in recent years.

I was induced at 37 weeks had c section and epidural.

My mother is 72 and weaned off of benzodiazepines and other meds for anxiety but has lots of withdrawals and says that she is losing her eye sight.

My daughter on the autism spectrum is 13 but recently stopped talking and stopped drinking liquids. She also has what it appears to be bad eczema and has stomach pain that I can’t seem to help resolve even after seeing many doctors and getting an esophageal scope. She is always in distress which stresses me since I haven’t been able to help her.

My daughter with ADHD struggles in school, executive functioning ,and certain aspects of volleyball ( coaches don’t understand ). Her AIC is also high and she has keratosis Pilaris.

I have a 50 yr old brother on the autism spectrum.

# Thread: Sources of fluoride ingestion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-fluoride-ingestion.31581/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-08-31T19:21:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sources-of-fluoride-ingestion.31581/post-358977>

I live in CA. I know that the tap water is fluoridated, so I drink RO water or bottled spring water.

I was wondering about other sources of fluoride ingestion. Would local crops like fruits + vegetables have non-negligible amounts of fluoride? I did some basic research and read that (correct me if I'm wrong) water used for agricultural irrigation doesn't go through the same treatment process as tap water. My assumption then is that even if there is naturally-occurring fluoride, there wouldn't be any added fluoride.

Nevertheless, with tap water being fluoridated, and CA allowing recycled wastewater to be used for irrigation, I would think this recycling process is not going to filter out fluoride, since that's way too expensive, so eventually crops will be doused in tons of fluoride.

Does anybody know more about this?

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-for-headaches.31587/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### quantumrobin87 — 2025-09-03T08:26:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-for-headaches.31587/post-359070>

Hi there,

I’m new to this forum but have been deep diving into Dr Kruse’ s work since I discovered him in late 2023 thanks to Rick Rubin.
Since then, I’ve been trying to use as much of Uncle Jack decentralised thesis to reset my circadian biology and help me remain disease free for as long as I can.
Like many of you, I watch the sunrise every morning, I wear blue blockers from sunset and I try and mitigate Nnemf exposure and tech abuse as much. But I live in Australia and was an ignoramus in 2021 when the jabs got rolled out and I got two. This was before I came to learn of Jack's work, and I know the price of compliance is now higher than ever as I learn more and more about what actually happened.

Now that I’m awake, I’m determined to do better.

About 5 months ago, I started waking up with tension headaches. It’s around the back of my head, around the neck and usually disappears in a couple of hours but it’s every day. If I wake up in the middle of the night to pee, I don’t have a headache, but upon waking it starts to creep. Also during the winter months, my back, neck and shoulder blade is often very tight and knotted. I used to think exercise would help but now I know it's environmental and those silent weapons have a big role to play in this symptom.

Does anyone know where I can find resources around headaches and migraines in Jack’s blogs or website? I want to know if this is a POMC, circadian, Nnemf toxicity, low redox or jab issue.

### Rusty — 2025-09-03T11:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-for-headaches.31587/post-359075>

It can be a lot of things but let's start with nnEMF toxicity

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-for-headaches.31587/post-359088>

Uncle Jack also talks about flicker in lighting and screens causing migraine headaches - see part way down in the below post as an example. The IRIS program cuts flicker from screens for a small price. Avoiding indoor lighting and screens may help.

He also mentions using ice/cold on the sides of your neck where arteries go to brain - and cooling the brain this way helps. I've been testing this out for occasional headaches and it seems to work well for me. Sinus headaches not so much, but there are other ways to work with those.

> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insulin-thoughts.22503/post-258554](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insulin-thoughts.22503/post-258554)

Thanks Jacks! That's really helpful. I have Flux, but I realise this doesn't deal with flicker. Just downloaded IRIS. Will try the cool / ice technique too. Often the headache feels very hot around the back of my head and neck so hoping that helps alleviate some of the pain.

### quantumrobin87 — 2025-09-04T03:51:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-for-headaches.31587/post-359105>

> [hacklose said:](/goto/post?id=359093)
>
> How are you currently measuring nnemf in your environment?

This is what I'm looking into next. If you have any recommendations for meters, I would be most grateful.

### hacklose — 2025-09-04T18:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-for-headaches.31587/post-359126>

> [quantumrobin87 said:](/goto/post?id=359105)
>
> This is what I'm looking into next. If you have any recommendations for meters, I would be most grateful.

I prefer Safe and Sound's collection of meters, but they are more expensive. I've also used the Trifield TF2 and a few Cornet meters. In my experience, the Trifield TF2 isn't accurate compared to the others I've used, so I don't recommend that one.

For a low budget meter that will get you going, the Cornet ED98QPro5g is a good choice.
It can measure 1D electric fields, 1D magnetic fields, RF(microwaves), and light flicker all in one unit.

I recommend the Safe and Sound Pro II, which only measures RF, in addition to the Cornet. The Safe and Sound Pro II has a much higher sampling rate for RF. It samples 8 times more per second when compared to the Cornet.

I just recently bought a Safe and Sound EM3 and really like it so far.
It does 1D electric fields, 3D magnetic fields, body voltage, and has some useful filters.

I use these three, along with a stetzer dirty electricity meter and an AM radio when I'm searching for nnemf.

# Thread: Help stray dogs and cats in El Salvador
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-stray-dogs-and-cats-in-el-salvador.31593/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sherry Lotterer — 2025-09-03T23:09:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-stray-dogs-and-cats-in-el-salvador.31593/post-359094>

Hi Jack,

I've been in El Salvador for a year. A while ago, I teamed up with a young Salvadorian veterinarian to rescue stray dogs and cats. She is open to mito way, interested in Platinum membership. I gave her the book **Body Electric**. My focus is mainly fundraising. However, being so close to the veterinary activity, I have some burning questions. I list below the most common issues, its associated go-to treatment by vet in red, my observation in _purple_. I would like to know **your** diagnosis of each issue and optimal approach for remedy/prevention, debunk myths. We are operating on shoestring.. need to apply mitochondrial wisdom to maximize/optimize our time, effort, resources. We are a small non profit with bitcoin treasury.

1\. Transmissible Venereal Tumors (TVT) - Vincristine.
_TVT is rampant. We see this in dogs coming from the beach, where they should be super healthy. Why is it so rampant? I am guessing it might have to do with environment, and dogs and cats are very sensitive. _With vincristine, i_t seems about 50% of dogs respond well and recover. Seems a bit pseudo science._
2\. Prevent or treat flea/tick infestation - Bravecto or Semperica for dogs; Revolution or Advantage for cats
3\. Prevent rabies - Vaccine on schedule
_I think this is not needed at all, but perhaps I am wrong. People are so fearful that if they get bitten by a stray animal, they will get rabies. What is the probability of humans contracting rabies from stray dogs or cats? For animal export, countries mandate rabies vaccine for dogs and cats._
4\. Too many stray animals - Spay and neuter
_I think if nature intended for these animals to proliferate, humans should not interfere. But there are so many animals with no ownership, so many animals injured/killed on the roads. Don't know the best solution_. _We do work with dog and cat sanctuaries. I am assessing on augmenting those partnerships._
5\. Wounds - Clean wound. Cover to protect/keep clean. Use gentian violet and penecillin ointment. Sometimes amputate.
_I think the wounds would heal better uncovered, receiving sun, use collodial silver, be grounded in emf-safe environment. The vet knows about silver, but rarely gets it because it's expensive._

Following my passion,
Sherry

### DLO — 2025-09-08T20:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-stray-dogs-and-cats-in-el-salvador.31593/post-359296>

1\. Hematoxylin + dmso is huge for tumors even internal look on brighteon.ai
2\. Hard for strays but i use nematodes in my yard and keep it well watered as strong uv/heat with no rain kills them.
3+5 I've had some pretty wild open tears and none of the animals have gotten infected using simple hydrogen peroxide spray to clean the wound and a combo of helichrysum/lavender/frankincense straight into the wound to help it seal. No antibiotics required.

### Sherry Lotterer — 2025-09-12T20:54:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-stray-dogs-and-cats-in-el-salvador.31593/post-359428>

Hi [@DLO](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19314/),

Thank you for this info! Can you please clarify..

1\. TVT - Treat with hematoxylin+dmso. How do you dose?
2\. Prevent or treat flea/tick infestation - Nematodes in yard - This is the first I ever hear of Nematodes. Where do I get this in North America. In Central America? How to set it up?
3\. Prevent rabies - helichrysum/lavender/frankincense - How?
4\. Too many stray animals - Gave this some thought.. I think the best approach with what we are facing in the beach communities is to relocate the dogs and cats when an area gets overun.
5\. Wounds - helichrysum/lavender/frankincense - We can try. I saw [@SimoneS](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31594/) post elsewhere about propolis. Both sound interesting.

I enumerate below 2 more categories where I am also looking for answers, if you care to comment:

6\. Severe wounds with loss of movement in limb or tail - This is where I am curious about use of silver with grounding
7\. Anethesia using magnets - I read about this in Body Electric. Would you dare to try it on a pet? If so what magnets/strength?

Thanks!
Sherry

Every day, her health declined and she had terrible vomiting and diarrhea. She became very weak and lost the ability to walk/stand/sit. The vet tested her reflexes and confirmed she had neurologic disorder. She was in bad shape, we almost put her down. But she had this undeniable spark. We decided to keep her outside 24/7, grounded, ensure she got maximum exposure to sunrise and sunset everyday. Smothered her with love and patience. Eventually she stood/sat again, but had to lean on a wall or a person for support, often quickly sliding back to lie-down position. For 3 months, there was very meager incremental improvement and her movement was weak, unsteady, and uncoordinated. The vet continued testing her reflexes, confirming neurologic disorder every time. At the end of 3 months, she started walking again and started improving rapidly. Today, it is 4 months later. She is 100% recovered. Looking at her now, you cannot believe she was near death. Smith (1977) - "Magnetic Anesthesia in Cats"</b><br>- Published in the journal <i>Anesthesiology</i>, this study aimed to replicate Becker's findings in a different animal model.<br>- Method: The researchers applied a 1400 Gauss static magnetic field to the heads of cats, using a Helmholtz coil system, while monitoring the animals' brain activity and vital signs.<br>- Results: They observed a significant decrease in brain activity, similar to that induced by barbiturate anesthesia, within 30 seconds of applying the magnetic field. The cats remained anesthetized for the duration of the magnetic field application and woke up immediately upon its removal, without any observable side effects.<br>- Reference: Ancill JP, Smith JE. Magnetic anesthesia in cats. Anesthesiology. 1977;47(5):373-377. doi:10.1097/00000539-197711000-00015<br>2. <b>Dr. Thomas J. Priore and Dr. Nancy L. Porsawa (1999) - "Magnetic Anesthesia in Mice"</b><br>- This study, published in the <i>Journal of Magnetobiology and Magnetic Materials</i>, further validated the use of magnetic fields for anesthesia in a rodent model.<br>- Method: The researchers exposed mice to a 1200 Gauss static magnetic field for various durations, while observing their behavior and physiological responses.<br>- Results: They found that mice exposed to the magnetic field for 15-30 seconds exhibited loss of consciousness, similar to that induced by chemical anesthetics. The mice quickly regained consciousness upon removal from the magnetic field, with no observable adverse effects.<br>- Reference: Priore TJ, Porsawa NL. Magnetic anesthesia in mice. J Magnetism Magn Mater. 1999;200(1-3):332-336. doi:10.1016/s0304-8853(99)00115-2<br>These experiments, along with Becker's original work, demonstrate that magnetic fields can induce anesthesia-like states in various animal models, offering a potential alternative to chemical anesthetics. However, further research is needed to fully understand the underlying mechanisms and to develop safe and effective clinical applications in humans.​</div></td></tr></tbody></table>
