# Thread: Atrial Fibrillation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/atrial-fibrillation.31595/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MillieMartire — 2025-09-04T03:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/atrial-fibrillation.31595/post-359100>

Hi. I have been following Jack for a little under a year through podcasts and reading blogs. I am a new member and just joined in the last month or two. Last week I was taken to ER due to rapid heart rate. I get it off and on. My iWatch alerted me to posible AFIB last year, but I have stopped wearing that due to emf and listening to Jack and following protocols. In ER I was in afib and they controlled it with medication. I have not taken any meds in 4 years (used to be on BP meds). I hate it that I am back. I practice circadian rhythm and wake up and go outside for sunrise and watch sunset and get sunlight any chance I get, especially at noon. I live in the Washington DC area and just listed our house to move out of here. I had lost a lot of weight (100 lbs) a few years ago on keto and IF and made the mistake of going on Semiglutide to lose the last 50. I had some adverse reactions, but stopped taking it when I heard Jack talk about it and did some research. No matter what I do the weight has come back (all except about 30 lbs) - I am starting the EPI paleo RX and have made changes to eat locally sourced and in season. I have purchased some red lights from EMR tek but they are taking forever to arrive. I try and do cold plunges as I am able. I am a member of Dr. Alexis bookclub and am trying to reduce nnEMFs. I work on the computer and need a couple more years of working before I can retire. We are trying to move lower lattitude and thought florida would be it, but my elderly mom and my sister need help in TX. WE are considering TX (Outside Dallas or outside Houston) until I heard Jack on the Sept Powow and he said neither is good. I am under an incredible amount of stress and now this afib has added to that. I would love some pointers on what I can do until the house sells and we can move. Also, I cannot seem to find anything on afib, so help locating info would be appreciated. I am working my way through the blogs and material. Thank you. I am excited to be here with like minded savages.

### Branden G — 2025-09-06T18:22:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/atrial-fibrillation.31595/post-359187>

I would say control what you can. The added stress isn't helping your heart any. Are you a mouth breather? I personally would think getting to lower latitude and around family would be beneficial. No huge metro area is great but if you can get out of a major city I would. Keep getting sun it may take months.

### MillieMartire — 2025-09-08T15:13:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/atrial-fibrillation.31595/post-359276>

> [Branden G said:](/goto/post?id=359187)
>
> I would say control what you can. The added stress isn't helping your heart any. Are you a mouth breather? I personally would think getting to lower latitude and around family would be beneficial. No huge metro area is great but if you can get out of a major city I would. Keep getting sun it may take months.

Thanks Branden! I was a mouth breather for a long time and a few months ago started using mouth tape and have been doing well with that after a couple of weeks to adjust.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-10-28T04:22:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/atrial-fibrillation.31595/post-361731>

Light at night and heart disease links are strong! This was just published this today in JAMA Network Open.

Using 13 million hours of personal light exposure data in >88,000 people, they found that those with the brightest nights had significantly higher risks of developing coronary artery disease, myocardial infarction, heart failure, **atrial fibrillation,** and stroke.

These associations were robust after adjusting for established cardiovascular risk factors, including physical activity, smoking, alcohol, diet, sleep duration, socioeconomic status, and polygenic risk. Keep your homes dark at night and get in the sun for photorepair of your arteries and heart!

[https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489)

### MillieMartire — 2025-10-28T14:10:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/atrial-fibrillation.31595/post-361749>

Thank you. I have been blocking blue light and night light for 18 mo. Not enough to undo the damage yet (I was a night owl for 40 years) - moving south to a better light environment trying to reduce nnEMFs within 8 weeks. I will keep reading and learning with a thankful heart that I found this info and even though I am older I can begin to heal!

### Mat.I — 2025-10-29T00:39:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/atrial-fibrillation.31595/post-361790>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=361731)
>
> Light at night and heart disease links are strong! This was just published this today in JAMA Network Open.
>
> Using 13 million hours of personal light exposure data in >88,000 people, they found that those with the brightest nights had significantly higher risks of developing coronary artery disease, myocardial infarction, heart failure, **atrial fibrillation,** and stroke.
>
> These associations were robust after adjusting for established cardiovascular risk factors, including physical activity, smoking, alcohol, diet, sleep duration, socioeconomic status, and polygenic risk. Keep your homes dark at night and get in the sun for photorepair of your arteries and heart!
>
> [https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489)

The "night" light exposure windows could have been tightened to get more accurate data. E.g.

Exposure: Personal Light Tracking
Extraction of personal day and night light exposures from wrist-worn light sensor data in this cohort has been previously reported.[32](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489#zoi251081r32) ,[33](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489#zoi251081r33),[35](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489#zoi251081r35) In short, sensor data (100 Hz) were downsampled, cleaned for periods of nonwear and data corruption, and transformed according to the device manual and subsequent testing under reference lighting conditions[35](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489#zoi251081r35) (eMethods in [Supplement 1](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489#note-ZOI251081-1)). Data were then grouped into 24-hour light exposure profiles for each participant, represented by mean light exposure within each of the 48 half-hour clock time intervals (eg, all light between 12:00 AM to 12:30 AM). Factor analysis was applied to the 24-hour light exposure profiles.[35](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489#zoi251081r35) This unsupervised analysis revealed 2 temporal light exposure clusters, which we labeled as day (07:30 AM to 8:30 PM) and night (12:30 AM to 06:00 AM) (eMethods in [Supplement 1](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489#note-ZOI251081-1)).

As "night" should begin post sunset when solar angle = less than 0? This would be the most circadian sensitive phase. They left a lot on the table here as they captured the worst of the worst night owls or disturbed sleepers.. Would have been good to have a contrast here to see the effects. Night should be defined by solar angle not clock time..

E.g. solar angle based or chronotype aligned definition of this (night) would have permitted biologically meaningful comparisons across seasons, and would have better quantified how > sunset light exposure, rather than late night illumination alone, contributes to cardiovascular risk. They would have this raw data available.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-09-04T16:28:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mrna-vaccines-currently-in-use-in-usa-cattle.31598/post-359120>

Grok:
Based on available information, the use of mRNA-based vaccines in livestock in the United States is limited, and most approved livestock vaccines are not mRNA-based. The USDA’s Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) and its Center for Veterinary Biologics (CVB) regulate veterinary biologics, including vaccines, and provide details on licensed products. Below is an analysis of mRNA-based vaccines for livestock, drawing from relevant sources:

### mRNA-Based Vaccines for Livestock​

1.  **Swine**:
    -   **SEQUIVITY (Merck Animal Health)**: This is the primary mRNA-based vaccine currently licensed for use in livestock in the U.S. Introduced in 2018, SEQUIVITY utilizes RNA particle technology to create customized prescription vaccines for swine. It targets strains of influenza A virus, porcine circovirus (PCV), rotavirus, sapovirus, and other pathogens.
    -   Specific products include:
        -   19A5.RC H1, RNA Particle 165A
        -   19A5.RD H1, RNA Particle 165A
        -   19A5.R8 N1 & N2, RNA Particle 165A
    -   These vaccines are tailored for specific herds and are not broadly applied across all swine populations.
2.  **Cattle**:
    -   **No mRNA Vaccines Approved**: As of the latest updates (2024-2025), no mRNA vaccines are licensed by the USDA for use in cattle. Research and trials for mRNA vaccines in cattle are ongoing, particularly for diseases like bovine respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) and Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza (HPAI). For example, Iowa State University is testing a novel mRNA vaccine for RSV, but it is not yet approved for commercial use.
    -   Field trials are underway for H5N1 vaccine candidates in dairy cows, but these are still in the experimental phase, and no mRNA-based vaccines for cattle have received USDA approval.
3.  **Poultry**:
    -   **Conditional License for Avian Influenza**: A conditional RNA-based vaccine for avian influenza (H5N2 Subtype, Killed Virus) has been licensed for poultry, as noted in a 2025 announcement. While described as an RNA vaccine, it is not explicitly confirmed as mRNA-based in the same way as SEQUIVITY. It may use a related RNA particle technology.
    -   Research is ongoing for mRNA vaccines targeting HPAI in poultry, but no fully licensed mRNA vaccines are currently available for widespread use.
4.  **Other Species**:
    -   A rabies vaccine (1905.D1 RNA Particle 165A) is listed as an RNA-based vaccine, but the species it applies to is not specified in available data. It is likely used in limited or experimental contexts.
    -   No mRNA vaccines are approved for other livestock species like sheep, goats, or horses, though research may be in progress.

### Key Points​

-   **Limited Use**: The only confirmed mRNA-based vaccine currently in use is SEQUIVITY for swine. Other RNA-based vaccines (e.g., for avian influenza or rabies) may use related technologies but are not explicitly mRNA-based or are conditionally licensed.
-   **Cattle and Poultry**: No mRNA vaccines are approved for cattle, and only one conditional RNA vaccine exists for poultry. Research is active, particularly for HPAI and RSV, but these are not yet commercially available.
-   **Regulatory Oversight**: All vaccines, including mRNA-based ones, undergo rigorous USDA APHIS and CVB review for safety, efficacy, and quality. Withdrawal periods ensure no vaccine components remain in meat or milk, addressing food safety concerns.
-   **Data Gaps**: The USDA’s Veterinary Biologics Product Summaries do not always specify the technology (e.g., mRNA vs. killed virus) for every vaccine. For a complete list, you would need to cross-reference product codes and manufacturer details, which may require contacting the CVB directly.

### How to Find More Details​

To identify which vaccines in the USDA’s database are mRNA-based:

1.  Visit the **APHIS Veterinary Biologics Product Summaries**: [APHIS Product Summaries](https://www.aphis.usda.gov/aphis/ourfocus/animalhealth/veterinary-biologics/product-summaries).
2.  Search by manufacturer (e.g., Merck Animal Health for SEQUIVITY) or product code (e.g., 19A5.RC for swine influenza).
3.  Check product descriptions or contact the CVB at [cvb@aphis.usda.gov](mailto:cvb@aphis.usda.gov) or (515) 337-6100 for clarification on whether a vaccine uses mRNA technology.
4.  Review CVB notices (e.g., CVB Notice 24-11, 24-13) for updates on new or conditional licenses, especially for HPAI vaccines.

### Challenges​

-   The USDA’s database does not explicitly tag vaccines as “mRNA-based,” so identifying them requires checking product descriptions or manufacturer details.
-   Ongoing research (e.g., for RSV or HPAI) means new mRNA vaccines may emerge, but they must pass USDA approval before use.
-   Misinformation on social media has exaggerated mRNA vaccine use in livestock, but current data confirms their limited application, primarily in swine.

### Paul T008 — 2025-09-05T14:17:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-moon-in-uk-7-september-2025.31601/post-359150>

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td>7 September 2025</td><td>Total lunar eclipse</td><td>Not to sure how much IR red there is is on Moon Lunar eclipse , Happy viewing<br>But The moon will rise above the horizon just in time for us to see this total lunar eclipse from the UK. The maximum will occur at 7.33pm BST from the UK, with the eclipse's actual maximum at 7.11pm when the Moon is below the horizon. The Moon will then gradually move out of Earth's umbra and penumbra until 9.55pm. As the Moon will be low on the horizon and quite difficult to see, find a high point with a clear view to the East to see the most of this eclipse.</td></tr></tbody></table>

### Schneg — 2025-09-06T16:21:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-qu%C3%A9bec-canada.31602/post-359184>

Hi everyone! I have been a silent lurker for a couple of month now, observing and learning slowly. I found uncle Jack on Spotify in May and since then
I can't stop listening to every podcast I find with him. I always felt something was wrong in this world, but never thought it was THAT bad. Thank you sir for your lifetime work. It is priceless, it's huge and inspiring.

I am a 44-year-old French Canadian and proud to be one of the few who refused the Covid vaccine around here. I never trusted that shit, it felt waaaaaay too suspicious. I was completely stunned to see the majority of people around me complying so easily, without question. I'm used to being a black sheep, but that one was hardcore.

I have few health problems, but enough to feel the need to seek solutions that my doctor seems unable to provide. Since I discovered Uncle Jack, I understand why now! I worked as a chef for 15 years and now as a carpenter/joiner for 6 years. So nutrition and physical health have always been present in my life, but I now understand that there are other, much more important issues. I've read Jack's book and have started reading Power, Sex and Suicide and The Body Electric. I've listened to several dozen hours of podcasts and I'm just starting to get a full picture of the issue. I still have a lot to learn, a lifetime in fact! But I think I've come to the right place to do it.

I started the leptin rx in June and I already see and feel result, it's amazing. I will probably be able to take 2 weeks off this winter and I would love to take the opportunity to visit El Salvador. I don't have a date yet or any specific plans but if you have any suggestions for a time when some of you will be there, I would love to take the opportunity to meet some of you! Since I'm very new to the Bitcoin universe (and because I'm a fucked Canadian), I don't have much money and will be traveling on a tight budget. So I'm open to any suggestions from those who are or have been in my situation.

Thank you uncle Jack for your wisdom and knowledge and thank you everyone for being here and sharing all that stuff.

See ya!
Patrick

# Thread: Eyes, brain, skull, and spine disease all begin here.........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eyes-brain-skull-and-spine-disease-all-begin-here.31603/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-06T19:56:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eyes-brain-skull-and-spine-disease-all-begin-here.31603/post-359190>

A new blog has been released with significant embryological implications for our modern world. Most won't see this wisdom, but I will force you to see what Nature is cooking for humanity if we persist. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-71-124096262](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-71-124096262)

While conventional medicine attributes nanophthalmos to genetic factors, such as mutations in PRSS56 or MFRP, which lead to compromised ocular growth, a decentralized medicine perspective shifts the focus to disrupted bioelectric buoyancy as the root cause. This buoyancy is a thermodynamic dance involving mitochondrial water production, melanin hydration, and photo-bioelectric gradients that sustain precise tissue expansion. Intrinsically tied to brain and skull development, it relies on ancient evolutionary adaptations from the Great Oxidation Event (GOE) around 2.4 billion years ago, when rising oxygen levels favored organisms with hydrated melanin and deuterium-depleted water (DDW) for efficient bioelectric signaling.

In this framework, the eye's growth falters when modern stressors, non-native electromagnetic fields (nnEMF) from devices and artificial light at night (ALAN, e.g., blue light), damage melanopsin (a light-sensitive protein in the retina), mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA), and the glyoxalase pathway (which detoxifies harmful byproducts). Dehydrated melanin transitions from a protective resistor to a hyper-conductive state, erasing the ultra-precise one-trillionth-amp DC currents needed for cellular renovation at cytochrome c oxidase, a key mitochondrial enzyme. Compounding this, contemporary diets and environments increase deuterium loading through the kinetic isotope effect (KIE), where heavier deuterium isotopes slow proton tunneling in the Grotthuss mechanism. This is a quantum process that facilitates efficient water conduction in cells. This isotopic burden, absent in GOE-adapted systems relying on DDW for optimal mitochondrial spin and energy transfer, hampers ocular morphogenesis, resulting in the short axial length characteristic of nanophthalmos.

The outcome?

A complex story of Turing's morphogenesis ideas in one disease. In 1952, Alan Turing, a British mathematician best known for his work on code-breaking and artificial intelligence, was convicted of engaging in homosexual acts and sentenced to chemical castration. Amid that personal drama, he still found the time to publish a visionary paper on the mathematics of regularly repeating patterns in nature, which could be applied to the stripes on tigers and zebra fish, the spots on leopards, and the spacing in rows of alligator teeth, to name a few. Turing's 1952 paper on morphogenesis reaction-diffusion model posits that patterns, such as spots, stripes, or even organ scaling, arise from the interplay of diffusible morphogens: an activator that promotes a trait and an inhibitor that suppresses it, diffusing at different rates to create instability in an otherwise homogeneous field. This model, once speculative, has gained empirical support in developmental biology, including eye formation, where morphogens like Sonic Hedgehog (Shh) and Fibroblast Growth Factor (FGF) act as inhibitors and activators, respectively.

In the context of nanophthalmos, a condition of proportionally small but structurally normal eyes. Turing's mechanism illuminates how disruptions in morphogen gradients during early embryogenesis can impair axial growth and scaling, linking to the bioelectric and quantum disruptions. Turing's model extends naturally to the eye's morphogenesis, which originates from the diencephalon and involves precise patterning of the optic vesicle, cup, and lens. Here, morphogens create spatial gradients that dictate tissue identity, proliferation, and size. For instance, Shh, secreted from the ventral midline, acts as a long-range inhibitor, repressing distal retinal markers, such as Pax6, while promoting proximal optic stalk identity.

FGF, often from the surface ectoderm or lens placode, serves as a short-range activator, inducing neural retina differentiation and optic cup invagination. This activator-inhibitor dynamic mirrors Turing's equations, where local activation and lateral inhibition generate patterns like the ventral optic fissure or retinal cell mosaics. A key example is the Pax6-Tgfb2-Fst network, which forms a Turing-like system in the optic vesicle. Pax6, the eye's master regulator, auto-activates with Follistatin (Fst) as a short-range enhancer, while TGF-beta2 (Tgfb2) inhibits over longer distances, polarizing the vesicle along proximal-distal axes and enabling spontaneous retinal organization, even in vitro organoids. This self-organization ensures coordinated growth between retina, lens, and surrounding tissues, tying into brain development via shared diencephalic origins. However, Turing's model alone doesn't fully explain scaling, how patterns adjust proportionally to organ size, as seen in varying egg sizes yielding complete chicks. Additional "tuning" mechanisms, like Hox genes in digit patterning, modulate wavelength (spacing) in Turing systems. In the eye, N-myc downstream of Shh and FGF coordinates retinal proliferation with overall eye size via cell cycle regulators like p27Kip1, ensuring proportional growth.

_**Disruptions here decouple retina from eye scaling, leading to thin retinas in small eyes seen in this disease. This blog parts the sea so one can see how brain, skull, and spinal morphology go awry via transgenerational sculpting of modern light.

**_

# Thread: Goats Milk For Infants
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goats-milk-for-infants.31605/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.AJ — 2025-09-07T14:04:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goats-milk-for-infants.31605/post-359205>

My wife is struggling to breast feed/produce enough milk (fucked redox, circadian mechanism, I know) and I'm wondering if using raw goats milk that I've sourced specifically from a local farmer (to avoid LNP's etc) would be a suitable substitute for breast milk. I remember reading before on twitter that goats milk is the most enzymatically similar milk to human breast milk so wondered if it was a safe alterative? Are there any major signs to look out for that indicate he is not reacting well to it?

Open to other suggestions if anyone has them.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-09-07T15:56:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goats-milk-for-infants.31605/post-359208>

Just to elaborate on this from my own research. MD's are saying that goats milk is not safe for babies under 1 year old due to lack of vitamins (iron b12 etc) and too much protein, which they say can cause kidney problems (retards) and lactose issues (lower in goats milk than cows). They instead recommend taking a fortified goats milk formula.

But if you think back to 200 years ago, before great companies like Nestle were making deuterium loaded formula for infants, surely the logical step for a mother who could not make enough milk was to feed the baby milk from the family farm. I don't think it was either drink moms milk or baby dies.

So basically I think all of the advice I'm reading today is bs.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-09-07T16:05:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goats-milk-for-infants.31605/post-359210>

Literally right above this grok regurgitated the "too much protein bad" then I ask for actual published studies or evidence showing kidney damage in infants from excess protein and got this:

There is limited direct evidence from published studies specifically linking high-protein milk, such as goat’s milk or high-protein infant formulas, to kidney damage in healthy infants. However, some studies and clinical observations address related concerns, particularly about the impact of high protein intake on infant kidney function. Below is a summary based on available research:

\### Key Findings from Relevant Studies
1\. \*\*High-Protein Formula and Kidney Function (Escribano et al., 2010)\*\*:
\- A European multicenter clinical trial examined 601 formula-fed infants assigned to either low-protein (LP) or high-protein (HP) formulas for the first year of life, compared with breastfed infants. At 6 months, infants on HP formula showed significantly higher kidney volume and serum urea levels compared to those on LP formula or breastfed infants. However, the study did not report direct kidney damage (e.g., acute kidney injury or chronic kidney disease) but noted that higher protein intake increased kidney size and workload, which could have long-term implications. The authors called for further research on long-term kidney function effects.\[\](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S008525381554876X)
\- \*\*Key Point\*\*: Increased kidney size and urea levels suggest a higher renal workload, but no conclusive evidence of damage was reported in healthy infants.

2\. \*\*Nutritional Management in Infants with CKD (Shaw et al., 2022)\*\*:
\- A review in \*Pediatric Nephrology\* discusses nutritional management for infants with pre-existing chronic kidney disease (CKD) stages 2–5. It notes that high protein intake, including from high-protein milk or formulas, can exacerbate issues in infants with CKD due to increased urea levels and potential hyperphosphatemia, which may strain already compromised kidneys. The study emphasizes adjusting protein intake to avoid excessive renal stress but does not directly link high-protein milk to kidney damage in healthy infants.\[\](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00467-022-05529-x)
\- \*\*Key Point\*\*: Infants with pre-existing kidney issues are more vulnerable to high protein intake, but this does not confirm damage in healthy infants.

3\. \*\*General Protein Intake and Kidney Health\*\*:
\- A 2018 meta-analysis by McMaster University researchers, published in \*The Journal of Nutrition\*, examined high-protein diets in healthy adults and found no evidence that high protein intake causes kidney damage in healthy individuals. While this study focused on adults, it challenges the broader myth that high protein inherently harms kidneys, suggesting that healthy kidneys can handle higher protein loads. No similar comprehensive meta-analysis exists specifically for infants.\[\](https://brighterworld.mcmaster.ca/articles/myth-busted-researchers-show-that-a-high-protein-diet-does-not-affect-kidney-function/)
\- \*\*Key Point\*\*: Extrapolating to infants, healthy kidneys may tolerate high protein without damage, but infants’ kidneys are less mature, so caution is warranted.

\### Context for Infants
\- \*\*Kidney Maturity\*\*: Infants have immature kidneys that are less efficient at handling high protein and mineral loads compared to adults. High-protein milk (e.g., goat’s milk or high-protein formulas) can increase renal solute load, leading to higher serum urea and potential dehydration, which could stress the kidneys.
\- \*\*Goat’s Milk Concerns\*\*: Goat’s milk is naturally high in protein (approximately 3.5–4 g/100 mL, compared to 1–1.5 g/100 mL in human milk) and minerals like potassium and phosphorus. While no studies directly confirm kidney damage from goat’s milk in healthy infants, its high protein and mineral content could pose risks, especially if unpasteurized or used as a primary milk source, due to potential dehydration or electrolyte imbalances.
\- \*\*No Direct Evidence of Damage\*\*: No studies explicitly document high-protein milk causing acute kidney injury or chronic kidney damage in healthy infants. Most concerns are theoretical, based on increased renal workload or risks in infants with pre-existing kidney conditions.

\### Limitations and Gaps
\- \*\*Lack of Specific Studies\*\*: There are no large-scale, randomized controlled trials directly investigating whether high-protein milk (e.g., goat’s milk or high-protein formulas) causes kidney damage in healthy infants. Most studies focus on infants with CKD or compare formula types without reporting overt kidney damage.
\- \*\*Long-Term Effects Unknown\*\*: As noted in the Escribano study, the long-term impact of increased kidney size or workload from high-protein intake in infancy is unclear and requires further research.
\- \*\*Confounding Factors\*\*: Studies on infants with CKD or other conditions often involve additional factors (e.g., dialysis, congenital anomalies), making it hard to isolate the effect of high-protein milk alone.

\### Conclusion
No published studies conclusively show that high-protein milk, such as goat’s milk or high-protein formulas, directly causes kidney damage in healthy infants. However, evidence suggests that high protein intake increases kidney workload, as seen in higher kidney volume and serum urea levels, which could pose risks, particularly for infants with pre-existing kidney issues or if high-protein milk is used exclusively without supplementation. For healthy infants, the American Academy of Pediatrics recommends breast milk or iron-fortified formula for the first 12 months, as high-protein milks like goat’s milk may stress immature kidneys and lack essential nutrients. Always consult a pediatrician before introducing high-protein milk to an infant’s diet.\[\](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S008525381554876X)\[\](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00467-022-05529-x)\[\](https://brighterworld.mcmaster.ca/articles/myth-busted-researchers-show-that-a-high-protein-diet-does-not-affect-kidney-function/)

# Thread: Goat Meat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goat-meat.31610/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rtd — 2025-09-08T15:44:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goat-meat.31610/post-359278>

Looking for a goat recipe, epi Paleo based.

2025-09-09: Update
Taste wise, I wanted to make a spicy coconut based curry. Accounting for the leptin protocol (no nightshades), using locally produced food and allowing some seasonal fruit, its shaping up to be a pumpkin cranberry goat meat stew using an Instant Pot. Helps that local farms grow all this stuff, cranberries are a few towns over.

Sear cubed meat with ghee, pressure cook the meat. Pumpkin cranberry is two parts: roasted pumpkin and stewed down cranberries; half puree and half chunky. Sauté shallots, garlic and basil (though basil is going to be added more towards end of stewing because it can burn easy). Slow cook all that stuff together. Could roast pumpkin seeds for texture, gonna pass on that.

# Thread: Kathleen’s optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathleen%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.31612/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kathleenharris — 2025-09-08T18:18:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathleen%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.31612/post-359291>

Hi I’m starting my optimal journal here, it’s going to be a long one. I’m Kathleen a 21 year old female living in bend Oregon(44 latitude). It’s in central Oregon know as high desert so not rainy like Portland area, lots of volcanic activity though. Great access to anything outdoors. 300 days of sun a year, 4 seasons, snowy winters. Currently healing from Lyme/co-infections and Covid vaccine injury. Found Dr. Kruse work 2 months ago.

I was Born is San Jose California and moved to bend Oregon when I was 1 year old. For the first 12 years we live out in the country before moving into town so I grew up outdoors with horses, showing cattle, helping my dad irrigate and cut hay etc. Mother born and raised in southern Washington around agriculture and farming and lived there until about 22 then moved to Bay Area for a long time working in high tech. since moving to Oregon she has been working remotely with frequent air travel, less now. Grandma also born and raised in southern Washington. Grandmas mother is Irish and her father is Spanish.

**History from the beginning:**

\-5th grade received concussion skiing with loss of consciousness. Don’t particularly remember how long it lasted. Know I was out of it for awhile but never saw a doctor or got treatment although I should have. Was by myself when it happened and didn’t tell my parents how serious it was.

\-severe chronic lower back pain developed the following summer. Made worse by activity and jumping. Saw several specialist over the next 4 years, they couldn’t find anything wrong and was told it was all in my head and diagnosed with chronic back pain, told to see a psychiatrist. Eventually I refused to go see any more doctors and just lived with the pain. Over this time did physical therapy, acupuncture, massage, cranial sacral etc to manage pain and get through sports.

\-7th grade received second concussion less serious from lacrosse. Took about 2 weeks to recover. Overall well being was good only had the chronic back pain still.

\-Doctors put me on gabapentin to try to relive the back pain but eventually just made me feel worse (in a fog) and didn’t help pain. Was on for about a year then came off.

\-sophomore year started oral birth control for acne

\-also have had bad eczema most my life and very dry skin

\-junior year of highschool Jan 2021 received 3rd concussion skiing. Developed severe symptoms afterwards. Whole body was affected. Nervous system dysregulation. Heart palpitations, high hr, headaches, vision disturbances, brain fog, out of it, severe right side neck pain, light and noise sensitivity,

Shortness of breath, fatigue. Took until about April to start feeling better although still had a lot of symptoms didn’t feel 100 percent. Started the lacrosse season at this time

\-primary care took bloodwork at checkup awhile after concussion. Found low iron and high crp, wanted me to go to rheumatologist.

\-April 24 received first round Pfizer Covid vaccine at school. (Turns out my lot is now in top 15 of ones with most adverse reactions)

\-in the next moths started developing more autoimmune like symptoms and just generally feeling blah. Didn’t really connect it to vaccine more so to my last concussion and possibly my back pain

\-may 27 received second dose of Pfizer Covid vaccine. General feeling of unwell continued. Wasn’t feeling like myself or 100% like I did before last concussion.

\-this summer my mom had brain surgery to remove a golf ball sized meningioma. She had a full recovery. I suspect it was from her constant nnEMF exposure working in high tech at computer all day being assaulted by blue light and emfs, rarely seeing sun. As well as the amount of aspartame she consumed from Diet Pepsi addiction (she has now stopped) and very frequent air travel for job. I have been helping her out not to adjust habits.

\-starting senior year of highschool still felt blah, fatigue and brain fog was growing, nauseous most days, harder for me to recover from things, would get heart palpitations occasionally. Still had chronic back pain and it was getting worse again.

\-would take 3 Advil every practice/ game during lacrosse season in order to get moving and make it through. Once I started moving couldn’t stop or else I would lock up and pain would get worse in back and hips.

\-December got 2 bulged discs during a jumping movement. Severe pain and tingling weakness in legs. Saw doctor, they did and MRI. During mri they saw arthritic findings in my si joints and sent me to rheumatologist. In the meantime did physical therapy for the bulged discs.

\-December 28 2022 got Covid, mild symptoms. Felt like a cold

\-saw rheumatologist around March, diagnosed me with ankylosing spondylitis despite being HLA-b27 negative. Had sacroiliitis in si joints on X-ray. High crp (12). Iron still low ferritin 16, iron saturation 16, transferrin 410. Thyroid levels were off as well.

\-got sick with bad cold in April that my body took a long time to recover from. Body felt like it was giving up.

\-rheumatologist wanted me to go on biologics to treat the AS but I refused. Tried nsaids instead.

\-autoimmune symptoms got worse feeling unwell although still functioning. GI symptoms worsening too, referred for colonoscopy

\-went on meloxicam to see if it would help for pain but it didn’t stopped after about 2 months

\-june 27 2022 received Pfizer booster

\-July 2nd ended up in er with severe heart palpatatioms shortness of breath and high hr. Told me nothing was wrong and offered me anxiety medication which I declined.

\-July 2022 got colonoscopy. Normal beside inflamed stomach. Prescribed ppi, and zofran for nausea only took ppi for about 2 months. And zofran when needed

\-tried taking celebrex next starting mid August started right before next concussion

\-august 20 received 4th concussion from dog, symptom onset after 2 days, similar to last one.

\-August 24, 4 days post concussion travelled to Syracuse New York to move into freshman year of college. The day I got there had bad reaction to celebrex acute nausea, burning in throat, neck pain, lower back pain, ache in leg, severe stomach cramps headaches that have got worse, dizziness, foggy in head, last night experienced chills. So stopped it immediately.

\-concussion symptoms still severe and appeared to be declining.

\-late September went to concussion clinic in Syracuse. Started me doing physical and occupational therapy and on amitriptyline to help with headaches. No improvement, wasn’t able to do anything, was in dorm room most of the time except for going to class which was hard to make it through. Had to take less classes

\-late November after thanksgiving they put me on celexa to try to help my sleep because I wasn’t sleeping. Again didn’t help and only worsened matters.

\-heart symptoms such as palpitations high heart rate spikes and blood pressure were all getting much worse as well.

\-December while home for break had mri on head because concussion wasn’t healing all negative.

\-beginning of January decided to come off celebrex because it wasn’t helping and started taking every other day for a week then stopped

\-Jan 14, ended up in ER with very severe withdrawal symptoms from it. I was on the floor shaking. Bad tremors palpitations very high blood pressure(180/120). They weren’t 100 percent sure if it was withdrawal or serotonin syndrome so they put me on a 4 day course of benzodiazepines to prevent siezure. Referred to get echo and ekg

\-Jan 16, saw a functional neurologist and did a one week intensive to treat post concussion symptoms

\-Jan 19 echo was normal ecg normal only Nonspecific ST abnormality

\-after this week returned to Syracuse to start second semester at a part time schedule

\-Jan 23rd ended up in ER in Syracuse again with extreme heart palpitations, and high heart rate around 170 that wouldn’t come down. Referred to cardiology for holter monitoring.

\-Stopped amitriptyline Jan 31

\-Feb 6 2023 saw cardiology mom had to come out to stay with me as I started tapering of amitriptaline as well was having additional withdrawal symptoms but not as bad as celexa. Could hardly get off couch, every time I stood up heart rate would spike, very dizzy weak shaky nauseous palpitations etc. was barely making it to my 3 classes. Still not sleeping much. When walking to class heart rate constantly at 170 or above and would stay around 150.

\-did holter monitoring for 3 days. Nothing abnormal beside heart rate spikes. Feb 26 at follow up Prescribed me beta blockers that I never took. Said I most likely have pots too.

\-March through April was doing slightly better now that the withdrawal effects weren’t as severe but still not doing well. Couldn’t go out except to class and to eat still very unwell mom came back again to stay with me the last 3 weeks of school to help me out and move out.

\-May 11 2023 moved back to bend Oregon from school. Started another 2 week intensive with functional neurologist to work on post concussion sympyoms as well as the heart regulation stuff. Definitely helped stabilize my heart rate and I felt a little bit better but overall still not very functional.

—August went to a 2 week intensive concussion clinic in Utah. Once returning home I again felt some improvement but didn’t last. Weren’t sure what was going on but we were chasing concussion because that’s what we thought was the source.

\-started really clean diet, Whole Foods, no processed foods or sugar or seed oils, dairy, gluten and drinking spring water

\-began discovering more of the functional medicine world and non toxic living practices

\-took a leave of absence from school wasn’t able to return

\-December 2023 went to a functional neurologist after I began to decline heavily again. Lost a lot of weight. Wasn’t even able to get up and make my own food. When we were there after intake and testing was told that my concussion was 95% healed and that I most likely had some chronic inflammatory infection going on instead that was triggered by concussion. So he started treating me for that while I was there as well as some concussion treatment. Did balance training, eye exercises, reaction training, neck exercise, hyperbaric, laser, red and blue light, biofeedback, compression, nerve stimulation etc. he also started be on some herbal therapies.

\-once home was tested for Lyme and coinfections. Was weak positive for Lyme and positive for erichlia, anaplasmosis and indeterminate for bartonella

\-went off birth control in January

\-January tested house for mold, found mold and moisture under my shower and had it remediated

\-continued herbal protocol for about 6 months as well as doing red light. At end of 6 months saw some improvement was a little more functional but still pretty limited on what I could do. But didn’t feel like I was dying everyday.

\-March started gaining excess weight

\-June 2024 Took a break on treatment.

\-September black dot appeared in vision in right eye just left of center.

\-Went to ophthalmologist. Found several older retinal scars in periphery in both eyes and new retinal scar that is what appeared in my central vision that was close to optic nerve. Diagnosed with multifocal choriretinitis. Also had flashing in eyes off and on. Also have floaters. Since then spot has faded and is now just a blurry spot with distortion. They tried to tell me it was autoimmune and there’s nothing I could do about it besides steroids and to go on a biologic or else I could lose more of my vision. I refused both these options and gave them a lot of pushback and information they didn’t like (thought I was crazy)

\-October 2024 had wisdom teeth removed and cavitations cleaned and filled that started forming under wisdom teeth with ozone and prf by biologic dental surgeon. Had cavitations sampled and tested.

\-January 2025 decided to seek further treatment for Lyme/co-infections as I was getting worse again

\-January 26 bumped head and had some minor concussions symptoms return

\-March 8th traveled to Germany and received 2 whole body hyperthermia, 2 weeks daily of laser pdt, 3 sessions h.e.l.p apheresis and a microbiome transfer over the course of 5 weeks. I also received several adjunct treatments and infusions.

\-while I was there they discovered my blood was super thick and clotting during the help apheresis procedure so decided to test it. Found I have micro clots and endothelial damage from the Covid vaccine. Which makes sense if I trace back all my symptoms and when they started to worsen. They put me on a protocol coming home for 12 weeks and put me on blood thinners. Pradaxa 75mg twice daily and 100mg aspirin once daily. I’m still on those currently but trying to come off soon. (Circulation has been better as well since going on)

\-I recently just did the Covid spike antibody blood test 2 weeks ago and my levels were >25,000, yikes I know off the charts. So that was very discouraging but am determined to drop them.

**CURRENT**:

\-2 months ago is when I found the field of quantum biology firstly through Justine stenger who thoroughly supports your work as well as using phospholipid complex’s and essential fatty acids to restore cell membranes, then learning about fascia and Mads T through his quantum biomechanics course that I’m taking. Then eventually found the Rick Rubin podcast and your work Dr. Kruse. Everything started to click and made so much sense. My mind was blown and couldn’t believe I had been missing this all this time. I’m so grateful I have been brought here. At this point I have dove into it all, have listened to hours of podcast and have read several blogs. There are so many and I’m trying to get through little by little. I love learning and this has ignited a new passion for me. Ive also started reading the body electric and the fourth phase of water. With many more on the docket.

\-Currently I wake up to watch the sunrise everyday grounded and stay out there for at least 2 hours. I get at least 45 mins of midday sun if not more and try to be outside as much as possible. I am building my tan, I’ve always been pale have freckles and burn very easily. I have also been pretty heat intolerant but that’s improving some since being back from Germany. I see sunset at night. I eat within 30 minutes of waking up. I drink structured spring water with minerals. Eat seasonal local Whole Foods, starting to incorporate more seafood. Wear red/orange blue blockers after sunset and yellow ones indoors during the day. I avoid artificial light as much as possible. I use red light. Currently I also do counterstrain physical therapy every couple weeks and am currently taking mads biomechanics course as well to correct things. Massage for pain management every couple weeks as well for my right neck,shoulder and trap. Working on nervous system regulation and do meditaion. I just had a remote consultation with a building biologist as I’m trying to reduce nnEMF exposure. I bought a trifield meter to test my home myself and found very high RF readings specifically in my bedroom. Had smart meter replaced. Come to find out my room is right between two cell towers .3 miles on either side. I turn my breakers and WiFi off at night. My plan is to buy a faraday canopy for my bed and in meantime am sleeping in a different room. My family is planning to move in the next year so cannopy is best fix for now.

Current top Symptoms:

Major Fatigue, brain fog(slight improvement since being on blood thinners), feeling o it of it, right side neck shoulder trap pain, back/hip pain, occasional heart palpitations and shortness of breath, right sided head pain sometimes sharp and brief sometimes more headache like. Vision issues, ocasional nausea, dizziness, light headed, perception issues, ocasional tinitus that comes and goes quickly. Overall general unwellness. Dry skin and eczema. Sleep is better but still not great and when I sleep bad my symptoms are far far worse.

Although I’m improved, more functional and can do more today compared to where I was at a year ago I still far from where I would like to be and still limited a lot in my life.

**FUTURE**:

My goal right now is to continue to learn and keep on these practices mitigate my nnEMF exposure and above all lower my spike protein antibodies. I’m looking into ivermectin treatment just trying to figure out a way to get it. And once I’m out of heavy nnEMF exposure and I have circadian principles mastered I think Methylene blue is an option as well. I hope to try to get off the blood thinners soon as well. Winter is approaching which I will utilize ct in my local environment and am trying to plan a trip to lower latitude for a while like El Salvador Costa Rica or Mexico to support my healing. Hopefully in November December or January. If anyone has recommendations for places to stay please let me know. I am also starting back at school online in January so I have more flexibility with my location and time for my own directed study. It’s easy to fall into regret over the past choices ive made from ignorance, lack of awareness, and misplaced trust in authority. But I’m done living like that and now I choose to take responsibility, educate myself, and make better decisions moving forward. I now have courage to resist what no longer serves truth or health

Anyone feel free to comment or give any recommendations, it’s all a journey and know I have soooooo much to still learn but I’m putting in the time to do it.

### AlbertD — 2025-09-12T05:45:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathleen%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.31612/post-359408>

Some advice for writing optimal journals/future posts.
1\. Keep it concise.
2\. Group information by problems. (Jab relevant info, meds, etc.)
3\. Don't need to organize it based on the timeline from start to finish. (More detailed headings would help; Timelines are messy (avoid))

I essentially have two desert problems, the internal and the external which also affects the internal.

Bend could be considered desert given its semi arid climate and lava field geography. So despite having good sun (for being at 44 latitude) I am lacking water and magnetism, 2 of the 3 pillars. Therefore trying to restore my internal desert to an oasis will be very difficult in my zipcode.

Second, my body and tissues have become a desert. The vaccine as well as my extreme exposure to RF and nnemf has disrupted and damaged the heme and has caused my mitochondria to become hypoxic. Added onto by the microclots from the spike protein from the vaccine.

The mitochondria rely on circadian timing to optimize energy transformation using the ETC using sunlight that has red and nir wavelengths. This enhances CCO activity which boosts ATP and reduces ROS. In my case the damaged heme can't absorb red and nir light effectively so the whole process becomes affected. The ETC can't function as it should so ATP drops and ROS rises which is increased in hypoxic state. I am dehydrated because without a functioning CCO I can't make sufficient DDW. The lack of water is exacerbated by the nnemf I have been exposed to. It also makes it so I can't protect my cells from oxygen. Also apoptosis fails since it's controlled by CCO. So without the water, the energy flow is disrupted and the membrane potential collapses and there is no cellular repair, no redox. Essentially being electrocuted from inside out. I am now shifted into warburg metabolism instead of using TCA cycle appropriately. This also affects the vitamin d metabolism via cyp because it's controlled by heme. The free iron from the heme damage can also accumulate and trigger ferroptosis which is the free iron and ROS creating lipid peroxidation. So because of the mismatch of mitochondria being hypoxic but having oxygen around the imbalance fosters heteroplasmy, which cant be taken care of because like mentioned earlier apoptosis is broken.

So essentially light can no longer properly energize the cells because of the damaged heme. I'm left as a desert.

So as far as a plan here's how things connect:

I need to repair heme and fix the vdr on imm. Shift out of hypoxia and dehydration and as you say “Re-establish the oceans inside my cells”

Adding in ivermectin to help reduce my spike load since my antibody level was greater than 25,000 could help to lower the assault on heme and reduce the free iron generation enabling my mitochondria to start to repair.

Diet and water: seasonal and local to give myself the light barcode of my environment. High in protein and fat, they make more water. Carbs only make 55 g of water so further dehydrating. TCA cycle makes 105. Helps re establish water production at CCO. Drinking DDW water to also help with the re-hydration to allow my body to absorb the light and have the ETC working properly as well as CCO.

Cold exposure: This will help my body generate the light from within while it's lacking in the winter months in my latitude while I'm not able to be at lower latitude.

Morning light and grounding: Getting free electrons from the earth, increases redox. Getting the red/nir light, allows me to be able to use the TCA cycle and repair and trigger CCO, creating DDW to keep electric current inside. Also helps increase ATP and lower ROS. All helps fix apoptosis to get rid of the bad. Sets circadian clock through SCN.

Reduce nnemf: Blocking blue artificial light after sunset and indoors during the day. Limiting tech use and reducing emf/rf in the house. Nnemf avoidance helps to reduce the dehydration. I have been sleeping in a different room since my room is being blasted with rf and have ordered an emf canopy for sleeping to further reduce exposure at night. My parents will be moving houses within next year to a different location.

Light exposure: Over the last 2 months I have been working to build my solar callus and am now tanner than I have been in multiple years but still have a ways to go since im pretty fair. Trying to be outside as much as possible to be exposed to the sun. Winter is approaching so I will plan to optimize sun exposure. I do have 2 red light panels and one also has uva uvb along with red and nir. I will plan to supplement with these throughout the winter. Hoping to be able to make it down to lower latitude to assist my body in healing in a conducive environment as i know my current zipcode is not what I need.

Questions:

### DrDoug — 2025-09-08T18:31:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wdr45-gene-mutation.31613/post-359292>

I am a developmental optometrist that sees a lot of IEP and kids with 504 plan needs. You can learn more about me at [www.stepheyoptometry.com](http://www.stepheyoptometry.com) and my podcast Move Look & Listen

My question today is "what treatment options are available for a 6 year old non verbal dysregulated 6 year old girl with a concurrent WDR45 gene mutation which apparently results in NBIA

-   **NBIA** stands for **Neurodegeneration with Brain Iron Accumulation**.
-   It’s not a single disorder but a _group of rare genetic conditions_ that share a key feature: **excess iron deposits in the basal ganglia**, a deep brain region critical for movement and cognition. Mutations in the **WDR45 gene** cause a specific form of NBIA called
    **Beta-Propeller Protein-Associated Neurodegeneration (BPAN)**.

Any non centralized insights would be welcome.

### Mat.I — 2025-09-12T07:56:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wdr45-gene-mutation.31613/post-359409>

I do not know much about this particular issue. However, on a basic level, I would keep the hardware (genetic) silent by maximising the software control (epigenetics) over this.. Maximise, light, water, magnetism and minimise nn-emf. These kids must be home schooled in a good environment. I take it BPAN would be driven by a FE3+ shifted terrain & would be driving the symptomatic ROS loops etc.. Homeschooling is a non-negotiable and staying tightly in sync with light and dark cycles with solid UV to drive regeneration is key. E.g. M1 shift to M2 & FE3+ shift to FE2+ (normal cycling) etc..

Spin-Selective Interactions: Biophotons from sources like NADH, cytochrome c, porphyrins (heme), ROS, or melanin carry spin that matches chiral heme structures, triggering excitation (right-handed) or absorption (left-handed). Red light's low-energy spin aligns with heme's Q-band absorption, promoting coherent electron flow in the ETC and CYP reactions without oxidative damage. Higher-frequency colors (e.g., blue) induce chaotic UPEs, altering iron oxidation states, increasing RBC turnover, and disrupting CYP heme, leading to pregnenolone steal and infertility.

Quantum Coherence and Morphogenesis: As per Turing's 1952 paper on morphogenesis, morphogens (now seen as UPE-driven in my light model) sculpt tissues via reaction-diffusion instabilities. Red light's preserved helicity maintains quantum coherence in water CDs and microtubules, optimizing AMO for protein bending (tertiary/quaternary structures) in CYP enzymes. This explains why red acts as a natural aromatase inhibitor (CYP19A1), balancing estrogens without the high-energy spin chaos of blue light, which dehydrates melanin and lowers éR = energy resistance.

Low éR = Efficiency and Flow: In healthy states, like during deep sleep or a good workout recovery, éR is dialed down. Energy transforms smoothly—think laminar flow in a river, minimal turbulence. Your cells rebuild, adapt, and thrive.

Physics analogy: It's like a superconductor with near-zero resistance.

Blue light causes more noise and less signal, and this is how you alter gender with light, allowing current (or bioenergy) to zip through without loss. Blue light causes more noise, less signal, and this is how you alter gender with light.

### SHANNON SELL — 2025-09-08T23:47:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diabetes-in-1918.31618/post-359302>

I am reading the book Shaking the Nickel Bush by Ralph Moody aloud to my children. In this book, the author is diagnosed at age 19 with type 1 Diabetes in 1918 and the experts gave him about 6 months to live. But another trusted doctor said this:

"There's few things that God's good sunshine and fresh air-with a bit of Common Sense thrown in-won't cure for a lad. Why don't you go back to Colorado where you were raised or better still to Arizona where tis warm weather all the winter long? Wear as little clothes as the law allows and let the sun shine at your body; there's no end to the wonders It works....I'll write out a diet for you to follow. Won't be tasty, but what's the odds if it does the trick? Now get your clothes on, ti's no good it'll be doing you to lie abed. "

Ralph Moody lived to the age of 83 years old. The mortality rate for type 1 Diabetes at the time was near 100%, and even after insulin discovery in 1922 remained near 50%

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-spark.31621/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dzmak — 2025-09-09T20:25:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-spark.31621/post-359336>

I started following Dr Kruse at the beginning of the year. My husband has been listening to reggae style music and played this song for me a while back. I just heard it again last weekend and it took on a much more significant meaning after what I've learned from Dr Kruse.

Just wanted to share because it felt like a summary of everything he has taught me.

## The Spark​

Lennon

Out of absence came the spark
Little flicker just
To start this creation
Waves rolling out
Infinite destinations
Coalesce, come apart
Generation, generation
Simplicity giving rise to
Boundless innovation

What is God?
But a word that people keep on taking for granted
Use it to sow the seeds of hatred that continue getting planted
But the mystery of life is so much bigger than a man's head
No matter how much he inflates it with the worship of the masses
As we segregate life by race, religion, politics, and classes
Illusions holding fast because we're holding to our past
It's time that we look past this
If we're hoping to unite
Recognition
Not the struggle, fuss and fight

From one light, one hum
One endless vibration
Came all life, all love
All manifestation
And our hearts, our minds
All our observations Is all of our existence seeking
Self realization

See the writing on the wall
Let it fall

Boundless innovation...

# Thread: Help with long covid/mitochondrial dysfunction?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-long-covid-mitochondrial-dysfunction.31622/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tommytomtom — 2025-09-09T20:41:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-long-covid-mitochondrial-dysfunction.31622/post-359337>

Hi, everybody! I just joined the forum, so I apologize if this is the wrong place to post this thread. I stumbled across Jack about a month ago on The Higherside Chats and Tetragrammaton, and my mind was promptly blown. I finished Epi-Paleo RX last week and picked up Robb Wolf's Paleo Solution, as Jack suggested (I'm about halfway through it). I'm planning on trying to talk my wife into adopting an epi-paleo diet to try to fix her mobility issues, which have severely affected her quality of life for the last three years, and I'm hoping someone here may have been in a similar boat at some point and can help us weather the storm, so to speak.

Extremely long story short, my wife, who is now 44, was diagnosed with an IgG subclass deficiency in 2019 and began receiving monthly IgG infusions shortly thereafter. She received three doses of the original recipe moderna mrna covid vaccine (the two initial shots and a booster dose she was offered because of her immune deficiency) in 2021, and we both tested positive for covid in January 2022. We both felt like we had a bad cold but seemed to be over the worst of it after a few days. Eight days after she tested positive, however, she was washing dishes in our sink, looked at me, and said, "Something's wrong with my legs." She collapsed, and I called an ambulance for her. At the ER, they ran a few tests that showed a phosphate deficiency, and, after giving her some potassium, she felt OK and was discharged. A few days later, she collapsed again, and we called another ambulance. She was admitted to the hospital for a few days; initially, she was unable to stand on her own at all. Curiously, the neurologist she saw commented that her legs appeared to be extremely strong, but she didn't have any stamina or endurance. The neurology team chalked it up to long covid and continued to give her potassium; after a few days, she was able to take a few steps with assistance again, and she was discharged. Over the next four months, I watched her slowly regain the full use of her legs through a combination of a phosphate-rich diet, physical therapy, and monthly IgG infusions, although the physical therapy sometimes set her back. By Easter of that year, we were thankful she could once again run and play with our daughter, who was 4 at the time, and we hoped we had closed a weird, anomalous chapter of our lives.

Then, two months later, she listened to the advice of her physicians and received a fourth dose of the original recipe vaccine (the first three were Moderna, and the fourth was Pfizer); three weeks later, she could barely walk again, and our lives have been upside-down ever since. Although she never again experienced the total loss of endurance she experienced in January 2022, she's needed a rollator and/or a mobility scooter to navigate the world outside our home ever since, and we've spent a good deal of our waking lives looking for answers or clues to reversing her condition. She began tracking her good days and bad days almost immediately afterward, and we noticed that she seemed to be at her strongest during a certain point of her menstrual cycle; her OB then prescribed a low dose of progesterone, which took her to an improved level of ambulation but still left her severely limited. She developed severe anxiety, dry mouth, and dry eyes, and she stopped working in March of 2024 to focus on her health full-time. She tried physical therapy a few times, but it only exacerbated her issues. In April of 2024, she began treatment at Mt. Sinai's long covid clinic in New York City, and she was prescribed LDN, which cleared up the dry mouth and dry eyes immediately but failed to resolve her leg issues.

All the while, most of the physicians we've seen have thought she is suffering from some sort of mitochondrial dysfunction either brought on by the virus, the vaccine, or some combination of both. She tried both Coq10 and alpha lipoic acid to try to strengthen or repair her mitochondria but was unable to tolerate either one (she has a history of stomach distress and has to be very careful about anything she ingests orally). Since 2022, we noticed that she seemed to be stronger during the summer months (we live in Northeastern Pennsylvania); then, about a month ago, I first heard of Jack, and I am hoping his theories on diet and light can help her return to full health and vitality. I am in the process of replacing all of the LED lights in our home with full spectrum incandescents from Healthlighting (they should arrive this week), and she and I have both started wearing clear blue light blocking lenses during the day and red lenses after sunset. I am planning on purchasing a MitoAdapt 4.0 red light panel as soon as one is available (the site is saying they will be back in stock Sept. 30), and we have begun sitting under a 150watt red sauna bulb for 20 minutes in the morning and 20 minutes in the evening in the meantime (is this doing anything for us? I have no idea, but I'm hoping it's not hurting?). I guess what I'm looking for here are dietary and/or light therapy recommendations (frequency, timing, duration, etc.) we can implement to try to improve or reverse her condition from anyone who may have had similar experiences; I personally think Jack is a genius, and I haven't missed a sunrise or a sunset the past two weeks in an attempt to maximize my own health and wellness. Thank you for your time and consideration.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### giedremail — 2025-09-09T22:35:34+0300

Thank you

### Martha Ray — 2025-10-06T19:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weather-radars.31623/post-360481>

I would like to know that as well.

### Zeke — 2025-10-06T20:13:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weather-radars.31623/post-360484>

I would like to know for Marine radar.

### Mat.I — 2025-10-07T05:57:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weather-radars.31623/post-360547>

Another issue with Radars is they are likely not being used as "advertised" or within the "advertised" limits.. Terrain & elevation is also critical too for determining exposure (very n=1 dependent)..

### Paul T008 — 2025-10-07T12:33:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weather-radars.31623/post-360563>

109 .13 on the timeline
How the aphids on a plant are affected and dance by each rotation of the radar ,

I keep re watching this video , as jack keeps publishing his blogs

### Rusty — 2025-10-07T13:52:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weather-radars.31623/post-360566>

Weather radars like NEXRAD have very high peak power (hundreds of kW) & large antenna gain, but their average power is far lower. That combination gives a very directive beam that can produce high instantaneous peaks yet a lower time-averaged power density at distance

From an engineering perspective, safety limits are based on this average power, the heating effect over time.

But from a biological perspective, researchers like Dimitris Panagopoulos argue that it’s the peak pulses, not the averages, that matter.

So to answer your question,

I'd say atleast 50-100 kms to be on the safe side.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-10-08T19:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weather-radars.31623/post-360677>

When they are pointed at you no difference is safe......kind like a CME.

### Zeke — 2025-10-09T01:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weather-radars.31623/post-360698>

Is the curvature of the earth a factor?

I would think there is some large safe distance.

My first short trip to El Salvador is the beginning of my new journey. I was in Shalpa beach at India's place and had a very pleasant stay. The grounding on the black beach can be felt and as you continue the benefits can be experienced. Since I came back, I am certain that leaving there will benefit me 100%. I am excited to say that I am in the process to make my life changing move to El Salvador n Atami. ### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-10T20:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frontotemporal-dysplasia-and-glp-1-drugs.31629/post-359361>

**Frontotemporal dysplasia and GLP-1 drugs........**

The central retinal pathways of the human eye contain our SCN, which is our master circadian clock. All clocks are flow meters of how entropy flows within that clock system in tissues. Nature ONLY uses PoW (proof of work) clocks in cells. Why does Nature, the only natural decentralized system, use PoW exclusively for its clocks? The answer is that "TIME" is the most valuable asset in a decentralized system. In reality, light photons never experience time. When proteins misfold, this is evidence of a broken PoW clock mechanism in protein function. Quantum thermodynamics is the discipline that concerns itself with the flow of information and energy while providing the right language for describing clocks. All clocks in nature must work by proof of work to describe information/energy consumption. A clock is anything that undergoes irreversible changes:

The more regular the ticks, the more accurate the clock. This is why the TDP-43 change alters the biological hash rate in the retina and brain, causing it to thin via OCT scanning. This makes this measure mimic a unit of the processing power of the brain's decentralized mitochondrial network.

### [FRONTOTEMPORAL DYSPLASIA AND GLP-1 DRUGS: WHY IS THERE A LINK?](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/frontotemporal-dysplasia-glp-1-drugs-why-link-jack-kruse-uvdge/)

The retina thins from the effect of man-made light and a lack of sunlight, and then your risk of Frontotemporal dementia rises. The OCT test is the best screening test for FTD.
