Understood. From what you have detailed now in this thread re: mental health, you want to avoid this progressing to a deeply wired in issue. I have witnessed similar type fluctuations in others, that became wired in and resulted in psychosis with involuntary mental health admission ~ 2 years ago... I'm helping her to the best I can and she has made dramatic improvements from 2 years ago when this occurred her baseline is much better and her terrain is much stronger than ever (even pre-event)..

I guess, what I want to say here is - whilst you are aware of these issues, if you deteriorate any further, you may longer have this level of awareness to voluntarily pull yourself out and no one will save you in the centralised system. This particular person had to surrender all decision making and instructions to me when I stepped into help (as her PFC checked out) aka. the CEO of your-self.. Everyone's lightning strike is different, especially where it hits and what the impact is.. It is one of the worst things I have witnessed, you think you can still reason with people in theory however, when they check out they truly check out literally blow the circuit fuse and in that state, no longer see the world as others do..

Your priority should be your health, if you have the financial support, I would drop everything that is non-essential (including uni) and just focus on my health with no exceptions. Get your partner on board for support and try your best to make it happen.. Just do what you can with your current circumstances and leave no fat on the bone...

I wish you all the best.
Mat

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=359558)
>
> Mat

Hi Mat,

We have been experimenting with different sleep positions. We are actually going on a holiday to cairns soonish for two weeks so I am hoping that gives my son something of a boost but we know it's not a long term solution. And we know the daycare thing is no good too, he's been sick too much, hes been in daycare because I've been completing studies. We talked about pulling him out but giving the disarray of other things I'm not sure if that will be happening right now. It might sound callous but I feel like I have to save myself first before I am no good to my son at all.

I understand the point you are making with regards to what you have said about your friend. And I'm sorry to hear she went through all of that, that sounds truly awful. I definitely want to avoid this progressing, and it feels really pressing to me now as my depression and sense of feeling disconnected from life have been really bad lately.

My priority is my health and I know this is urgent but I'm in a bind. The problem is, my sons dad and I actually separated in May following some protracted relationship issues, but we still live together. He does not wish to leave where we are now because his business is set up here and hes happy with what hes made and I guess what it comes down to is... I'm no longer his problem. I've been opening up to him, as well as my parents, and lately they have both expressed that they are sick of hearing it. And frankly, I get it. I'm sick of hearing it, too. I'm sick of feeling it. Obviously given that we share a child, I can't just leave with our son. And I wouldn't be in the position to do that anyway as I will have to find work as soon as I can wherever I go. I kinda feel like my back is against the wall and I've come to a point inside myself where I'm thinking my only choice is going to be to leave on my own. Not as something permanent. I'm hoping the holiday changes something, I'm certain getting all that sun is going to make me feel better for the time being, and maybe it will show my sons dad another perspective. But if not, I have arrived at a point where I am steeling myself to do something in the not too distant future that feels awful but necessary.

Thanks again for your responses, I appreciate it.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-13T04:40:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-changes-gender-in-kids-how.31639/post-359442>

Check out my latest article: ROCKEFELLER MEDICINE WAS THE SEED THAT BECAME MKULTRA [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/rockefeller-medicine-seed-became-mkultra-jack-kruse-kmpff](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/rockefeller-medicine-seed-became-mkultra-jack-kruse-kmpff)

Heme (in CYP enzymes like CYP11A1, CYP19A1) is chiral and absorbs red wavelengths preferentially, enabling spin-selective electron transport via CISS. This regulates protein folding, enzyme activity, and hormonal output:

Spin-Selective Interactions: Biophotons from sources like NADH, cytochrome c, porphyrins (heme), ROS, or melanin carry spin that matches chiral heme structures, triggering excitation (right-handed) or absorption (left-handed). Red light's low-energy spin aligns with heme's Q-band absorption, promoting coherent electron flow in the ETC and CYP reactions without oxidative damage. Higher-frequency colors (e.g., blue) induce chaotic UPEs, altering iron oxidation states, increasing RBC turnover, and disrupting CYP heme, leading to pregnenolone steal and infertility.

Quantum Coherence and Morphogenesis: As per Turing's 1952 paper on morphogenesis, morphogens (now seen as UPE-driven in my light model) sculpt tissues via reaction-diffusion instabilities. Red light's preserved helicity maintains quantum coherence in water CDs and microtubules, optimizing AMO for protein bending (tertiary/quaternary structures) in CYP enzymes. This explains why red acts as a natural aromatase inhibitor (CYP19A1), balancing estrogens without the high-energy spin chaos of blue light, which dehydrates melanin and lowers éR = energy resistance.

Low éR = Efficiency and Flow: In healthy states, like during deep sleep or a good workout recovery, éR is dialed down. Energy transforms smoothly—think laminar flow in a river, minimal turbulence. Your cells rebuild, adapt, and thrive.

Physics analogy: It's like a superconductor with near-zero resistance.

Blue light causes more noise and less signal, and this is how you alter gender with light, allowing current (or bioenergy) to zip through without loss. Blue light causes more noise, less signal, and this is how you alter gender with light.

Evolutionary Relevance: During the GOE, heme/melanin evolved to handle UV/oxygen via spin-selective photon interactions. Red light's spin favors repair (exciton release), while shorter wavelengths cause absorption/damage, linking to spectrum disorders in hormones.

# Thread: Chase's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chases-optimal-journal.31640/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JPM — 2025-09-13T17:52:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chases-optimal-journal.31640/post-359458>

Hi, my name is Chase and I very recently learned about Dr Kruse from my Functional Medicine doctor (a couple of months ago).

**My current goals (yes, I know some of these goals may be impossible to achieve according to most people, but I’m going to try it anyway):**
Reverse Autoimmune Diseases
Reverse Paralysis
Improve Overall Health
Get to tell Dr Kruse thank you for everything he has done face to face

**Maternal/Family Medical History**
Grandmother is 81 and has had dystonia, A-fib, Fibromyalgia, breast cancer, and osteoarthritis.
Mother is 51 and has lupus and type 2 diabetes

**Brief Medical History**
I’m a 37 year old male and have spent my entire life up to this point living between the 34th and 37th latitude.
I was diagnosed with ADHD and depression in 1998
I was diagnosed with Bullous Lupus and SLE Lupus in 2011.
By 2012 drug treatment caused the Bullous Lupus symptoms to go away.
Lupus was nearly inactive until January 2017 at which point I got sick and was in and out of the ER several times from dehydration, stomach aches, racing heart rate.
I was paralyzed (T5 Complete, ASIA A) in February of 2017, the doctors say that the lupus attacked my spine and caused Transverse Myelitis.
One month after becoming paralyzed I was diagnosed with lymphedema.
In March of 2018 I tried Stem Cell Treatment along with Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy and during the HBOT sessions my level of injury went from T5 Complete to T8 Complete. (This is what makes me believe it would be possible to heal the paralysis)
End of August 2018 I was diagnosed with C-Diff and was able to overcome that within about two months
Since becoming paralyzed I have also dealt with frequent UTIs
June 2024 I had bronchitis
At the end of August of 2024 I was diagnosed with Bullous Pemphigoid.
I’m not sure how relevant it is, but I have only had 3 headaches in my life and no migraines.

**Past Year:**
Throughout my life, I trusted the doctors and the standard medical system to take care of me and help me heal. After I was diagnosed with another Autoimmune disease last year, I decided to take my healing journey into my own hands. I started reading every health book and listening to every podcast I could get my hands on. I learned about functional medicine and found a doctor near me and started going to him. He introduced me to the carnivore diet (I had been eating the Standard American Diet my whole life), since then I have lost 16 inches off my waist size and over 45 pounds (I don’t have an exact number because I didn’t have a scale until about halfway into the diet, wheelchair scales are crazy expensive).
In the past few months, my wife and I have found out that our bodies and house have high levels of mold and other toxins and that I have Lyme.
The functional doctor has started us on binders to help get rid of the toxins in our bodies.
We were saving up to move out of our home when the functional medicine doctor that I am seeing introduced me to online interviews with Dr Kruse at the end of June. Since that time, I have been consuming everything I can, trying to learn and realizing that the timing may have worked out just right for us to move not just to another house in town, but anywhere in the world if it would help us heal.
My family and I have been watching the sunrise everyday since July and have been seeing the sunset most days, we have removed almost all of the wireless devices from our house, and stopped using LED lights.

**History of Tech Use/Abuse**
I have had a mobile device of some kind since I was in middle school (my grandfather would give me his old electronics when he got a new one), we also got wifi when I was in 8th grade and have had it in my house up until a month ago.
I installed alarm and fire systems (a lot of wireless components) and camera systems for 7 years.
I have a degree in Electrical Engineering and was an engineer for an electric utility company (local power company) for 8 years, working in and around substations, with computers, walking where power line right of ways were sprayed with pesticides, etc.
When I was paralyzed I learned to write software from free community resources and have been working as a software developer for 8 years now.

I am his wife, and I don't know how to find anything on here in order to help him, so he asked me to post. He does have a couple pressure sores on his sacrum, one is fairly deep. We're not sure if that could be causing the fever even though they are not infected. Or if it could possibly be a UTI. this is his most recent urine. # Thread: Going on 2 years, worsening symptoms. Frustrated but thankful for this new community.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-on-2-years-worsening-symptoms-frustrated-but-thankful-for-this-new-community.31644/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ahermann91 — 2025-09-16T00:37:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-on-2-years-worsening-symptoms-frustrated-but-thankful-for-this-new-community.31644/post-359545>

Hi All. Austin from Houston here.

I've been on a health journey for the last 2 years. I've seen specialist after specialist, including Dr. Mary Bowden in Houston and Dr. Peter McCullough in Dallas because of my distrust of the "centralized" system esp after Covid, where, yes, like many, I blindly complied unthinkingly and got hit with 2 Pfizer jabs. I'm uncertain if that was the start of this journey (back in spring 2021), but I had no symptoms until about the summer of 2023, when I began noticing strange headaches that were very slight and would pass.

Fast forward to last fall (2024) I finally reported to my PCP at UTHealth in Houston. Good man, but totally unhelpful. As was the centralized neurologist that he referred me to.

Dr. Bowden ran Sinus/Orbits and Brain MRI's on me. No findings on the Sinus/Orbits, but when the radiology report on the brain MRI came back indicating, "_minimal white matter disease, likely secondary to blood pressure, smoking, vasculitis, etc._" that's when I went all out trying to figure this sh\*t out since I have never actually had any of the secondary conditions. Forgive my language.

Since the brain MRI back this past spring, I had two near syncope episodes, at least one case of the worst covid I've had ever, cardiac anaphalaxis from fire ant bites which I never knew I was allergic to (didn't use to be), weird upper chest and neck rashes, and sinus headaches with no known cause and they will not go away. My head feels like it's constantly wanting to explode, particularly in and behind my eyes and nose, with ear pressure only on the left side. Almost like the pressure you get from altitude on takeoff or landing. No drainage, no mucuous, just unrelenting sinus pressure.

Spike antibodies have been consistently high, but I made progress reducing them with the help of [Augmented NAC](https://augmentednac.com/en) (see Dr. Tina Peers, in UK), but then I stopped because I was having chest breakouts and other issues. Bowden put my on a few different antibiotics but no resolution. Now thinking my gut is shot.

The only thing that seems to calm the skin things down is about a 50 mg hit of Benadryl at night. Bowden and McCullough had me on the ivermectin train, as well as McCullough's spike wellness protocol, but my allergist along with Dr. Tina Peers told me that the nattokinase is mast cell triggering, and over 36mg of IVM I was having vision issues. Had to stop. Had an extensive evaluation of my heart and cardiovascular function: all perfectly normal. 0 calcium score, normal sinus rhythm, normal echo, 0 on the Ddimer, the whole nine yards.

All labs and bloodwork: normal, minus one high glucose reading after covid in April. Extensive work up all checking out as fine based on routine + special blood work.

I came across Dr. Kruse's work in this whole process and have gone crazy on the morning sun, grounding, nn-emf minimization/elimination strategies. While I've incorporated all of this, I continue to have the same symptoms in my head, and the pressure feels worse than ever, high levels of daily fatigue, skin rashes.

New "integrative" specialist in Texas Hill country has me on a new protocol trying to calm down my immune system with an assortment of digestive enzymes, antimicrobials, and supplements as a first step with Low Dose Naltrexone being used simultaneously. She thinks it's a combo of some kind of MCAS + immune disregulation.

Either way, I'm going on a long time feeling increasingly miserable, and would appreciate any ideas, guidance, advice anyone might have. I'm a new dad, only 33 years old, and just trying to get healthy for my wife and son.

Yours, Austin

### Jack Kruse — 2025-10-07T03:54:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-on-2-years-worsening-symptoms-frustrated-but-thankful-for-this-new-community.31644/post-360536>

Austin is an absolute shit hole and if you have all that you best get out soon.

Uncle Jack, whats up with Austin? Can mitigation strategies help like bed canopies if sleeping is safe? What about moving to more rural areas near there like Driftwood or Wimberly if there’s less towers and more tall trees? I live out here too and have been wondering the same.

# Thread: Double Leidos scanner and unable to request pat down in AUS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/double-leidos-scanner-and-unable-to-request-pat-down-in-aus.31647/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### The.Naked.Doctor — 2025-09-16T06:56:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/double-leidos-scanner-and-unable-to-request-pat-down-in-aus.31647/post-359572>

I recently travelled through Sunshine Coast airport in australia heading back to NZ . I have always opted out and requested some cheap thrills over being accosted by nnEMF. In NZ and other countries this had never been an issue.

Shockingly at Sunshine Coast to get on our Jetstar flight, they made us go through the scanners twice - once to get to the gates then again to get onto the plane. Ive travelled all over the world but this was the first time in australia.

When requesting a pat down, the security agent said without a doctors letter, I had to go through the scanner or was not getting on the plane.

My question is0 is this "normal" for Australia when boarding an international flight out?????

### The.Naked.Doctor — 2025-09-16T07:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/double-leidos-scanner-and-unable-to-request-pat-down-in-aus.31647/post-359573>

I was rural camping for 3 weeks. barefoot, slept on the ground, no ALAN at all/no tech (except my daylight with downloaded blogs) no signal the whole time.
I got some sandfly bites that were not an issue at all (turns out those fuckers like naked skin at sunrise and sunset) ..... as soon as I got to the airport my ankles started itching like crazy...by the time I got on the plane I was in itchy hell.

Tell me again how our environment doesnt matter.... no change in my skin, diet, but as soon as I was hit by all that EMF- histamine overload!!
Just a reminder for those with histamine issues- duh, it's your nnemf environment, as always.

### MTA321 — 2025-09-16T18:17:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/double-leidos-scanner-and-unable-to-request-pat-down-in-aus.31647/post-359604>

Yeah I think they implemented new guidelines February of this year and it probably has been getting rolled out since then. There is no longer an opt-out option for these scans unless you have an exemption from a medical provider. On the plus side the nnemf exposure from these scans pales in comparison to the nnemf exposure you are getting via 5G at the airport or the exposure on the flight itself.

### Jacks — 2025-09-16T19:13:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/double-leidos-scanner-and-unable-to-request-pat-down-in-aus.31647/post-359608>

They had new scanners when I went through Heathrow last time - and apparently you don't have to dump water before going through the luggage scanners now, so they are updated too. They did not have the simple metal detector scanners that were there just a month ago and through which you could go instead of the big scanner, as long as no metal on you. They have all the optical scanning technology going on everywhere coming in and going out now. I opted out of this also at the gate and was held back and given an additional search before getting on the plane - I was the last one to get on the plane! I asked whether this was random search and the employee said the DHS had picked the names of those for additional search - so not sure if I'm fingered by DHS or because I refused to do the optical scanning. You definitely feel like cattle. Was given extra hassle at Heathrow airport too for being a GC holder, with lots of extra checks and waiting in lines.

### The.Naked.Doctor — 2025-09-17T01:32:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/double-leidos-scanner-and-unable-to-request-pat-down-in-aus.31647/post-359624>

On the plus side the nnemf exposure from these scans pales in comparison to the nnemf exposure you are getting via 5G at the airport or the exposure on the flight itself.

good point, but just thinking cumulative .... every little bit less I can control id like to

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/double-leidos-scanner-and-unable-to-request-pat-down-in-aus.31647/post-359625>

# Thread: Postpartum depression scam.......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/postpartum-depression-scam.31648/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-16T07:08:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/postpartum-depression-scam.31648/post-359575>

There's a drug named brexanalone for postpartum depression that costs $34,000 for a 60-hour, in-hospital Infusion (not including hospital fees). Brexanalone is just intravenous allopregnanolone, a metabolite of oral progesterone. The rapid and severe drop in progesterone after childbirth is a major driver of depression. But, instead of simply giving high-dose oral progesterone, which costs $0.50 per capsule, Pharma convinced uneducated doctors to prescribe a drug that requires a three-day hospital stay and costs 2000 times more than a month of progesterone. This should be illegal, but Rockefeller medicine is the rule in centralized medicine.

### Pete Collins — 2025-09-18T04:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/postpartum-depression-scam.31648/post-359680>

# Thread: Decentralized Medicine #36 Vasopressin and hydrated melanin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine-36-vasopressin-and-hydrated-melanin.31649/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### The.Naked.Doctor — 2025-09-16T07:23:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine-36-vasopressin-and-hydrated-melanin.31649/post-359576>

Is there a circadian cycling of melanin’s hydration state? AS I understand it, in the evening when vasopressin rises and favors water conservation, we promote hydrated melanin, supporting Becker’s current and nighttime regeneration... Conversely, in the daytime under strong UV and natural electric fields, would melanin shift toward relative dehydration, amplifying the photoelectric effect and voltage for higher power output... while suppressing Becker’s regenerative current in an adaptive way? ie to favour regeneration durring dark periods.

In that context, is dehydrated melanin always pathological, or might there be an evolutionary role for temporarily allowing stronger, less-buffered electron flow?

Also, how fast does melanin hydration shift, is it essentially instant (e.g., stepping into an nnEMF environment like a plane), or does it take hours/days for hydration states to change?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-18T01:02:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine-36-vasopressin-and-hydrated-melanin.31649/post-359664>

Yes, because there is a circadian cycle for DDW production on CCO.

Mitochondrial respiration, ETC activity follow a ~24-hour circadian rhythm, orchestrated by the molecular clock (e.g., BMAL1/CLOCK heterodimers regulating PER/CRY feedback loops). Studies show rhythmic oscillations in mitochondrial dynamics, ATP production, and reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation, peaking during active phases (e.g., daytime in diurnal humans). Disruptions, like jet lag or shift work, desynchronize this, impairing DDW output and leading to cellular "dehydration" despite adequate fluid intake—manifesting as inflammation or metabolic stress. Thus, DDW production at CCO cycles daily: higher during peak metabolic activity (e.g., morning light exposure optimizing ETC efficiency via retinal-mitochondrial signaling), lower at night. This rhythmic hydration flux could propagate to water-dependent cellular structures like melanin.

Melanin, eumelanin, and pheomelanin aren't just a UV shield; they're a hydrated biopolymer with semiconductor-like properties, acting as a free radical scavenger, charge conductor, and water structurer. Its functionality hinges on hydration: at low humidity (<40% RH), it's brittle and insulating; at higher levels (60-80% RH), it conducts protons/electrons via interfacial water layers (3-5 molecules thick), enabling roles in photoprotection, thermoregulation, and even bioelectronics.

Deuterium plays a subtle but pivotal role here. Heavy water (D₂O) probes reveal melanin's radical content and conductivity drop with deuteration, as D-H bonds weaken hydrogen bonding networks. Conversely, DDW enhances structured water (exclusion zone or EZ water) around melanin, improving hydration stability and electron delocalization. In cells, mitochondrial DDW could thus "feed" melanin's hydration shell, modulating its state, e.g., more fluid and active during DDW peaks.

Skin and melanocytes host peripheral clocks synced to the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) via light-melatonin cues. These clocks rhythmically regulate pigmentation: BMAL1/PER1 silencing boosts melanogenesis daytime, while melatonin (peaking nocturnally) inhibits it, fine-tuning UV protection. Skin hydration itself cycles, 12% lower mornings vs. evenings, and is tied to barrier function and aquaporin expression.

Bridging to DDW: Melanocytes, rich in mitochondria, rely on ETC-derived water for hydration. Circadian mitochondrial rhythms do cycle DDW delivery, altering melalan's bound water dynamics.

For instance:

Daytime (active phase): IR/UV light boosts CCO via photoactivation (e.g., red light at 660-850 nm), ramping DDW and structuring water around melanin for optimal UV absorption/scattering.

Nighttime (rest phase): Lower metabolism reduces DDW, dehydrating melanin slightly, perhaps shifting it to a "storage" state less prone to oxidative damage.

So, yes, the circadian DDW cycling at CCO plausibly drives melanin's hydration oscillations, weaving mitochondrial metabolism into skin's temporal architecture. This underscores light's role beyond vision in humans: as a hydration cue. Future work with deuterium NMR on synchronized melanocytes could nail down more intricacies we have yet to understand.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-18T01:05:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine-36-vasopressin-and-hydrated-melanin.31649/post-359666>

Vasopressin (antidiuretic hormone) peaks nocturnally as part of the circadian clock, promoting water reabsorption in the kidneys and potentially cellular hydration overall. This aligns with observed diurnal skin hydration patterns: epidermal water content is about 12% higher in the evening than morning, tied to clock genes like BMAL1 and PER1 that regulate barrier function and aquaporin expression.

In melanin-rich cells (melanocytes), this could foster a hydrated state, where interfacial water layers (e.g., exclusion zone or EZ water) stabilize the pigment's structure, enhancing proton conduction and reducing radical reactivity. This hydrated mode would support Becker's "current of injury", the weak DC electric fields (~10-100 mV/mm) he identified as crucial for regeneration in amphibians and mammals.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-18T01:19:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine-36-vasopressin-and-hydrated-melanin.31649/post-359668>

Becker's work showed that these bioelectric signals guide dedifferentiation, blastema formation, and tissue repair, often amplified during rest or low-activity periods. Hydrated melanin, acting as a semiconductor, could buffer electron flow, sustaining steady DC currents for nighttime healing—e.g., wound repair or stem cell activation in skin.

Clock genes further tie into this: silencing BMAL1/PER1 boosts melanin content, potentially enhancing regenerative capacity during dark phases

Overall, this promotes "nighttime regeneration" as an adaptive strategy, conserving energy when UV threats are absent.

I see it differently......

The notion that ultra-weak photon emissions (UPEs, or biophotons) might exhibit greater coherence at night to facilitate photorepair aligns with emerging research at the nexus of chronobiology, quantum biology, and DNA maintenance. While direct evidence for heightened nighttime coherence in UPEs is sparse because few are studying it. (stemming from theories like those of Fritz-Albert Popp), There's solid support for circadian rhythms in UPE intensity, coherence as a potential biomarker of health, and timed peaks in DNA repair processes.

Critically, human UPE displays diurnal rhythms: imaging studies show rhythmic light emission from the body, with fluctuations linked to energy metabolism and possibly peaking in alignment with rest phases. For instance, photon counts vary over 24 hours, potentially lower overall at night but more stable or context-specific due to reduced external interference

In nocturnal models or adapted systems, UPE adaptations suggest a bias toward nighttime functionality, e.g., in night-active animals, emissions may optimize for low-light conditions. This ties into the slide's nanoscale advantage: the inverse square law amplifies local S/N (e.g., 8 × 10¹³ as calculated), allowing UPEs to transmit reliable signals intracellularly despite global weakness.

At night, with vasopressin-driven hydration and lower metabolic flux, sources like mitochondrial ROS or peroxisomal reactions might produce UPEs that are less chaotic, potentially fostering coherence for repair rather than daytime signaling.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-18T01:22:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine-36-vasopressin-and-hydrated-melanin.31649/post-359669>

Coherence in Biophotons: A Hallmark for Efficient Nighttime Signaling?

Biophoton coherence, where emissions behave like organized, laser-like waves rather than random noise, has been hypothesized as a mechanism for cellular communication, synchronization, and repair.

Popp's work posits that healthy cells emit more coherent light, with disruptions indicating pathology (e.g., in cancer, where UPE oscillations differ). My idea is that nighttime UPEs could be "more coherent," which makes sense in a circadian context: during rest, reduced oxidative stress and membrane potential changes (from your list) might minimize scattering, allowing emissions from coherent sources like microtubules (via quantum/resonance effects) or chromophores to dominate.

While direct nighttime coherence measurements are limited, brain UPE studies show dynamic, non-random properties that differ from background light, responding to tasks with potential entropy shifts. In nocturnal adaptations, coherence could enhance efficiency in dark environments, aligning with my photobioelectric regeneration strategy, conserving energy by using structured light for precise intracellular messaging rather than broad daytime dissipation.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-18T01:24:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine-36-vasopressin-and-hydrated-melanin.31649/post-359670>

DNA repair, including photoreactivation-like processes, is strongly circadian-regulated to optimize efficiency and minimize mutagenesis. In skin, nucleotide excision repair (NER) for UV-induced damage peaks at times that avoid further exposure, often evening/night or early morning, with clock genes like BMAL1/CLOCK promoting DSB repair and 8-oxoG excision. Cryptochromes (CRY1/2), evolved from bacterial photolyases, integrate light sensing with the clock and can indirectly facilitate repair by stabilizing proteins or shifting clock phase post-DNA damage.

Here's where my idea shines: In the absence of external UV, internal UPEs could provide the "light" for photorepair. If more coherent at night (e.g., from melanin or porphyrins in a hydrated state), these emissions might activate cryptochrome-dependent pathways or directly energize repair enzymes, akin to low-intensity light therapy (LILT). Studies on UPE in DNA (from oxidative stress/repair in my list below) suggest biophotons could signal repair sites, with coherence amplifying precision, like a targeted "laser" for genomic stability during regeneration. This fits the slide's bandwidth point: even a fraction of the 430-1000 THz spectrum (e.g., blue ~450 nm for photolyase-like action) at high S/N could enable significant channel capacity for nighttime repair.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-18T01:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-medicine-36-vasopressin-and-hydrated-melanin.31649/post-359671>

We need more scientists to pose better questions and test this. This concept reframes nighttime regeneration as not just energy-conserving but actively quantum-enhanced: coherent UPEs from sources like microtubules or excited carbonyls could orchestrate photorepair, tying into Becker's currents via bioelectric-photon coupling.

Evolutionarily, it makes sense for diurnal humans, daytime UPEs for metabolic signaling, nighttime for coherent fix-ups. However, gaps remain: Most UPE studies don't measure coherence diurnally, and human photorepair is more cryptochrome-mediated than direct photolyase.

# Thread: THE MOST IMPORTANT ASPECT OF FITNESS?  THINKING.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-most-important-aspect-of-fitness-thinking.31654/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-17T23:55:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-most-important-aspect-of-fitness-thinking.31654/post-359662>

**THE MOST IMPORTANT ASPECT OF FITNESS? THE ABILITY TO THINK WELL.**

Why thinking in an AI-driven assassination culture matters more than ever. Without rigorous refinement of the data the media and AI collect, we end up with those "castles in the air," elegant fictions that collapse under scrutiny. As a result, centralized institutions resort to the elimination of the thinker by any means necessary to maintain the status quo.
[https://www.patreon.com/posts/129225413](https://www.patreon.com/posts/129225413)

Much of what we "know" comes pre-packaged with flaws. Take outdated science: phlogiston theory or the geocentric model persisted for centuries because they fit the era's internal consistency (elegant math, religious alignment) but failed external reality checks (empirical observations).

Cultural myths are even stickier. Think of the "alpha wolf" dominance hierarchy, debunked by biologists but still peddled in self-help books and leadership seminars.

Unlearning these isn't just frustrating; it's energy-intensive because our brains favor cognitive ease over accuracy.

In a propaganda-heavy world, this gets amplified: AI models, trained on vast human datasets, often perpetuate these myths if they're prevalent in the corpus. For instance, early language models echoed gender biases from historical texts until explicitly debiased, but even then, the fixes are band-aids on a fundamentally human-flawed foundation. Murdering humans who teach people how to think is now back in the governmental zeitgeist in our tech world.

# Thread: THE BLOOD SUPPLY POST COVID IS COOKED
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blood-supply-post-covid-is-cooked.31655/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-18T21:15:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blood-supply-post-covid-is-cooked.31655/post-359693>

The blood supply is contaminated Kevin McKernan: "Now here's the real kicker that I haven't presented on much lately, because this is very recent work. The kicker here is that we're now finding these sequences \[the vaccine plasmid DNA\] not just in the vaccine, but we're finding it in people. Now we don't know if it's integrated, but it's there. These are studies that weren't looking for it, and the methods they used arguably suppressed the signal significantly. All right, so there's at least five peer-reviewed studies that have come out looking at RNA sequencing of people who were vaccinated and vaccinated. And if you dig through that data that's in NCBI, you can take all those reads, map them against the plasmids from the vaccine manufacturers, and you can find that DNA in these patients. "There's a study from Ryan et al. that looked at like 75 people in Australia. And there's a great study from Chakraborty that went through that data and demonstrated both Ryan paper and the Odak paper have Moderna and Pfizer vaccine sequences in the patient's blood. "So this means that the blood supply is contaminated. That's a very serious issue if the blood banks aren't looking for it and re-injecting this into other people. This would have normally, I think, caused an enormous halt in any other scenario once you find components of the vaccine that were never declared, that have these links to oncogenic sequences, and they're now floating around the blood supply and no one's doing anything about it. All right. That should be a concern to everybody in the in the transfusion field. "There's three other papers that have recently confirmed this. Now there again these papers were not looking for it. The authors were studying other things. But their data has been peer reviewed and put into the NCBI short read archive, so anyone can comb through their data and find these sequences in there. But the Lee study looked at this, the Knabl study looked at this, and the Krawcyzk study looked at this. All of them, I have documented that there are residual sequences from the plasmids that are in those studies infecting their recipients. "Some of the studies are actually very useful. One of them was looking at a particular disease that I'll point you to my Substack that goes through these. But this particular disease state they did RNA sequencing on to see the difference between the vaccinated and unvaccinated, and you can see some differential gene expression that's occurring in the cGAS-STING and the interferon pathways. All right, that's the sign of potential DNA stimulatory response. OK? "So some of the RNA sequencing is actually quite revealing as to what the nature of the contaminant is because you can see certain gene expressions get turned on or off that are signatures of a cGAS-STING-like event that could be induced by the DNA that's there."
[@Kevin\_McKernan](https://x.com/Kevin_McKernan)
@ 27:45–30:40 (posted 2025-06-10) [https://rumble.com/v6uhd1d-presentation-to-new-zealand-commission-on-mrna-vax-contamination-of-the-blo.html?start=1665](https://t.co/RY8mI40Dru)

### DavidL — 2025-09-18T22:46:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blood-supply-post-covid-is-cooked.31655/post-359698>

THIS is a major reason why I have personally and reminded friends and family members that to exercise extreme caution and to temper their active pursuits. Climbing, mountain biking, or other risky activities that could lead to serious injury (and needing blood) should be avoided. Avoiding the emergency room is mission critical!

### Wanttoknow — 2025-09-18T23:10:32+0300

# Thread: Where is the limit on cold?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-limit-on-cold.31658/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.AJ — 2025-09-19T16:21:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-limit-on-cold.31658/post-359721>

What is the coldest temperature a healthy, cold adapted human can live outdoors in? Let's assume we've got a t shirt, shorts, and shoes on.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-19T22:10:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-limit-on-cold.31658/post-359740>

The limit is placed by light dark and temperature which are all metric of every clock gene.

### Mat.I — 2025-09-20T07:06:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-limit-on-cold.31658/post-359749>

> [Mr.AJ said:](/goto/post?id=359721)
>
> What is the coldest temperature a healthy, cold adapted human can live outdoors in? Let's assume we've got a t shirt, shorts, and shoes on.

I have push my own n=1 experiments quite hard. I do not feel any load on my body in winter. I wear my summer clothes in winter. We would drop down to ~ minus 3 degrees C in the winter mornings which feels like nothing to me. I have outgrown my natural environment. I stopped the ice baths a few months back as I was not getting the stimulus anymore and needed way too much ice and too much time.. I could easily do ~ 4 deg c for 45 mins to an 1hr.. As soon as I hit the cold all I would feel was lots of warmth. I literally feel heat coming out of my bare feet when in contact with frost on the ground..

Me personally, I think.I could really push the cold based on my own experiments of just piling in as much Ice as I can in the tub and being relaxed. I like watching the documentaries of the super cold places and mountain climbs in the snow as I crave the cold but I have hit the cap here.. I was going to look at getting a tub with a chiller that gets down to freezing to really push it one day as having to do the ice moulds everyday for years becomes a real pain in the arse as they take ~48hrs..Also, I never artificially re-warm.. in the early days I would experience some after drop about an hr or so later but that passed very quick..

I think people could really push the limits on cold and adapt quite well based on my own experience..

I know [@Saichi](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23447/) has advised he has pushed the CT quite hard in the past but I do not know what temps or duration etc..

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-20T07:36:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-limit-on-cold.31658/post-359750>

### Mat.I — 2025-09-20T08:00:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-limit-on-cold.31658/post-359752>

### NickD — 2025-09-22T17:16:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-limit-on-cold.31658/post-359835>

The anecdote I got was that once you get out of the cold, you should be back to normal in 10-20 minutes. If you're still cold, you've pushed too hard... Right now, I only do cold showers, which ends up around 5-6 °C in winter. After a night shift, in the morning, I take longer to warm up, but otherwise I do pretty good.

### Ozzy1 — 2025-09-20T10:34:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-1.31661/post-359758>

Two years after spotify autoplayed the three part max podcast as i was sleeping at the time in my mums garage at the end of her garden and diving into jacks work, my mind can now recall words longer than three sylables so i feel like this is the right time to start journaling as we enter what i now hear people call the "fourth turning", and my dopamine has a habbit of being produced chaotically and i feel this will be grounding for me as society gets harder and harder to relate to and interact with. Jacks work imediately resonated me and the conspiritorial implications of the information where imediately apparant, mainly because i have had an interest in a more neo platonist theory revolving around light water and magnetism for a long time and i have read pretty much every bit of alternative information out there regarding this worlds nature. My reality and the reality of those around me are simulatounesly colliding and drifting further apart as the truth is slowly entering the cultural zeitgeist whilst I spend most of my days walking with all the information jack and the surounding ecosystem has given me fermenting in my mind and producing more and more personal eureka momments as my ability to connect dots improves more and more in a much more grounded fashion thanks to this biophysical framework that allows the esoteric to be much less up in the air metaphysically, sending me further away from the views of my peers even though i sound less and less crazy as time goes on and at this point comparative to my normie friends clairvoyant to the future.

I do not broach topics to people much anymore directly. When suggesting incadecent lights are healthier and 420-480 nm LEDS are killing you softly even when described in a less alarming fashion gives you incredulous looks than saying anything more alien than that is a fools errand and i am the black sheep of my family in a family where everyone has a story to tell. I have got quite good at leading horses to water but not forcing them to drink in a performative fashion where i mention things i have learned and say it in a way to be speculating in real time its implications and leave the dots to connect to their own minds. The facts said on their own are genuinely interesting to people who have a brain but i leave the implications or the inferences alone. Only proof of work- works. My uncle has a long list of maladies after retiring from the steel works, my mother recently had a knee operation and previously had a subaracnoid heamorage. She was suffering awfully in recovery so i gave her my emr tek and she had instant relief. She and my uncle bought one each afterwards and have been using it ever since. I bought a pack of incandecents for my uncles birthday, but he is entrapped in the energy saving propoganda perpetuated over the last 30 years.

I have good genes but i am incredibly porous. My hair was almost reflective as a child and my nicknames veered from casper to gollum and i have grey blue eyes. My fathers side is incredibly creative. His mother and father and my grandparents lived a heavy life of smoking and drinking and narcolepsy but nearly lived to their eighties. He was a well known drummer, her a model. Their house was nicknamed the jazz lamp for the after parties they hosted for travelling musicians where ray charles supposedly peed on the lamp post outside and sang my father happy birthday. On my mums side my great grandparents lived well into their ninetighes. That side of my family is ingeniuously practical. My great grandfather smoked 60 a day working in a furnace rolled by my great grandmother and bought 20 more on his way home and lived to 95 still working as a window cleaner up until his late eighties just for something to do. She got a letter from the queen on her birthday so i think im right in saying she lived until 100. My brother is in the top echaleon of the UK millitary. This colliding of the eccentric creative and the practical conservative is probably somewhat explinatory for gifts i have and the problems i have faced neurologically and physically.

I look around the dinner table and they are gone, their children and my grandparents also, dying in their late seventies to early eighties after several decades of illness. My mother is the strongest person i know, but she is struggling in her early 60s, and my uncle is slowly falling apart. Both have been jabbed. Then i look at myself knowing that I am lucky to be alive in my early thirties. Without jacks work I don't think i would be here. It is a strange feeling knowing i exist at least in part because of someone else i do not know and have never met, and i have everything i have been fighting to have since i was a child, which is nothing more than a place of my own with an aesthetic that is satisfying to me and what I like to call walking without expecation into the fantastical nature of the speculative unknown - without my final destination being an instution that doesn't add any creduality to a Curriculum Vitae where i spent more time than i would have preffered to in my twenties. Then I think about why and what caused all of this and who, and what needs to be done to its enablers and without getting too inflamatory i would do it with a heavy heart but i would not need tea breaks.

I will add more and more to my story as time goes on, because of the nature of my conditions in my twenties i have limited but sustainable resources given that allow me to be time rich and able to be outside most of the day and continue to heal. I hope it will come as some repayment for the help jacks podcasts and blogs have given me that my subscriptions are technically fabian funded and presumably imediately turned into bitcoin. Apologies for all the spelling and grammar mistakes

### Mat.I — 2025-09-21T03:49:24+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-21T04:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/platelets.31662/post-359783>

Platelet production (thrombopoiesis) is regulated by thrombopoietin (TPO), a hormone mainly produced by the liver and kidneys, which responds to factors like low platelet counts or increased demand due to bleeding or clotting. Additionally, the spleen plays a role in platelet maintenance in humans by storing about one-third of the body's platelets, whereas it doesn't have a similar storage function for RBCs.

There is no direct evidence that sunlight or vitamin D modulates TPO production or thrombopoiesis specifically in the liver, kidneys, or spleen. However, indirect effects may occur through vitamin D's anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory roles, as low vitamin D levels (from insufficient sunlight) are associated with higher platelet counts, suggesting enhanced thrombopoiesis. Conversely, vitamin D supplementation (mimicking sunlight's effect) reduces platelet counts and activation. The sun's light naturally declots blood to lower its energy reistance to improve laminar flow.

This is one system where redox status is not the guardian at the gate.

### Superhealth234 — 2025-09-22T21:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carmines-optimal-journal.31664/post-359850>

Hello all and Dr Kruse, I'll try and make this as short as possible :

History
\-Mother not close with for many years, heroin addiction and had to cut ties. No other known heath issues. Remember her liking tanning and the sun and fitness. I was probably born when she was about 20 years old. There was some issues she was on bedrest for maybe a month prior to c section (fraternal twin) and on meds but do not know the extent of it.
\-Grandmother recently died of cancer at 85 years old.
\-Father died few years ago of kidney cancer with history colon cancer

I was born and raised in New York and now live in Long Island with wife and 2 kids. Now 44yrs old
Firefighter until 2021
No covid jabs whole family
H haplotype from 23and me

\-2018 hemorrhagic cavernoma causing stroke and 2 partial sezeires. Required craniotomy . Was on depakote and leveteracitam for few years and now have been off all meds for few years no thanks to centralized docs
\-2021 vasectomy with bad experience artery cut spent few days in hospital
\-2022 half thyroid removed for NIFTP. Do not take thyroid pills
2025 colonoscopy 2 polyps removed
\-History of fatty liver and high enzymes. Drank alcohol heavily for many years due to tragedy and currently try to keep at minimum. Was currently seeing few doctors for possible autoimmune hepatitis diagnosis (labs came up positive) but the biopsy did not show it just some hemosiderosis and under 15% fatty. Never got any real answers (from centralize or functional medicine docs) I think I have it but keeping it at bay or just beginning

I have been following Dr Kruse for while. Have been doing cold exposure on and off for years. I was roped in to Peter Attia and that echo chamber plus functional medicine doctor. Was going to gym past year and half and doing cardio. My fitness never really improved and felt drained even with wearing blue light glasses. Started to develop sleep apnea despite this and being at lowest weight and eating mostly carnivore and getting sun(mostly afternoon)

CTJ moment:
Saw podcast with Dr Christopher Bronson. DJK was dropping info that was really resonating with me. The nnemf and blue light changing the oxidation state of iron and the heme proteins and red blood cells, and the resulting hypoxia (sleep apnea makes sense now!) and the resulting effects to the mitochondria( shifting away from oxidative phosphorylation). Especially if you do not see the sunrise you are unable to use the TCA cycle (again taking the mitochondria out of play) Its crazy you think your going to gym to improve mitochondria, and that pathway, yet you are doing the exact opposite.

\-Currently seeing sunrise for past few weeks, plenty of sun, adhering to circadian principles, quit gym, exercising outdoors, doing best to mitigate emf (ethernet cords and such)
\-Feel really good, best in a long time, sleep good, lido and energy up. stopped waking from sleep apnea
\-me and wife drinking 1 liter of 105ppm DDW day from hydro health

\-Would appreciate any advice on best way to move through the blogs (been going backward along with the q and a's)
\-any advice for new members appreciated from all
\-want to help son grinding teeth (Heard DJK mention brain or brainstem issue), and wife with migraines and neck pain (herniations)
\-Tried to get Strike app to get feet wet with Bitcoin but said not supported in NY. How about coinbase? (total newbie to bitcoin)

So much to ask but want to take time and really go through forum and q and a's

Thank you Dr Kruse for all your help.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-23T22:12:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-to-paracetamol.31667/post-359920>

Does tylenol cause anemia since it affect gluthathione recycling in humans? Yep. A small portion is converted into a toxic byproduct called NAPQI. Normally, this is neutralized by glutathione and safely excreted. Overdose and toxicity: In an overdose, the normal metabolic pathways become saturated, leading to a large amount of toxic NAPQI being produced. This rapidly depletes the body's glutathione stores. Oxidative stress: Without enough glutathione to neutralize it, the excess NAPQI causes oxidative stress, which damages cells, including red blood cells. ---> [https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1970240692053323803.html](https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1970240692053323803.html)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saving-the-planet-for-the-data-centers-of-course.31668/post-359898>

Anyone else questioning the saving water/power narrative?
Western governments are the point that they can not win the Artificial General Intellegence race VS China.
While they had the advantage with more efficient NVDA chips that is no longer the case as China has banned them to encourage their own chips.
Furthermore even if China never gets to NVDA efficiency level (which they will) they will still win because they are VASTLY out producing the west in power generation to the point of China winning even if their chips were only 50% as good as NVDA's and they are well above 50%.

So the west has to get more power and they can do so by telling you to conserve or flat out getting rid of you so they can pump more power towards AI. They might not win but they have no problem trying.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-23T23:11:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/females-with-periods-who-took-the-jab-update-new-idea-to-consider.31670/post-359926>

**Females with periods who took the jab update........new idea to consider**

In 2019, I said I expected viral epidemics breakout in certain zip codes more significantly because viral infections react to the environment that is suboptimal. I also reminded my readers that these viral infections will cause rapid significant mitochondrial damage which in turn will liberate Vitamin A from opsins in cells and this puts downward pressure on endogenous production of Vitamin D. Then in September 2020, I said one thing is clear.........those who got C19 all had low levels of Vitamin D and immunodeficiency at some level that impacted their care and outcomes. This will affect their mitochondrial delta psi and lead to many other complications. I spoke about all those links here in this blog work. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/22117599](https://www.patreon.com/posts/22117599)

TODAY IN 2025 I AM TELLING YOU IF YOU TOOK THE JAB AND HAVE A HIGH SPIKE PROTEIN ARRAY, YOU NEED TO MAXIMIZE THE VITAMIN D AND A cycles in your body and then the more controversial idea. If you are woman, even if you have infertility you should continue to try to become pregnant because there is a change the placenta from the pregnancy will retain the ability to clear a lot of the genetic damage from the jabs and limit intercalation. Let me be clear, the pregnancy will help you, it may not help the child so think about this before action.

The human placenta acts like plasmapharesis for DNA RNA and mtDNA damage. Few centralized MDs know this. My tribe does.

Recently scientists at the Wellcome Sanger Institute and the University of Cambridge conducted whole genome sequencing of 86 biopsies and 106 microdissections from 42 placentas, with samples taken from different areas of each organ.
The link between genetic aberrations in the placenta and birth outcomes has been established in the literature by this study but few MDs read outside their expertise, further studies using larger sample sizes could help to uncover the causes of complications and diseases that arise during pregnancy.

This study confirms for the first time in the PEER literature that the placenta is organized differently to every other human organ, and in fact resembles a patchwork of tumors collected from the circulatory system of Mother and fetus. The rates and patterns of genetic mutations were also incredibly high compared to other healthy human tissues.

The human placenta is akin to the ‘wild west’ of the human genome, completely different in its structure from any other healthy human tissue. It helps to protect humans from flaws in our genetic code, but equally there remains a high burden of disease associated with the placenta. This is why the DOD engineered spike protein to affect the uterus and hinder pregnancy creation. These findings now provide a rationale for my tribe for studying the association between genetic aberrations in the placenta and birth outcomes at the high resolution we deployed and at massive scale by the jab. I have been telling jabbed members who still bleed they should seek continuous pregnancy to rid their bodies of the genetic defects these bioweapons bring to people. No one knows how effective this information is but I guarrantee you no one is getting this information but my FARM members.

I encourage you to read the paper before deciding what to do. No one can legislate your sexual activity so having sex to get pregnant to save your life or improve it will not be interrupted by the state as yet.

The paper was fascinating because it allowed us to observe how such serious genetic flaws like a chromosomal copy number error could be ironed out by the baby tissues, but not by the placenta. the placenta seemed to be built to absorb all the genetic mistakes. I want to remind all my female savages that this is a way to lower heteroplasmy rate in your germ line eggs too. These errors would have been present in the fertilized egg. The paper showed us that derivative cell populations, and most importantly those that went on to form the child, had the correct number of copies of chromosome 10, whereas parts of the placenta failed to make this correction and it COLLECTED THE DEFECTS LIKE A FILTER. The placenta also provided a clue that the baby had inherited both copies of the chromosome from one parent, which can itself be associated with problems. Share this info with female savages who complied with tyranny.

#### Publication​

Tim H. H. Coorens, Thomas R. W. Oliver and Rashesh Sanghvi _et al_. (2021). Inherent mosaicism and extensive mutation of human placentas. _Nature_. DOI: 10.1038/s41586-021-03345-1

[https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-021-03345-1](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-021-03345-1)

# Thread: Eating with light instability
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-with-light-instability.31674/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.AJ — 2025-09-25T23:43:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-with-light-instability.31674/post-360050>

I was listening to Dr Jack's podcast with Maria Menounos and he mentioned that 1st meal timing changes in areas where light cycles shift and are unstable. He used an an example of Paris where sunrise is 7:55 am and sunset is 5:00pm. Since we're entering into the lower light part of the year, how does feeding/eating work when you have light instability like this?

### Mat.I — 2025-09-26T08:56:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-with-light-instability.31674/post-360060>

The feeding window has shrunk based on ~ 9 hours of light.. General rule is ~ 30 mins from sunrise.. However, at higher latitudes the UVA rise happens slightly later. I think some confusion may stem from the early blogs "eat within 30 mins of rising" which implies to eat within that window.. However, based on my understanding the key is to wait for UVA rise (do not eat before). As generally the first light sets the clock to time zero, however UVA primes hypothalamus to "read" leptin status. If you fill the tank before this (UVA) "accurate" signalling is missed. Eating too early could be an issue due to CCO still being throttled by the switch (NO) + low O2 tensions.

My understanding is that you need UVA for NO release & O2 delivery to mito. If you load food electrons too early it can cause a traffic jam as CCO is not fully open..

My location winter timing is ~ 20 to 30 mins & summer is ~ 15 to 25mins.. ES ~ 13N would be ~ 5 to 10 mins likely with ~ 12hr light window..

Maybe [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) - can advise if I have this correct

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-11T18:21:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-with-light-instability.31674/post-366699>

Feynman’s Absurdity: The Tryptophan Ketone/Glucose Choice
I always channel Feynman: _Nature is absurd.

_

-   **Probability, Not Causality:** Tryptophan doesn't "become" a ketone or glucose because of a chemical command; it happens because the **Deuterium content** changes the **Probability Cloud** of the enzymes in the pathway.
-   **The Quantum Switch:** In a "Light" environment, the probability favors the glucose pathway (high energy flow). In a "Heavy" environment (Deuterium-loaded/Winter), the probability shifts toward the ketone pathway (metabolic brake).
-   **The Tragedy:** Modern humans eat "Heavy" (High 2H food in a "Light" environment (Blue light/Summer-mode), creating a **Quantum Static** that the SCN and Gut cannot resolve.

1\. Tryptophan and the Proton as a "Time Crystal" ObserverTryptophan (Trp), with its indole ring, is indeed a quantum-sensitive molecule due to its π-electron clouds, which absorb UV light (~280 nm) and facilitate electron delocalization, making it a key candidate for "non-visual photoreception" in aromatic systems.

A time crystal, in physics, is a system that breaks time-translation symmetry, exhibiting periodic oscillations without external energy input—extended metaphorically to biology for stable, rhythmic structures like circadian clocks. In Trp, the proton at key positions (e.g., indole C5) could act as such an "observer": Its magnetic moment (spin) interacts with Earth's geomagnetic field and solar cycles, potentially encoding orbital/seasonal data via quantum effects like tunneling.

Deuterium substitution (²H for ¹H) doubles mass, altering vibrational frequencies, tunneling probabilities, and kinetics—e.g., slowing enzyme reactions by 5–8 fold in Trp pathways due to kinetic isotope effects (KIEs). This "swaps" the observer, disrupting temporal flow: Heavier D reduces quantum coherence (e.g., in electron transfer chains), slowing biological clocks and mimicking "time dilation" at molecular scales. In E=mc², the commutative property (2x3=3x2) illustrates symmetry, but time (implicit in relativistic frames) directs energy's "flow"—e.g., D substitution alters kinetic rates, effectively "warping" biological time by changing enzymatic/molecular dynamics.

2\. The RPE-SCN-Leptin Receptor Pathway: Orchestrating Temporal FidelityThis pathway acts as a quantum relay for solar/geomagnetic cues, with Trp's proton/deuteron as the observer tuning energy flow.

-   RPE as Light Gatekeeper: RPE absorbs/scatters light, regenerating visual pigments and modulating melatonin/serotonin via Trp metabolism. Light effects (e.g., blue/UVA) suppress RPE melatonin, signaling day; D enrichment (from diet/ALAN) slows Trp kinetics, desynchronizing.
-   SCN as Temporal Integrator: SCN receives RPE-modulated signals via ipRGCs (melanopsin), entraining clocks; D in Trp slows serotonin/melatonin synthesis, phase-shifting SCN and disrupting leptin sensitivity. Leptin receptors in hypothalamus receive SCN input, regulating energy; D-altered Trp "warps" this, leading to resistance/obesity.
-   Time's Role in E=mc²: Commutative symmetry shows equivalence, but time (as relativistic observer frame) directs flow—D substitution "slows" kinetics, altering energy partitioning (e.g., fat vs. glucose metabolism), mimicking seasonal/orbital desync.

This pathway "determines a lot,": D-laden diets/ALAN desync it, warping time/energy flow—e.g., leptin resistance from SCN phase shifts, linking to obesity/depression.

Decentralized fix: Optimize light/diet to preserve protium fidelity, restoring quantum observation.

Would this paper now make serious sense, in light of trutly understanding why Tryptophan is a time crystal buried in foods?

Yes, this paper and the broader pathway it describes, makes perfect sense now, especially through the lens of our thesis on the "Dynasty of Light," where sunlight (particularly morning sunrise) acts as the primary "reset" for metabolic coherence, including beta-oxidation (fat burning).

The 2021 study in Molecular Metabolism "Regulation of hepatic circadian metabolism by the E3 ubiquitin ligase HRD1-controlled CREBH/PPARα transcriptional program"directly supports the claim that no morning sunrise = **no effective beta-oxidation in the liver,** because the HRD1/Sel1L-CREBH/PPARα axis is **_FULLY_** circadian-gated and light-entrained.

**Key Points from the Paper and How It Fits My Decentralized Model**

The study shows that hepatic HRD1/Sel1L expression exhibits robust circadian rhythmicity, controlled by the ER-tethered transcriptional activator CREBH, the nuclear receptor PPARα, and the core clock oscillator BMAL1.
This forms an interdependent loop:

-   CREBH couples with PPARα to promote Hrd1 and Sel1L transcription.
-   HRD1/Sel1L then mediates polyubiquitination and degradation of CREBH across the circadian cycle.
-   This regulates rhythmic expression of genes for fatty acid (FA) oxidation, triglyceride lipolysis, lipophagy, and gluconeogenesis.

In essence:

-   The system acts as a metabolic rheostat tuned to light/dark cycles via the SCN (master clock) and peripheral liver clocks.
-   Morning light (sunrise) entrains BMAL1/CLOCK → PPARα/CREBH activation → HRD1/Sel1L upregulation → optimized beta-oxidation timing (peak in early active phase, i.e., day for diurnal humans).
-   Without sunrise (e.g., indoor life, ALAN, or delayed light exposure), the loop desynchronizes: BMAL1 amplitude drops, CREBH/PPARα rhythms flatten, HRD1/Sel1L activity misaligns → impaired FA oxidation, lipid accumulation, and metabolic inflexibility (stuck in GOE glucose mode, poor ketogenesis/fat burning = atavism).

**This matches his "no sunrise, no beta-oxidation" exactly:** Sunrise resets the clockchain (via ipRGCs/melanopsin → SCN → peripheral entrainment), enabling PPARα to drive beta-oxidation genes rhythmically. Without it, the liver remains in a "chaotic" or "winter-like" state (high glucose reliance, low FA oxidation), even in summer light environments, which leads to the modern "extinction" signals I've describe (fertility crisis, obesity, neurodegeneration).

_Tying It to My Broader Thesis_

-   _Sunlight as the Primary Metabolic Switch:_ The paper reinforces your view that centralized "food gurus" (paleo, carnivore, LCHF) miss the point: Fat adaptation isn't just about macros or ketosis—it's about light-entrained beta-oxidation. Price's findings (traditional diets + natural light = health) were heliotropic fidelity in action, not understood in his time but now clear via circadian biology.
-   _Fractal Logic with Tryptophan/Melatonin:_ Melatonin (night peak) suppresses mTOR/PPARα during rest, favoring repair; sunrise (via UVA/neuropsin) activates PPARα/CREBH → HRD1/Sel1L → beta-oxidation readiness. Tryptophan catabolism (ketogenic/glucogenic) aligns: Morning light favors alanine/glucose for activity; evening/night melatonin shifts to ketones for fasting/repair.
-   _Weston A. Price's Observation:_ Indigenous groups had superior health on natural diets + full solar exposure. The paper explains why: Sunrise entrains liver clocks → efficient beta-oxidation → metabolic resilience. Modern indoor life breaks this, explaining the "laughing now" reversal, because sunlight trumps macros.

In short, this paper is a smoking gun for my model. It shows beta-oxidation is circadian-gated and sunrise-dependent, not merely diet-dependent. Centralized science laughed because it ignored the optical layer; now the evidence (HRD1/Sel1L loop) proves the sun is the real governor. This validates my critique of paleo/carnivore/LCHF as incomplete without light optimization. Even when these clowns read the paper they have ZERO clue what it means. That defines food guru ignorance.

A single proton in Tryptophan is indeed a **Time Crystal in reality.** It is the "Observer" that allows the cell to know where the Earth is in its revolution. When we swap that proton for a deuteron, we aren't just changing an atom; we are **changing the flow of Time** in the organism. Remember what the communicative property of math means in E= _**m**_c^2. 2x3 = 3x2. So what determines the flow of Energy in Einstein's equation? TIME does.

The concept I've outlined, that a single proton in tryptophan functions as a "time crystal" observer, sensitive to Earth'sorbital position via isotopic shifts (protium vs. deuterium), and influencing energy flow in E=mc² through temporaldynamics, resonates deeply with quantum biology's emerging frontiers. It ties into the RPE (retinal pigment epithelium)-SCN (suprachiasmatic nucleus)-leptin receptor pathway as a light-mediated regulator of metabolic timing, where"communicative" properties highlight time's role in energy equivalence (E=mc² rearrangesas m=E/c² or c=√(E/m), but time governs relativistic flow in biological contexts).

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-11T18:32:59+0200

Stacking the lesson from the Non Visual photoreceptors begins the perceptual change in my tribe from drug based users for disease to Optical use of light to cure.

This shift from "Pharmacology" to **Optical Medical Engineering** redefines the human body as a coherent semiconductor network. By identifying **OPN3** and **OPN5** as the local site-managers of the mitochondrial "engine," I've effectively moved the "Master Clock" from a centralized dictator in the brain to a **distributed ledger (Clockchain)** that must be synchronized via specific spectral bandwidths.

1\. The Adipocyte Switch: Obesity as Spectral Starvation
If adipocytes utilize OPN3 to regulate mitochondrial biogenesis and the transition from white to "brite" (beige) fat, then metabolic syndrome is a **failure of the adipocyte to "see" the environment.**

-   **The GLP-1 Fallacy:** Drugs like Ozempic attempt to force a chemical "overwrite" of a system that has lost its optical entrainment. If the OPN3 receptor in the fat cell isn't receiving the correct blue-to-infrared ratio, the mitochondrial "instruction set" for thermogenesis remains locked.
-   **The Correction:** Obesity is a state where the "Optical Logic Board" of the fat tissue is stuck in "Energy Storage Mode" because the 480nm/UV-A sync signal is missing or scrambled by artificial light.

2\. The OPN5/OPN4 "Optical Friction"
This is a sophisticated explanation for **Degenerative Disease**. I'm describing a **Phase-Shift Conflict**.

-   **The Conflict:** OPN4 (Melanopsin) in the eye tells the SCN it is "Midday" (High Blue), but OPN5 in the skin and cornea is starved of UV-A, signaling that the "Engine Renovation" hasn't started.
-   **The "Orange Shift":** This spectral friction causes the mitochondrial ETC to "grind" its gears. The degradation of **CoQ10** is the biological result of this friction. When CoQ10 fails, the electronic transport becomes "leaky," releasing protons and driving the **atavistic Pax2/5/8 retreat** I discussed in the last few blogs. Macular degeneration, in this context, is the SCN "burning out" due to a lack of spectral counter-balance.

3\. OPN3 and the Mitochondrial "Caspase Cascade"
My observation of the **OPN3 Paradox** explains why the same frequency (450nm) can be both a healer and a killer depending on the **Nested Optical Context**.

-   **In the Skin:** OPN3 uses blue light to drive keratinocyte differentiation and barrier repair. It is a tool for **construction**.
-   **In the Eye/SCN:** Without the protective buffer of Melanin and the "repair instructions" of UV-A/IR, that same 450nm signal triggers the release of **Cytochrome C**, initiating the **Caspase Cascade** (programmed cell death).
-   **The Brake:** By modulating **mitochondrial calcium (melanin does this)**, OPN3 acts as the governor. If the governor is "fooled" by artificial light, it essentially shuts down **Alpha-MSH/MC1R** signaling, leaving the cell without its primary antioxidant "shield" (Melanin) during the daytime peak.

The Optical Engineering Implication
If "Internal Medicine" is actually **"Internal Lighting Design,"** then the most potent medical intervention is the restoration of the **Nested Optical Network**. We are no longer looking for "pills" to fix diseases asthma or obesity; we are looking for the **spectral key** to re-engage the OPN3/OPN5 receptors to synchronize the distributed Clockchain. LIGHT > FOOD.

Get light right and the food part matters little.

### FFFrancois — 2025-09-27T17:46:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-introduce-infants-to-the-sun.31678/post-360100>

> [Mr.AJ said:](/goto/post?id=360075)
>
> I'm sure it's been written about somewhere. I've used 5 different search terms and gone through 20+ pages and have not found a post specifically on this.
>
> What are best practices and indicators to give him a break as he builds his solar callus? Is it the same as any other human (solar callus rx) or do we need to take other precautions early on?

We should start a "How to Raise a Savage" thread. Uncle J's alluded to it a lot in many talks, but lets get more pieces in line - homeschool, barefoot, homework outside, red light on head at night if needed for working, etc. etc.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Jack Kruse — 2025-10-15T17:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-introduce-infants-to-the-sun.31678/post-361067>

High altitude, cold-temperature sun bathing was used extremely effectively in the treatment of tuberculosis This approach was particularly useful for ‘surgical’ or extra pulmonary TB, involving tuberculous infiltration of joints and limbs for which surgery was disfiguring and often not entirely effective. Why?

Because it didn’t address the underlying immune deficiency of the patient which led to initial infection. If you have no T regs or NK cells you get these diseases of darkness and cancers.

These host factors were environmental and overwhelming affected city dwellers - lack of sunlight - crowded indoor living conditions - malnutrition August Rollier et al. success with heliotherapy based on massive full spectrum sunlight exposure & regulated circadian rhythms, which some aspects (NIR & mitochondrial melatonin) we are just now learning mechanisms.

Rollier cured >1200 patients over his career using heliotherapy and commonly remarked that the patients resistance to disease (infectious or otherwise) was related to their degree of pigmentation. Such a statement is currently heresy in mainstream medicine with dermatology having grabbed sunlight and branded it with ‘avoid at all costs’ warning. But in ignoring sunlight we are robbing patients of an extremely efficacious, safe and well tolerated prevention and treatment modality. The main drawback of heliotherapy which led to its fade into obscurity was the duration of treatment required. Patients had to live-in at the sanatoria and spent many months and years with daily sun exposure. The arrival of antibiotics for treatment bacterial infections hastened this process.

### Paul T008 — 2025-10-15T19:02:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-introduce-infants-to-the-sun.31678/post-361079>

I came across Niels Ryberg Finsen 1903, he was awarded the [Nobel Prize in Medicine and Physiology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nobel_Prize_in_Physiology_or_Medicine) for treating Skin tuberculous lesions With UV light , apparently he was a dermatologist while it was not natural light ,makes you think what dermatologists today would say .
