### DLO — 2025-12-23T05:37:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-those-that-underestimate-ai.31954/post-364160>

#### [**Robert Kiyosaki**](https://www.facebook.com/RobertKiyosaki?__cft__[0]=AZYA1CXFTDf9Y7cUN62G_IG6ZKhAIVo0cgNAL4e4aT7rbVYKWeX-EMDkvC-oWn5yZni-W02rxmLsDKr34bNoMIJzJTTgOXZac1o6VVnWL8ESZ2YJo9Gc-NL33BjFo8CjtAgNYY3fW9HT0dOV3pvB7EfUkAB2JewCBDT0XNmwqUrBAD5C9eodlc4GKXaQuMldGnI&__tn__=-UC%2CP-R)​

[srSpedontoc6fa16hmhm2t1amg1mcc7l586m00u3tu3923i7fllm046hh770](https://www.facebook.com/?__cft__[0]=AZYA1CXFTDf9Y7cUN62G_IG6ZKhAIVo0cgNAL4e4aT7rbVYKWeX-EMDkvC-oWn5yZni-W02rxmLsDKr34bNoMIJzJTTgOXZac1o6VVnWL8ESZ2YJo9Gc-NL33BjFo8CjtAgNYY3fW9HT0dOV3pvB7EfUkAB2JewCBDT0XNmwqUrBAD5C9eodlc4GKXaQuMldGnI&__tn__=%2CO%2CP-R#?bac) ·

People Think AI Is Making Everything Cheaper.
It Isn’t. It’s Just Moving the Money.
Imagine firing your copywriter.
Good writer.
Talented.
Costs you $100,000 a year.
Now an AI tool does 80–90% of that work for $30 a month.
Sounds like progress, right?
But here’s what actually happened.
Your electricity bill went up.
Your cloud and software bills went up.
Your “AI tools” stack costs more than your old team.
You didn’t save money.
The money just moved.
And if you think this story is about copywriters, you’re missing the real lesson.
AI is driving the cost of labor toward zero in many fields:
• Writing
• Translation
• Coding
• Customer support
• Design
• Research
Economists call this deflation at the margin.
The cost of producing one more unit of work collapses.
Historically, that should make things cheaper for everyone.
But that’s not what’s happening.
Why?
Because while AI is deflating what you sell, it’s inflating what you depend on.
AI doesn’t run on air.
It runs on:
• Data centers
• Chips
• Electricity
• Cooling
• Bandwidth
• Cloud infrastructure
Large AI models consume enormous amounts of energy and compute — not once, but continuously.
Training is expensive.
Inference is ongoing.
Scaling never stops.
Who pays for that?
Not the founders.
You do.
Through:
• Higher utility rates
• Rising cloud bills
• More subscriptions
• Higher cost of digital infrastructure
So here’s the real dynamic:
AI deflates labor income,
but concentrates capital income.
Your skills become cheaper.
The infrastructure becomes more valuable.
That’s not an accident.
That’s how every technological revolution works.
The printing press made books cheap.
But then:
• Everyone had to be literate
• Then everyone needed schooling
• Then a degree
• Then a master’s degree
The cost of staying relevant kept rising.
Same pattern. New technology.
AI increases productivity.
But instead of lowering prices, most of the gains flow upward:
• Higher margins
• Greater market concentration
• More power for infrastructure owners
That’s why the winners in AI aren’t freelancers.
They’re:
• Chip makers
• Cloud providers
• Data center owners
• Energy producers
• Platform monopolies
They own the picks and shovels.
Everyone else is running faster just to stay in place.
Here’s the part most people don’t understand:
This isn’t a worker problem.
It’s a positioning problem.
If your income depends on selling hours, tasks, or interchangeable skills — AI is competition.
If your income depends on owning assets, systems, or leverage — AI is a multiplier.
I’m not anti-AI.
I use it.
But I understand the game.
Every major technological “revolution” is also a wealth transfer.
This one just has better marketing.
So the real question isn’t:
“How do I use AI?”
It’s:
“Where am I positioned in the value chain?”
Because in every gold rush, most people dig.
And a few sell shovels.
That’s where the real money has always been.

### DLO — 2025-12-24T05:07:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-those-that-underestimate-ai.31954/post-364186>

#### [**Robert Kiyosaki**](https://www.facebook.com/RobertKiyosaki?__cft__[0]=AZb_o3b-tWynu9A8Bh-7wUw1G-dOkc07v-3MUwvJBFMBDHsQtVnelaBEjFcJJdBh4QjZWTba7iB0nUeM_uERFBR5V4j6LM_k4EfGqz7hVf7oTqdwoZcxkZvl5GaAu8GsH2Flqipo6ZBVShVfCSepSpHfonh96laU21B4IC73_CnX7VTzFh1lzEsh0qsildpVvi4&__tn__=-UC%2CP-R)​

[edptosrSno1t7c6ac651mau0145fa3839hhhu59f1418hg15tm0alm40021c](https://www.facebook.com/?__cft__[0]=AZb_o3b-tWynu9A8Bh-7wUw1G-dOkc07v-3MUwvJBFMBDHsQtVnelaBEjFcJJdBh4QjZWTba7iB0nUeM_uERFBR5V4j6LM_k4EfGqz7hVf7oTqdwoZcxkZvl5GaAu8GsH2Flqipo6ZBVShVfCSepSpHfonh96laU21B4IC73_CnX7VTzFh1lzEsh0qsildpVvi4&__tn__=%2CO%2CP-R#?aeg) ·

The rich use AI the same way they use debt.
The poor use AI the same way they use Google.
Most people don’t understand what that means yet.
But they will.
Poor people use AI to do their work.
\- To write emails.
\- To summarize documents.
\- To answer questions faster.
That’s helpful.
But it doesn’t change their income.
It just makes them a more efficient employee.
Rich people use AI the way they use debt — as leverage.
They don’t ask,
“How can this help me work harder?”
They ask,
“How can this replace labor?”
“How can this scale without more people?”
“How can this run 24/7 without me?”
That’s the same mindset they use with money.
Poor people use debt to buy liabilities.
Cars. Phones. Lifestyle.
Rich people use debt to buy assets.
Businesses. Systems. Cash flow.
AI is no different.
Use AI to search, and you stay dependent.
Use AI to build systems, and you create income.
Schools teach people to avoid debt.
\- They teach people to “use AI responsibly.”
\- They don’t teach leverage.
\- They don’t teach ownership.
The rich aren’t trying to be better workers with AI.
They’re trying to own the machine.
And in the AI age, whoever owns the system controls the money

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t-in-boys-lower-iq-queer-behavior-and-shortened-longevity.31960/post-363378>

**LOW T in boys = Lower IQ, Queer behavior, and shortened longevity.**

Tonight's physics/biology factoid: The average indiginous man in Boliva is 5'3. The shortest humans tend to live longer. Shortness is linked to sex steroid hormone production and anti-Kasha rules. Testosterone is linked with IRA and NIR light exposure to make DDW from the heme protein CCO in the matrix. This is key in humans and explains why when men age heteroplasmy rises and more red light from the sun is needed to maintain their T.

This country is at the 10-21 degrees latitude South and is well within the tropics. The elevation of this country, where 90% of its population density is ridiculously high. It ranges 9000 feet to 19,500 ft were 90% of people live. This elevation allows for massive assimilation of UV light. The main features of Bolivia's geography include the Altiplano, a highland plateau of the Andes, and Lake Titicaca, the highest navigable lake on Earth, which is shared with Peru.

The most prominent feature of the Altiplano is the large lake at its northern end, Lake Titicaca. At 3,811 m (12,503 ft) above sea level, it is the highest commercially navigable body of water in the world. Some of the oldest healthy people on Earth come from Tribes in this region. The eastern lowlands include all of Bolivia north and east of the Andes. Although comprising over two-thirds of the national territory, the region is sparsely populated.

The tallest people on Earth live in Netherlands: The Netherlands is the tallest country in the world, where the average height of men is an incredible six feet. The latitude is 51-53.5 degrees North. It is outside the tropics and does not have a lot of UV power.

The Netherlands is geographically a very low and flat country, with about 26% of its area and 21% of its population located below sea level, and only about 50% of its land exceeding one metre above sea level. The quantum yield of this country is quite low because of its latitude and altitude. The Dutch population had a boom after the Second World War, but now has a low birth rate that has caused extremely low population growth: 2025 saw the lowest absolute population growth since 1900.

# Thread: How bad are TV towers?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-11-30T16:33:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-363392>

I recently moved to Florida and pretty much every EMF measurement I've made is excellent in / around my home.

E fields close to 0, M field 0.03-0.15 mG range (when all power is off).
RF from Safe and Sound Pro II peaks are in the 0.1-2 uW/m2 range, lots of mature tree cover on the property.
However, when I use my Esmog Spion 5G meter, with the HF antenna in the 200-600 MHz, I see measurements of 45-180 uW/m2.

Some notes:
\- Looks like SS Pro II can go down to 200 MHz, but I've read that it's most accurate 400 MHz and above.
\- Esmog Spion generally reads much higher (like 2-10x higher) than Safe and Sound EM3 or Safe and Sound Pro II. Maybe it measures peaks better?
\- Doing an antenna search around, I'm nowhere near any cell tower, whether 5G or not. I can empirically validate this by my terrible cell signal here.

The one thing I do find is a TV tower broadcasting at 158 MHz only 2-3 miles away from my home.
How bad are TV tower frequencies for mitochondrial health? Everything else measures perfectly here.

It's probably impossible to avoid TV towers as they have much lower frequencies than cell towers and they surely cover way more of the US than cell towers.

### AlbertW — 2026-01-22T17:33:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-365733>

Curious if anybody else has an Esmog Spion 5G meter and has measured if they see way higher readings on Esmog than SSPro 2 (likely due to TV)

### AlbertW — 2026-01-26T01:50:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-365870>

I bought a small spectrum analyzer (tinySA Ultra+) to figure out what RF bands are actually present around my home.

**What I did:** I used the tinySA’s Channel power measurement (integrates power over a chosen bandwidth). There were a couple of narrow peaks in the <200 MHz region (consistent with FM and a VHF TV channel), and there are also one or more **wide 6-MHz-type blocks** higher up in frequency (consistent with UHF TV channels centered near ~500 MHz). In the “hot spots” in my house, the channel-power integral in the UHF-TV-ish region was roughly an order of magnitude+ higher than most other bands as measured at the analyzer input. (I understand this is received power into the analyzer, not directly “power density in space.”)

**Why I’m confused:** My Safe & Sound Pro II generally reads low (single-digit µW/m²), while the 5G Esmog Spion meter can read much higher. The Safe & Sound Pro II’s published specs say it has an **e**ffective measurement range of 200 MHz–8 GHz and a true response range of 400 MHz–7.2 GHz (±6 dB). The 500 MHz range is well within the true response range...

I know that mapping received power on the tinySA Ultra+ to real power density isn't trivial due to antenna coupling / response changes with frequency. But the tinySA's received power in the 500 MHz UHF TV range is 95%+ of the total received power... Can the antenna coupling / response changes with frequency be varying that much? Just don't understand how SS Pro II can miss out on this.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-05T02:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366372>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=365870)
>
> Sound Pro II’s published specs say it has an **e**ffective measurement range of 200 MHz–8 GHz and a true response range of 400 MHz–7.2 GHz (±6 dB). The 500 MHz range is well within the true response range...

Part of the answer here - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-–-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-356524](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-356524)

### AlbertW — 2026-02-05T03:25:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366376>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=366372)
>
> Part of the answer here - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-–-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-356524](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-356524)

Yes I looked at exactly this. For 500 MHz range it’s -4 to -5 dB ish, which is at most a 3x understatement. I don’t get how I can then see SSPro measuring 0-3 uW/m2 range but esmog claims 180. Maybe esmog’s response curve is even worse? I checked with an HF59B and consistently got 3-5 uW/m2.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-05T04:56:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366379>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=366376)
>
> Yes I looked at exactly this. For 500 MHz range it’s -4 to -5 dB ish, which is at most a 3x understatement. I don’t get how I can then see SSPro measuring 0-3 uW/m2 range but esmog claims 180. Maybe esmog’s response curve is even worse? I checked with an HF59B and consistently got 3-5 uW/m2.

I will explain why when I have a moment free.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-08T10:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366536>

At ~ 500MHz WL is ~ 60cm.. so antenna coupling would dominate the measurement error. S &S would.use a very small internal broadband antenna..Thus coupling would not be great and impacted by the polarization.. the - 4 to - 5db good plane wave coupling and does not factor in the "pre-detection" losses.. The gigahertz internals are different they use a better scaled antenna with a superheterodyne receiver with rms/log style detection etc..The outlier would be Esmog.. Tinysa from what I looked at would measure received power at the resonant ext antenna (not spatial power density).

Personally, I would rely upon the Gigahertz Solutions meter. Assuming your model measures the frequency.. Can you link the Esmog meter have you got specs on this?

### Mat.I — 2026-02-08T10:36:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366537>

Also, what external antenna did you use on the GS meter? I thought the stock ones cut off ~ 700 but the internals support down to 27.. From memory the lower frequency ext antenna was UBB27 model..

### Mat.I — 2026-02-08T10:45:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366538>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=366537)
>
> Also, what external antenna did you use on the GS meter? I thought the stock ones cut off ~ 700 but the internals support down to 27.. From memory the lower frequency ext antenna was UBB27 model..

Effective element lengths are typically wavlength /4 or 3 for accuracy.. You will still pick larger up, but just less accurate. 700 = 43cm.. so the standard antenna would likely be based on an accuracy element of /4 or 3 of WL.

### AlbertW — 2026-02-08T16:02:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366540>

[@Mat.I](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26512/) Tracks for the info on the antenna details. Very helpful. I will investigate more.

Can you elaborate on what you mean by pre detection losses in SS?

And yes Tinysa indeed just measures received antenna power. I wanted to determine the source of my high esmog readings.

I am indeed using UBB27 omni antenna with HF59B which goes down to 27 MHz.

This is the esmog meter: [https://priggen.com/5g-esmog-spion-electrosmog-meter-50hz-8ghz](https://priggen.com/5g-esmog-spion-electrosmog-meter-50hz-8ghz)

What’s interesting to me is that with the SS, I see significant attenuation inside a canopy at my house, from 1-2 uW/m2 down to 0.01 or often 0.000. The GS with UBB27 shows barely any attenuation in measurement, so I’m guessing very low frequency waves are really the main problem at my place…radio, TV, etc. Always in the 4-5 uW/m2 everywhere in the house.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-10T02:23:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366619>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=366540)
>
> Can you elaborate on what you mean by pre detection losses in SS?

Explained above - SS uses a very small internal broadband antenna which is very small electrically relative to the wavelength you are measuring (this results in pre-detection losses).. Why the mismatch in test conditions accuracy vs what you have measured was explained in the thread I linked re: the test tone generator used.. is a clean CW/narrow band test signal indicative of real world test conditions experienced out in the field? Case of garbage in = garbage out.

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=366540)
>
> I am indeed using UBB27 omni antenna with HF59B which goes down to 27 MHz.

That is good.

I cringe when people rely on S&S as their only and primary meter (in the professional space). Good broadband activity indicator (like a sniffer I guess) so it has it's place as a tool but should not be relied upon for quantitative power density measurements in isolation IMO. Some won't like this at all (that means they do not appreciate the basic physics behind it).

### [Home | Armstrong Economics](https://www.armstrongeconomics.com/)

 www.armstrongeconomics.com

-   Armstrong built an “Economic Confidence Model” that tracks repeating business and confidence cycles: a core 8.6‑year cycle (3,141 days, tying to π×1000π×1000) nested in longer 51.6‑year and ~309‑year waves.
-   He feeds a massive historical database (including centuries of economic and even coinage data) into a proprietary computer system he calls “Socrates,” which spits out timing windows for turning points in markets, politics, and geopolitics.
-   This approach pushes him toward “when” and “directional change” (turning points up/down, calm/crisis) rather than precise earnings, valuations, or fundamentals in the usual Wall Street sense.

Now if you want to pin down an actual date for the next pandemic-like crisis, you take “when Covid hit” as March 11, 2020 (the WHO’s official pandemic declaration), then 8.6 years later lands in late 2028, so we are a bit over 6 years in and roughly 2.5 years away from that 8.6‑year point.

## 8.6 years from the start of​

Armstrong’s basic cycle is 8.6 years, which is 3,141 days. You can approximate 8.6 years in calendar time as 8 years + about 7.2 months.
Using simple calendar approximations:

-   From March 11, 2020
    -   +8 years → March 11, 2028
    -   +0.6 of a year (~7.2 months) → around late October 2028
    -   So 8.6 years from the WHO pandemic declaration is roughly late October 2028.

Their presence went down noticeably here in North Georgia while the government was shut down, but they've come back spraying more than ever.

There were tons in Las Vegas when I was out there for a couple days last month.

Can anyone in FL verify that there are fewer trails now that they passed the law against geoengineering planes taking off from Florida airports?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-12-06T21:45:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anywhere-with-no-trails-in-the-sky.31967/post-363643>

Like I said CHEMTRAILS (weather modifications) are real - BUT . . .
Are you on a flight path? There are 10’s of thousands of commercial flights every day.
Plane goes over. Altitude + weather conditions allow the trail to linger. The trail drifts, the next plane flys over etc. All of a sudden the sky is covered.
Do you see more in the winter than the summer or vica-versa? Morning vs afternoon? Is it a high or low pressure day where you live?

My wife never payed much attention to the sky/clouds. All of a sudden she stated looking up (with all the talk of chemtrails etc) and was amazed at all the different cloud formations at different times. That is so unusual she would say about a VERY normal formation - that she had never noticed before. We live under the main north/south flight path on the west coast. Some days lots of flights bit no contrails, other days (different weather) lines all over the sky. I was a professional photographer, a natural sky is important in photos, I pay attention. Lingering contrails happen . . . and so do chemtrails.

If you are no way near a flight path and you see “trails” criss crossing the sky, good chance they are spraying.

### Dana10 — 2025-12-07T03:29:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anywhere-with-no-trails-in-the-sky.31967/post-363653>

>
> There were tons in Las Vegas when I was out there for a couple days last month.
>
> Can anyone in FL verify that there are fewer trails now that they passed the law against geoengineering planes taking off from Florida airports?

We have had the exact same thing happen here in Jacksonville (northeast Florida)—-less trails during the government shutdown, but now it is out of control. They are spraying us like cockroaches.

### Evee — 2025-12-07T19:31:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anywhere-with-no-trails-in-the-sky.31967/post-363668>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=363643)

> Plane goes over. Altitude + weather conditions allow the trail to linger. The trail drifts, the next plane flys over etc. All of a sudden the sky is covered.

All of a sudden she stated looking up (with all the talk of chemtrails etc) and was amazed at all the different cloud formations at different times. That is so unusual she would say about a VERY normal formation - that she had never noticed before. We live under the main north/south flight path on the west coast. Some days lots of flights bit no contrails, other days (different weather) lines all over the sky. I was a professional photographer, a natural sky is important in photos, I pay attention. Lingering contrails happen . . . and so do chemtrails.
>
> If you are no way near a flight path and you see “trails” criss crossing the sky, good chance they are spraying.

I’m at various places in the southwest some heavier flight path than others, but when you’re in Joshua Tree with like zero humidity and some days the sky is clear blue and some days it’s criss crossed to oblivion until you have what looks like humidity (but isn’t) to the point that it affects the perceived sunlight, as if there’s cloud cover (but there isn’t) you know they’re spraying. I’ve been watching the skies since 2023 and went to PDC MX and at that time at least, there were few lingering trails ever, like 1 or 2 in the near 2 months I was there. (Not so with Merida) So yes I can tell the difference between regular plane exhaust and geo engineering. It was hella obvious coming back to the US we’re getting dumped on. Not to mention regular plane exhaust doesn’t have the silvery metallic that lingers for hours and changes the sunlight or cause sinus irritation

### Evee — 2025-12-07T19:42:51+0200

Delighted to now be part of Dr. Kruse's Klub. I am intrigued to learn more about the power of natural light particularly healing powers. I reflect back on many years of winter vacations to warm sunny locations and how much better I always. Often spent 6-10 hours outside daily, while away. My simple thinking was the longer daylight hours counteracted SAD (seasonal affective Disorder). Now, I am learning that even the walking barefoot on the beach at the edge of the water and grounding was also contributing to my sense of well being. I also thought that the food being fresher, and more varied than what I ate at home was important, but now, I wonder if it was actually more the natural light, than the food that was the most impactful on my health. Looking forward to this next chapter of learning and working towards improving health for my family. thank you Dr. Kruse.

# Thread: non EMF emitting garage door opener

### Mr.AJ — 2025-12-02T16:02:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-emf-emitting-garage-door-opener.31971/post-363515>

Looking for a new garage door opener that has zero wifi/bluetooth or any "Smart" Tech. Just a normal one from 20 years ago that you can open and close with a remote but does not emit anything else.

Has anyone found one like this? Everything I've been seeing online and in stores now is "smart"

### knees4bukele — 2025-12-02T18:03:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-emf-emitting-garage-door-opener.31971/post-363521>

I'd look for a used/unused older garage door opener that you can plug in and test with your RF meter that it doesn't radiate.
Some "smart" garage door openers have a seperate wifi/bluetooth section that can be unplugged.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-12-02T21:51:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-emf-emitting-garage-door-opener.31971/post-363528>

In the low nnEMF world - “smart” = opening and closing the door by hand.

I have an older one ~ 315MHz only emits when you press the button on the remote for a period of milliseconds at very low pd. The newer ones are garbage as they are either constant or have beacon pulses even when not in use.

Did you notice a brand? I might try just matching the other one I have in my garage but it's been quite hard finding no RF ones in major stores/amazon.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-12-03T21:03:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-emf-emitting-garage-door-opener.31971/post-363565>

I believe ALL garage door openers will be some kind of RF . . . Other than your hand
Just like the key fob, you push the button, it send out a few milli-second pulse of RF and the door operates.

A few mins on a cell phone is probably 1 million x more RF than your garage door opener.
The time you have spent on your computer working on this is more RF exposure than a lifetime of garage door opening.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-12-03T21:13:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-emf-emitting-garage-door-opener.31971/post-363566>

>
> A few mins on a cell phone is probably 1 million x more RF than your garage door opener.
> The time you have spent on your computer working on this is more RF exposure than a lifetime of garage door opening.

I understand. I would just prefer one where when I press the button to use the garage door opener that's the only time it is pulsing / using any RF. My current one is blasting RF 24/7 from the opener itself. (Bluetooth/wifi enabled and can not be shut off or ripped out). Seems way worse than using an old fashioned one where when I press the button there's RF and otherwise it's silent.

And like the measurement next to the opener was quite high. >30k uW/m2 iirc.

And ya I'm too lazy to just open / close it by hand. The way my house is built the logistics would be retarded.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-12-03T21:47:21+0200

Seems way worse than using an old fashioned one where when I press the button there's RF and otherwise it's silent.
>

>
> And ya I'm too lazy to just open / close it by hand. The way my house is built the logistics would be retarded.

Before you find an alternative - either take the battery out OR put it in a Faraday bag. (A small tin can would work)

Many basic ones on this site in Australia - [https://garagedooropenerremotes.com...BwE&gbraid=0AAAAADlV0fUyOduHLqepAWCuV3CKDXBly](https://garagedooropenerremotes.com.au/products/garage-door-openers/panel-tilt-motors?_gl=1*8jv21e*_up*MQ..*_gs*MQ..&gclid=EAIaIQobChMI8sqQ6pelkQMVWTCDAx3hNxAHEAAYAiAAEgK4SfD_BwE&gbraid=0AAAAADlV0fUyOduHLqepAWCuV3CKDXBly)

### Mat.I — 2025-12-05T02:31:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-emf-emitting-garage-door-opener.31971/post-363598>

# Thread: My 12 year old son
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-12-year-old-son.31973/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### josephine — 2025-12-03T07:56:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-12-year-old-son.31973/post-363552>

Hello, I'm not sure where I should be posting this or if I should booking a session with Dr Jack? Please advice if so.

I have a 12 yr old child (almost 13.) When he was eight, his Grade 3 teacher told me he wouldn’t be able to cope with Grade 4 because he couldn’t keep up. I had him fully assessed. The specialist said he was normal but had severely slow processing speed.

I researched everything I could (before I looked into Dr Kruse work) and ended up buying a hyperbaric oxygen chamber. I gave him around 110 sessions at 1.4 ATA with roughly 30% oxygen. The change was remarkable — it was like a light switched on. Everyone saw how much faster he became. When he was reassessed, his processing speed had improved by 39%. It was genuinely life-changing. He went from getting C’s and D’s to A’s and B’s, his piano and clarinet improvement was remarkable, he flourished.

This year, at age 12, his teacher mentioned he was a bit slower than the others. Over the past month, however, he has become noticeably slower, and he has pointed this out himself. In class, for example, there might be 30 questions — every student will finish, and he’ll still be on number 12.

He’s about to start high school, and I’m extremely worried. I have 7 weeks to help him (after that much less time). I’m also unsure what to do about HBOT. Jack often says HBOT isn’t ideal for most people, so I’m scared to repeat a treatment that worked brilliantly last time as I don't understand how to control for the dangers.

My son also had a palate expander this year (before i knew more) and has been mouth-taping at night. The expander opened his airway, stopped the constant stuffy nose, and even ended the bed-wetting — so part of me wonders if simply getting more oxygen every day has already changed things and might be affecting how he responds?

I keep thinking: should I repeat HBOT but only after getting everything else in place? Would this be okay to do? I'm not sure of what protocol. We live on a sub tropical island outside brisbane, so apart from being in the prison of Oz, it is great for the essential of sunlight. These are the things I'm thinking with question marks where i dont know.

• sunrise
• get a full tan
• I live on the beach, so lots of grounding
• he has a daylight computer
• I have a full body redlight panel?
• I have a redlight cranial helmet with Vlight (bought for Parkinson’s)?
• would a keto diet help?
• cold plunging for a 12 yr old?
• I have hydrogen generator – inhalation?
• hydrogen baths (he loves them)?
• sauna (I have a Clearlight)?
• Hbot at highly pressures, or higher oxygen, no oxygen, lower pressure?

We already do zero blue light past sundown.

Did a little research and...once again, Jack is right. Looks like epigenetic in humans influences evolution a lot more than we are taught. Mitochondrial reaction to environment seems to determine adaptation and evolution. Evidence:

-   The descendants of the **1980s Ethiopian famine** show metabolic gene expression changes **in one generation**.
-   Children of Holocaust survivors show altered **stress response genes**.
-   High-altitude Tibetans evolved hypoxia tolerance in **3,000 years**, not 50,000.
-   Coastal populations who ate marine DHA evolved **larger brains** and different skull shapes faster than inland populations.
-   Light-skinned Europeans emerged in only **6–8,000 years**, not 40,000 as previously thought.
-   Indian subcontinent peoples vary from nearly Nordic to deep black, **within the same ethnicity**, depending on latitude.

If you knew evolution history you'd realize how bad Big Harma is raping humanity with nonsense science.

Evolution of Endogenous Melanin: A Photonic-Adaptive Intro to Evolution

From atomic fundamentals, melanin's evolution reflects life's adaptation to photonic and oxidative pressures, transitioning from surface protector to internal sculptor in complex eukaryotes.

Pre-GOE (~>3.5 Ga): Absent ozone, Earth's surface bathed in intense UV (290-400 nm) bombarded early cells. Ancient melanins evolved before life did, creating polymeric semiconductors from dirty chemisty on the surface of Earth from phenolic precursors filled with aromatic amino acids. The first two domains of life come on board around 3.4-4.0 bya and start incorparating allomelanins and through a quantum evolution newer versions with new abilities emerged in prokaryotes/eukaryotes (fungi) as UV shields over a billion years, absorbing photons to prevent DNA damage and radical formation. Fossil melanin traces (1.6 Ga) suggest early roles in energy dissipation, predating complex life which occured via radiosynthesis. It appears radiosynthesis predates any photosynthesis on Earth at its origins.

GOE (~2.4 Ga): Cyanobacterial O2 surge created "dirty" chemistry where ROS spikes and bioavailable transition metals (Fe, Cu, Mn, Mo, Ca and deuterium) fueling Fenton reactions. Melanin evolved enhanced chelation of these metals, binding them to buffer ROS while enabling detox (via superoxide scavenging). Melanin evolved to use photons to release these metals in a controlled fashion to control early biochemical reactions that occur from 3.5 -2.0 billion years ago. This evolution does not stop until organisms begin to optimized aerobic metabolism in the GOE, allowing eukaryotic complexity amid oxidative bottlenecks; melanin acted as an "electronic sink", and as such, allowed life to bridge Earth's anaerobic origins to aerobic an world in the GOE.

Post-KT (~66 Ma): Asteroid impact triggered "impact winter" which led to a global dust/aerosols blocked sunlight for months-years, collapsing photosynthesis, exterminating 75% species (including non-avian dinosaurs). Sudden sunlight resurgence (1-10 ka post-impact) flooded survivors with UV/visible flux, favoring rapid radiations in mammals/birds/angiosperms. Complex eukaryotes leveraged neuropsin (OPN5, UV-A sensor at 380 nm, evolved ~500 Ma in vertebrates but specialized post-160 Ma) to entrain clocks and sense photonic recovery.

This drove neural crest innovation: Surface melanin-producing melanocytes migrated inward (via SOX10/WNT/EDN3 signaling) to sculpt tissues, via internal pigmentation for radiosynthesis, metal chelation, splitting of water, thermoregulation, camouflage, and optical electronic signaling in rebuilding ecosystems. Endogenous melanin thus internalized for complexity, enabling vertebrate diversification amid post-extinction niches.

Melanin's broadband absorption evolved for photonic purity, minimizing the dirty chemistry of Nitrogen/Sulfur/Oxygen interference in UPE signaling, turning cells into optical electronic sensors post-GOE/KT fluxes.

> Evolution of Endogenous Melanin: A Photonic-Adaptive Intro to Evolution
>
> From atomic fundamentals, melanin's evolution reflects life's adaptation to photonic and oxidative pressures, transitioning from surface protector to internal sculptor in complex eukaryotes.
>
> Pre-GOE (~>3.5 Ga): Absent ozone, Earth's surface bathed in intense UV (290-400 nm) bombarded early cells. Ancient melanins evolved before life did, creating polymeric semiconductors from dirty chemisty on the surface of Earth from phenolic precursors filled with aromatic amino acids. The first two domains of life come on board around 3.4-4.0 bya and start incorparating allomelanins and through a quantum evolution newer versions with new abilities emerged in prokaryotes/eukaryotes (fungi) as UV shields over a billion years, absorbing photons to prevent DNA damage and radical formation. Fossil melanin traces (1.6 Ga) suggest early roles in energy dissipation, predating complex life which occured via radiosynthesis. It appears radiosynthesis predates any photosynthesis on Earth at its origins.
>
> GOE (~2.4 Ga): Cyanobacterial O2 surge created "dirty" chemistry where ROS spikes and bioavailable transition metals (Fe, Cu, Mn, Mo, Ca and deuterium) fueling Fenton reactions. Melanin evolved enhanced chelation of these metals, binding them to buffer ROS while enabling detox (via superoxide scavenging). Melanin evolved to use photons to release these metals in a controlled fashion to control early biochemical reactions that occur from 3.5 -2.0 billion years ago. This evolution does not stop until organisms begin to optimized aerobic metabolism in the GOE, allowing eukaryotic complexity amid oxidative bottlenecks; melanin acted as an "electronic sink", and as such, allowed life to bridge Earth's anaerobic origins to aerobic an world in the GOE.
>
> Post-KT (~66 Ma): Asteroid impact triggered "impact winter" which led to a global dust/aerosols blocked sunlight for months-years, collapsing photosynthesis, exterminating 75% species (including non-avian dinosaurs). Sudden sunlight resurgence (1-10 ka post-impact) flooded survivors with UV/visible flux, favoring rapid radiations in mammals/birds/angiosperms. Complex eukaryotes leveraged neuropsin (OPN5, UV-A sensor at 380 nm, evolved ~500 Ma in vertebrates but specialized post-160 Ma) to entrain clocks and sense photonic recovery.
>
> This drove neural crest innovation: Surface melanin-producing melanocytes migrated inward (via SOX10/WNT/EDN3 signaling) to sculpt tissues, via internal pigmentation for radiosynthesis, metal chelation, splitting of water, thermoregulation, camouflage, and optical electronic signaling in rebuilding ecosystems. Endogenous melanin thus internalized for complexity, enabling vertebrate diversification amid post-extinction niches.
>
> Melanin's broadband absorption evolved for photonic purity, minimizing the dirty chemistry of Nitrogen/Sulfur/Oxygen interference in UPE signaling, turning cells into optical electronic sensors post-GOE/KT fluxes.

Is it just those 5 metals that melanin chelates? What about mercury, arsenic, lead that people are often inadvertently consuming?

# Thread: Help needed re: mitigating buried electrical cable issues in a coastal area with a high water table
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-needed-re-mitigating-buried-electrical-cable-issues-in-a-coastal-area-with-a-high-water-table.31984/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Wilma Without a W — 2025-12-07T00:02:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-needed-re-mitigating-buried-electrical-cable-issues-in-a-coastal-area-with-a-high-water-table.31984/post-363647>

Hi, peeps.

Got a bit of a dilemma in a house I'm rehabbing in the costa del Sol area of El Salvador, which is located in a condo/development which is right on the beach, which means that the water table is fairly high, especially during the rainy season.

The electrician had what I thought was a great idea in that he moved the electric meter from the wall of the house to a location on a perimeter wall which is right on the driveway, far from the house.

Unfortunately, that meant that he had to bury the cables going from the meter to the house along said perimeter wall. The cables are encased in orange PVC piping which is rated for ground contact and they're buried maybe a foot and a half down.

There are at least three ground rods which follow the same route maybe 2 m apart starting from the wall of the house towards where the meter is.

I okayed this thinking that it was preferable to having a rat's nest of cables right above my head.

But now I'm doing research specific to this area and what I'm reading is to bury the cable about 2 m down, which is going to definitely hit the water table here.

And in my limited understanding hitting the water table isn't so much an issue for the cables themselves because that orange PVC pipe is rated for ground contact, but that the water or moisture in the ground can actually amplify the range that the nnEMF travel.

The electrician is coming tomorrow and so I have to tell him what I want done because I don't think the way it is currently is going to be okay from an nnemf perspective.

Does anyone have any educated thoughts on the issue?

I suppose once he connects the cables to the meter and the other end into the breaker box I'll be able to take some readings and have a better idea of what's going on.

The unfortunate bit is that once he connects the cables it'll be a lot more work to disconnect everything to bury it lower.

Thanks in advance for any thoughts on the matter.

### Mat.I — 2025-12-08T07:40:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-needed-re-mitigating-buried-electrical-cable-issues-in-a-coastal-area-with-a-high-water-table.31984/post-363683>

> [Wilma Without a W said:](/goto/post?id=363647)
>
> Does anyone have any educated thoughts on the issue?

Not enough technical info here to add meaningful feedback.. What type of underground cable.is proposed? Service current rating? Your original readings? Single or 3 phase? What is the purpose of the 3 x ground rods ?

### Mat.I — 2025-12-08T07:45:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-needed-re-mitigating-buried-electrical-cable-issues-in-a-coastal-area-with-a-high-water-table.31984/post-363684>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=363683)
>
> Not enough technical info here to add meaningful feedback.. What type of underground cable.is proposed? Service current rating? Your original readings? Single or 3 phase? What is the purpose of the 3 x ground rods ?

Also did any one check for service side Neutral issues and imbalances? E.g. what are your N vs A clamp meter loads?? Was neutral to ground current t checked? Plus N.to E volts.. I wouldn't be making any substantial changes until this basic level is checked...

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-12-08T21:09:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-needed-re-mitigating-buried-electrical-cable-issues-in-a-coastal-area-with-a-high-water-table.31984/post-363703>

Hi, Mat. Thanks for your reply. I'm going to need to ask the electrician the why of the three ground rods. I didn't have the house formally checked out from an nnemf perspective when I purchased it, and I'm in the process of trying to set up a proper evaluation now, before I go any further. It might be that how it is right now is sufficient, I don't know. I'll post back when I have some information for you. Thanks again.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-12-08T21:56:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-needed-re-mitigating-buried-electrical-cable-issues-in-a-coastal-area-with-a-high-water-table.31984/post-363706>

While I was in ES I got to measure/test a few houses.
At least one, every time an electrician did some work, like adding Air Conditioning etc they ran a new wire from the meter base, to a new breaker panel, then ran the wires in the house. Small house BUT they had 5 breaker panels …
Is Hugo (American trained electrician) still in ES? WhatsApp +1(832)866-9695

### Mat.I — 2025-12-09T01:46:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-needed-re-mitigating-buried-electrical-cable-issues-in-a-coastal-area-with-a-high-water-table.31984/post-363712>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=363706)
>
> Small house BUT they had 5 breaker panels …

Sounds like a mess. The net current would likely be quite bad with all those branches with additional alternative paths (if not done correctly, which I guess would be highly likely, due to lack of policing/regs). The mention of 3 x earth rods is already ringing some alarm bells...

# Thread: Palpitations advice
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/palpitations-advice.31985/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### S Hudson — 2025-12-07T00:57:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/palpitations-advice.31985/post-363651>

Around this time of year I have a tendency to get palpitations of varying severity (been that way for about 4 years). After seeing the cardiologist for a full cardiac screen 2 years back, he diagnosed it as Vagal nerve agitation due to sinus irritation from fungal spores caused by the increased moisture (I live in a very wooded area). Apparently, according to him at least, its a common presentation for a 53 year old male at this time of year.
Last year it was barely noticeable.

This is the only year I've done any meaningful sun exposure, and I've been hitting every sunrise. There was a period at the beginning of the year, but, surprise surprise, most of spring and summer was palpitations free.

This November, its come on like a freight train which I find strangely ironic.
I had booked several weeks away in the sun, to return just before xmas, but I really don't feel safe flying with the symptoms experienced this week and have cancelled my trip away with the hope that I can be away for most of winter post new year.

I live in 5g dead zone with even limited 4g, I sleep with the electricity off and have no wifi and have meter tested my sleep sanctuary. The palpitations seem to be better with light exercise, and although my heart rate is elevated at rest, it's not by much. I know I need to get into the sun, but I'll admit that fear of falling ill abroad and the discomfort of this is stopping me. Or is this my mind just putting road blocks up to stop me making the required change? Wouldn't surprise me.

It can be a horrible symptom, that almost feeds itself.

I suspect that there is an anxiety aspect to this, with the two conditions feeding on each other.
Jack, would you still recommend the cold therapy for someone with this presentation, or do you have insufficient info to make that call?

Thanks
SH

### Ryan M — 2026-01-10T15:07:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/palpitations-advice.31985/post-365085>

I have very similar symptoms. If I exercise hard in the winter, I feel fine during, but then feel poorly for days after. I can get away with more exertion in the summer. Uncle referred to cardiac apoptosis in response to my thread. As a result of years of exercising excessively and probably a faulty download.

### S Hudson — 2026-01-10T23:07:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/palpitations-advice.31985/post-365103>

Alpha lipoic acid neutralizes mycotoxins. Usually low magnesium or low selenium make the palpitations worse- using brazil nuts for selenium and green vegetables/green drinks for magnesium may help. Monotonal humming 3-5min 3 or 4x per day (Dr Goldman-Humming Effect) may improve vagal function. You are wise to avoid the non-native EMFs that can cause or aggravate those symptoms.

sunshine vacation, i Know a 90 year old with Afib and taking blood thinners who jsut flew to Mexico for a month from the PNW (surely not enough at that age but its his 10 decade of heteroplasmy), i think Uncle Jacks said numerous times those sunshine trips (3-4 months long!) are MANDATORY INWINTER if we are in our 6th decade of heteroplasmy, need that nitric oxide NO to flow plus all the red light and the UVB etc. Even though you're 53, perhaps your heteroplasmy in that area is already in its 7th decade not 6th as your age says it is.

# Thread: Advice for Reversing Pelvic Subluxation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### haileyweston — 2025-12-07T19:37:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363669>

Hello, I’m looking to help a family member who’s in her 20s and had just gone through an accident and broke her pelvis. She had screws placed in her SI joints but still had problems and went to see many doctors who denied her complaint of constant low back and butt pain especially on the right but somehow managed to find an ortho surgeon who found she had a significant posterior subluxation (~1.1cm) and arthritis in her left ilium from the first ortho surgeon not placing long enough screws in her pelvis. There is also a ~1cm leg length discrepancy but she can’t feel it whilst walking. Unlike prior to the accident, she can’t sit for long periods of time without hurting and finds standing to be more comfortable. She’s refusing SI fusion surgery due to potential downstream complications and distrust in the medical system. She already knows a little of Dr. Kruse’s work and has never missed a sunrise, focused on grounding and seafood and avoids nnEMF and artificial lights and working at night as much as possible but is unsure of whether she can reverse her subluxation and if so, what else can she do and what activities she should avoid moving forward. We tried researching everything we could but couldn’t find anything specific for pelvic subluxations. Second surgeon has already removed the screws in her pelvis to see if that resolves the pain (short answer: it doesn’t). She doesn’t have any health issues prior to this accident. If she needs to book a consult, please let me know so I can pass the info to her. Thanks.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-12-08T22:06:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363707>

### haileyweston — 2025-12-10T09:59:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363752>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=363707)
>
> [View attachment 32786](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/32786/)

Dr. Kruse, thank you for your response. We are familiar with this diagram from your blog posts and are aware that circadian biology is important in bone metabolism but we were wondering how and why this bone subluxation occurred and whether photorepair using the sun is the major thing left that is needed to reverse this subluxation. Would any exercise help reverse this? If you have any webinar or Q&A that explains this, it would be tremendously helpful. Every orthopedic surgeon we’ve talked to said it’s irreversible and most don’t know how to explain how it happened but something tells us that may not be the case here.

### digitald3 — 2025-12-10T16:04:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363755>

I was talking to an ER doc yesterday and showed him the xrays of his wife's worn jaw joint and I said that the other side is starting to catch up and he mentioned, the pelvis, and said whenever I see a fractured pelvis in the ER I always look to the other side as its a ring and a ring doesn't break in just one place, if it does the whole ring deforms.. so it sounds like she had surgery to the actual fracture and the other side is out of whack now too... In the right environment the body can heal.. where does your family member live? Sounds like she needs the tropics with feet and hands in the sand every morning and lots and lots of sun on her pelvis and dark dark nights.. this most likely won't happen in North America

### haileyweston — 2025-12-10T16:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363757>

> [digitald3 said:](/goto/post?id=363755)
>
> I was talking to an ER doc yesterday and showed him the xrays of his wife's worn jaw joint and I said that the other side is starting to catch up and he mentioned, the pelvis, and said whenever I see a fractured pelvis in the ER I always look to the other side as its a ring and a ring doesn't break in just one place, if it does the whole ring deforms.. so it sounds like she had surgery to the actual fracture and the other side is out of whack now too... In the right environment the body can heal.. where does your family member live? Sounds like she needs the tropics with feet and hands in the sand every morning and lots and lots of sun on her pelvis and dark dark nights.. this most likely won't happen in North America

Her pelvis actually broke on both of the SI joints and multiple places at the front so she had a pelvic ex fix as well, but somehow the subluxation only occurred on the left SI joint, which had more screws than the right…. She’s living in northern Australia where the accident happened and the subluxation was found by the radiologist about 2 months after her initial pelvic surgery while she was still staying at the hospital but the other doctors in Australia didn’t tell us about it and we didn’t find out about it until after a year later when we decided to find a doctor outside of Australia who pointed out the huge problem.

And thank you very much for the advice. If that’s all it takes to reverse this subluxation, we will go ahead with the advice. We’ve started becoming our own ‘doctors’ after completely losing trust in the medical system through this incident but it’s been difficult to find any research on how and why this happened and what could work until my family member stumbled across Dr. Kruse’s work and decided to give it a try.

### digitald3 — 2025-12-11T02:23:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363773>

I’m not an expert on the pelvis. But know there are lots of things connected to it. I did take gross anatomy and dissected one. So a pelvis fracture is a big deal. I wouldn’t imagine a surgeon could put it back together without some problems. I’m sure there is a surgeon that will tell you they can. But I have my doubts. If it was my family member I’d probably explore physical therapy and myofascial release. Even yoga at the beach safely. That said the repair and regeneration codes are laid out in this forum. Light water and magnetism. Northern Australia may have the light and water but be deficient in magnetism. Which is why she may need less desert and more tropics

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connections-in-el-salvador.31988/post-363673>

Hey everyone, I'm Julian, a dentist from Canada. I'm looking to come down to ES in 2026 to look at real estate. Would love to connect with some of the communities down there and check out different areas. Probably most interested in being close to the beach in an area known for good surfing. I messaged Tom but would love to connect with anyone else down there too.

Side note: I'm 37yo and intend to continue practicing dentistry. As far as I know I can't do that in ES (I have a Canadian license and a US degree), so I've looked in to places where that would be possible and its more of the Carribean islands (St. Lucia, Barbados, BVI's, USVI's, etc.) where I could get a license to practice. Wondering if there are any other docs in here going through a similar decision-making process.

Cheers,

Julian

Considered a per diem position going back and forth to US frequently to build out a home there, but ideally would love to create and serve to make a living in El Salvador to stay. Any creative thoughts on your part about how this could work for you? Best of effort and luck!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-lansky-bailed-on-israel-roy-cohn-was-created.31990/post-363692>

Cohn was a hardcore pervert homosexual. How did he use this? As a wedge to get rid of McCarthy while weakening the US military to work for Israeli interests. In NYC when gay legislation was proposed to change the the cityscape Cohn actually said in the media he would oppose New York’s first gay rights legislation by arguing that “homosexual teachers are a grave threat to our children. This was him baiting the hook again for depravity. Another psy ops success.

He had used this trick to take down McCarthy once the useful idiot for Tel Aviv was neutralized.

How did Cohn use McCarthy? He used the TOP Gun Strategy. That is why it was shown in the movie decades later. Bring your enemies closer and kill them.

Cohn made sure on TV in the 1950s to make sure there nothing discreet about the Senator henchman’s tactics. Cohn goal was simple. To infiltrate the military with commie fascist who were gay. That is what a subversive zionist wants. To weaken the enemy. On TV Cohn threatened to “wreck the Army” and warned the Secretary of the Army he would be “through”. Senator McCarthy, whose committee had already, at Cohn’s legal urging, launched an investigation into alleged Communist infiltration at the Fort Monmouth military base, accused the Army of using “blackmail” and holding Mr Schine “hostage”.

What did Time magazine do to bait the hook for Cohn? They pulled out Bernays playbook and portrayed Schine as a rich, handsome Greek God. Go back and look at how Time described him. Time magazine called Schine a man with the “build and features of a junior-grade Greek god.” Why? Cohn knew SunTzu book well. Many knew he was a hardcore homosexual with deep proclivities because of his FBI dossier held by Hoover. He lead the enemy to believe that he was afraid of being outed a gay man. And if he was, he knew the politicians would come after him. Top Gun style.

Hiow did Cohn kill McCarthy once he became useless to Israel?

The moment in the hearings that historians have said to have led to Senator McCarthy’s fall was with his confrontation with the Army’s ram-rod chief counsel, Joseph Welch. After the Senator had attacked a young lawyer from Mr Welch’s law firm, he responded: “Until this moment, Senator, I think I never really gauged your cruelty or your recklessness.” McCarthy felt protected by Cohn's attacking style so he adopted it. Sadly, MCCarthy was no Cohn. As the Wisconsin senator attempted to renew his attack, Mr Welch cut him off with the now-famous and devastating line: “Senator; you've done enough. Have you no sense of decency, sir? At long last, have you left no sense of decency?”

The Psy Ops from FBI Hoover: A "pixie" Cohn gave the FBI to use.

Questioning a member of Senator McCarthy’s staff, Mr Welch asked repeatedly about the origin of a disputed picture that had been placed in evidence, eventually asking whether it “came from a pixie”. When the Senator foolishly interjected to ask what a pixie was, Mr Welch, who wearing “the look of the happy leprechaun” – shot back: “a pixie is a close relative of a fairy.” The room erupted in nervous laughter, because homosexuality was part of this drama. This was the first time during the hearing that anyone had addressed the homosexual undercurrent between Cohn and Schine, because it was so central to psy ops being sold.

Cohn and Tel Aviv's goal was to make America's military weak and beholden to Israel. They wanted the American military infiltrated with beta's

Why did cohn use his own gayness to be the psy ops? Because he knew the real weakness of the American political scene in the 1950s.
Cohn wanted the rubicon to be crossed, so it would be open season on McCarthy. Cohn was cross-examined about whether he had ever stayed the night and had breakfast at Mr Schine’s apartment. Joining the chase, Senators asked Cohn whether he had a “special interest” in Mr Schine and his McCarthy appointment and questioned his “passionate anxiety” to retain Mr Schine’s services for the committee. McCarthy was played like a fiddle by Cohn. Why? Being Gay in 1950s America was more toxic then being a Communist Bolshevik. Cohn used this ruthlessly.

Arrows from politicians were fired from all directions. Representatives from both parties, and from both ends of the ideological spectrum felt no moral qualms attacking gay people in furtherance of their political agendas. For congressmen, the bought and paid from press, and the viewing public, it was simply accepted that gay men and women “deserved to be objects of derision”.

All this, of course, was at a time when the federal government was purging gays and lesbians from its workforce, while the military ferreted out “sexual deviants” within the services. This is what Cohn wanted done and he crafted a clever legal strategy to ruin the military from within. McCarthy was Israel useful idiot. “The lavender scare” – in which Cohn and his boss were ringleaders and participants, underlined that no charge, not even that of being a Bolshevik Communist, was more toxic than that of being, or suspected of being, gay. Know your history folks.

# Thread: LEVEL UP OR DUMBING IT DOWN?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/level-up-or-dumbing-it-down.31992/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-12-08T16:54:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/level-up-or-dumbing-it-down.31992/post-363696>

**LEVEL UP OR DUMBING IT DOWN?**

Why you should make people level up to your work and not dumb it down for them.

Are you sure we aren’t all animals?

In 1931, two psychologists, Winthrop and Luella Kellogg, began an experiment to see if a chimpanzee could learn human behavior by raising it with their own baby. The idea was to see if the chimp, named Gua, would learn to walk upright, speak, and engage in other human-like activities.

For the first few months, the experiment was going well. Gua learned to mimic some of the actions of Kelloggs’ son, Donald.

However, after 9 months, the Kelloggs were forced to cancel the experiment. It turns out that Donald was beginning to behave like an ape himself, mimicking Gua’s behaviors and even attempting to communicate with grunts and other animal-like sounds.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/figuring-out-how-the-ancients-learned-proper-earthquake-protections.31999/post-363786>

It took me years to realize that Inca and pre-Columbian architecture is directly related to the structure of the corn kernels. In a western model of thought, one might judge the shapes as irregular, but in a universal thought, everything is a correlation between the cosmos, science, art and humanity." (Fractal Nature)

As you can see, the organic growth forms are represented in a logarithmic way, and the fact that these pentagonal, hexagonal and heptagonal blocks coincide with the corn forms in South/Central America.

The lack of symmetry in the walls helps dissipate the energy of the earthquakes. Symmetry links to energy production in the cristae of mitochondria. They were incredible engineers who paid attention to what Nature provided. Japanese researchers studied Machu Picchu after the Kobe earthquake and realized that these Inca structures had not been damaged by any earthquake.

# Thread: New member from Chernobyl
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-from-chernobyl.32001/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrew.S — 2025-12-11T17:17:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-from-chernobyl.32001/post-363792>

New member here, Andrew 34 y/o, born/raised and currently living in San Diego, CA. I've been working in the health and wellness industry for the past 16 years as a personal trainer and massage therapist operating my own business and loving it. I am also a bitcoiner, which is how I originally came across Jacks work after watching his time hacks for longevity video from BTC Prague about 4 ish months ago, which then led me to the Breedlove series. Since 2020 my hackles have been up and after hearing what Jack said in the Breedlove series, it tied so many of the seemingly disconnected "conspiracy theories" I was aware of together...and to see the connection to BTC \*\*\*mind blown\*\*\*-I was finally able to make some sense of all the madness.

In regards to physical health, I have no debilitating issues to speak of in spite of living in Chernobyl but gut health in general (BMs) is stilling one of my areas for improvement. I've switched to the epi-paleo protocol for diet, and have only seem mild improvements (primarily when I was able to get better sunlight on my torso a few months ago-at the time of this post it's about 2 weeks away from winter equinox so good sunlight has been harder to come by). I did not get jabbed but did catch covid 2xs + was exposed via shedding from previously partner. I went to get checked for spike proteins yesterday-so results to come soon.

My health and freedom are two of the primary core values I live by and it's becoming more clear to me that my time in Chernobyl will be ending soon-I've always had this pull to go move somewhere different but I've always defaulted to, "why would I leave? the weather is great, my family is here, and my business is here?" As the pull to lay roots and start a family is becoming my top priority, I now understand from a health and a freedom perspective, why the move is an imperative. Funny enough, before coming across Jack, I was on a surf trip in El Salvador last April in La Liberdad and loved it...the writing is on the wall...

I look forward to making connections, sharing insights, and learning more about light/water/magnetism and the beautiful country of El Salvador!

Hi Mat - I know microwaves heating food - kills food BUT induction uses magnetic fields to heat the pan - not the food directly - so is it “killing“ the food also?

Serious question because I don’t know.

I do know induction puts out a magnetic field that is bad for humans - which is why I walk away and let it heat/cook.
I also use Gas to cook when I have to attend to the food.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-12-17T06:06:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-cooking.32005/post-363978>

> [Pratima said:](/goto/post?id=363949)
>
> I thought the EMFs altered the structure of the food.....not sure where I got that from though.

RF/microwave alters the structure of food - in fact KILLs it.
I am not aware of IR (infrared) having the same affect. I have always considered IR as being a natural heat source.

### Mat.I — 2025-12-17T07:01:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-cooking.32005/post-363981>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=363977)
>
> Hi Mat - I know microwaves heating food - kills food BUT induction uses magnetic fields to heat the pan - not the food directly - so is it “killing“ the food also?
>
> Serious question because I don’t know.
>
> I do know induction puts out a magnetic field that is bad for humans - which is why I walk away and let it heat/cook.
> I also use Gas to cook when I have to attend to the food.

[@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) - I have nothing absolute concrete on this other than apply first principles. I agree re: the microwave effects.. However, think about how induction operates, it using rapidly oscillating mag fields (tens of khz) to induce currents in the pan which heats / cooks the food by surface area contact etc.. If you are being super technical the resistance of the pan would convert these "eddy" currents into heat via joule heating. So looking at the basics of this poses the following question - what is cooking your food that you ingest? IMO you have gone too far downstream re: mag field > eddy currents > pan heats > IR + conduction (you are at the last 2). It is the final carrier but at the cost of delivering the mag fields. So directly (i.e. direct heating) in isolation is not what I consider as "bad" it is the indirect effects that your food is being "cooked" in a highly oscillating mag field.

Some will say this doesn't matter as once it hits your body it is processed and dealt with etc.. However, I do not think it is optimal as you are introducing all this artificial noise into your food as the main driver of the cooking process. I believe that the pathway to heat matters as there is "structure" and "information" in food. What will add more disorder heat alone or oscillating high frequency mag fields + heat? = more noise. Think about what impact this would have on foods water, proteins + lipids etc..

So looking at the basics of this poses the following question - what is cooking your food that you ingest? IMO you have gone too far downstream re: mag field > eddy currents > pan heats > IR + conduction (you are at the last 2). It is the final carrier but at the cost of delivering the mag fields. So directly (i.e. direct heating) in isolation is not what I consider as "bad" it is the indirect effects that your food is being "cooked" in a highly oscillating mag field.
>
> Some will say this doesn't matter as once it hits your body it is processed and dealt with etc.. However, I do not think it is optimal as you are introducing all this artificial noise into your food as the main driver of the cooking process. I believe that the pathway to heat matters as there is "structure" and "information" in food. What will add more disorder heat alone or oscillating high frequency mag fields + heat? = more noise. Think about what impact this would have on foods water, proteins + lipids etc..

Also, just to be clear I'm not saying it kills the food. I'm saying IMO I believe it is not good.

# Thread: Light, Water, Grounding and a crow
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-water-grounding-and-a-crow.32007/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JenniferM — 2025-12-13T22:27:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-water-grounding-and-a-crow.32007/post-363860>

We had a crow that started hanging around about 2 years ago. We became friends during that time. He would greet me in the mornings from his perch in a pine tree. Eventually he would accept pears and begin to talk to me in crow squawk. I would respond like I was talking to a baby, lol. The most awesome part of befriending Oscar the crow is when I discovered Dr. Kruse and began watching the sunrise and getting sun throughout the day. Oscar seemed to be pleased I was getting up earlier and spending more time outside. He is even more vocal in the early mornings now, like he is happier That I am out there for the sunrise. He watches me all the time. Today I was getting all the sun mother nature would give me. Oscar flew over to perch in a tree across from. While flying over he dropped a feather. I watched it fall. Got up and found the feather in the field, held it up to him and said Thank you. He flew away to do crow things I guess like he always does. It made me realize just how important that connection to all things nature is amazing when you least expect it. I love Oscar the crow and the feather is now my new bookmark. Thanks Dr. Kruse

### KFDags — 2025-12-13T23:20:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-water-grounding-and-a-crow.32007/post-363863>

That is so great! Crows are a deeply unappreciated species of bird.

# Thread: Gabriel's Hidden Path: A Privacy Junkie's Quest for Optimal Living
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### privacyjunkie — 2025-12-15T04:04:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/post-363892>

Hi everyone,

My name is Gabriel, but here I'm PrivacyJunkie. My username aligns with my work as a cybersecurity and risk advisor for a big global firm. I've spent my entire career in IT, and honestly, it's a double-edged sword. Without awareness of tech's downsides, we all pay a heavy price eventually. Turning 48 soon, and I'm grateful: no known acute or chronic health issues holding me back. In my personal life, I teach people how to build real digital resilience.

About a year ago, everything shifted. I watched Dr. Jack Kruse's interview on the Danny Jones podcast, and it cracked open a Pandora's box. The past 12 months have been an intense, accelerated rewiring of my life.

Here's what's transformed:

-   My relationship with the sun: I used to avoid it (not from mainstream "sun is evil" dogma, just... hiding for whatever reason). Now? I've built a serious solar callus through rigorous, strategic exposure. My skin tells the story.
-   Natural light/dark cycles: Morning sunlight rituals, daytime exposure, and a dialed-in sleep schedule aligned with nature.
-   nnEMF awareness: Deep dive into non-native electromagnetic fields, researching, measuring, and mitigating RF, AC electric, and magnetic fields everywhere in my environment.
-   Food and eating: Less overall intake thanks to consistent UV exposure, almost entirely seasonal/local, and heavy on DHA-rich sources.
-   Mindset and psychology: Ruthlessly questioning old beliefs, rethinking my purpose, social interactions—and seeing how these physical changes have reshaped my inner world.

There are probably more shifts I haven't listed, but you get the idea.

How I learn and explore: Mostly through Dr. Jack Kruse's content (videos, interviews, podcasts, X posts, and now his blog posts, starting from the beginning and catching up). Plus independent research, books, and papers.

Books I've finished (planning to reread many annually, no particular order):

-   The Body Electric
-   Cross Currents
-   The Invisible Rainbow
-   Going Somewhere
-   Epi-Paleo Rx
-   Power, Sex, and Suicide
-   Electromagnetism & Life (currently reading)
-   Various EMF books on measurement, education, and mitigation
-   Some key papers by Allan Frey
-   Please feel free to suggest anything that's worth reading in your opinion that aligns with the topic

(Still hunting for an affordable copy of _Light in Shaping Life_ by Roeland van Wijk—open to tips!)

My aim?
Keep optimising my own life and share what works with those around me. That said, I know not everyone's ready for ideas that upend everything they've been told. When people resist hard, like they're drowning and trying to drag you down, sometimes you have to prioritize your own oxygen.

I'd love to connect with anyone on a similar path: sharing experiences, discussing ideas, brainstorming experiments, or just exploring these rabbit holes together.

Looking forward to the conversations!

### privacyjunkie — 2025-12-16T07:20:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/post-363932>

Hey everyone,

Hope you're all doing well!

I'm dealing with severe flight anxiety that's really intense, I've got two long flights coming up in about 2 months (roughly 10 hours each), and I'm already having panic attacks just thinking about it.
Flying has always been a huge challenge for me in the past, even though I've managed it before.

I've tried lorazepam + propranolol, and it helps a bit but feels like it's just on the edge, not quite enough.
I'm thinking I might need something a bit stronger and longer-lasting to make it bearable.

Reaching out to see if anyone here faces similar issues and what meds (or combos) have worked for you to knock down the anxiety hard enough to get through longer flights.

Thanks so much in advance for any advice or stories!

### Jacks — 2025-12-16T20:01:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/post-363948>

I went through a period of time when I also had a difficult time flying due to anxiety, especially on long flights. I used alcohol to numb it back then and watched movies continuously to mentally block out the fact I was in a metal tube miles above the ground without having control and being stuck in a tight seat with little personal space! I tried Kava tea a couple of times and that helped but that can have some side effects. I turned to breathing exercises that actually really helped me - such as box breathing etc. So that is what I would recommend. Also meditation practice helps. Drinking salty water I have found helps with anxiety too.

That phase of anxiety with flying appears to have passed for me now and I don't have issues any more. Now I just read a book and listen to music. I still have a difficult time sleeping on planes. I have found the newer Airbus (double decker) planes are a lot quieter and more comfortable than the 747s and I try to upgrade if possible to have more personal space.

### privacyjunkie — 2025-12-16T20:11:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/post-363950>

> [Jacks said:](/goto/post?id=363948)
>
> I went through a period of time when I also had a difficult time flying due to anxiety, especially on long flights. I used alcohol to numb it back then and watched movies continuously to mentally block out the fact I was in a metal tube miles above the ground without having control and being stuck in a tight seat with little personal space! I tried Kava tea a couple of times and that helped but that can have some side effects. I turned to breathing exercises that actually really helped me - such as box breathing etc. So that is what I would recommend. Also meditation practice helps. Drinking salty water I have found helps with anxiety too.
>
> That phase of anxiety with flying appears to have passed for me now and I don't have issues any more. Now I just read a book and listen to music. I still have a difficult time sleeping on planes. I have found the newer Airbus (double decker) planes are a lot quieter and more comfortable than the 747s and I try to upgrade if possible to have more personal space.

I appreciate your response Jacks. I do have a bit of balance issues, maybe some sort of vertigo, which amplifies everything. I am preparing heavily for this journey from the circadian perspective. Building a lot of melanin in my skin, very rigorous day/night routine, and a lot of grounding.
I also support this training/preparation with DHA rich foods and high quality structured spring water.

# Thread: Building something real in Central America
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-something-real-in-central-america.32018/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chozami — 2025-12-17T00:54:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-something-real-in-central-america.32018/post-363964>

Hello everyone,

My name is Tim. I’m currently in Patagonia, Argentina, but I’m actively orienting toward warmer latitudes closer to the equator.

I’m not new to Jack’s work, and I’m not here to debate theory. I’m here because I’m interested in building real-world systems that align with the biology we talk about here.

My background and interests are practical:
\- soil regeneration and mineral cycling
\- biochar (small-scale, appropriate tech, low-input systems)
\- food production infrastructure
\- working with land-based projects rather than extracting from them

I’m looking for people who are actually doing something on the ground — farms, land projects, experimental builds, or communities where biology, light, water, and food are taken seriously.

I’m location-flexible, but climate matters. I function best in warm, high-light environments and am currently exploring options in Central America and southern Mexico.

If anyone here is involved in land-based projects, regenerative food systems, or experimental builds near the equator — or knows people who are — I’d be glad to connect.

Not looking for ideology. Looking for alignment and work.
\- Tim

### dkennis — 2025-12-17T04:11:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-something-real-in-central-america.32018/post-363972>

Heaveno, (rather than hello)
I am very new on here too.
Your post inspired me to share a link to a documentary that is free on You Tube.
I have given away thirty copies that I paid $10/each.
I hope it isn't ideological
It is about a no till style of gardening.
It sure softens the soil and brings up the words.
