### quackbooze — 2026-03-04T22:28:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-computer.32107/post-368107>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=368103)
>
> I hope the daylight phone has a battery disconnect button Not just an on/off switch.

Highly doubt they'll incorporate that, but that would be a very nice feature.
Hardware level cutoffs for the transceivers, camera(s), mics would be amazing, but highly doubt they will do that either.

I've played around with a Mudita Kompakt that has an Eink screen and offline+ mode. Offline+ mode cuts the cell transceiver and microphones at the hardware level and also cuts the camera, bluetooth, and wifi at the software level with one button on the side of the phone. Unfortunately the phone is pretty buggy and the eink screen is slow to respond to touch. It also doesn't have a removable battery. When the phone is in offline+ mode sometimes texts won't come through when switching it back off offline+ mode. It works pretty well, but definitely has issues.

I have an older 4G LTE flip phone with a battery that is very easy to remove that I keep in my car when I go out.
Otherwise I don't use a cell phone at this point. They really are terrible for your health, I know you know.

# Thread: Living in California recovering from MRSA with company and chlorine dioxide.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-california-recovering-from-mrsa-with-company-and-chlorine-dioxide.32111/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shanti — 2026-01-08T23:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-california-recovering-from-mrsa-with-company-and-chlorine-dioxide.32111/post-365011>

After 8 months of MRSA I returned home to California after 14 months in Costa Rica (I had spent 1/2 the year there the past 5 years.)

So I have been home with my husband and grown daughters for the past 4 months healing. It's been nice to be with people who love and accept me unconditionally even though I had mrsa. People on general seem terrified of it so it was quite an isolating experience. Perhaps it's been the companionship opportunity with my husband--waking up early, love making, and seeing the sunrise together --that has been my best medicine.

I bought a canopy we sleep in so the emfs are low--.089 microwatts per m². I found a spot on the soft dimondia to outside to live during the day at around 2mw/m². I spend a lot of time in the water where the radio frequencies are low--swimming in the ocean or sitting in our cold plunge. When it's cold out I am usually in the pool/jacuzzi to get the natural light and for the emf protection. The emfs are high at the beach here but it's my social time. I have learned this past year how important socializing is for me. (Sunrise/sunshine/darkness is number one but companionship is #2, and essential for my survival).

I look back on the El Salvador gathering I attended with Anna Marshall last year with gratitude for being able to connect with the tribe. You guys are my tribe. Here in California I find community with others who share common interests but the tribe --the peeps that I share common values with---are few. They are living under different paradigms that I left behind many years ago.

After 8 months and 8 rounds of antibiotics I started taking chlorine dioxide in my water at the recommendation of one of my many Costa Rica doctors. I thought to buy a book to research it more when I got to the States but it turns out that it is highly sensored and no books were available. That piqued my interest. So the past 4 months I have been adding it to my water, spraying it on my skin, and doing enemas with it. I think it has helped because it decreases the load of bacteria and viruses and so takes the burden (of mrsa) off the immune system. Does anyone else use this? Or know much about it? I am wondering how long I should continue with it.

Love, Shanti

### Mat.I — 2026-01-09T08:55:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-california-recovering-from-mrsa-with-company-and-chlorine-dioxide.32111/post-365030>

I do not know much about CD, however my basic understanding is that it could make your situation worst as it is technically an electron stealer which will lower redox further and will likely oxidise iron and push you towards FE3+ state which is not good as you have an underlying terrain issue so you need to do things that will build the terrain and not break it down..

Could be similar to removing mold from wood with sand paper (looks clean) but each pass eats away at the wood then it eventually fails..

Maybe wait for others or Jack to chime.in on this.

### JenniferM — 2026-01-09T20:44:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-california-recovering-from-mrsa-with-company-and-chlorine-dioxide.32111/post-365046>

Andrew Kaufman M.D., he has a website.
I researched CD for cancer and decided against it.

### Shanti — 2026-01-09T21:56:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-california-recovering-from-mrsa-with-company-and-chlorine-dioxide.32111/post-365054>

Doesn't Chlorine Dioxide have a negative charge like our bodies and its good bugs while the bad bugs and their waste material have a positive electrical change?

After 5 months on it I am doing remarkably/feeling better and stronger every day and my redox seems strong. After 8 months of antibiotics my microbiom might have been destroyed and although I still have more gas than before-- it's improving. My husband got a bad flu for two weeks last month and I didn't ---even though we slept together through it.

The biggest challenge from the MRSA I faced was intrusive thoughts and bouts of sadness/emptiness/anxiety and that has finally cleared up too. (I'm wondering that the devil isn't a man with horns but rather the tiniest of microbes overwhelming our physiology and then our psychological health. MRSA was a body snatcher.)

### Mat.I — 2026-01-09T22:30:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-california-recovering-from-mrsa-with-company-and-chlorine-dioxide.32111/post-365059>

> [Shanti said:](/goto/post?id=365054)
>
> Doesn't Chlorine Dioxide have a negative charge like our bodies a

My understanding it would be somewhat electrically neutral but a strong oxidiser so it pulls electrons from whatever it contacts. What happens when electrons are stripped faster than they can be replaced chronically = lower redox. Keep in mind that iron is a one electron switch e.g. fe2 + > fe3+ so this shift is easy and comes at a high cost..

Chlorine Dioxide is amazing. Go to [Www.andreaskalcker.com](http://Www.andreaskalcker.com). Learn about it. **_It adds oxygen to the body_** and the body distributes it through the interstitial tissue. It does no harm if taken as CDS chlorine dioxide solution. There are many many testimonies...

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-14T03:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-california-recovering-from-mrsa-with-company-and-chlorine-dioxide.32111/post-365295>

Anyone who actually reads the blogs should stop and read this again, **_"it adds oxygen to the body"

Anyone see a problem with this logic?_**

Oxygen, is essential for energy generation, but it can cause oxidative damage to proteins, lipids, and DNA when it accumulates in places that redox status says it should not be. It can also accumulate in areas where melanin is absent. Melanin and melatonin are modes evolved to counteract this damage, but an imbalance favoring pro-oxidants leads to oxidative stress. Oxygen’s dual role necessitates a delicate balance and this means any addition of oxygen in tissues must be stochastically balance. If it is not you are playing with fire, literally and figuratively

Andreas Ludwig Kalcker** is a biophysical researcher of German origin who has spent most of his life in Spain and now lives for many years in Switzerland. His main areas of research include the therapeutic application of frequencies, where he has worked in recent years partly for the University of Bern (Inselspital). "

### janisu — 2026-01-14T04:55:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-california-recovering-from-mrsa-with-company-and-chlorine-dioxide.32111/post-365298>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=365296)
>
> Sorry but this is very bad information that will harm people and same with when I looked at the website.

**Is CDS an oxidant?**

Yes, chlorine dioxide in the form of CDS is an oxidant, **but it also acts as an antioxidant!** It is capable of oxidizing pathogens and acidic substances with an oxidation-reduction potential (ORP) lower than its ORP of 0.95V. What very few people know is that at the same time, it can reduce harmful free radicals with a high ORP, such as hydroxyl groups (OH•) with an ORP of 2.8V, converting them into water. This little-known fact makes it a safe, all-purpose tool.

Perhaps it's been the companionship opportunity with my husband--waking up early, love making, and seeing the sunrise together --that has been my best medicine.
>
> I bought a canopy we sleep in so the emfs are low--.089 microwatts per m². I found a spot on the soft dimondia to outside to live during the day at around 2mw/m². I spend a lot of time in the water where the radio frequencies are low--swimming in the ocean or sitting in our cold plunge. When it's cold out I am usually in the pool/jacuzzi to get the natural light and for the emf protection. The emfs are high at the beach here but it's my social time. I have learned this past year how important socializing is for me. (Sunrise/sunshine/darkness is number one but companionship is #2, and essential for my survival).
>
> I look back on the El Salvador gathering I attended with Anna Marshall last year with gratitude for being able to connect with the tribe. You guys are my tribe. Here in California I find community with others who share common interests but the tribe --the peeps that I share common values with---are few. They are living under different paradigms that I left behind many years ago.
>
> After 8 months and 8 rounds of antibiotics I started taking chlorine dioxide in my water at the recommendation of one of my many Costa Rica doctors. I thought to buy a book to research it more when I got to the States but it turns out that it is highly sensored and no books were available. That piqued my interest. So the past 4 months I have been adding it to my water, spraying it on my skin, and doing enemas with it. I think it has helped because it decreases the load of bacteria and viruses and so takes the burden (of mrsa) off the immune system. Does anyone else use this? Or know much about it? I am wondering how long I should continue with it.
>
> Love, Shanti

Hi Shanti,

I'm confused: are you talking about being, for the last 8 months, simply a healthy **carrier** of MRSA and being mostly around healthy people (ex: you were tested, and you are asymptomatic but colonized with the bacteria and you don't work in hospitals/with immunocompromised sick people),
or are you talking about having and active MRSA **infection** that would not heal for 8 months (wounds with pus, scabs or other infections) positive with that resistant staphylococcus aureus bacteria when they swab it?

First case is usually really not a big deal; some people are just positive for it, and it doesn't seem to go away, but it doesn't infect them or "burden" them, it's just sitting there, part of other bacterias just sitting there...
Think about how many millions of carriers we have walking around, without knowing it (but also without being affected by it, at all)

Not trying to be dismissive, and maybe they haven't explained it to you
or maybe I totally misunderstood your situation

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-17T19:59:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-california-recovering-from-mrsa-with-company-and-chlorine-dioxide.32111/post-365505>

Kurt Warner shared the lesson that changed his entire career and it applies to everything from the recent "Passing The Torch Podcast. He sat on the bench for 4 years in college at Northern Iowa.

When a friend asked the coaches why he wasn't playing, the answer wasn't what he expected:

"The reason I wasn't playing was because I was not very good in practice."

His first reaction? Allen Iverson mode. "Practice? What're you talking about, practice?"

But then he did the math.

"In college we play 12 games in 365 days. In the NFL we play 16 games in 365 days."

That's less than 5% of your year. "95% of our lives are lived in practice.

99.9% of my members lives are spent in environments filled with polarized light.

And Warner realized this was where the biggest impression we make on people, the way people can understand and really realize who we are, is what we do every day in practice." This is the 95% Rule.

And it applies to everything - sports, business, relationships, life and quantum biology.

1: Show Up With Your Best Effort - Compete and give your best every single day. People can't question how you show up - your effort, attitude, and actions. Consistency removes doubt.

2: Trust Is Built In Practice, Not Games - Trust is earned in the thousands of moments before it's given. Before you can be trusted, people want to know you're dependable. Every day. Not just when it matters.

3: Master Daily Consistency - Success isn't about intensity - it's about consistency of reading, assimilating, and asking questions about the blogs to learn. It is not about trying to get your answer in a tweet or a thread. Your habits compound. What you do daily defines who you become.

4: Big Moments Are Earned In Small Moments - The little details make the biggest difference. Greatness starts with preparation - it's earned in the boredom of doing the work when no one's watching.

Excellence, just like health isn't an event - It's a habit.

Practice is where trust is built. In the sun without polarizing influence of man made light.

Stop making excuses and playing the victim card.

How you show up daily is who you really are.

# Thread: Matt's Optimal Journal

### MattB99 — 2026-01-10T01:45:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-optimal-journal.32115/post-365070>

Hello. I am so happy to have found Uncle Jack's online resources and am looking forward to connecting with this community. I wish I could have found this information sooner (beginning to understand why I didn't). I've been listening obsessively to Uncle Jack's podcasts on youtube for the last few months and am now cramming as much blog and webinar content into my cranium as my limited bandwidth and comprehension will allow.

**Location**
I live in south eastern British Columbia in a town called Kimberley. It is a small town, about 8,000 people, at relatively high elevation, ~1,100m (3,650ft) near the 49th lattitude. It is on the edge the tectonic plate west of the Rocky Mountains. There are probaly pros and cons to living here. Look forward to hearing your thoughts. Mentally preparing for exile.
PROS:
\- hotsprings
\- low population density
\- hot springs
\- good spring water near my house
\- high altitude (potential UVA in winter)
CONS:
\- living near a mine site (geopathic stress? / X factors)
\- high altitude
\- high latitude
\- far from ocean
\- Canadian politics / brainwashed population

**Maternal History**
My maternal grandmother was a nurse. She lived until her early 80s and died of cancer. My mother was also a nurse. My understanding was she had difficulty having children before I was born - 2 miscarriages that I am aware of. I was the first born, by cesarian weighing almost 10 pounds. I was born with a lymphatic defect in my neck which required surgery at 13 months. I have one sister who was also born by cesarian, also large. She had no congenital defects. I had one other sister who was born with an enlarged heart. She passed away at 13 months.

**Health History**
My neck surgery damaged some nerves in my neck causing some muscle atrophy and scoliosis. The death of my sister was emotionally traumatic to me and my family. I have had anxiety as long as I can remember. Luckily my family was outdoor oriented. We did lot of outdoor activities when I was a kid which probably compensated somewhat for my compromised maternal epigenetics and the trauma of losing a sister when I was 5.
I had my first episode of major depression half way through my university career in 2009. I went to the doctor and started taking some depression/anxiety meds. I was able to return to university and complete my degree but relapsed again shortly after. I moved home after my relapse, recovered a bit, started working again and then relapsed again with a major suicidal obsession. I admited myselft to the hospital for a month. I was assigned a psychiatrist and took every on and off label pharmaceutical he could think of. I was then diagnosed with dythymia or treatment resistant depression. I eventually gave up on doctors and prescriptions and went down the naturpathic/functional medicine path without much sucess. I came out of my major depression but have been slogging along in an apathetic, anhedonic, low-agency state ever since. I have had a lot of cavities as well over the past 10 years.

**Current Health Challenges**
Persistant depression
Cavity/tooth infection
Tinnitus (started recently after tooth infection)

**Current Rx**
1hr walk at daybreak until after sunrise in the nearby forest.
nnEMF search and destroy mission: Hard wiring internet devices Trifield2 meter -> kill MAG/ELEC/RF. Swapping out all LEDs for incandesants.
BAB: ~100g Salmon with eggs and animal protein for breakfast. Butter in coffee.
Cold baths, 55F for 5 mins. Working up from here.
Ketogenic, local, seasonal diet as much as possible.
Walk at sunset.
Regular outside breaks during the day.
Lights out after dark as soon as possible. Need to black out my bedroom windows to block LED street lamps and neighbours' lights. Currently using a sleep mask.
Buying BTC

**Next Health Focused Actions**
Working on finding friends. Have lost most of them and not initiated any new freindships over the years due to depression, apathy, low dopamine.
Saving up for magnetico matress.
Saving up for tropical vacation.
Saving up for EMF consult.
Looking for outdoor low nnEMF work enviroment.

### Alex_fr — 2026-01-10T20:40:09+0200

Hello everyone,
When picking a place to live, how important is one's haplotype?
ie: I'm T2B, could it be better for me to live at the tropic instead of the equator and to accept a litlle les UV in exchange for access to cold ocean water?

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-10T22:37:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-haplotype.32116/post-365100>

How mitochondria make radicals in an environment.

**WHERE DID HAPLOTYPES COME FROM?**
We have several levels of energy available to cells.
1\. Biochemical level where box car pathway ideas reign
2\. Solid state level where semiconduction rules
3\. Non-linear optical where melanin & water dominate
4\. Quantum coherence & entanglement level
5\. Optical photonics of nonvisual photoreception
6\. Natural emergent magnetic monopoles
7\. Where this 3-hour lecture is: The edge of my science THE NEUTRINO/ ANTI-neutrino level

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/april-2016-what-77738160](https://www.patreon.com/posts/april-2016-what-77738160)

The tilt of the Earth creates a latitude map. This map is geometric. The tilt of our planet makes our seasons. At the equator, where we evolved, the sun varies very little. The further we go from the equator, the greater the variation in light. How do our mitochondria code work in this situation? How do plant chloroplasts handle this?

Your latitude determines the dose of terrestrial sunlight and radiation your colony of mitochondria receives. This location affects your colony of mitochondria and this is codified in their electromagnetic footprint to create very specific free radical chemicals that acts as beacons as EMF's vary. The incident EMF they sense leads to a particular response in your cells. Is this electromagnetic fingerprint buried in your skin color, your haplotype, and SNP patterns? It turns out it is. Examine the haplotype maps of humans created by Dr. Doug Wallace. The changes in the sun and Earth's magnetic field caused haplotypes in humans.

**Earth’s Tilt: Latitude, Light, and Seasons**

Earth’s 23.5° tilt spins a latitude map, equator (0°) gets steady sun (12h daily, 300-500 W/m², NASA data), poles (90°) swing from 24h light to dark over 6-month seasons. Seasons bloom, equatorial light’s flat (1-2% variance), higher latitudes amplify (30°N hits ~30% swing). Sunlight’s spectrum (250-780 nm) UV, visible, IR, NIR shifts too: equator maxes UVB (290-315 nm), poles lean IR (650-950 nm). Mitochondria (level 1-2) and chloroplasts (level 3) sense this by using latitude’s their EMF dial. That dial becomes your UPE signal that links to the electronic structure of proteins/peptides and their absorption spectra.

Mitochondria: Coding the Light Gradient

-   Equator (0°): Steady sun are the mito’s colony (level 1) basks in UV-rich light (4-6 eV). H⁺ spins entangle (CDs, level 4), melanin’s H₂O split (200-1200 nm, level 3) floods e⁻—NAD+ hums at cytochrome 1, ΔΨm (mito potential) peaks (~150-200 mV). Haplotypes, Wallace’s mtDNA maps (2010) clearly show equatorial roots (L0-L3, Africa): tight ETC, high redox, low heteroplasmy. Skin is designed to be dark—melanin maxes UV catch (POMC, level 5), biophotons (200-350 nm) sync neuroectoderm (my Tetragrammaton podcast catch).
-   Higher Latitudes (30°-60°): Light swings forces the mito matrix to adapt. UV dips, NIR must rise—H⁺ spins tweak (level 6), e⁻ flow leans on food (photosynthetic web, level 1). Wallace’s haplotypes shift—H, J, U (Europe) mtDNA codes for colder, dimmer climes: looser ETC, more ROS to warm (level 4). Skin pales, less melanin, more Vitamin D (295 nm) via cholesterol (level 5). SNPs/SAP vary, like MC1R which affects POMC translation to tune melanin down, optics shift (ε\_r ~78, scatter rises).

**EMF Fingerprint:** Latitude’s dose—UV/IR mix—codes mito’s electromagnetic footprint. mtDNA haplotypes (Wallace’s work, level 2) log it—equatorial L clades pack dense spin (high O₂), temperate H/U skew ROS (low UV). Skin color tracks—melanin’s UV shield fades poleward, SNPs (e.g., SLC24A5) adjust opsin response (level 5). Tetragrammaton bombshell: “Mito senses sunlight via blood” (Part 2, ~00:22:32)—latitude’s EMF writes the script.

**Plant Chloroplasts: Latitude’s Green Response**

-   Equator: Steady 300-500 W/m²—chloroplasts (level 3) split H₂O with PSII (680 nm, 1.82 eV), e⁻ flood PSI (700 nm), O₂ spikes. UV-rich—bandgap (2-2.5 eV) maxes—photosynthesis churns glucose, no downtime. No seasons—chlorophyll’s steady, entropy’s low (sharp 1410 nm).
-   Higher Latitudes: Seasonal swings—summer UV (295 nm) drives PSII, winter IR (700 nm) slows it. Chloroplasts adapt—more carotenoids (450-570 nm) catch dim light, less chlorophyll—O₂ drops, entropy rises (1510 nm blur). Plants dorm—seeds wait, syncing mito’s food web (level 1).

Link: G-class UV spike (200-300 Ma) juiced chloroplasts—O₂ soared, mammals’ mito ate it (level 1-3). Sunlight’s reversible—plants split H₂O, mito remakes it—latitude tunes both (your podcast: “Thermodynamic givens,” Part 1, ~00:44:08).

**Big Pharma’s Grift: UV-Only Misses Latitude**

-   Grift: Blocks UV (SPF, shades)—ignores 6 colors (390-780 nm, level 5). Latitude’s lost—equator needs UV, poles need IR—grift’s one-size-fits-all spikes entropy (1510 nm). Mito’s pseudohypoxic—NAD+ falls, optics blur (level 4)—POMC starves (level 3), health pays (Part 2, ~01:09:43: “Ignorance metastasis”).
-   Decentralized Truth: Sun’s 7 hues—latitude-coded—sync POMC/melanin (level 5), H⁺ spins (level 6). Wallace’s maps—L to H—prove it: mito’s EMF footprint shifts, skin/SNPs tag it (level 2). Noether holds—energy curves right, entropy’s low (1410 nm).

**Noether’s Symmetry: Latitude’s Cosmic Code**

UV spike (200-300 Ma) broke time symmetry—O₂ rose, POMC conserved (level 7). Latitude maps it—equator’s steady, poles swing—mito and chloroplasts code the tilt’s EMF (level 4). Block sun—grift’s lie—entropy wins, optics mis-curve (level 5). Tetragrammaton: “Melanin survived KT” (Part 1, ~00:44:33)—latitude’s fingerprint endures.

**The Flow of Nature**

You do not need WiFi on . . . You can get a “doggle” for your iPad and hard wire (ethernet) directly to your modem . . . And turn WiFi and Bluetooth OFF. NEVER use your iPad while charging unless you use Apple’s grounded power cord. From a WiFi point of view there is little difference between the iPad and the Daylight, both need to be hard wired to reduce RF. The BIG advantage of the daylight is no blue light from the screen.

Your earthing mattress - might - be making things worse IF you do not disconnect the 60Hz (50Hz) electric field in your room.
IF your phone is plugged in charging AND not on airplane mode it maybe also be contributing to your problems.
A medical person would need to speak to the hydrogen machine BUT the power to your room NEEDS to be turned off and IF you need something plugged in (powered up) run an extension cord in from another room and have the machine by the door as far from you as possible.

My advice from an EMF point of view (I’m not a medical person) turn off the power to your room, get rid of all the electrical crap, buy a battery operated white noise machine IF your really need it. Sounds like you have nothing to loose by trying this for a week to see if any of it helps.

You say you are in a flight path . . . How close to the airport? IF close THAT could be a major issue for you . . . Airport radar.

Healing is not a completion, there is no meaningful comparison with this stuff. Just know, VERY sick people have improved AND recovered when they do the work.

### Mat.I — 2026-01-11T05:59:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what%E2%80%99s-the-most-impressive-testimonial.32117/post-365115>

My advice (firstly I'm an engineer not a Dr) - do not worry about anyone else but your own n=1.. It will not change anything, I know you way want some reassurance, but what other options are there. Do not worry about listening to Jack's podcasts on WIFI as this is damaging your system (in survival mode) further. Do not use the grounding sheets ( I will link a thread for you) as this can make your issues worst..

I have seen your other posts and opened your photos it is evident you have longstanding lack of UV exposure + redox and vasopressin issues.

Make sure the BB is well versed in NNEMF assessments and mitigation methods.

Based on your current status you should not be on any screen or tech especially indoors and/or after sunset that should be upheld.

Basics are -
\- Never miss a sunrise get outside and get the light in your eyes.
\- as much direct contact grounding as possible (feet on grass etc..)
\- Eat mainly protein / fat for first meal ~ 30 mins from sunrise
\- get as much midday sun as possible (but you will have to go very easy on this at the start as your terrain will not be able to handle too much) alternate between direct & indirect &/or shaded sun. Be outside as much as possible.
\- watch the sunset and stay dark after it (no exceptions period). No light at all. Sit and talk or just sit and relax/visualise your healing. If you really have to - use a candle (but complete darkness should be the goal).
\- aim for 2 to 3 g of DHA per day from the marine whole food chain (you will likely have to slowly work towards this). Not from supps.
\- get black out curtains for bedrooms and aim for complete darkness with no electronics in the room.
\- Until the BB arrives unplug your WIFI modem/router and kill the circuit breakers to the bedroom circuit or areas frequently used (where possible).
\- Use your phone as little as possible and keep on flight mode when not in use.. Turn off any wifi / BT / smart devices in your house.
\- Understand that Brown gas may help short term re: kidney function but note that H2 lowers redox pressure (not good in the long term). This is especially problematic at night as vasopressin is programmed at this time + in a low redox system this can blunt oxygen / hypoxia signalling. Note: kidney filtration improving necessarily does not = regulation improving. Also note this can be causing your BP to dip during sleep.

Goal should be to rebuild redox. The above is needed to help towards that goal.

Work closely with a MD who is well versed in Jack's work or Jack him-self to help with medication adjustments/tapers along the way (if safe to do so).

Do not give up hope, stay positive and forget about the VAD... Keep your thoughts focussed on connecting back with nature (disconnecting from research and tech) and healing... Give it a try.

Re: grounding inside (non-technical explanation) - you become an attractor for feilds even if there is a built in resistor (like most grounding products have. If connected to the GPO earth you become part of the earth and in most cases it is dirty.. If you use a stake outside you still have issues as you have completed the circuit (you are allowing current to flow through your body to earth)..
These small currents can be very disruptive for people.
Ultimately, you have traded pressure (voltage only) I.e. potential for current flow should a path to earth be provided for actual current flow through your body. This is far more disruptive than push/pull pressures (not being grounded).

I wrote a post on this that I will link below. If you want to use those products inside (for static discharge) IMO certain benchmarks must be met to limit any harm (which are outlined in the post).

Haven't read the Kruse for dummies, however H2 makes no sense for chronic use. Concentration and delivery would be higher in the inhalation.

Why would you want to scavenge all ROS (and including RNS indirectly) to lower redox pressure.. this will/could have 2 different end points subject to the terrain. Low redox - further destroy the system and hinder repair vs higher redox it could shut down any adaptive signalling etc..

ROS are needed for feedback and signalling. Makes no sense to wipe them. Only cases I could think of are acute one offs to limit damage. Do not think any case could be made for chronic use..

No worries. ROS/RNS are spin active radicals. If they get neutralised and/or cut short, this in.it.self collapses spin dependent signalling.. Also they are like redox pressure sensors hence why this lowers redox pressure as accurate feedback is not received.. Additionally, this would shorten the electron.dwell time which at the back end would.result in weaker less coherent UPEs etc..What happens when no error signals are received - the system assumes adequacy (even if this is false upstream).

### Anna Dunn — 2026-01-17T01:17:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-hydrogen-water-inhalation-close-the-gates-to-heaven-and-hell.32119/post-365468>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=365189)
>
> Haven't read the Kruse for dummies, however H2 makes no sense for chronic use. Concentration and delivery would be higher in the inhalation.
>
> Why would you want to scavenge all ROS (and including RNS indirectly) to lower redox pressure.. this will/could have 2 different end points subject to the terrain. Low redox - further destroy the system and hinder repair vs higher redox it could shut down any adaptive signalling etc..
>
> ROS are needed for feedback and signalling. Makes no sense to wipe them. Only cases I could think of are acute one offs to limit damage. Do not think any case could be made for chronic use..

I have used Brown‘s gas for 2 years (breathing it at night). It fixed my eGFR.

I could be wrong but it’s my understanding that molecular hydrogen (H₂) is selective rather than broad-spectrum in its antioxidant effects.

It reacts in a biologically relevant way with:

-   •OH (hydroxyl radical) — extremely reactive, non-specific, and purely damaging
-   ONOO⁻ (peroxynitrite) — highly destructive nitrating/oxidising species

It does not significantly react with the following physiological redox species:

-   Superoxide (O₂•⁻) — precursor signalling species
    Rapidly converted to hydrogen peroxide; not a stable signal itself.
-   Hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂) — signalling molecule
    Primary ROS used for physiological redox signalling.
-   Nitric oxide (NO) — signalling molecule

See

[https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC25747486/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC25747486/)

I haven’t been breathing pure hydrogen though- it‘s Brown’s gas- water gets split into 2/3 hydrogen and 1/3 oxygen. I’m worried about the increased 02.

### Anna Dunn — 2026-01-17T02:39:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-hydrogen-water-inhalation-close-the-gates-to-heaven-and-hell.32119/post-365473>

>

>
> Also, I just cannot see benefits in chronic use of this irrespective of what that paper said. Does it correct the boundary conditions that created the dysfunction to begin with.

I’m not sure re masked versus fixed but what caused it was probably the very heavy oxalate excretion when my GP changed my diet to low oxalate. In primary hyperoxaluria patients, they have a liver which genetically makes more endogenous oxalate. After liver transplant, their body starts excreting it which then tends to blow out their kidneys. I was suffering end stage oxalate toxicity symptoms (dystonic seizures, symptoms of high ICP, microvascular damage) just prior to getting dx by my GP.
It was extremely obvious how much my MCAS and dystonic seizure activity improved with the molecular hydrogen, so I’m terrified of going even one night without. My machine broke at one point and my husband was going to drive from Brisbane to Sydney to pick up another one second hand at a cost of 5k, that’s how reliant I am on it.

The machine doesn’t fix the cause of my disease at all but I feel like it’s a Band-Aid stopping me from having a heart attack (I have vasospastic and microvascular angina). It also reduces my akathisia which is next level torture when it gets bad.

Re masked it or fixed it. I Think fixed it- see
Li J, et al. Hydrogen-rich water attenuates renal injury in hyperoxaluria-induced kidney damage.
Oxidative Medicine and Cellular Longevity, 2017.
Hydrogen reduced tubular oxidative stress, inflammation, and fibrosis in an oxalate-driven kidney injury model.
There are other papers, too.

### Mat.I — 2026-01-17T09:08:53+0200

I found this paper the 2017 did not show.

From what I gathered it is supportive of injury attenuation whilst administering HRW.. No withdrawal period or follow up. The million dollar question would be does function remain if washed out (i.e. when the HRW is removed). This was not done..

They basically dampened / turned down the stress pathways (HRW supressed the cascade pathway). So can it be protective &/or masking (when administered) yes possibly. Does it fix the boundaries - no evidence of this. Case of capacity vs tolerance.

Maybe it can help tolerate excretion and mask symptoms (raises the symptom ceiling) that would otherwise prevent you from continuing..

A way to look at it could be blood markers tell you how fast fluid is being filtered vs urine tests which tell you how the actual machinery is working..

Thanks, Mat

# Thread: DIY spectrometer using a smartphone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-spectrometer-using-a-smartphone.32120/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daulatwant — 2026-01-12T16:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-spectrometer-using-a-smartphone.32120/post-365205>

There is tutorials how to make basic spectrometers like this, I tried making one before with a webcam and it didn't produce very good results. But this time I tried using my phone instead as the camera. I downloaded iVCam software on pc and phone that lets you use phone as webcam over USB and it has good controls for the camera. Then I used Theremino Spectrometer software on Windows. And calibrated it using a CFL bulb, calibration needs to be redone every time cause this is a loose contraption, and moving things will mess up the calibration. A piece of CD is used to scatter the different wavelengths, I cut slots into a bottle cap to hold it and fixed it with a screw through the outside of the box into the bottle cap, I can twist the screw to change the CD angle. Lining everything up so the light hits the CD just right is tricky.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_20260112_132453-jpg.33119/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_20260112_132531-jpg.33120/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_20260112_132411-jpg.33122/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_20260112_125826-jpg.33118/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/red-filter-incandescent-jpg.33133/)

### Daulatwant — 2026-01-12T16:39:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-spectrometer-using-a-smartphone.32120/post-365206>

Here is the spectrum of a small dim incandescent, obviously that's not what the spectrum should look like, the red drops off because cameras have an infrared filter built in.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/incandescent-jpg.33125/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_20260112_125638-jpg.33131/)

But besides the relative brightness of each wavelength not being very accurate. I was surprised how sensitive it turned out being.

I bought some phosphor red led (blue diode converted to red) to test out, and it was able to pick up the blue light.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_20251223_162121-jpg.33124/)

Here I measured a tiny very dim 660nm led and then a 590nm led and it was spot on.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/spectrum-deep-red-jpg.33126/) [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/asd-jpg.33127/)
This is more like what the previous red led should have looked like. The little, ripples along the bottom is just camera noise cause these measurements were very dim.

### Daulatwant — 2026-01-12T16:52:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-spectrometer-using-a-smartphone.32120/post-365207>

This is my Samsung phone OLED screen with blue and green completely blocked by software.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/untitled-jpg.33128/)

This is an old laptop LCD with F.LUX set to the lowest setting. At first glance it looks like it's doing something, this is what I meant saying it's surprisingly sensitive, again the rippling at along the bottom is just the noise floor of the camera at a high ISO level and you can still pick up on the small amount of blue light. But tilting the display reveals lots of blue and green leaking at an angle through the LCD. This as an old LCD, I don't know how an IPS LCD would compare.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/lcd-jpg.33129/) .[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/lcd-from-an-angle-jpg.33130/)

Quite useful for testing LED lights.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-29T14:59:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-intensity-ultrasound.32121/post-366087>

When you have an increase in the change of your tumor and you are using another doctor's protocol you have a duty to discuss your new adaptations with them. You should not force your wishes onto someone else.

Generally when you have measured your environment and you have VERIFIED there is no polarized light around you then you might ask the question, is this other doctor's protocol doing something to cause the size and shape changes in my tumor or in their mitochondria.

What centralized MDs should be worried about in this case is the tumor is going further into the GOE state and de-differentiating into a more malignant state. But decentralized clinicians know there is another possibility as well. Decentrlaized clinicians can educate you on this topic generally. Let me be clear. I am not your doctor. You and your husband told me you have other doctors specific to your cancer care. USE THEM and begin to question them ASAP.

When size changes occur it is evidence of thermodynamic change. This could be good or bad but it needs investigation with imaging. You want to see if the growth is associated with invasion or not. The best imaging here is MRI and/or PET/SPECT scaning. What should they be looking for? Whether there is extension into nodes or adjacent tissue planes or not. If tissue planes are destroyed you have to worry about a change in differentiation. Since you have cancer experts and Makis on your cases these are the things they should be DISCUSSING with you.

If those things aren't present then you want to see if there is a change in shadowing or melanin adjacent to the tumors to show there is actions happening on the mitochondria of the cancer cells specifically.

Since you are using the OTHER doctor's protocol these would be WISE things to consider.

Ivermectin, Fenbendazole, Mebendazole are key parts of his protocol. These anthelmintics act as
**redox-disruptors** that target the very metal-dependent pathways I’ve highlighted in my decentralized thesis. I know Makis has no idea what that implies and means. You should because you are supposed to be reading and understanding. While centralized fucks think these drugs are only viewed purely as "parasite killers," their molecular interactions with **Fe, Cu, Mn, and Mo** reveal a profound impact on mitochondrial biology and UPE signaling. Melanin chelates all of them so they remain controlled to make sure mitochondrial respond with a coherent message.

This implies that the protocol drugs might cause changes in your tumor doesn't it. This is why Makis needs to follow up with you.

**1\. Ivermectin: The Mitochondrial "Uncoupler" and ROS Generator**
Ivermectin (IVM) primarily interacts with the system by inducing mitochondrial dysfunction and altering redox balance.

-   **Copper (Cu) & Iron (Fe) Interaction:** IVM has been shown to induce **mitochondrial damage** and apoptosis, specifically leading to the release of **cytochrome C which is** a heme protein whose function is entirely dependent on its **Copper** and **Iron** centers.
-   **Manganese (Mn) & SOD2:** IVM treatment upregulates the production of **Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS)**. In my framework, this would place a massive demand on **Mn-SOD (SOD2)** to manage the resulting "oxidative noise." If the Mn-SOD shield is overwhelmed, the resulting ROS flux drives cell apoptosis and this would induce a change in the tumor as it loses energy, a mechanism being explored for its antitumor effects.
-   **UPE Signaling:** By decreasing mitochondrial membrane potential and respiration in cancer cells, IVM disrupts the coherent "light" (UPE) signaling required for cellular adaptation.

**2\. Mebendazole & Fenbendazole: The Iron-Dependent "Starvers"**
The benzimidazoles (MBZ and FBZ) have a distinct relationship with iron status and mitochondrial integrity.

-   **Iron (Fe) Dependence:** Mebendazole is significantly **less effective in iron-deficient environments**. Clinical studies suggest that for infections like hookworm, iron supplementation is often required alongside MBZ to clear anemia and restore efficacy. This suggests MBZ may leverage or require the host's iron-dependent pathways to finalize its anthelmintic or antitumor effects.
-   **Manganese (Mn) & Mitochondrial Stability:** Both drugs target the **mitochondria of the germinal layer** in parasites, leading to decreased ATP production. In mammalian cells, FBZ has been noted to inhibit tumor growth specifically in models used to study **mitochondrial genes**, indicating a direct interaction with the organelle's metal-tuned hardware.
-   **Molybdenum (Mo) and Sulfur:** There is no direct documented interaction between these drugs and Molybdenum. However, since MBZ and FBZ can impact liver function (the site of major Mo-dependent enzyme activity in humans), prolonged high-dose therapy requires monitoring for metabolic "interference".

**3\. Key Interactions Summary**

| Drug | Primary Metal Interaction | Impact on a Decentrlaized Medical Framework |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Ivermectin** | **Cu/Fe** (via Cytochrome c) | Increases "oxidative noise" (ROS), overwhelming the **Mn-SOD** shield. |
| **Mebendazole** | **Fe** (Direct requirement) | Efficacy is tied to iron status; potentially acts as a "metabolic lock" in the matrix. |
| **Fenbendazole** | **Mn/Fe**(Mitochondrial genes) | Disrupts mitochondrial tubulin and ATP synthesis, altering the UPE signaling field. |

Cancer is a GOE state of health. In a "Cambrian" state of health, these drugs would act as massive stressors by increasing ROS (UPE noise) and potentially sequestering or demanding metals like **Iron** and **Copper**. This makes them potent against "noisy" systems like cancer or parasites but highlights why they must be used carefully in a system already struggling with **DHA-depletion** or **pseudohypoxia**. You should continue your education to supplement your experts wisdom if you are interesting in your own time bank account.

Here is a reference that is topical to your problem. [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads...nce-ideas-inspired-by-fiat.32197/#post-365986](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-mitochrondrial-science-ideas-inspired-by-fiat.32197/#post-365986)

> Julz, stay strong and keep hoping. I really think you can do it!
> Unlce Jack may sound vague sometimes, but he tries to recommend you the important things. But without continuing monitoring and testing of your body and disease progress, he cannot explicitly say what to do.
> The drugs can have positive effects on the tumor but negative effects on your body. That's why caution and monitoring is key.
>
> The drugs change the redox potential.
>
> -   **Potential tumor effects** ("changes" as predicted): Possible apoptosis/necrosis leading to shrinkage, stabilization, or marker drops on follow-up scans (CT/PET/MRI), bloodwork (e.g. markers), or symptoms. Initial inflammation ("flare") could mimic progression on imaging — hence the need for expert follow-up.
> -   **Risks and downsides of continuation/escalation**:
>     -   **Cumulative toxicity**: Liver enzyme elevation/hepatotoxicity is the main concern with prolonged high-dose use (common to all three; benzimidazoles and IVM are hepatically metabolized). Reversible in many cases but requires monitoring. GI upset, fatigue, rare bone marrow effects (MBZ), or neurotoxicity (high-dose IVM: dizziness, confusion).
>     -   Additive effects in combination: potential for amplified liver stress or oxidative burden on healthy mitochondria.
>     -   In "vulnerable" patients (DHA-depleted/pseudohypoxic): risk of harming normal cells via excessive ROS.
> -   **Monitoring needs** (why Makis follow-up really matters practically): Liver function tests (LFTs), CBC, renal panel regularly; serial imaging/tumor markers; symptom tracking. Dose adjustments or breaks
> -   further treatment implies targeted redox/mitochondrial/UPE disruption with expected tumor changes
>
> What could this mean (after monitoring test results) :
> \- Iron panel (ferritin, serum iron, TIBC/TSAT, transferrin saturation) + ceruloplasmin/copper. May be avoid all iron supplements, iron-fortified foods, and high-heme red meat if ferritin is elevated
> \- Mild natural modulators/chelators: Curcumin (liposomal or with piperine) + quercetin. Optional: EGCG (green tea extract in green tea, matcha, supplements)
> \- Support copper balance: If ceruloplasmin is low, discuss gentle copper sources (e.g., 1–2 mg/day under guidance).
> \- Manganese: (Test RBC manganese) supplement if low --> direct cofactor for _Superoxiddismutase 2 = MnSOD_
> \- may be Melatonin at bedtime, Ubiquinol (CoQ10), R-Alpha lipoic acid, PQQ (promotes mito biogenesis), NAC (glutathione precursor; also excellent liver support), Nrf2 support (upregulates endogenous SOD2/GPx)
> **probably always good:** DHA daily (high-quality triglyceride or phospholipid form; fish oil or algae-based; shellfish, wild caught fish). Consider testing the Omega-3/6 Index --> DHA depletion as a key vulnerability that promotes incoherent signaling and pseudohypoxia. DHA is integral to mitochondrial cardiolipin and membrane quantum properties; replenishing it supports coherent electron flow and reduces baseline oxidative leakage in normal cells.
> Consult with your doctor or uncle jack.
> **Look out for the lifeboats**

I would heavily caution on advising on supplements. Dr Kruse has said always redox before detox. with supplementing with pills there is the risk of overwhelming the melanin in the body since all of the supplements contain heavy metals. In the forum there is data regarding melatonin causing more problems down the road for continues use (why advise melatonin?) and firmly avoiding adding cysteine to food, likely including NAC since there isn't a tightly controlled mechanism to stop the conversion to L-cysteine, hence increased cysteine levels, which results in the melanin being formed being pheomelanin (the one that creates ROS from photosensitization) instead of eumelanin which is what is needed to handle UV. with all supplements there is the deuterium bomb risk so better to avoid overall.

all steps should be consulted with her doctors not based off an AI analysis. this type of advice can and will cause more harm than good. Dr. Kruse's stance is clear on supplement gurus

# Thread: Alex's Journal: hyperhidrosis and dysautonomia

### Alex_fr — 2026-01-13T14:49:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal-hyperhidrosis-and-dysautonomia.32126/post-365260>

Hi everyone,

Starting this journal to document my improvments or lack thereof, as I get closer to the Equator and further away from nnEMF.
My issue is a form of Dysautonomia driven by a small gain of function mutation in the gene encoding for Nav1.7.
The results is a persistent Na+ leak in the neurons where Nav1.7 is expressed, resulting in hyperexcitability of these neurons.
This hyperexcitability leads to the following symptoms: sympathetic overactivity, hyperhidrosis, gut hypersensitivity, more prone to visceral anxiety, bronchoconstriction, etc.

I know the decentralized medicine perspective says this is an environment problem and not a genetic problem.
But I'll only be able to confirm this once I get my environment right and get rid of these symptoms.

Best,
Alex

### Alex_fr — 2026-01-27T21:29:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal-hyperhidrosis-and-dysautonomia.32126/post-365984>

Just received my results from biochemical Mitochondria testing by MitoSwab, a buccal mitochondria enzyme assay.
Not sure what Jack would think of these type of tests but my result clearly indicate my issue is a mitochondrial dysfunction:

\- citrate synthase (mitochondrial content): 60% of mean → low-normal

\- respiratory chain complex I (RC-I): 131% of mean → normal-high

\- respiratory chain complex II (RC-II): 54% of mean → low-normal

\- respiratory chain complex II + III (RC-II+III): 174% of mean → slightly elevated

\- respiratory chain complex IV (RC-IV): 355% of mean → massively elevated

Overall No mitochondrial deficiency with preserved electron entry (complex I & II) BUT extreme overactivity of complex IV relative to mitochondrial content shows mitochondria are over-driven, with a strong compensatory upregulation of the terminal oxygen-consuming step (complex IV).

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-28T16:56:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal-hyperhidrosis-and-dysautonomia.32126/post-366033>

**Relationship Between Hyperhidrosis and Dysautonomia**

Hyperhidrosis is frequently recognized as a specific symptom of a broader autonomic dysfunction.

In cases involving the upper neck:
**1\. Localized Sweating:** Irritation of the sympathetic fibers around the vertebral artery often causes sweating or flushing on only **one side of the face**.
**2\. Systemic Dysautonomia:** If the compression affects the brainstem's ability to regulate the whole body, you might experience more generalized symptoms like **heart palpitations, temperature dysregulation, or "drop attacks"** (sudden weakness).
3\. _**Other relationships to be explored are found below**_

**A. Vertebrobasilar Insufficiency (VBI):** The bony bridge can compress or "kink" the **vertebral artery**, especially during head rotation. This reduces blood flow to the brainstem, which houses the primary control centers for the autonomic nervous system. This can manifest as **dizziness, fainting (syncope), and nausea**, all signs of dysautonomia.

**B. Barré-Liéou Syndrome:** This is a specific cluster of symptoms caused by irritation of the **posterior cervical sympathetic chain** (the nerves that control "automatic" functions like sweating and heart rate) due to cervical spine issues. Symptoms often include **unilateral facial sweating**, flushing, blurred vision, and ear ringing (tinnitus). Tinnitus brings the link to melanin dysfunction in the stria medullaris as I have laid out painstakingly on 7 Patreon blogs. It signifies an nnEMF etiology to the Hyperhidrosis and dysautonomia.

**C. Trigemino-Autonomic Activation:** When the C1 nerve or the vertebral artery is irritated by the ponticulus posticus, the signal is processed in the **Trigeminocervical Complex**. This can trigger a "reflex" in the autonomic system, leading to **craniofacial hyperhidrosis** (sweating on the face/forehead), nasal congestion, or eye-watering. This can also be stimulated by demyelination in this region by melanin POMC defects, DHA defects in the central retinal pathways, or polarization toxicity that affects the nerve complex that links these two disease. Both, **hyperhidrosis** and **dysautonomia** are located in two distinct but interconnected systems: the **Central Autonomic Network (CAN)** in the brainstem and the **Peripheral Sympathetic Chain** in the neck. Hyperhidrosis in this scenario is typically a "positive" neurological phenomenon (overactivity) caused by irritation of the **Superior Cervical Sympathetic Ganglion (SCG)** and the **Periarterial Carotid Plexus (lots of POMC)**.

The broader "dysautonomia" (dizziness, heart rate changes, nausea) stems from a defect in the **Lower Brainstem**, specifically the **Nucleus Tractus Solitarius (NTS)** and the **Ventrolateral Medulla**. These are the primary control centers for blood pressure and heart rate, located in the **medulla oblongata** of the brainstem. When these brainstem centers are deprived of oxygen-rich blood, like we see when Fe is forced into the +3 state over the +2 state, they fail to regulate the autonomic system correctly. This is why nnEMF can cause this syndrome. This results in the "mismatch" symptoms of dysautonomia, such as **postural dizziness, syncope (fainting), or "drop attacks"** where the legs suddenly give out.

Because these symptoms are often positional, it is highly recommended to speak with a radiologist specialist about a [Digital Motion X-ray (DMX)](https://caringmedical.com/prolotherapy-news/ponticulus-posticus/) or a [CT Angiogram](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5623291/) to see how your vertebral artery behaves when you move your neck.

**Complex IV: Massively Elevated (355%)**
This is the most striking part of your result. In mitochondrial diagnostics, a massively elevated Complex IV often represents a **compensatory response**.

Implications? Your cells are trying to "overclock" the final step of the energy production line because earlier steps (like Complex II) are failing.

**Link to Symptoms:** Complex IV (Cytochrome c Oxidase) is the "oxygen sink." If it's hyperactive, you may be experiencing high levels of **oxidative stress** or "leaking" electrons. This is the nnEMF trigger you missed. This can lead to **central sensitization because** the brainstem and nerves (like the C1 and Trigeminal convergence) become hypersensitive, turning minor mechanical irritation in your neck motions into your two symptoms in the brainstem likely causing slight de melanation and demyelination as the blogs lay out.

**Complex II: Low-Normal (54%)**

Complex II is unique because it is the only part of the ETC that is also a direct member of the **Krebs Cycle** (Succinate Dehydrogenase). This links it to the TCA cycle. The blogs have a lot to say about this. TCA cycle is critical in myelin biology too. A deficit here suggests a bottleneck in the [TCA cycle](https://www.medlink.com/articles/complex-ii-deficiency). You aren't processing fuel efficiently at the entry point. My bet = deuterium blockade.
Complex II dysfunction is strongly associated with [autonomic nervous system instability](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6653231/). When energy supply to the **medulla (brainstem)** is inconsistent due to this bottleneck, it can cause the "glitches" you feel, like **hyperhidrosis** and heart rate variability.

**Citrate Synthase: Low-Normal (60%)**

Citrate Synthase is a marker of **mitochondrial density** (how many mitochondria you actually have).

**What it means:** You have a [lower-than-average population of mitochondria](https://www.religendx.com/neurology/mitoswab-test/) per cell based on this # = mitochondrial biogenesis failure.

**Link to Symptoms:** With fewer "engines" (mitochondria) and a bottleneck at Complex II, your body has very little **buffer** for stress. Physical triggers, like turning your head and slightly compressing the vertebral artery, nnEMF, lack of melanin, drain your remaining "battery" instantly, leading to dizziness or pain flare-ups.

**Complex I: Normal-High (131%)**

Complex I is where electrons from NADH enter the chain. Your "entry gate" for carbohydrates/proteins remains wide open, but since Complex II is low, the system is **unbalanced**. This often leads to a "backpressure" of electrons in ETC, which increases **Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS). ROS** production = aberrent UPE transformation = disease symptoms.

Your symptoms = The "Perfect nnEMF melanin Storm"
Your symptoms are likely a combination of **structural** and **metabolic** factors linked to melanin loss and mitochondrial chelation issues that cause alien UPE formation. DHA can shield a lot of this. So can CT, but melanin renovation and a strict circadian light diet are critical.

In the paradigm of **Quantum Biology and decentrlaized medicine**, your biochemical data and anatomy suggest a **decoupled light-water-magnetism system**. Your symptoms represent a classic "Perfect Storm" where **nnEMF (non-native electromagnetic fields)**and low solar yield meet a compromised mitochondrial engine.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-28T17:26:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal-hyperhidrosis-and-dysautonomia.32126/post-366037>

> [Alex\_fr said:](/goto/post?id=365279)
>
> What is the mechanism linking WNT10a to Hyperhidrosis ?

WNT10A is a critical ligand for the **canonical β-catenin pathway**, which regulates the proliferation and specialized differentiation of adult epithelial progenitors in sweat ducts. The most likely cause is atrophic skin from a lack of sun. Getting this after pregnancy means the baby stole your DHA, Vitamin D and you lost more surface and endogenous melanin due to your lack of sunlight. Another perfect storm that pushed your biology back to the GOE.

**Localized Over-secretion:** While mutations generally cause sweat duct regression, the localized hyperhidrosis on palms and soles is believed to stem from **variable compensatory mechanisms due to melanin loss or myelin loss in the nerve that innervate the sweat ducts.

Barrier Defects = Vitamin A problems = ROR/RXR blindness due to nnEMF abuse:** Loss of WNT10A causes a deficiency in **specialized keratins** (like KRT9), leading to a thinner or abnormal epidermal barrier. This can cause localized "leakiness" or a mismatch in the autonomic sudomotor response, where the body over-activates specific sweat glands to compensate for a failing skin barrier or heat regulation issues elsewhere.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-28T17:36:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal-hyperhidrosis-and-dysautonomia.32126/post-366039>

When human embryonic sweat gland germs develop normally, but the **ducts fail to extend or maintain themselves** postnatally due to decreased basal cell proliferation it is usually due to a lack of light. This problem is also present in psoriasis which is associated with basal skin problems.
This often creates a "dry skin" phenotype over most of the body **(hypohidrosis)**. The hyperhidrosis seen in **Odonto-onycho-dermal dysplasia** (OODD) is localized specifically to **ectodermal derivatives** (palms/soles) where the structural "glitch" in Wnt-active stem cells results in an uncontrolled or hyper-reactive secretory response from the remaining glands.

WNT10A is also expressed in the **central nervous system** (including the medulla and spinal cord) and has been linked to **neuropathic pain pathways** and synaptic function. **WNT10A:** Mutations cause [severe hypodontia (missing teeth)](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28589954/) and [malformed tooth roots](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7442305/).

**Oxalate is a potent inhibitor of Succinate Dehydrogenase (Complex II) because it competes with succinate for the active site.**

**The Mechanism:** Oxalate mimics the structure of dicarboxylic acids. When it binds to Complex II, it completely halts the Krebs cycle and electron entry at that point. This is why people with oxalate issues need AM sun to make the TCA kickstart again. They need to overdose on sunlight because their mitochondria are effectively chronically depolarized. This is why their skin is atrophic and lacks melanin and nerves lack myelin.

This situation worsens the existing bottlenecks cause by oxalate dumping because it forces **Complex IV (CCO)** to work even harder to maintain a membrane potential, which accelerates their "Alien UPEs" creation ( Popp's photon leakage) to cause all their symptoms. It can get so bad it can put you in bed like you are a cripple. Most centralized docs have zero clue about how Kreb's bicycle operates in these situations.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-28T17:40:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal-hyperhidrosis-and-dysautonomia.32126/post-366040>

Chelation of Matrix Metal Ions (Fe, Mn, Cu) = oxalate problem = why their mitochondria are demolished = no melanin controller.
Oxalate has an extreme affinity for positively charged metal ions, forming insoluble crystals or stable complexes that "strip" the matrix of its functional catalysts:

**Manganese (Mn) & SOD2:** Oxalate chelates **Mn2+**, the essential cofactor for **Manganese Superoxide Dismutase (SOD2)**. Without Mn, your mitochondria cannot neutralize the superoxide produced by your overactive Complex IV. This leads to massive **oxidative stress** in the Trigeminocervical Complex = cause massive pain.

**Iron (Fe) & Heme Groups:** Oxalate can interfere with the availability of iron for **Iron-Sulfur (Fe-S) clusters** found in Complexes I, II, and III. This physically degrades the "wiring" of your electron transport chain = why they are energy vampires for themselves and others

**Copper (Cu) & Complex IV:** While your Complex IV is high, it requires **Copper** to function. High oxalate levels can sequester copper, potentially leading to the production of dysfunctional, "broken" Complex IV enzymes that consume oxygen but produce heat and ROS instead of ATP. You also cannot make DDW at CCO = makes all tissues deserts.

Oxalates disrupt the **Melanin-Mitochondria link** by depleting the cell of **Molybdenum (Mo)** and **Copper**. These are required for tyrosinase (melanin production) and sulfite oxidase. This is why these people are PALE AS FUCK AND SICK AS FUCK.

A "Molybdenum dump" caused by oxalate toxicity prevents the detoxification of sulfites, which are neurotoxic and can trigger the hyperhidrosis and dysautonomia that many experience.

It is decnetralized medicine 101.

As for the xanthelasma- yeah I have trouble burning fat-i need to take honey all through the night. When I tried full carni my body shut down. Oxalate sits on enzymes in the fat pathway. ### econberkeley — 2026-01-13T19:18:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-sleep-pressure-after-getting-up-at-night.32128/post-365270>

I started grounding and looking towards the east during sunrise for 15-30 minutes about two weeks ago. I also started to go out when uvb available in houston,tx. I even laid naked about an hour when sun available and temperatures were mild for few days In the beginning,I felt great and had so much energy. However, starting 3 days ago , I started feeling totally awake when I get up at night ( I have an urological condition) and could not get back to sleep. I felt zero sleep pressure. Is this because I got so much cortisol in the morning from infrared light?

### Mr.AJ — 2026-01-13T20:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-sleep-pressure-after-getting-up-at-night.32128/post-365271>

There can be a case where during longer dark cycles it's normal to have a short wake up in the middle of the night and then fall back to sleep in 30 min to an hour.

But, it's probably the case that it's winter and light cycles are shorter/less intense which means you have less margin of safety after sunset. You live in Houston which means nnEMF and use of ALAN are likely the problem.

Test your environment. Only use candles after sunset. No screens. No LED's.

I'm telling you this from experience. Every time I try to use a screen post sunset even with IRIS on I get QE#47 bitch slapped.

edit: Probably need more sun time during the day also. = more melatonin production during the day.

### ToothDriller — 2026-01-13T21:15:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-sleep-pressure-after-getting-up-at-night.32128/post-365273>

So regarding your condition. Enlarged Prostate or frequency? I ask because if your are not staying in a proper sleep cycle your brain registers your bladder is full. Actually we never completely empty our bladders always some left. So if you are in a deep sleep say REM and you have a breathing issue that pulls you out and you wake up and say I gotta go. Its the breathing issue that created that signal
So my first thought is there more a sleep issue? Snoring, UARS, or sleep apnea. If so there are things I can suggest to help that are non-invasive.
Second I agree with AJ in that make sure your blue light is blocked at night.
More sun during the day means more melatonin as long as no sunglasses which based on your comments I doubt you wear them.
There are many factors so I would give it a few days and don't stress to much about it. Hit that sunset light to signal your body, minimize or eliminate food within a few hours of bedtime and make sure you block blue light.
Even lights from the street can signal the body to wake up so make sure nothing outside changed either.

### econberkeley — 2026-01-15T01:49:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-sleep-pressure-after-getting-up-at-night.32128/post-365350>

It looks like I was misunderstood. I am sorry if I was not clear enough. I have a urological/neurological condition called SRPE. It is a bitch. I get up several times every night because of it. I have that close to 3 years. There is no known cure for it.

Two things I added during the last 2 weeks. Firstly, I went out right after sunrise, grounded myself and look at the east for 15-30 minutes. Also, I also sunbathed fully naked several times during that time period at average uv level of 4. I felt great for the first 10 days or so. Then, I started being awake and not being able to going back to sleep after getting up the first time at night. Getting up during the night is normal for me, but I never experienced being fully awake after waking up. I felt like it was 10 is the morning. It was weird. Is this because I was exposed to too much infrared light in the morning that my cortizol level went up so much that it suppressed the melotonin? Does this make sense? Thank you for all the replies. Much appreciated.

One more thing. I have not make any changes with my indoor environment yet. I did not see any clear list of things to buy to make my indoor environment less blue light toxic. Please feel free to share if you have any.

### digitald3 — 2026-01-15T03:12:51+0200

>
> Two things I added during the last 2 weeks. Firstly, I went out right after sunrise, grounded myself and look at the east for 15-30 minutes. Also, I also sunbathed fully naked several times during that time period at average uv level of 4. I felt great for the first 10 days or so. Then, I started being awake and not being able to going back to sleep after getting up the first time at night. Getting up during the night is normal for me, but I never experienced being fully awake after waking up. I felt like it was 10 is the morning. It was weird. Is this because I was exposed to too much infrared light in the morning that my cortizol level went up so much that it suppressed the melotonin? Does this make sense? Thank you for all the replies. Much appreciated.
>
> One more thing. I have not make any changes with my indoor environment yet. I did not see any clear list of things to buy to make my indoor environment less blue light toxic. Please feel free to share if you have any.

after a quick search on SRPE, it appears there is a strong correlation between SRPE and Obstructive Sleep Apnea... have you had a sleep study to rule that in or out? Maybe an interim fix of the OSA will temporally help relieve some symptoms

as to the comment about SRPE having no known cure.. most of us are here because [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) has shown us time and time again that through decentralized approaches there is very little that can't be cured... except for a few true genetic diseases most things can be managed if not cured through a better environment and Dr. Jacks protocols.. the link to OSA is of interest with SRPE, and obviously your condition may not be related to OSA.. but if it is, then sure there are quite a few members here that have solved their OSA through decentralized ideas.. and from where I'm at right now, if they can cure their OSA by just going in the sun, I don't see why you can't solve SRPE as well.. OSA is a problem of too much oxygen rather than not enough.. when the mitochondria lose power they create more oxidative stress, and if there isn't enough melatonin on board to mop it up the oxidative stress creates more and more inflammation which causes the tissues to become larger and inflamed... When there is this much inflammation the heme proteins aren't not functioning.. like a blown circuit... cytochrome C oxidase or CCO is the main heme protein that is required to make water inside the cell... when that isn't working you can't make water and your mitochondria lose power and all sorts of bad things... most of them are related to the problem of OSA and most likely SRPE... The nnEMF in your home or sleeping area are of concern because the nnemf on its own will destroy the heme based proteins and destroy the ability to make water inside your cells... you must see the sunrise everyday, you need to get as much UV as you can which could be hard in January in North America and you have to block blue light at night and as [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) said, nnemf like from you wifi router or the electrical plug can destroy the water you must make to make your mitochondria work better... this is where you really need to test, either learn how to do it yourself or higher a building biologist... and if I was having SRPE 3-5 times a night I call today and try get a building biologist out tomorrow and not DIY it... Note, I'm just a dentist that got to Uncle Jack trying to solve for why just changing the anatomy didn't fix OSA.. and I found an entire world that is brand-new to me...

Edit to add that if OSA is a piece of the problem, you may have an obstruction somewhere that needs to be investigated.. Low or poor tongue posture often ends up in the back of the throat blocking off the airway.. A deviated septum can cause nasal breathing to be an issue... these are layers above the mitochondria but got that way because of low powered mitochondria. in the dental airway space, ie if you found an "airway aware" dentist you will most likely hear that the reason you can't get back to sleep is because of the obstructed airway and your body is tired of trying to breath so you just stay awake... Its a reasonable theory, but from first principles its more likely that too much blue light at night is depleting the melatonin stores that help get back to sleep..

### econberkeley — 2026-01-18T02:51:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-sleep-pressure-after-getting-up-at-night.32128/post-365513>

Thank you Doug.

1\. I still did not get any answer on this one. Did any of you were wild awake at night few days after starting daily sunrise and afternoon fully naked light exposure?
2\. My biological dentist told me that my airway is not wide enough so I went to sleep doctor and took the test. It turns out I have mild to moderate sleep apnea despite the fact that I don't snore. I used it for about 1 year and I was able to sleep longer. However, I stopped when I got tinnitus. Tinnitus made it hard to use cpap so I stopped 6 months ago. I recently listened to Jack Kruse about sleep apnea and he does not recommend cpap machines to treat it. He mentioned what it is not healthy to use it and it may cause cancer. Also, he mentioned that sleep apnea is not caused by narrow/obstructed airway, but it is a mitochondrial disease caused by lack of sun.

I am part of the SRPE FB group. Nobody in the group was able to solve this issue so far despite all their continuous efforts. I will continue to follow Jack's protocol and try to beat the odds.

By the way, my ENT found no deviated septum in me.

yes there are problems with cpap and centralized approaches to sleep apnea.. although staying alive is important too... if you had less SRPE while using it, then it makes sense that the two are connected. The cpap isn't the best answer but It can be useful at times.. the problem is the tinnitus.. and that's related to the apnea too!

what you are describing is what Dr Jacks last few blogs are trying to teach us... your heme based proteins need renovation... you must rebuild them before you can rebuild your melanin... the tinnitus is due to melanin loss in your ears... the apnea is there because there is no melanin inside of you as well.... to rebuild the heme proteins one needs lots and lots of morning am sunlight and nnemf modification.. with no matrix water things won't work well.

there's a guy on X with the handle [@helios\_brah](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29519/) and he might be here too.. I'm not sure.. he solved his apnea with the sun and then rented an air bnb and it all came back.. he is very sensitive to nnEMF.. and my guess is this is something for you to seriously investigate..

As Dr. Jack always says, you got sick in your current environment and since things like Apnea and tinnitus and most likely SRPE are environmental problems, you can't get well in the same environment you got sick in.. the question is can you actually stay in your home and hometown or do you need to head for a better sun environment..

### Anna Dunn — 2026-01-14T22:38:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c-diff-treatments.32135/post-365333>

I got c diff about 5 months ago after 3 antibiotics to treat a staph infection on my hand. It got treated with the antibiotic, dificid, but it came back at half force (diarrhoea restricted to daytime). I applied for a pill fecal transplant but in the end I decided not to do it because:
1) they would not tell me about the screening process used
2) they required me to take further antibiotics to clear the way, so to speak, to allow the bacteria a chance- i was concerned that if the pill fecal transplant didn’t work then the further antibiotics would make things worse. Also, I’m extremely reactive to antibiotics- in a dangerous way- it winds up my MCAS and therefore my microvascular angina and prevent me from sleeping more than an hr
3) I heard some do worse after fecal transplant

I used various probiotics and over the past 5 months my BMs improved steadily until about 2 weeks ago when I stupidly had added sugar to my electrolyte drink for 2 days and this set things back a bit.
At the moment my bowel is not good but not terrible. Loose BMs but not full on diarrhoea with occasional c diff smell. I’m wondering if I should
1) try the pill fecal transplant without first doing antibiotics
2) do nothing but expose my stomach to light and hope it returns to normal over the next 6 months
thank you

btw I can’t eat fermeneed foods as I have severe mast cell reactions to them
