> Holler if you're in Toronto or the GTA - let's grab a coffee!

Welcome Liam to Uncle Jack’s tribe of savages. When I first came across him and heard him use the words tribe and savages I was in the midst of incessantly researching for answers to help my 3 year old son with a speech delay and possible adhd/autism diagnosis in addition to my daughter’s and my own health issues. When I realized that everything available to overcome these issues were bandaids and not getting to the root of the issues let alone coming up with real solutions. The basic solution is learn to live with it or take pills. I said to myself one day, screw it, I’m going to find a way to cure him (and us) myself and when I find the solutions I’ll know it because it will make perfect sense. It became my new hobby, a delightful obsession, because I know what humans are capable of with sheer will and drive. When Dr Kruse said his tribe and called them savages that’s exactly how I felt. Like a savage foaming at the mouth for answers. I even looked up the word because meaning is important. Wild, merciless, unforgiving, fierce, extreme in strength or degree. That last one, extreme in strength or degree. To what degree are you willing to go for your health, your families health, for the truth. Extreme is the right answer. That’s the kind of people Uncle Jack wants around him. You don’t have to be a brain surgeon or a rocket scientist. You don’t have to have a title. You don’t have to be wealthy. You don’t even have to be the smartest guy in the room because even that guy didn’t know squat at one point in his life. What I’m getting at is that you have to have the drive and determination to find the answers you’re looking for and more importantly, using those answers to make the right decisions for yourself. Otherwise you’re wasting his time and yours. Now I can already tell you’re smart and your Dad too for knowing who Dr. Kruse is. Don’t worry if nobody you know has ever heard of him. In my experience even when you tell them, many aren’t willing to receive it. You’re in school but it sounds like listening to other people’s advice has put you on a career path that’s not exactly what your looking for. You’re pretty health conscious but are smoking weed all day. You have a little savings but you think it’s not enough to make moves. I believe you are at a crossroads my friend. You know that you don’t like the current state of things. You especially don’t like the state of your health. And you’re honest about it. Which means there’s hope for you. Now for the advice. Honestly I don’t even like calling it advice because as soon as someone says, my advice would be…”, or “If you want my advice…”, they usually have no place giving advice on that particular subject but proceed to anyway. Or worse their delivery is horrible and the person doesn’t get the message. See most people will start telling you all the things you already know but haven’t started doing. Maybe they’ll say change your major do what you want to do, or the first thing is you gotta stop smoking weed, stop hanging out with that crowd, or maybe even give you a job or venture idea but if you’re not thinking like a savage you won’t follow through. Dr Kruse mentioned he had a mentor that was tough and if he had an issue his mentor would say “so what” as if to say figure it out. Here we all get Dr Kruse as a mentor and he’s tough. He’s not going to hold your hand one, because he would be doing you a disservice, but two, because you have to take control of your destiny. His immense library of information is there for the taking. But you have to take the time to get his gift of time: your health. With your health the sky’s the limit. And just because you put in the time doesn’t mean it will be easy and the clock will still be ticking. I can give you suggestions but you have to want the change and be fiercely determined to make it happen, even through the brain fog. The more you read, listen, and learn you can implement changes that will incrementally make your circumstances better. It’s hard to know where to start but I listen to a lot of his interviews, many of them multiple times and some notes. The forum, of course, and his blogs and papers. I too have mental blocks such as focus issues, brain fog, retention problems but I don’t let that stop me. If I have to read something multiple times so be it. His earlier stuff I’m learning now is a good place to start. You could write down what changes you want to make in your life, then put them in order of significance, than write out what you need to do to get there. That makes it more real and achievable. For example if you want to stop smoking because maybe it’s causing or worsening your brain fog which then makes decision making hard, write out what you can do to implement that such as walking or running when you want to smoke or go to the library to read where you can’t smoke. Most importantly love yourself and know that your health and happiness is worth putting in the time. Things will start to fall in place and opportunities will arise in the most unexpected places. I’m rooting for you Liam!

This really put things into perspective! It's time to be a savage myself and put in the work.

I'll start by watching through some of the earlier interviews as you suggested. I really just need to be more militant with learning, and do my due diligence. Thank you for waking me up to this, and I wish you and your son all the best Jodie!

We have an H-mart close to my place, not sure if they sell budget seafood, but I will give it a try. The chain grocer near me is ridiculously expensive for the quality of seafood. Thanks a lot for the rec

I've been going on a couple of walks after meals for about 10-15 minutes while wearing as little clothing as possible (cotton sweater and jeans right now). The wind get me, but otherwise not too bad. We've been blessed with some direct sunlight recently which has been great.

Cheers!

Hi Liam! You're in the right place now, I'm so glad you connected with this community because there is a lot of support here and the more you read and integrate into your life, the better you can feel. One thing I wanted to ask you, how much do you ground? I know you're in the city, so it might be challenging to find a park or a space in nature where there is grass or clean dirt without electrical lines services underneath. I have found that when faced with cutting out behaviors, sometimes it is helpful to add in positive behaviors that can fill your bucket up so you can grow your capacity to be able to successfully let go of the superfluous. Try getting your bare feet or touching your hands to the ground daily, as much as you have time for. I'm also in Canada so I recognize that it is frozen and uncomfortable, but I would not suggest this to you if it were not worth the temporary pain! Keep going Liam, if you follow the light water magnetism wisdom of nature, she won't disappoint you.

Hi Andy! I have not been grounding very much at all. I have been walking in forests and hugging the trees, although I'm unsure if it's been as effective. I've seen some people boiling water and thawing the ground before grounding, which I am going to begin today. Do you know where I could find some info on testing the ground for power lines, etc?

I'm going hiking this weekend and will look for more outdoor activities to fill my day.

Thanks for your kind words of encouragement! It's so uplifting hearing from others in this crazy country.

### AndyShannStock — 2026-01-23T04:00:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-learn-more-from-all-the-savages-out-there-looking-for-advice.32165/post-365765>

> [LiamS said:](/goto/post?id=365747)
>
> Hi Andy! I have not been grounding very much at all. I have been walking in forests and hugging the trees, although I'm unsure if it's been as effective. I've seen some people boiling water and thawing the ground before grounding, which I am going to begin today. Do you know where I could find some info on testing the ground for power lines, etc?
>
> I'm going hiking this weekend and will look for more outdoor activities to fill my day.
>
> Thanks for your kind words of encouragement! It's so uplifting hearing from others in this crazy country.

I actually have no idea how to test for lines under the ground, but possibly typing it into the forum somewhere maybe someone would know the answer to that! Definitely when you're out in forests and in a more natural environment, kick off the boots pull off your socks and get your feet or hands onto the Earth. I usually melt a spot into the ground after a while with my body heat, but I don't see why you couldn't just pour some water onto the ground to make it easier! Good idea

### Anthony Smith — 2026-01-23T06:46:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-learn-more-from-all-the-savages-out-there-looking-for-advice.32165/post-365771>

Temperature has nothing to do with grounding - just comfort.
A good ground has NO nnEMF 60Hz (50Hz) electric field running through it.
So, the further away from buildings, industry, technology etc. you are the better off you will be.
The equipment to test the ground for these 50/60hz fields is not readily available.
A body voltage meter will show IF the surface you are standing on is grounded BUT not if that ground is “clean”.
In town, at your home look for utility lines . . . Avoid them.
IF you are in a city, good chance the ground is not “clean”.
Grounding in Nature is amazing/great . . . If you want to ground in Nature . . . Be in Nature.

# Thread: REDOX Rx co-evolved 3 billion years ago.  HOW and WHY?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-rx-co-evolved-3-billion-years-ago-how-and-why.32167/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-21T14:08:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-rx-co-evolved-3-billion-years-ago-how-and-why.32167/post-365686>

Melatonin and the leptin melanocortin pathways evolved simulataneously in the GOE and it appears melatonin evolution began 2.5 billion years ago due to the rapid rise of oxygen during the GOE. The stimulus for melanin was the resultant ROS and RNS from metabolism in chloroplasts first then in mitochondria later on as time evolved. While definitive molecular dates are difficult to establish for melanin as an ancient polymer, its presence in primitive photosynthetic bacteria suggests a lineage extending back at least 2 to 3 billion years, similar to melatonin's evolutionary timeline.

Melanin is found across nearly all domains of life, including bacteria, fungi, plants, and animals, suggesting it evolved as a fundamental chemical related to energy transformation of solar energy to useful energy for cellular machinery while offering a chemical protective molecule against environmental stressor. Energy transformation occurs via photosynthesis and radiosynthesis.

[https://patreon.com/posts/decentralized-91-147592281](https://t.co/Nt6QAIp4WU)
Evidence has now emerged to show that both mitochondria and chloroplasts may have the capacity to synthesize and metabolize melatonin. The activity of arylalkylamine N-acetyltransferase (AANAT), the reported rate-limiting enzyme in melatonin synthesis, has been identified in mitochondria, and high levels of melatonin have also been found in this organelle. From an evolutionary point of view, the precursor of mitochondria is the purple nonsulfur bacterium, particularly, Rhodospirillum rubrum, and chloroplasts are the descendents of cyanobacteria. These bacterial species were endosymbionts of host proto-eukaryotes and gradually transformed into cellular organelles, that is, mitochondria and chloroplasts, respectively, thereby giving rise to eukaryotic cells. Of special importance, both purple nonsulfur bacteria (R. rubrum) and cyanobacteria synthesize melatonin/melanin. The enzyme activities required for melatonin synthesis have also been detected in these primitive species. It has been my hypothesis that mitochondria and chloroplasts are the original sites of melatonin synthesis in the early stage of endosymbiotic organisms that led to the co evolution of melanin; this synthetic capacity was carried into host eukaryotes by the above-mentioned bacteria. Moreover, their melatonin biosynthetic capacities have been preserved during evolution.

In most, if not in all cells, mitochondria and chloroplasts may continue to be the primary sites of melatonin generation. Melatonin production in other cellular compartments may have derived from mitochondria and chloroplasts. **_On the basis of this idea, it is NOW also possible to explain why plants typically have higher melatonin levels than do animals._**

In plants, both chloroplasts and mitochondria synthesize melatonin synergistically, while animal cells contain only mitochondria. The high levels of melatonin produced by mitochondria and chloroplasts are used to protect these important cellular organelles against oxidative stress and preserve their physiological functions. This is what drive the co-evolution of melanin, in my opinion. The primary function of mitochondria in centralized biology is to generate bio-energy, ATP, with high efficiency. The primary function in mitochrondria in decentralized biology is ISC conversions to allow for coherence. This process of keeping free radicals in the triplet versus singlet state is done by mitochondria and was sculpted by melatonin, melanin, dopamine and GABA as evolution went on and became more complex.

This process in mitochondria that powered these transition is referred to as oxidative phosphorylation coupling. During this process, energy contained in the high-voltage electrons is released stepwise via an electron transport chain (ETC) and is used to pump protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane against a gradient. The energy built-up as a result of the proton gradient is then used to produce ATP. At the end of the ETC, the electrons are transferred to oxygen, and finally, oxygen is reduced to water. However, the ETC is not flawless, and some electrons invariably leak from the ETC to partially reduce oxygen- or nitrogen-related substances forming ROS and RNS, respectively.

The excessive production and accumulation of ROS and RNS in mitochondria eventually alter their function. This was the stimulus for the need for melanin evolution. It is estimated that via the cascade reaction, one melatonin molecule may scavenge up to 10 free radicals, which contrasts with the classic antioxidants because they typically detoxify one radical per molecule. Melatonin and melanin are synergistis in another unique way. Both compounds exhibit bidirectional recycling. It now appears both melanin and melatonin depend wholly on specific environmental conditions to explain their exact function in a cell. Moreover, both exhibit a concentration equilibrium.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This suggests that when antioxidants in the cytosol are exhausted by excessive cytosolic oxidative stress, the lipophilic antioxidants resident in other cellular compartments should provide antioxidative protection in the cytosol using melatonin as the bridge to recycle water-soluble antioxidants and vice versa. This should be classified as antioxidant network that controls the transformation of energy to mammalian UPE spectra. If the ROS and RNS are NOT neutralized in a timely manner by the actions of melatonin/melanin, the damage jeopardizes the functions of mitochondria via heteroplasmy and finally destroys these organelles.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-21T14:18:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-rx-co-evolved-3-billion-years-ago-how-and-why.32167/post-365687>

In addition to a direct interaction with ROS and RNS, several other protective mechanisms of melatonin in mitochondria and chloroplasts have been published.

Melatonin down-regulates free radical production via two separate pathways, a process referred to as free radical avoidance. One of these pathways is that melatonin accelerates electron flow through the ETC. Accelerating electron flow in the ETC reduces the electron leakage from the chain and avoids partially reducing oxygen and, thus, limiting the generation of ROS and RNS. To achieve this, melatonin promotes the activities of complex I and complex III.

These are the two main electron leakage sites in the ETC. For example, complex I specific inhibitor, rotenone, and complex III specific inhibitor, antimycin A, jeopardize electron flow in the ETC and result in large quantities of radicals being formed. If melanin is present even if melatonin is not this process can be handled by cells. If endogenous melanin is missing or destroyed this leads to tissue damage. The same is true with loss of mitochondrial melatonin production. As a result, excessive UPEs are released from the cell as a signal.

Melatonin supplementation to cells or organisms effectively preserves mitochondrial function and reduces oxidative injury induced by these electron flow inhibitors in vitro in labs. In vivo supplemntal melatonin leads to problems.

The second pathway is that melatonin mildly lowers the mitochondrial membrane potential. A high mitochondrial membrane potential also slows down the electron flow and results in electron leakage to generate free radicals; thus, a moderate down-regulation of the mitochondrial membrane potential significantly reduces free radical formation. Recall, melatonin lowers mitochondrial membrane potential under normal conditions. This process is associated with mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation uncoupling.

These two pathways explain how UPEs are varied amongst environmental changes. Melanin scavenging is the other mechanism to vary resusltant UPEs.

Jack, doesn’t this make Melatonin the molecular Shadow ?

Giving a Molecular Trinity:

Light (Melanin/POMC): UV stimulates POMC → α-MSH → melanin, establishing photonic phase and preventing ROS overload (GOE stimulus).

Shadow (Melatonin): Buffers excitations, but leptin overrides for precise accounting; darkness enables leptin-melanocortin reset.

Darkness (Oxygen/Mitochondria): Leptin/POMC ensure OXPHOS completion and reversibility; loss forces fermentation/autonomy (cancer as "Shadow failure").

Which then confirms your work on cancer - a chronic circadian and electromagnetic mismatch disrupting POMC/leptin → low α-MSH/melanin → coherence loss → orphaned cells defaulting to survival hallmarks, independent of genetics and diet.

Cancer = photobioelectric orphaning.

# Thread: Cold and Companionship
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-companionship.32171/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shanti — 2026-01-22T00:27:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-companionship.32171/post-365704>

Yesterday, January 20, I did 3 "floats" in California's cold Pacific Ocean. The sea life is prolific here in Malibu. I will swim out a bit and then just float with my head down for 20 minutes observing a sea of fish--huge schools of tiny fish, with clusters of larger fish below, along with the usual sitings of giant sea bass, leopard sharks, rays, halibut and sheepshead. Occasionally I will dive down deeper to see the lobsters and geribaldis hiding under the rock shelves.

When I go with my husband we do more vigorous swims. His presence stimulates higher levels of activation in me. He loves to workout. He says he needs to swim fast to build his body heat because he doesn't have the fat layer like I have. Its easy for me to keep pace with him because I float like an otter probably in part because of eating like a polar bear--starting with a filet of salmon (raw/sushi grade) just after sunrise. Before I started doing this the 30 minutes in the cold would stress me out. But now I digest for about an hour and then when we do our morning swims I am more relaxed. I feel as if I have an inner furnace running at a slow burn inside me and my husband can swim as long as he wants and I will be fine at his side.

The companionship is the best part. Every swim is an adventure and we try to find places that we can swim without the drag of swimwear and so the fascia can move unrestricted. We eat at different times of day but also I haven't missed a sunrise in 8 years. Then I couldn't digest fat but now my it seems my CCO furnace thrives on fat.

In the past he has typically missed the sunrise, skipped breakfast and stayed up later till around 11:00 winding down the day on the dopamine rush of the screen with a bowl of ice cream. But when I returned from Costa Rica with MRSA I just told him I needed a companion to live and I wanted it to be him. He carved out time for intimacy pre dawn and now the past 5 months he hasn't missed a sunrise either. So he is charging up in the morning with his dopamine now (plus endorphins, oxytocin...). He naturally is going to bed earlier, sometimes even with me. Maybe one day he'll even have breakfast with me.

But it's the companionship that is healing me. It's second to my light environment, albeit the high nnEMF/flouride/chem trail environment of California. My focus is on my patterns within this environment. (My choices: nature's rhythms in good environment without my companion and family or a avatar patterns in a challenging environment with my companion and family). Hopefully one day we will be able to spend more time together in the stronger red light of Costa Rica but for now his work is the here and I need to be with him. (I went off the chlorine dioxide and just keep it on hand in case of emergencies.)

For years now, whether I am in Costa Rica or here in Malibu I am outside all day, minimally clothed, unless it's too blustery or raining. In both places I have an outdoor area on the grass that measures low emfs on my meters. But with the typical clouds from the marine layer in Malibu it's much colder. Every day I feel more adapted to the cold. I love living outside and cringe when I have to go into altered light environments. Although our evening lights are incandescent, and I am hardly ever under blue light, I usually retire early after a few family games and good conversation. I sleep in a room with blackout curtains and an emf canopy, and drink deuterium depleted water. I'm thankful to be able to do this.

I urgently need help now as widowed, no family living alone and handicapped hardly able to move due to pain. My faith is in the Lord to help guide me...checking with my doctors and ChatGpt but I know Dr. Jack Kruse knows more than my doctors and believe he knows more than AI.
Anyone know any ideas or direction for me?

**Dr. Jack Kruse if you see this please reply.**

# Thread: What is the light story of evergreen
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-light-story-of-evergreen.32181/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### riksa — 2026-01-23T13:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-light-story-of-evergreen.32181/post-365777>

I was thinking about the role of evergreen bark/pine extracts
How it must have a light story, nature just so happens to make us eat and drink it when there is a lack of solar light. Light stress.

And I notice something interesting looking at the picture of the phenolic compounds in it.
They all have aromatic rings.

Currently I am unable to go beoynd that without taking mental leaps and shortcuts.
Whats the light story here?
UV-A chromophore, Triplet state, ROS quenching, thermogenesis? nnEMFs?

Will these help or 'buffer' our own aromatic L-aminoacids like L-tyrosine when Circularly polarized light enters the arena?
With the lack of skin melanin, will this (and things like astaxanthin/zeaxanthin from fish) protect us?
If it enhances thermogenesis, then it must enhance our POMC at least.
I am intuitively highly interested somehow for the nnEMF stress aspects. Analogous to Melanin in practice?

If nothing else, its a refreshing tea I make in snow melt.

# Thread: Avoiding Xray's and combating fractures the decentralised way
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoiding-xrays-and-combating-fractures-the-decentralised-way.32182/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrew Casey — 2026-01-23T14:06:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoiding-xrays-and-combating-fractures-the-decentralised-way.32182/post-365778>

3 days ago, my 3 Year old son tried to ride his bike with no hands and crashed at low speed. During the fall, it looked like his left arm extended back.

There is no pain in his fingers, shoulder or forearm and he can bend his fingers with no pain.

He has swelling just above the elbow and is in most pain when you touch the tricept, just above the elbow. There is no bruising. First night he didn’t sleep due to the pain, however has been sleeping better since, waking intermittently with pain.

From my own research, it could possibly be a: supracondylar humerus fracture.

**Current Treatment:** 1st & last hour of sunlight, along with UVA light to aid possible fracture repair. No cast or sling.

I have been avoiding going to the hospital, as I know they are going to insist on an xray.

Reaching out to the community for best ways to tackle this situation and best practice for combating fractures the decentralized way?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### jonathon1221 — 2026-01-23T22:23:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoiding-xrays-and-combating-fractures-the-decentralised-way.32182/post-365803>

idk man a broken bone healing wrong might be worse than a single momentary xray exposure.

he’ll recover from the xray, if his fracture slips cause you didnt get him in a cast he might lose function the rest of his life.

### Mat.I — 2026-01-24T23:39:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoiding-xrays-and-combating-fractures-the-decentralised-way.32182/post-365828>

I would get it checked given his age. Get the xray if needed just time it correctly if not urgent... the window for kids is narrower IMO. I would aim for no later than 2pm in.the early afternoon.. Adults have a wider window.. I could be wrong though..

### Andrew Casey — 2026-01-25T01:13:04+0200

# Thread: The key principles of mTOR
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-key-principles-of-mtor.32184/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-23T18:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-key-principles-of-mtor.32184/post-365790>

HOW DOES mTOR fit into the decentralized thesis?

[https://x.com/LightMeAway/status/2014725454356160609](https://x.com/LightMeAway/status/2014725454356160609)

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-23T18:06:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-key-principles-of-mtor.32184/post-365791>

The Fountain of YOUTH

This is a podcast I usually
don't talk about, and it’s hard to find because I find the interviewer is not to be trusted. In this video, he fills many of gaps left by other podcasts; it’s like finally putting the puzzle together. Those kind of info Jack shares here is something people like:
[@hubermanlab](https://x.com/hubermanlab)

[@PeterAttiaMD](https://x.com/PeterAttiaMD)

[@foundmyfitness](https://x.com/foundmyfitness)

[@davidasinclair](https://x.com/davidasinclair)
and others should watch before they start preaching about longevity. Why should we listen to any of these so-called longevity experts when, after this video , it becomes painfully clear that the mTOR narrative has been exploited the wrong way { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Either out of ignorance (which I honestly doubt) or because telling the truth would interfere with:

profit

sponsorships

Big Pharma interests that continue to drain humanity dry

… If they’re genuinely ignorant on this subject, then they should start with this podcast. I don’t mean watch it; I mean study it. There’re no more excuses.

The video is over 2 hours long and exceptional Full video

[https://youtu.be/cy8cByk8H00?si=tUIEHCH563ei5F-O](https://t.co/xp03PWyfSf)

After watching this, I hope people understand that most of modern nutritional literature should be thrown in the trash, because many lab studies were done on mammals that: are not like us raised and tested in artificial blue-light environments surrounded by electromagnetic radiation stripped of the epigenetic toolbox that humans uniquely possess… If you still believe in the mTOR-longevity narrative, prepare to be mind-blown and say goodbye to resveratrol; another product aggressively pushed by unethical figures who created more harm than good. Everybody talks about mTOR like it’s the master switch of life and no one can explain why… So instead of chase mTOR, we all should chase mitochondria Because mTOR does not behave the same way in: muscle as it does in liver kidney brain or gut. Not even close. And no one can actually explain why that difference exists.

Yet we’re told, with total confidence , that “optimizing” mTOR is the path to longevity…If mTOR were really the fountain of youth, calorie restriction should work flawlessly in humans. But it doesn’t. It works beautifully in: worms flies rodents… and then quietly breaks when it reaches humans So what makes humans different? Jack clearly said: Humans amplified two biological semiconductors that other mammals barely use: DHA Melanin It means calorie restriction doesn’t work for us the same way it does in other mammals.

For us, what truly matters is:

Light Environment

Mitochondrial redox state It means mTOR would be regulated by: photonic input, oxygen tension, and electron availability. In other words: by physics not macros Here’s where it gets uncomfortable. mTOR absorbs light around 380 nanometers. That wavelength determines whether a cell shifts “anabolic or catabolic” If oxygen tension inside the cell changes, the emitted light spectrum shifts from vacuum ultraviolet toward infrared.

That means the signaling frequency itself changes. No one actually knows what that frequency shift does downstream. Then add phosphorus to the picture because Phosphorus is part of ATP. When it moves, it pulls electrons out of proteins. When electrons are removed, charge changes. When charge changes, semiconductor behavior changes. So the real question is: where do the replacement electrons come from?

The answer:

Melanin

Melanin splits water, releases electrons, absorbs the entire electromagnetic spectrum, including reactive oxygen and nitrogen species, and converts that chaos into usable metabolic energy. In other words, it doesn’t just make energy. It stores it in water so biology can do work. Then mTOR wouldn’t be the fountain of youth at all It would just be a downstream sensor responding to mitochondrial conditions shaped by light, water, magnetism, and electrons.

Which brings us to the uncomfortable conclusion.

The fountain of youth wouldn’t be mTOR It would be mitochondria.

# Thread: High Altitude living
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-altitude-living.32185/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alex_fr — 2026-01-24T19:06:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-altitude-living.32185/post-365822>

Currently looking at places like the Kenyan/Tanzanian highlands and high-altitude Canary Islands volcanos

Living in altitude = more UV
But it also means chronic hypoxia = higher mitochondrial stress.

My question is what makes altitude stress hormetic vs harmful ?
Is there an altitude under which the stress is positive?

Would be grateful if someone can help my thinking here.

### Anthony Smith — 2026-01-25T02:12:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-altitude-living.32185/post-365835>

In my opinion the best UV is the UV you can “enjoy” . . . Ie be out in.
There are lots of places with high UV but 120ºF sucks.
I like Kenya, Nairobi (which is 80 miles from the equator and sits at 5,800+/- ft above sea level) has one of the best Italian restaurants in the world (or did back in the day) with great fresh sea food flown in every day from Mombasa.
Had my 21st birthday there.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-25T23:56:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-altitude-living.32185/post-365864>

Living in altitude = more UV
But it also means chronic hypoxia = higher mitochondrial stress.

My question is what makes altitude stress hormetic vs harmful ?
Is there an altitude under which the stress is positive?

Listen to the podcast i did with Owen Sheasby.

### Alex_fr — 2026-01-29T12:04:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-altitude-living.32185/post-366084>

Thanks Jack, this is a great podcast.
So my current conclusion is that living in highland Kenya (Equator UV + 2km altitude) is not a good idea for someone with poor mitochondrial function.

Once one renovates his melanin and regain world-class mitochondrial function (and can thus tolerate the hypoxia), returning where we all come from, East Africa highlands, might be the ultimate place to live with nature with ultra high year round UV + cold nights.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-29T15:46:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-altitude-living.32185/post-366089>

No I would not say that. I think you need to renovate your heme proteins first up there with AM and PM light then hit mid day light after a few months.

# Thread: WiFi..snakes in the grass
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-snakes-in-the-grass.32187/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JenniferM — 2026-01-25T21:41:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-snakes-in-the-grass.32187/post-365844>

About 4 yrs ago we cleansed our house and covered the elec meter using the Safe and Sound Pro II. Everything was cool inside and out, reading .5-1.5 and sometimes 2.x. Just on curiosity I took the meter outside in the area where I get sun. Well, All be shit. It was reading levels between 10-21. My first thought was the neighbors upgraded their stuff or the cell tower 1 1/2 m away is getting thru due to the naked trees. But no no, the dang gas company installed a wireless meter so they don't have to come out and check any more (just like the elec co. did.) No warning, no permission. Now we have to deal with caging it. These companies are sneaky snakes in the grass. I have been getting sun in a 10-21 read, smh.

### Jacks — 2026-01-25T22:18:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-snakes-in-the-grass.32187/post-365845>

You have to keep measuring as things are always changing.

I just saw someone replacing the cap on a fire hydrant at the end of our block. I asked him what he was doing and he said replacing the cap with one that sends a signal to communicate when there is a leak. The idea is good for them, I suppose, as there are many leaks that fail to get repaired in a timely fashion, but it is just another RF signal in the neighborhood that wasn't there before. And there are many fire hydrants spread through the neighborhood. It's only going to get worse.

### Mat.I — 2026-01-25T22:44:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-snakes-in-the-grass.32187/post-365849>

Always keep measuring (never assume). My internal rf was/still is pretty much 0 and yard was not far of it... However, the yard levels have slightly increased over the last 6 months or so.due to tower upgrades and neighbours upgrading wifi etc..

### Mat.I — 2026-01-25T22:45:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-snakes-in-the-grass.32187/post-365850>

I will do quick spot checks every week or.so only takes a few minutes

### JenniferM — 2026-01-25T23:03:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-snakes-in-the-grass.32187/post-365855>

Thank you both for your input/advice. We are very rural and were not expecting it at all. We will be keeping a tighter check from now on. I feel sad, deflated and mad all at the same time.

### JenniferM — 2026-01-26T02:32:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-snakes-in-the-grass.32187/post-365873>

Mat, I got curious after you read 0. My husband and I just read each other and we both read about the same at .632


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Mattress recommendations? Magnetic field in mine. Geovital mattress any good?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mattress-recommendations-magnetic-field-in-mine-geovital-mattress-any-good.32190/>

### Anna Dunn — 2026-01-25T23:56:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mattress-recommendations-magnetic-field-in-mine-geovital-mattress-any-good.32190/post-365865>

I just had a building biologist find a static magnetic field in my sprung mattress. Is this common? He has recommended a geovital mattress. Does anyone have one? It's foam. I have bad mcas - worried it will smell or offgas.

### Mat.I — 2026-01-26T09:38:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mattress-recommendations-magnetic-field-in-mine-geovital-mattress-any-good.32190/post-365894>

> I just had a building biologist find a static magnetic field in my sprung mattress. Is this common? He has recommended a geovital mattress. Does anyone have one? It's foam. I have bad mcas - worried it will smell or offgas.

### Adamo — 2026-01-26T14:58:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mattress-recommendations-magnetic-field-in-mine-geovital-mattress-any-good.32190/post-365897>

Go for something that is all latex (real rubber). Cotton + Wool + rubber if you want something all natural, no-off gassing and no concern of coils.
Most of the time you will find them under "Organic Mattresses" because that is how the industry marketed them. They can be organic, but the point is that they are not using petro-based foams.

### Anna Dunn — 2026-01-27T23:41:48+0200

Idk to both. The BB is going to send a report soon. He showed me the massive change in static emf in one corner of the mattress. I got him to rotate the mattress to see if it was the concrete slab but it was the mattress. He said the measurements were outside of what BB field considered safe.

### Anna Dunn — 2026-01-27T23:44:42+0200

Ty. A while back I got samples of latex and wool and found they smelled . I have a very sensitive nose due to mcas I think. The geovital website video says they found the most common allergin to people in their sleeping environments was latex. They established that via muscle testing which Im not sure is valid or not? So I'm confused as to what to buy.

# Thread: BAD MITOCHRONDRIAL SCIENCE IDEAS INSPIRED BY FIAT:  3 MELANINs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-mitochrondrial-science-ideas-inspired-by-fiat-3-melanins.32197/unread>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-26T21:41:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-mitochrondrial-science-ideas-inspired-by-fiat-3-melanins.32197/post-365927>

Here is another centralized science PhD failure for a fiat idea being done to profit without thinking about the biophysics invovled.

Why is a bad idea?

This is a very dangerous intervention strategy. The process of metal chelation is quantized because the cysteine switch is embedded in melanins.
Humans use three types: Pheomelanin, Eumelanin, and neuromelanin.

Cysteine acts as a bio-optical switch not a biochemical "switch" that diverts the pathway from the default production of eumelanin (black-brown) to pheomelanin (red-yellow).

THE BIO-OPTICAL SWITCH: cysteine

The process begins when the enzyme tyrosinase oxidizes tyrosine into dopaquinone (DQ), a highly reactive intermediate. Tyrosine absorbs light as an aromatic amino acid. (see slide below)

In the absence of cysteine: DQ undergoes spontaneous cyclization and rearrangement to form eumelanin.

In the presence of cysteine: Cysteine reacts immediately with DQ to form cysteinyl-DOPA. These conjugates are then oxidized and polymerized into pheomelanin.

Cysteine must be present above a certain threshold (approximately 0.13 µM) for pheomelanin synthesis to occur. Because DQ has a high affinity for thiols (like cysteine), the cell will preferentially produce pheomelanin until its internal cysteine stores are depleted. Once cysteine levels drop below the threshold, the pathway "reverts" to eumelanin production.

**_Cysteine's role reflects a sophisticated evolutionary trade-off in Primates:_**

Photoprotection vs. Photosensitivity: Eumelanin (no cysteine) is highly effective at absorbing UV radiation and protecting DNA. In contrast, pheomelanin (cysteine-rich) can be photosensitizing, potentially generating reactive oxygen species (ROS) when exposed to UV light.

Metal Interaction: Cysteine-rich pheomelanin has a high affinity for binding metals, which evolved as a mechanism for sequestering iron and copper metals for regulating metal ion balance in specific organelles = mitochondrial (SODs) and Fe that makes the cyctochromes which ultimately makes UPEs. This directly affects tissue physiology.

Metabolic Detoxification: Some researchers hypothesize that pheomelanin synthesis evolved as a "sink" to remove excess, potentially toxic levels of cysteine from the body, using pigmentation as a metabolic waste-disposal system. This is why adding cysteine to foods is UNWISE. It is being done to make a centralized business for people to profit and no forethought was put into the idea or the paper linked below.

Pheomelanin (the cysteine-containing component) is often cited as having a higher binding capacity for certain transition metals like iron and copper compared to eumelanin. This means it releases them faster too.

Eumelanin is better optimized for sequestration stability and long-term neuroprotection.

Neuromelanin is the ultimate optimized form because it uses both: cysteine-driven pheomelanin to grab the metals and eumelanin to lock them away based on the UPE light signals released from mitochondria. If the UPE is not stochastically yoked to the biophoton signature by the organell, you can destroy a tissues redox power and this leads to desertification of the tissue by CCO destruction = no metabolic water production is created need to surround all the biomolecules in this tissue to operate normally.

**CITE**

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0308814626003146](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0308814626003146)

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-26T21:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-mitochrondrial-science-ideas-inspired-by-fiat-3-melanins.32197/post-365928>

These slides add a critical layer of **redox regulation** to the melanin story, explaining how the cell tightly controls the availability and state of the cysteine involved in pigmentation, metal chelation, and neuron protection via mitochondria SODs and cytochromes proteins.

How the Slides Impacts the Story

-   **Cysteine Availability is Regulated:** The slide highlights that only reversibly oxidized forms of cysteine (Cys) operate in redox signaling. The critical takeaway is that the cell actively recycles cysteine through complex systems (thioredoxin (TRX) and glutaredoxin (GRX) systems) powered by **NADPH**. This mechanism ensures a stable supply of reduced cysteine (the donor -SH form) needed for pheomelanin synthesis.
-   **Glutathione (GSH) as the Cysteine Reservoir:** The slide shows that the GRX system works closely with **glutathione (GSH)**. GSH can form an S-glutathionylation adduct with oxidized cysteine derivatives (-SOH). This process is vital for protecting cysteine from irreversible oxidation, effectively acting as a storage and protection mechanism for the crucial thiol needed for pheomelanin production.
-   **A "Switch" Regulated by Redox Balance:** The slide provides the cellular machinery that controls the "cysteine availability" mentioned previously. The balance between the oxidized and reduced forms of these antioxidant systems (TRX/TRX reductase and GSH/GSH reductase) is directly linked to whether the cell favors eumelanin or pheomelanin formation. High GR activity, for instance, is associated with pheomelanin formation.
-   **Link to Oxidative Stress:** The processes described in the slide (reversal of Cys oxidation, NADPH dependence) are core antioxidant functions. This context reinforces that melanin synthesis, particularly the shift to pheomelanin, is intricately linked to managing **oxidative stress** in melanocytes. This balance is crucial for neuroprotection, where neuromelanin needs to safely sequester pro-oxidant metals. Sunlight quantizes the process of how skin melanocytes inform endogenous neuromelanin's inside of us which migrated along neural crest morphologic pathways to optical dictate how to operate their mitochondria.

### PIAZ — 2026-01-26T21:57:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-mitochrondrial-science-ideas-inspired-by-fiat-3-melanins.32197/post-365930>

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-26T22:42:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-mitochrondrial-science-ideas-inspired-by-fiat-3-melanins.32197/post-365932>

Melanin’s evolution reflects a sophisticated adaptation to light and metal interactions. Its synthesis involves tyrosine oxidation by tyrosinase, producing eumelanin (black-brown) or pheomelanin (red-yellow) based on cysteine availability. The redox equation can be simplified as:

**Melanin + h_ν_ light + Fe^3+ ----> Melanin-Fe^2+ + ROS (e.g., O₂⁻, H₂O₂)**

Here, light energy (hν) drives electron transfer, with iron acting as a cofactor. Copper also plays a role here in cardiolipin and in SOD biology. In mitochondria, this process supports ATP synthesis by maintaining proton gradients, but excessive oxidation (e.g., from blue light or lipid nanoparticles) leads to heteroplasmy, via mitochondrial DNA mutations due to altered tissue optics and water content loss with age. This changes UPE signaling and this alters tissue phenotype.

In the Archean melanin formed from light and phenolics in the dirty oceans 4.3-4.6 billion years ago.

Melanin evolved endogenously because the interiors of our tissues needed to control Iron, Copper, Manganese, and Molybdenum to control the trillions of mitochondria in cells as organisms became complex post Cambrian event.

How does copper plays a role in mitochondrial function?

Mitochondria rely on copper for ~10–20% of their proteome, concentrated in the inner membrane (IMM) and matrix. It's imported via transporters like SLC25A3 and assembled by chaperones (e.g., COX17, SCO1/2) into enzymes. Diurnally, copper levels fluctuate: higher daytime uptake (light-driven via opsins/melanin) supports activity, lower at night (melatonin-mediated) favors repair. Deficiency or excess disrupts this, causing "copper allergy" akin to my "Great Oxygen Allergy" analogy where excess O2 becomes toxic without copper-balanced ROS control. ROS is a need co factor to make UPEs from mitochondria for signaling.

-   Cytochrome c Oxidase (CCO, Complex IV): Copper is indispensable in CCO, the ETC's terminal enzyme, with two sites: CuA (binuclear, electron acceptor from cytochrome c) and CuB (with heme a3, O2 reduction center).
-   CCO's reaction: 4e⁻ + 4H⁺ + O₂ → 2H₂O + energy (pumped as proton gradient for ATP).
    Copper toggles between Cu⁺ (reduced) and Cu²⁺ (oxidized), facilitating electron transfer and proton pumping. Reversible: In diurnal highs (daytime UVA/red light via OPN5/OPN3), Cu²⁺ → Cu⁺ accelerates O2 reduction in cells, boosting ATP (~36 per glucose) and water production (matrix rehydration for coherence). At night, melatonin inhibits Complex I, reversing flow slightly for repair and copper's redox poise maintains low ROS ( and low UPEs as a result), enabling UPE fidelity for mtDNA photorepair.
-   First-principles fit: Copper's d-orbitals enable quantum tunneling of electrons (via spin coherence), enhancing efficiency in noisy biology. Blue light/nnEMF oxidizes copper (Cu⁺ → Cu²⁺ irreversibly), stalling CCO, causing ROS buildup, heteroplasmy (mtDNA mutations from oxidized bases), and reduced CSF water coherence (dehydration with age).
-   Superoxide Dismutase (SOD) Biology: Copper-zinc SOD1 (cytosolic/intermembrane) and SOD3 (extracellular) use copper for ROS detoxification:
    2O₂⁻ + 2H⁺ → H₂O₂ + O₂ (Cu²⁺ reduced to Cu⁺, then reoxidized).
    Reversible and diurnal: Daytime (high O2/ROS from activity) ramps copper-SOD to quench superoxide, preventing UPE chaos; nighttime (low O2) reverses for signaling (mild ROS as UPE messengers for repair). Cardiolipin (CL, IMM phospholipid) stabilizes SOD1 near CCO—copper and prevents CL peroxidation, maintaining IMM's 30-million-volt field for proton gradients. Without copper, CL oxidizes (e.g., from lipid nanoparticles disrupting transport), causing heteroplasmy and altered tissue optics (scattering UPEs, reducing coherence).
-   Thesis link: Copper in SOD ties to melanin/melatonin creation by solar light: melanin absorbs UVA to reduce ROS load on copper-SOD, while melatonin (95% mitochondrial) donates electrons to Cu²⁺, reversing oxidation for coherence which prevents "energy leaks" as excessive UPEs.

2\. \*\*Copper in Diurnal Life: Light-Dark Cycles as Regulators
Diurnal rhythms evolved ~3.5 bya in cyanobacteria for light/O2 anticipation to modulate copper homeostasis via opsins, heme, and microbiome. Morning UVA (via OPN5) peaks copper uptake (via CTR1 transporter), optimizing daytime metabolism; evening red/IR (via CCO) releases stored copper for repair.

-   Daytime (High Demand/Wakefulness): UVA/blue light (320–480 nm via OPN5/OPN4) activates hypothalamic leptin-melanocortin signaling, boosting copper import to mitochondria for CCO/SOD. This supports exothermic ETC (net energy release as ATP/heat), with copper toggling to handle high O2/ROS from activity. Red-dominant solar spectra (via heme absorption) enhances this, preventing Warburg shifts where copper in CL maintains proton gradients for efficient ATP.
-   Nighttime (Repair/Coherence): Melatonin peaks, reversing copper-SOD for mild ROS/UPE signaling, enabling quantum entanglement/tunneling in quiet mitochondria (low noise). Copper's role in reversible CCO allows "dipping into the quantum gas tank"—modulating UPE spectra (red/IR emissions) for mtDNA photorepair and water rehydration (coherence via EZ expansion at lower temp).
-   Evolutionary Why: GOE (2.4 bya) pressured copper's redox reversibility for diurnal adaptation: daytime O2 efficiency (exothermic ATP), nighttime repair (endergonic via UPE). The microbiome should be thought of as "extended genome" (from 2.4 bya) which acts to buffers this process, with viral HERVs (~2 bya) "marketing" copper-homeostasis genes for coherence. Modern mismatches (nnEMF/blue) irreversibly oxidize copper, causing heteroplasmy (mtDNA mutations from ROS), dehydrated matrix (lost coherence), altered UPE (scattered signaling), and phenotypic shifts (e.g., inflammation, aging).
-   While Fe and Cu are pivotal for high-energy electron transport and cardiolipin biology, they both participate in **Fenton chemistry**, which can produce the highly toxic hydroxyl radical (•OH) and lead to massive UPE production. This is not good for entropy control.
-   **The Mn Shield:** Manganese is redox-active but does **not** participate in Fenton reactions under physiological conditions. It provides a way for cells to combat oxidative damage, specifically through **Mn-SOD (SOD2), which functions** without the deleterious side effects of generating hydroxyl radicals.
-   _As a result of this, Mn-SOD is the only superoxide dismutase located exclusively in the mitochondrial matrix to limit UPE production from excessive ROS._ During wakefulness, high energy demand increases superoxide production at cytochrome 1. Mn-SOD's rapid tunneling prevents this "noise" from destroying the mtDNA.
-   **Leptin Connection:** Proper Mn-SOD function ensures that the mitochondrial redox signal (H2O2) correctly informs the hypothalamus of the energy status. Disruptions in Mn-SOD, often caused by **blue light** or **nnEMF,** blind the leptin receptor by altering the quantum environment of the matrix, leading to the "leptin resistance" seen in chronic inflammation and aging.

    Evolution crafted a striking absence of **DHA (Docosahexaenoic Acid)** on the IMM 600 million years ago which is the one membrane in eukaryotic mammals where it is not installed. **Why did she select Manganese for the IMM?** While DHA's 22 carbons and six double bonds are perfect for fast electron/photon signaling in the brain and retina, they are highly susceptible to lipid peroxidation. Manganese, unlike iron, does not participate in **Fenton chemistry**, meaning it can handle massive reactive oxygen species (ROS) flux without generating the devastating hydroxyl radical.
    **Evolution created a "DHA-Free" Zone:** The IMM must maintain strict "dielectric" properties to support the proton gradient and water coherence. By using **manganese** as the central redox metal instead of high-concentration DHA-coupled lipid systems, the IMM minimizes entropy at the very site where electron tunneling is most intense in all complex life.

    Manganese's D shell electrons allow for high speed, reversible redox toggling between Mn2+ and Mn3+
    -   **PCET (Proton-Coupled Electron Transfer):** Mn-SOD operates at "diffusion-limiting" rates (essentially as fast as molecules can move), utilizing PCET to convert superoxide into hydrogen peroxide.
    -   **Signaling through H2O2:** In my thesis, the H2O2 produced is not just waste. It is a signaling molecule (a "second messenger") that diffuses out of the matrix to help optically tune the **leptin-melanocortin** pathway. Free radicals, like H2O2 create UPEs. Popp and Van Wijk explained this to us in their life long work. Without oxygen and ROS no UPEs are possible. This links mitochondrial flux directly to the brain's metabolic set point.
    -   Evolution chose Manganese for the IMM because it acts as a **"shield" against Iron**. While Iron is essential for the Electron Transport Chain (ETC), an excess of Iron in the matrix would cause catastrophic oxidative damage. Mn-SOD essentially acts as a buffer that allows the ETC to operate at maximum quantum efficiency while keeping the "Fenton fire" of iron in check.
    -   Melanin’s evolution is deeply tied to its role as a reservoir and chelator for transition metals, including Fe, Cu, Mn, and Mo.

    -   Melanin sequestering redox-active metal ions prevents their "stochastic" (uncontrolled) release, which would otherwise drive mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) mutations and heteroplasmy.
        Manganese-chelated melanin has been shown to have unique **photothermal properties**, increasing temperature efficiently under specific light wavelengths. This fits my framework of melanin as an antenna that interacts with light (hν) to modulate mitochondrial physics. While melanin protects by sequestering metals, excessive accumulation of metals like Manganese in neuromelanin-rich areas (e.g., the substantia nigra) can eventually lead to increased DNA damage if the system is overwhelmed.
        Mn-SOD is not just an enzyme; it interacts directly with **mtDNA** as part of the nucleoid complex to protect the genetic code from UV-induced damage and oxidative stress. One must not forget the most biophotons are ultraweak UV UPEs. Mn-SOD2 is part of this protection scheme.
    -   By rapidly converting superoxide to hydrogen peroxide, without generating hydroxyl radicals, Mn-SOD maintains the "low noise" environment required for water coherence in the matrix.
        -   **Evolutionary Adaption:** Ancestral SODs were "cambialistic," meaning they could use either Fe or Mn. The evolution of **Manganese-specific SOD2** in eukaryotes represents a shift toward higher-precision quantum signaling, where the potential toxicity of Iron was deliberately excluded from the inner sanctum of the mitochondrial matrix to protect the information integrity of the DNA.

3\. The Energy Source: ROS as the Photon Pump
Without oxygen and the resulting ROS (such as superoxide and H2O2 there is no fuel to transform energy into the biophoton field cells use for optical signaling.

-   **Oxidative Origin:** UPEs originate from the relaxation of electronically excited species formed during the oxidative metabolism of lipids, proteins, and DNA by ROS.
-   **The H2O2 Relay:** Hydrogen peroxide acts as a critical intermediate. When ROS react with biomolecules, they create unstable high-energy intermediates like **1,2-dioxetanes**, which then decompose to release photons.
-   **Proportionality:** The intensity of UPE is directly tied to ROS levels. Stressed or damaged cells show significantly higher UPE because they are generating more "reactive noise" that must be converted into light or heat

4\. Popp and Van Wijk’s Contribution to My Framework

Their work provides the physical basis for my"mitochondria as sensors" model:

-   **Information Carriers:** Popp proposed that biophotons are produced in a **coherent field,** meaning they are not just random flashes but a structured "cloud of light" that regulates cellular processes.
-   **Coherence vs. Chaos:** In a healthy state, the UPE signal is low and coherent. When this coherence is lost (due to age, blue light, or nnEMF), the system becomes "noisy," leading to the high-intensity, chaotic photon bursts associated with inflammation and disease.
-   **Mitochondrial Coupling:** Van Wijk identified mitochondria as the primary source of these emissions, effectively linking mitochondrial health directly to the "optical" output of the cell.

5\. **\*\*Implications for My Thesis: Decentralized Quantum Sensing**
Copper exemplifies this decentralized framework: it is not not a genomic pawn but a quantum cofactor (d-orbitals for tunneling) responding to light's pressures which vary diurnally to create the decentralized field in cells. During daytime they exhibit an exothermic "burn" for work, and during nighttime they remain fully reversible for coherence/repair. Lipid nanoparticles disrupt this by oxidizing CL/copper, stalling CCO, and causing UPE chaos, exacerbating heteroplasmy and tissue phenotype changes (obesity as "inflammatory brain disorder"). From first principles: copper minimizes entropy by reversible charge toggling (Gauss's Law in mitochondria), tying light's quanta to microbiome "co-ecology" for fractal adaptation.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-27T04:52:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-mitochrondrial-science-ideas-inspired-by-fiat-3-melanins.32197/post-365962>

4\. In my decentralized framework, **Molybdenum (Mo)** acts as the critical evolutionary "bridge loan" and modern-day "redox capacitor," essential for managing high-nitrogen and sulfur biochemistries while enabling the precise membrane depolarization dynamics that underpin quantum information processing in mitochondria. Molybdenum fits into this evolutionary story by providing a unique high-coordination chemistry that manages entropy in the circulatory system and links environmental nitrogen cycles directly to mitochondrial function.

**The Molybdenum "Bridge" to DHA and Eukaryogenesis**

Molybdenum's unique chemistry provided the necessary conditions for early life to manage highly toxic nitrogen and sulfur compounds, enabling the evolution of complex organisms that could eventually synthesize and utilize specialized lipids like DHA.

-   **Nitrogen Assimilation:** Molybdenum enzymes (like nitrate reductase) allowed ancient life to reduce toxic nitrate, a critical step for life to manage nitrogen balance. Without this, complex eukaryotic life, and the eventual development of robust, DHA-rich membranes in mammals would not have been possible.
-   **A Prerequisite for Complexity:** Mo's ability to handle high nitrogen and sulfate biochemistries in the circulatory system essentially "cleaned up" the internal environment of mammals who rapidly evovled the ability to evolve endogenous melanin to provide the necessary stability for the integration of the endosymbiotic event that created mitochondria for eukaryotes. Without this step in the GOE complex life would have stalled.

5\. Molybdenum Enzymes in Modern Mitochondria

Molybdenum remains cemented in modern mitochondria today, acting as an essential cofactor for four key enzymes that are vital for detoxification and managing the "redox noise" of common dietary inputs:

-   **Sulfite Oxidase:** Manages toxic sulfites from sulfur-containing amino acids.
-   **Xanthine Oxidase:** Involved in purine metabolism and uric acid production.
-   **Aldehyde Oxidase** and **Nitrate Reductase:** Involved in various detoxification pathways.

6\. Mo's Role in Quantum-Thermodynamic Function

Molybdenum's deep relevance to my framework lies in its impact on membrane potential and UPE (ultraweak photon emission) signaling:

-   **Membrane Depolarization Control:** The activity of these Mo-dependent enzymes ensures that the mitochondrial membrane can depolarize correctly. In a quantum context, this controlled depolarization is not just about ion flow; it is the precise release of potential energy that maintains the coherence and efficiency of the system.
-   **Managing UPE Noise:** By efficiently neutralizing metabolic toxins (like excess sulfites or aldehydes), Mo enzymes reduce chemical "noise" that would otherwise interfere with the subtle UPE signaling and water coherence within the cell.

Jack, if I’m reading this correctly, you’re now describing how the VDR “brake” actually works at the molecular level with photonic metal coordination enabling controlled metabolic throttling.

That brake only functions when solar photons properly coordinate metal centres. Vit D supps can activate the VDR signal without that photonic context, like pressing a brake pedal with the hydraulics disconnected.

The signal is present, but it can’t do real work.

This is where the Temporal Coherence Index (TCI) could turn your thesis into something clinically actionable.

By applying a Structure Factor (S) to vit D - measuring not just how much D is present, but how clean and coordinated the signal is, we can get a systems-level readout of whether photonic metal coordination is actually happening.

In American football terms, it tells us whether the Head Coach (hypothalamus) can actually hear the Quarterback (mitochondria).

The Stadium Clock - Vitamin D
Sets the seasonal strategy
Only accurate when solar photons have actually coordinated Cu, Mn, Mo, and melanin synthesis
Supp D = broken clock (the signal is visible, but it’s disconnected from the game situation)

The Scoreboard - Leptin (redox signalling context)
Leptin isn’t motivation or fuel availability - it’s the scoreboard.
Specifically, it’s a readout of whether the mitochondrial matrix is signaling cleanly.

Your mechanism shows how that signal originates:

Mn-SOD2, confined to the mitochondrial matrix, converts superoxide (O₂⁻) into H₂O₂ at diffusion-limited rates
Superoxide is noise; H₂O₂ is the encoded redox signal
That H₂O₂ tone feeds the leptin–melanocortin pathway

So leptin reflects whether oxidative metabolism is coherent, safe, and sustainable, not whether calories are available or effort is sufficient.

When Mn-SOD signalling is clean, the scoreboard tells the truth.
When the matrix is noisy, the scoreboard lies, even if leptin levels are high.

That’s why “leptin resistance” isn’t a motivation failure or a receptor problem. It’s what happens when redox noise drowns out the signal, and the brain correctly ignores static.

The Noise-Cancelling Headset — Molybdenum

Clears metabolic “crowd noise” (sulfites, aldehydes, uric acid)
Enables hypothalamus to hear mitochondrial signals
Without it, even perfect Mn-SOD function can’t communicate upstream

When the molybdenum redox capacitor is overwhelmed, coordination fails, not because the players are weak, but because communication collapses.

So, when TCI shows a mismatch, the problem isn’t effort or fuel.

It’s governance failure.

The stadium is full of metabolic static, the clock is unreliable, the scoreboard is lying, the coach is calling plays blind to field position, and the lights are flickering chaotically, forcing the team to run plays from the wrong playbook at the wrong time!

Then, in that case, the Chappuis absorption of ozone is the Shadow!

Before the GOE, there was effectively no significant Chappuis filtering; after the rise of oxygen and ozone, a weak but wavelength-selective veil appeared in the heart of the visible spectrum that melanin would have been reading. It was not full blackout, and it was not raw light. It was an intermediate state, a spectral shadow, laid across part of the Fraunhofer architecture, including the sodium D region.

In this sense, ozone did not merely block radiation; it introduced a new photonic “grammar” between naked Archean sunlight and darkness.

So, if melanin is indeed the primordial reader of life’s code, then ozone did not merely protect life from excess radiation; it may have altered the very rules by which light organised metals, redox chemistry, and eventually biochemistry itself.

By sharply filtering the short-wave UV and weakly modulating the visible through the Chappuis bands, it restructured the spectral environment reaching Earth’s surface. In that sense, post-GOE biology evolved not under raw solar input, but under sunlight already processed by atmospheric chemistry. This makes it plausible that ancient broadband photonic interfaces such as melanin, and later wavelength-sensitive systems such as tryptophan–FICZ–AhR signaling, were shaped by different versions of the same solar signal.

What had once been unshielded Archean power became, after the GOE, increasingly regulated photonic information.

Sumerian temples, 3000 BC: "Utu is the source of all life. Bathe in his rays daily."
Rome, 6th century AD: "The Justinian Code now grants every citizen a legal right to sunlight."
Victorian England: "Pale skin marks the lady. Tanned skin marks the field hand."
1903: "The sun cures tuberculosis. The Nobel Prize confirms it."
1933: "We now treat 165 different diseases with sunlight alone."
1980s: "The sun causes skin cancer. Apply factor 30 and stay in the shade."
1990s: "Block all UV. Wear hats. Stay indoors. Use the spray."
2010s: "Vitamin D deficiency is now a national health crisis."
2020s: "Take a supplement. Do not, under any circumstances, get the vitamin from the source it was designed to come from."
2026: "Hawaii has now banned six of the chemicals you've been told to put on your face for forty years."
You: noticing that the source of life worshipped by every civilisation in history was relabelled as a carcinogen the same century the supplement was patented.

This "photonic grammar" is the missing link in the scaling problem I write about in my work. I’ve identified that the **Great Oxidation Event (GOE)** didn't just give us oxygen to breathe; it created a **spectral filter** that shifted life from raw power to **regulated information**.

**1\. The Fraunhofer Architecture and the "Shadow"**

The **Fraunhofer lines** are the "barcodes" of solar chemistry. When Ozone O3 appeared, it didn't just block UV-C; through the **Chappuis bands** (450–750 nm), it cast a "shadow" specifically over the **Sodium D region** (approx 589 nm).

**The Molecular Brake:** This Chappuis "veil" provides the specific "grammar" for the **VDR (Vitamin D Receptor)** to act as a metabolic brake.

**Photonic Metal Coordination:** Melanin, as the "primordial reader," coordinates metal centers (like the trace **Lanthanum** or **Iron** in the "soup") using these specific Fraunhofer gaps. If the "Shadow" of ozone is missing, or if you block the light with sunscreen, the metals cannot coordinate, and the VDR brake "fails open," leading to the metabolic "throttling" issues we call disease.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/screenshot-2025-08-10-at-6-56-30%E2%80%AFam-png.36531/)

**2\. T.W.A.T. (Tina Will Add Time)**

My slide and the timeline above highlight the **"Century of Deception"**:

**The 1933 Peak:** 165 diseases treated with light. This was the era when we still understood the **photonic coordination** of the VDR.

**The 1980s-2020s Decline:** By relabeling the sun as a carcinogen and pushing the "Vitamin D Pill" illusion, Big Pharma effectively **removed the solar signal from the metal centers.

The Result:** Without the "photonic grammar" of the Fraunhofer lines, the VDR cannot throttle the immune system. This is why we see the **cytokine storms** and **autism signals** (lower sulfate/low dielectric) in a sun-deprived population.

**3\. Ozone: The Rules of the Game**

Post-GOE biology evolved to read the **processed signal**.
**AhR and FICZ:** The **Aryl Hydrocarbon Receptor (AhR)** is a "light antenna" that uses **Tryptophan** to sense UV-A. When UV-A hits Tryptophan, it creates **FICZ**, a high-affinity ligand for AhR.
**The Hijack:** In our modern environment (nnEMF and Blue Light), we are "un-condensing" the water and blocking the Fraunhofer architecture. This "blurs" the FICZ signal. Without the **Chappuis Shadow** to provide contrast, the **VDR/AhR cross-talk** fails.

**My Decentralized Summary:**

The sun isn't just a light bulb; it's a **pulsed, filtered, and coded power supply**. Melanin is the "software" that reads the **Fraunhofer/Chappuis code** to organize the "soup" (metals and ions). When you replace the sun with a supplement, you are giving the body the "ink" but taking away the "book." This is why **Hawaii’s ban** on sunscreen chemicals in 2026 is so significant, it's the first macroscopic recognition that we've been "poisoning the antenna" in centralized medicine allowing the Rockefeller dynasty to sell the ignorant drugs over Nature.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-08T22:11:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ophthalmologists.32198/post-366559>

In the context of my "human decentralized blockchain" and atavism thesis, eye floaters are not merely age-related debris;
they represent a **failure of the developmental "clearing" program** that separates the high-resolution mammalian camera eye from its ancient, vascularized, and multi-functional predecessors.

To understand this as an atavism as I did with autism in QE#45, we have to look at the **Succession of Vitreous Phases** and the role of the **Hyaloid Artery**.

1\. The Primary Vitreous: The "Ancestral" Shadow
During embryonic development, the human eye is temporarily an ancestral, "rhabdomeric-like" sensory organ.

-   **The Primary Vitreous:** This is the first "version" of your eye’s interior. It is highly cellular and packed with the **hyaloid vasculature,** a network of blood vessels that feeds the growing lens.
-   **The Atavistic Link:** Many lower vertebrates and primitive ancestors required internal ocular vasculature throughout their lives. In humans, this is a **vestigial "placeholder"** that must be fully deleted to achieve the high-resolution, avascular (clear) state of a modern mammal.

2\. Floaters as "Failed Deletion" (Regression Failure)
A "floater" is often a fragment of this ancient primary vitreous or the hyaloid artery that failed to be properly photorepaired or "renovated" or resorbed.

-   **Cloquet’s Canal:** When the hyaloid artery regresses, it leaves behind a vestigial tube called Cloquet’s canal.
-   **The Signal:** If the **POMC/Melanopsin** axis is under stress (high Deuterium/low CCO yield), the body lacks the "dielectric energy" to complete the enzymatic cleanup of these proteinaceous remnants.
-   **Atavistic Result:** The eye essentially "reverts" to the cluttered, vascularized state of the primary vitreous. You are literally seeing the **shadows of your own evolutionary past** because the "high-resolution" cleanup program stalled out.

I bet you see now why I warned you the eye doctor is impotent to explain this to you. If they were MD members I would direct them here for the answers ----> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-eyes-and-bad-ears-amd-tinnitus-epigenetics.32232/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-eyes-and-bad-ears-amd-tinnitus-epigenetics.32232/)

3\. The Lens Evolution Connection: Avascularity is the Goal
The transition from **PaxB** (hybrid) to **Pax6** (specialized) mirrors the transition from a vascularized "sensing" organ to a clear "focusing" lens.

-   **The Metabolic Cost:** Maintaining a clear, avascular vitreous and lens is metabolically expensive. It requires a perfect **antioxidant/redox balance** to prevent collagen fibrils from clumping (syneresis).
-   **The CCO/Deuterium Link:** If your mitochondrial ATPase is "stuttering," the first thing to collapse is the **dielectric hydration** of the vitreous. The 99% water matrix loses its "charge," the collagen fibrils aggregate, and the "atavistic debris" becomes visible as floaters.

4\. Floaters as "Vascular Noise"
Just as I've described Nystagmus as "movement noise," in albinism or tinnitus, floaters are **"structural noise"**. ---->

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-color-change.30362/#post-366528](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-color-change.30362/#post-366528)

-   They are the physical manifestation of the **Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) collapse** in the anterior chamber of the eye.
-   When the "melanin shield" or the CCO engine fails, the vitreous can no longer stay in its "liquid crystalline" state. It degrades into pockets of liquid and solids.

**The Conclusion:** Eye floaters are the "broadcast" that your system has lost the energy to maintain its **Advanced Mammalian Status**. You are sliding back toward the "cellular/vascular vitreous" of a 6-week-old embryo or a primitive vertebrate ancestor.
**Sunrise with 350-1000 nm light is the key to photorepair of floaters. The red light (670nm) specifically "re-energizes" the vitreous collagen to potentially reverse this aggregation and restore the dielectric constant of the eye but it needs the 380 to renovate the circadian mess with it.**
Heme protein renovation is fundamentally tied to sunlight, with research confirming that **Ultraviolet A (UVA)** radiation in the **320–380 nm** range is a primary driver of this process. As highlighted in the slide above, this interaction is a critical component of a "photorepair" system that resyncs cellular metabolism.

**The Role of 380 nm UVA in Heme Renovation

Transcriptional Stimulation:** UVA radiation (320–380 nm) strongly stimulates the expression of the human **heme oxygenase 1 (HO-1)** gene by enhancing its transcription rate. HO-1 is the rate-limiting enzyme responsible for breaking down heme into biliverdin, iron, and carbon monoxide.

**Protein-DNA Binding Alteration:** Exposure to these specific UVA wavelengths causes an immediate alteration in how specific protein complexes bind to the promoter of the heme oxygenase gene, occurring even without new protein synthesis.

**System Resync:** This process, often referred to as "engine renovation," is part of a larger photorepair mechanism involving **neuropsin (OPN5)**, which is sensitive to 380 nm light. It coordinates with molecules like **NAD+** and **SIRT1** to optimize mitochondrial function and the "melanin-semiconductor".

**Melatonin and Photoprotection**
While UVA drives the renovation of heme, it also induces oxidative stress. **Melatonin, created inside the engine,** acts as a crucial "buffer" in this system:

**Mitochondrial Support:** Melatonin enhances mitochondrial ATP synthesis and reduces reactive oxygen species (ROS) formation triggered by UV radiation.

**Gene Reprogramming:** Melatonin induces **Nrf2-HO-1 reprogramming**, which corrects oscillatory patterns in circadian clock genes (like _Clock_, _Bmal1_, and _Per_) that may be disrupted by stress or environmental factors.

**Direct Interaction:** Melatonin can synergistically increase heme oxygenase-1 expression, providing neuroprotective and photoprotective effects.
In this decentralized framework, **sunrise** serves as the initial trigger, providing the necessary 380 nm UVA "instruction" to renovate heme proteins and resync the body's internal clocks before higher-energy UV rays arrive later in the day.
