# Thread: 3 weeks into 14 hrs a day of sunlight- Up and down and all over the place. Is this to be expected?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-into-14-hrs-a-day-of-sunlight-up-and-down-and-all-over-the-place-is-this-to-be-expected.32200/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 53
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anna Dunn — 2026-01-27T23:07:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-into-14-hrs-a-day-of-sunlight-up-and-down-and-all-over-the-place-is-this-to-be-expected.32200/post-365993>

53 years old. Symptoms/dx. Oxalate toxicity, debilitating nocturnal dystonic jerks, severe insomnia, severe MCAS, dysautonomia, peripheral neuropathy, microvascular/vasospastic angina, interstitial cystitis, severe depression/anxiety, akathisia, osteoporosis, white matter lesions, heterozygous for wnt10a (ectodermal dysplasia), hEDS (though that might be a misdiagnosis), polydipsia/polyurea, white matter lesions, borderline enlarged aorta, mitral valve prolapse, craniocervical instability, atlantoaxial instability, hypoplastic nonflowing left jugular, compressed right jugular. Chiari zero. Probable tethered spinal cord, endometriosis, adenomyosis.
I spend my days in a recliner.

I’ve been following Jack's advice on extended sun exposure for 3 weeks now, and the first week was really intense with vastly increased oxalate dumping. By week 2-3 I noticed improvements—my energy, sleep, and even akathisia were improving. Then, on Sunday I had the best day in 5 years. I overdid it with some physical exertion (climbing steps to front yard), and since then, I’ve been dealing with pounding heart, severe agitation and complete insomina. I'm on 2mg prednisolone so I might have used up my cortisol climbing the steps and had a delayed adrenaline response. On the second night of complete insomnia and wired hell I took temazepam to sleep (I only normally take in once every two months when desperate, whereas before it was nightly). Temaz usually gets me a couple of hrs sleep at most due to prior use. After waking yesterday from about 3 hrs of temaz assisted sleep, I was completely bedbound the whole day, barely able to lift my head. I fell asleep during the day for the first time in 5 years (usually Im too agitated or get zapped in the head). I only slept an hr last night. I’m really struggling today, feeling panicked about what the next few days, weeks and months will bring.

Other strange things since increased light exposure have been red blotches on my skin and hundreds of tiny blisters (not sunburn) which Jack says is normal.

I’ve been living with these health issues for 16 years but catastrophically so for 5 years and while I’ve seen some improvements with a low oxalate diet over the past 29 months and also with the recent light exposure, the toll it’s taken on my mental and physical state is heavy. I realize I'm only 3 weeks in but I’m feeling like I’m at a breaking point. Have others had intense fluctuations like this in the early weeks? What kind of recovery timeline should I realistically expect? I’m looking for some success stories, particularly from those who have experienced severe health challenges like mine.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-03T02:56:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-into-14-hrs-a-day-of-sunlight-up-and-down-and-all-over-the-place-is-this-to-be-expected.32200/post-366251>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

POTS + dysautonomia + MCAS + connective tissue disorder + CCI/AAI = SCREAMS YOUR IMM IS FACING A ROS FIRE. That is why Mo and Cu are toast. If you do not get how the wiring diagram was built in the crucible of the GOE and how it goes wrong under the modern nnEMF environment nothing will help you.

Molybdenum (Mo) was critical in early life forms for handling high-energy redox reactions, particularly in enzymes that detoxify sulfur and nitrogen compounds. Enzymes like sulfite oxidase (which converts toxic sulfite to sulfate) and xanthine oxidase (involved in purine catabolism, producing uric acid as an antioxidant) rely on Mo cofactors to act as electron acceptors or "sinks," preventing cellular damage from reactive species. These were essential during the Great Oxidation Event (~2.4 billion years ago), when oxygen levels rose, and life transitioned from sulfur/nitrogen-rich, anaerobic environments to oxygen-dominated ones. Mo availability limited the need for nitrogen fixation and sulfur cycling in early oceans, influencing evolutionary bottlenecks.

So when you have these problems it also points to tissues lacking DHA too. This is why EHS patients become more EMF sensitive. No DHA in membranes and no myelin. They become super reactive to modern environments. Before docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) became prevalent in neural tissues (around the Cambrian, aiding membrane fluidity and signaling in complex brains), Mo-enzymes eould have provided a primitive way to manage energy transitions and oxidative stress. Life back then was not complex so it did not face what the most complex eukaryotes face today.

At the atomic level, life emerged from elements that can form stable bonds and handle electron transfers, carbon for structure, hydrogen for bonds, oxygen for oxidation, nitrogen and sulfur for catalysis and signaling, but they also evolved as potential toxins in excess that became the stimulus for new evolutionary system. High-energy transitions (like those in redox reactions) require "sinks" to absorb or dissipate excess electrons without causing damage, like free radicals that break bonds.

Molybdenum (Mo), a transition metal, is ideal for this: It can cycle through multiple oxidation states (Mo(IV) to Mo(VI)), acting as an electron acceptor in enzymes. In primordial environments—think Archean oceans rich in hydrogen sulfide (H2S) and ammonia (NH3), but low in oxygen—Mo would have been scarce but crucial for detoxifying sulfur and nitrogen compounds. Sulfite oxidase uses Mo to oxidize toxic sulfite (SO3^2-) to safe sulfate (SO4^2-), dumping electrons safely. Xanthine oxidase does similar for nitrogenous wastes, turning xanthine to uric acid while managing ROS (reactive oxygen species). Without such sinks, cells "short-circuit"—membranes rupture, proteins denature from unchecked oxidation.Evolution favors survival: As oxygen levels rose (Great Oxidation Event, 2.4 Ga), life had to adapt from anaerobic, sulfur/nitrogen-heavy to aerobic ones. This pressured selection for better detox systems, setting the stage for complexity explosions like the Cambrian (540 Ma), where oxygen enabled larger bodies and neural tissues.

**WHAT NONE OF YOU HAVE CONNECTED?**

Xanthine oxidase (XO) is an enzyme primarily known for catalyzing the oxidation of hypoxanthine to xanthine and then to uric acid in purine metabolism. In its oxidase form, it generates reactive oxygen species (ROS) which liberate UPEs. Some of the ROS are the superoxide anion (O₂⁻) and hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂), which contribute to oxidative stress.In collagen biology, XO primarily exerts indirect effects through ROS production, influencing collagen synthesis, degradation, structure, and remodeling in various tissues (e.g., skin, heart, joints, cartilage). ROS creates UPEs that sculpt your collagen based system, yet some of you keep wonder how it all connects. READ the blogs. Learn the lesson because you keep shitting the bed. These effects are often detrimental in pathological contexts like collagen vascular diseases with inflammation, injury, fibrosis, or osteoarthritis.

This occurs in a concentration-dependent manner and may contribute to impaired tissue repair, such as reduced type II collagen in acute joint injury.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-03T03:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-into-14-hrs-a-day-of-sunlight-up-and-down-and-all-over-the-place-is-this-to-be-expected.32200/post-366253>

The more data I throw at some of you the more retards you listen to.

O-derived ROS activate matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs), enzymes that degrade extracellular matrix components including collagen. For instance:

Increased activity of MMP-2, MMP-9, MMP-13, and gelatinases.

In cardiac fibroblasts, oxidative stress from XO boosts MMP levels while suppressing fibrillar collagen synthesis, potentially leading to matrix imbalance in remodeling or failure.

In joint injury, elevated XO activity correlates with oxidative damage and inhibited new collagen synthesis, hindering repair.

You all have Stockholm syndrome.

Superoxide from XO inhibits collagen gelation (fibril formation) in vitro, altering collagen's biochemical integrity and preventing normal heat-induced polymerization.

ROS transforms to UPEs and that light sculpts your tisues and your life. If you want to know why you bodies are fialing you look at the GOE story in the fucking blogs. This is really getting naseating how bad some of you are following data and breadcrumbs.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-03T03:08:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-into-14-hrs-a-day-of-sunlight-up-and-down-and-all-over-the-place-is-this-to-be-expected.32200/post-366255>

We all have context-specific roles for this effect

In skin/dermal fibroblasts, XO-induced ROS decrease collagen while sometimes increasing glycosaminoglycans.

In cardiac tissue, oxidative stress from XO contributes to reduced collagen deposition and increased MMP activity, implicated in myocardial remodeling.

In osteoarthritis or cartilage, XO (expressed in damaged areas) promotes pathological mineralization and may link to collagen changes via ROS.

In wound healing, XO has physiological roles, but excess activity can be injurious.

Overall, XO's main impact on collagen biology is pathological via oxidative stress: it tilts the balance toward reduced synthesis, enhanced enzymatic degradation (via MMPs), and direct oxidative modifications that impair collagen assembly and function. Inhibiting XO (with allopurinol) sometimes can mitigates these symptoms of these effects in experimental models, suggesting therapeutic potential in conditions involving excessive oxidative stress and ECM dysregulation. I reject that notion because sunlight reverse them all without drugs. The only question is how long and how much grounding with it, and how dark does it need to be in the reversal. The depth of the hole you're in determines the answers to these questions.

I might be smart and wise but I am not a prophet on the timing for reversals because I do not have an intracellular UPE spectrometer.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=366258)
>
> I do not think it is intentional but when I see people tell other hey look at this guys approach and they lead the dying right into the centralized buzz saw it is unbelievable what happens on these forums. I am awestruck at how gullible people are when they fed the truth but still quote and send people to the lies. Nature is so simple that rhino's get it. They do not need me. They live in the sun and dark and they ground.
>
> They do nothing else. They eat what is at their zip code.
>
> The saddest aspect of my existance right now is that my thesis delivers Nature's scienceand I keep spitting out knowledge faster than my tribe can gather its wisdom.
>
> The deepest melancholy in my current existence stems from this: my thesis was meant to advance understanding, yet it feels perpetually outrun by the sheer velocity of discovery. Prestigious outlets in PEER review are captured yet, the broader centralized scientific ecosystem keep unleashing superfluous knowledge at a pace that centralized clinicians can scarcely assimilate, let alone fully integrate into the work of solving problems for our patients.
>
> It's the quiet tragedy of trying to build on giants' shoulders while the giants themselves multiply faster than we can climb.

I get this. I think it is just the reality of the machine that we are all up against... We need you to keep at it though as you are reaching and changing peoples lives in good ways.. I always try to do my part on here and when I meet others to help spread the message. It is very apparent that other members are doing the same..

Lots of things deeply shit me and my tolerance is generally low for people who don't get the message. I thought about this recently more following an exchange I had with some one and asked myself why the hell does it make me so angry when ppl don't take on board or get what I'm saying etc.. Well I care about people, even strangers and want people to live a better life and not suffer etc.. Why aren't they listening??? Well what's the intent? I concluded there was no intent here that it was a capacity issue (i.e. you can not reason someone out of a redox state).. So you make a decision to try to get the message across in different ways or let nature teach them a lesson to redirect the course (which in most cases still won't work). So me personally, I have learnt you have to walk away from some as they will never get the message (which is hard especially when you are trying to protect others they may be responsible for).

To add to this social media is a complete shit show.. You have so many "influencers" talking absolute shit and giving bad advice to people that are not in a state to think for themselves.. Even in the "quantum health" space it is just absolute garbage and shit. Then some portray to be experts with the heavy use of AI not disclosed to their followers...

With the ramping of AI, I believe the centralised journal volumes will grow with superfluous crap that feeds their narrative and paints it in a way that incentivises the puppeteers.

I think the only way forward is to keep spreading the message as people will continued to be reached and they will start to question things.. Be ok with not getting a 100% success rate.

As the saying goes - it is not meant to be pretty or easy... If it was, everyone would be doing it. Only few are cut out to be doing what you have been doing over the years.

### Szen — 2026-02-03T07:00:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-into-14-hrs-a-day-of-sunlight-up-and-down-and-all-over-the-place-is-this-to-be-expected.32200/post-366267>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=366259)
>
> I'm growing tired of it.

I left the old video game community I supported for over 20 years for similar reasons. Frustration of being the last person to care deeply about a topic and push for its advance on a code/data level with only a handful of other persons to speak with, while the majority focused only on superficial noise and didn't study let alone apply what I had clearly put forth. I eventually broke because there were no newcomers interested in investing beyond nostalgia / a single question (or intimidated by the decades of accumulated science just to explain a simple wisdom), and too much bullshit from a few specific old timers who cared only for aesthetics and not substance. The pattern is the same across the various communities I've looked at.

The key difference here is that you have something valuable and true that makes a real change, and so there is constant new blood in the community for the seeds to be planted. There will be black swans among the newbies who'll come out from the trail you are building and continue the blockchain, and they will keep coming because they see the vibrant signal of your truth and proof of work you're putting out among the background noise. Not to mention the many lurkers who join quietly and are without a word, yet are present behind the scenes studying your work until it is the right time to emerge.

The work is not easy, but the heights it will reach are worth the lows. Don't forget your circle of six and a good night's sleep - they are there for you to lean on in times like this.

### Anna Dunn — 2026-02-03T10:05:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-into-14-hrs-a-day-of-sunlight-up-and-down-and-all-over-the-place-is-this-to-be-expected.32200/post-366271>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=366259)
>
> I'm growing tired of it. Maybe it is time I just close it all down.

I'm sick enough that I was approved for voluntary assisted dying in Switzerland- the airline refused my passage due to my being too ill. 2 out of 7days last week I was so low in energy I needed to be carried to the toilet. I've been a member for less than 4 weeks and have done the following already:
1) had a building biologist inspection
2) spent about 12k reducing nnEMFs including researching and buying a new mattress, buying a daylight computer, replacing devices with battery operated ones. Kill switch and cabled Internet are booked in to be installed
3) sat outside everyday for 14hrs
4) purchased sun-thru swimwear
5) listened to more than a year's worth of pow wows
6) started reading the blogs
7) had a tradesperson visit to organize repair of an outdoor area
8) purchased outdoor furniture
9) influenced my family's behavior-my entire family now gets up at 5am to sit outside. We use red light or candies at night and go to bed early. My kids do their homework in the sun.
10) asked locals how to get fresher fish so I can tolerate it with mcas

My best friend is currently being killed quite quickly by her lovely onco and dr. and she still cannot be bothered to get out of the house for even 20 mins in the morning. I have talked her into DDW, but she as they are making her continuously worse she says well its just water. ffs.
You have changed and saved so many, please do not shut this down.

### OwenDavis — 2026-02-03T23:33:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-into-14-hrs-a-day-of-sunlight-up-and-down-and-all-over-the-place-is-this-to-be-expected.32200/post-366325>

> [Anna Dunn said:](/goto/post?id=366245)
>
> Oh, wow! Nice to meet u. I'm sorry you are going through this, too. It's grim. Chat gpt thinks my hEDS is a misdiagnosis as I'm heterozygous for WNT10a which can present similarly. I've been all over the shop the last 3.5 weeks. The highs higher and the lows lower. On average, better, I think. I've had 2 days this last week where I've been unable to get up/walk. I think the sun is supercharging oxalate removal and I run out of some nutrient or mineral. What has been your past diet? I am oxalate toxic after having eating between about 2-3g of oxalate a day for at least a decade. Sally norton has a YouTube interview on oxalate toxicity and histamine-she talks about how oxalate causes lax tissue. Have u heard of rib eye rach? She was like us - she had multiple surgeries including a spinal fusion but didn't get better until she turned carnivore. She can now trek. She has now added back some things like cheese etc. There's also another woman called Becky. I think she is called Becky's healing journey on Instagram. Same shit show as rib eye rach-fusion plus other surgeries but didn't help much. She then went low oxalate . I can't recall if carni or not but she's heavily dumping oxalate. After having dumped oxalate for 29 months I feel my joint instabilty is less bad. I have the same superdry skin. when I scrape it white powder comes off. I'm not sure if it's superfine oxalate mixed with skin cells. I feel for you re the burning plastic. There's no way I could tolerate that. The neighbour behind us used to use his fireplace in winter and I'd the mcas would put me into panic attacks. I had a big fight with him and had to complain to the council to get him to stop. When I was hospitalised, nurses perfumes would give me brain zaps. So would sounds-even a door shutting. I'd very much like to stay in contact. Who is jacob btw? Which country/city r u originally from? Btw-in total I've seen several EDS patients say they improved on carni. Can I ask what you've been eating? Ie did u previously have a high oxalate diet?

I'm just following the Jack recommendation on diet - eating local whole foods at the prescribed times, with daily fish and avoiding things I know I react to. Also keeping histamines down for MCAS. Sunlight is the main focus now, and keeping the bedroom as dark as practicable at night, so trying to keep things simple. Sounds like your food sensitivities are next level though, I'm sorry.

FWIW I did manage to get mostly over this and was doing really well from 2023 to end of 2025 (back into mountain climbing and work after being mostly disabled with CXA of 124 degrees in flexion) but sx never fully went away. Then it all flared up again in December. So healing from this is clearly possible and hopefully we can get over it in earnest this time around.

Thanks for the info Jack - good to have confirmation we're on the right track. Sorry you feel like you're flogging a dead horse, but seeing all the mito frothers down here at Atami every sunrise, with reinforcements arriving weekly, it's clear something's getting through. I will say that I'm planning to take in as much as I can from the blogs as soon as I'm no longer fked in the head from this sx flare up.

Dr. Kruse, reading this hits hard with me. I never understood people who just go along to get along because the Dr and staff are so nice, then tell you all the meds they are on. Screw that. I am one of the dumbest on the forum (I know you agree) but leveling up each day. This forum has rearranged my personal living habits for the better. I began with the simplest things under your leadership that became the biggest impact on my case so far (light, grounding, water, oysters and other varieties), stopping certain supplements because I now know the sun provides, the nnEMF's, wifi, ect. I read your blogs every single day and take notes I feel will help me. My tumor markers were cut in half, bloodwork is straightening out, I feel better inside and out now. I have blood draw today and PET scan tomorrow. The combined surveillance info will help me know more of what I need to hunt for thru you. You and this forum are so important and I respect and value all the different content that is ONLY HERE.
Respectfully,
Jen

### TANNER — 2026-02-04T19:03:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-into-14-hrs-a-day-of-sunlight-up-and-down-and-all-over-the-place-is-this-to-be-expected.32200/post-366364>

> [Anna Dunn said:](/goto/post?id=365993)
>
> 53 years old. Symptoms/dx. Oxalate toxicity, debilitating nocturnal dystonic jerks, severe insomnia, severe MCAS, dysautonomia, peripheral neuropathy, microvascular/vasospastic angina, interstitial cystitis, severe depression/anxiety, akathisia, osteoporosis, white matter lesions, heterozygous for wnt10a (ectodermal dysplasia), hEDS (though that might be a misdiagnosis), polydipsia/polyurea, white matter lesions, borderline enlarged aorta, mitral valve prolapse, craniocervical instability, atlantoaxial instability, hypoplastic nonflowing left jugular, compressed right jugular. Chiari zero. Probable tethered spinal cord, endometriosis, adenomyosis.
> I spend my days in a recliner.
>
> I’ve been following Jack's advice on extended sun exposure for 3 weeks now, and the first week was really intense with vastly increased oxalate dumping. By week 2-3 I noticed improvements—my energy, sleep, and even akathisia were improving. Then, on Sunday I had the best day in 5 years. I overdid it with some physical exertion (climbing steps to front yard), and since then, I’ve been dealing with pounding heart, severe agitation and complete insomina. I'm on 2mg prednisolone so I might have used up my cortisol climbing the steps and had a delayed adrenaline response. On the second night of complete insomnia and wired hell I took temazepam to sleep (I only normally take in once every two months when desperate, whereas before it was nightly). Temaz usually gets me a couple of hrs sleep at most due to prior use. After waking yesterday from about 3 hrs of temaz assisted sleep, I was completely bedbound the whole day, barely able to lift my head. I fell asleep during the day for the first time in 5 years (usually Im too agitated or get zapped in the head). I only slept an hr last night. I’m really struggling today, feeling panicked about what the next few days, weeks and months will bring.
>
> Other strange things since increased light exposure have been red blotches on my skin and hundreds of tiny blisters (not sunburn) which Jack says is normal.
>
> I’ve been living with these health issues for 16 years but catastrophically so for 5 years and while I’ve seen some improvements with a low oxalate diet over the past 29 months and also with the recent light exposure, the toll it’s taken on my mental and physical state is heavy. I realize I'm only 3 weeks in but I’m feeling like I’m at a breaking point. Have others had intense fluctuations like this in the early weeks? What kind of recovery timeline should I realistically expect? I’m looking for some success stories, particularly from those who have experienced severe health challenges like mine.

You are most likely oxalate "dumping".
Once this is completed you will be feeling better. My hubby had vertigo from dumping nuts out of his system.
and now 3 years of following Dr Kruse we are doing well. SEEING THE SUNRISE EVERY DAY... Grounding and sun exposure daily, staying COLD in Michigan.and no Blue lights at night, NONE!!

Well I care about people, even strangers and want people to live a better life and not suffer etc.. Why aren't they listening??? Well what's the intent? I concluded there was no intent here that it was a capacity issue (i.e. you can not reason someone out of a redox state).. So you make a decision to try to get the message across in different ways or let nature teach them a lesson to redirect the course (which in most cases still won't work). So me personally, I have learnt you have to walk away from some as they will never get the message (which is hard especially when you are trying to protect others they may be responsible for).

Even in the "quantum health" space it is just absolute garbage and shit. Then some portray to be experts with the heavy use of AI not disclosed to their followers...
>
> With the ramping of AI, I believe the centralised journal volumes will grow with superfluous crap that feeds their narrative and paints it in a way that incentivises the puppeteers.
>
> I think the only way forward is to keep spreading the message as people will continued to be reached and they will start to question things.. Be ok with not getting a 100% success rate.
>
> As the saying goes - it is not meant to be pretty or easy... If it was, everyone would be doing it. Only few are cut out to be doing what you have been doing over the years

Starting from the age of 16, I often had intuitive feelings about health and the world around me, but I doubted myself and allowed others — especially those in positions of authority — to shape my beliefs. I gaslit myself, suppressing what I instinctively knew was true. For example, when I learned about biology and physics in school, I could sense that things like living next to a power substation and staying indoors weren’t in harmony with our natural biology, but I ignored those feelings because I was taught to trust what others told me.
I followed mainstream advice like the food pyramid, limiting sun exposure, and always wearing sunglasses outside, believing that the experts knew better. But deep down, I always had a sense that something didn’t quite add up, even though I let the influence of authority push me into following their guidance.
It’s not so much that I’m inherently dim; it’s more that I lacked confidence in trusting my own instincts and reasoning. I have always been academically strong, with a PhD and first-class honours in biomechanics, but I struggled to trust myself. Looking back, I feel extremely frustrated and even angry that I let others sway me. I remember at work saying things like, “I prefer my power and signals in shielded cables,” only to get mocked with questions like, “Are you a tin foil hat wearer?”
But now, I realize trusting my instincts and questioning what I was taught is the key to understanding the truth of my health and the world around me.

>
> POTS + dysautonomia + MCAS + connective tissue disorder + CCI/AAI = SCREAMS YOUR IMM IS FACING A ROS FIRE. That is why Mo and Cu are toast. If you do not get how the wiring diagram was built in the crucible of the GOE and how it goes wrong under the modern nnEMF environment nothing will help you.
>
> Molybdenum (Mo) was critical in early life forms for handling high-energy redox reactions, particularly in enzymes that detoxify sulfur and nitrogen compounds. Enzymes like sulfite oxidase (which converts toxic sulfite to sulfate) and xanthine oxidase (involved in purine catabolism, producing uric acid as an antioxidant) rely on Mo cofactors to act as electron acceptors or "sinks," preventing cellular damage from reactive species. These were essential during the Great Oxidation Event (~2.4 billion years ago), when oxygen levels rose, and life transitioned from sulfur/nitrogen-rich, anaerobic environments to oxygen-dominated ones. Mo availability limited the need for nitrogen fixation and sulfur cycling in early oceans, influencing evolutionary bottlenecks.
>
> So when you have these problems it also points to tissues lacking DHA too. This is why EHS patients become more EMF sensitive. No DHA in membranes and no myelin. They become super reactive to modern environments. Before docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) became prevalent in neural tissues (around the Cambrian, aiding membrane fluidity and signaling in complex brains), Mo-enzymes eould have provided a primitive way to manage energy transitions and oxidative stress. Life back then was not complex so it did not face what the most complex eukaryotes face today.
>
> At the atomic level, life emerged from elements that can form stable bonds and handle electron transfers, carbon for structure, hydrogen for bonds, oxygen for oxidation, nitrogen and sulfur for catalysis and signaling, but they also evolved as potential toxins in excess that became the stimulus for new evolutionary system. High-energy transitions (like those in redox reactions) require "sinks" to absorb or dissipate excess electrons without causing damage, like free radicals that break bonds.
>
> Molybdenum (Mo), a transition metal, is ideal for this: It can cycle through multiple oxidation states (Mo(IV) to Mo(VI)), acting as an electron acceptor in enzymes. In primordial environments—think Archean oceans rich in hydrogen sulfide (H2S) and ammonia (NH3), but low in oxygen—Mo would have been scarce but crucial for detoxifying sulfur and nitrogen compounds. Sulfite oxidase uses Mo to oxidize toxic sulfite (SO3^2-) to safe sulfate (SO4^2-), dumping electrons safely. Xanthine oxidase does similar for nitrogenous wastes, turning xanthine to uric acid while managing ROS (reactive oxygen species). Without such sinks, cells "short-circuit"—membranes rupture, proteins denature from unchecked oxidation.Evolution favors survival: As oxygen levels rose (Great Oxidation Event, 2.4 Ga), life had to adapt from anaerobic, sulfur/nitrogen-heavy to aerobic ones. This pressured selection for better detox systems, setting the stage for complexity explosions like the Cambrian (540 Ma), where oxygen enabled larger bodies and neural tissues.
>
> **WHAT NONE OF YOU HAVE CONNECTED?**
>
> Xanthine oxidase (XO) is an enzyme primarily known for catalyzing the oxidation of hypoxanthine to xanthine and then to uric acid in purine metabolism. In its oxidase form, it generates reactive oxygen species (ROS) which liberate UPEs. Some of the ROS are the superoxide anion (O₂⁻) and hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂), which contribute to oxidative stress.In collagen biology, XO primarily exerts indirect effects through ROS production, influencing collagen synthesis, degradation, structure, and remodeling in various tissues (e.g., skin, heart, joints, cartilage). ROS creates UPEs that sculpt your collagen based system, yet some of you keep wonder how it all connects. READ the blogs. Learn the lesson because you keep shitting the bed. These effects are often detrimental in pathological contexts like collagen vascular diseases with inflammation, injury, fibrosis, or osteoarthritis.

Wow! I only suspected EDS connective tissue disorder because of yoga injuries but just came across something briefly looking at Facebook about dental procedures and people with EDS being resistant to anesthesia! Then of course I remembered that time at age 17 when I woke up in the middle of dental surgery. Paralyzed but completely conscious and unable to tell anyone I was awake. Ouch!

And of course I have the MCAS and all the other stuff you listed that I didn’t know about until putting the Lyme pieces together 6 years ago and having many Lyme docs diagnose me with all that stuff.

Now of course I know it’s mitochondrial damage. I HAVE MOVED FROM COLORADO! Finding my N=1 and found my sweet spot for healing on this Earth.

All is well but I just wasn’t 100% about the EDS and don’t want to be a hypochondriac. Being a mitochondriac is way better for my health anyway.

Also my ankles would always sprain when I ran track for 5 years and was always last in the race and lagging behind in practices. Probably the EDS. Makes sense now.

This really has been going on for a lifetime.

I am taking it seriously. Even if I need a break from the blogs while I just LIVE the mito life and HEAL.

Cool piece of the puzzle though as I really want to understand how these life experiences and traumas are all from damaged mitochondria. It’s just one more piece. The science stuff and big words are over my head while I live in my left brain for a while. I might switch back to the right but only if my health requires. I’m doing great. ITS NOT DEPRESSING! It’s pretty cool how this explains why I sucked so bad at track and the no pain no gain attitude 40 years ago was not doing me any good but rather harming me. ### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-28T15:16:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-cold-thermogenesis.32203/post-366025>

In my decentralized thesis, **Cold Thermogenesis (CT)** is the ultimate "quantum bypass" protocol. It utilizes the laws of thermodynamics to restore electronic coherence when the **DHA Faraday cage** has been compromised and the **SCN-melanopsin** system is blinded by "light smog of ROR/RXR destruction."

**1\. The Physical Shrinkage: Closing the Tunneling Gap**

The most profound effect of CT is the physical contraction of the mitochondrial matrix and its respiratory proteins. Recal mitochondria are not static they change their size and shape due to thermodynamics. When they do change shape it alters some of the metal atoms on the IMM and that changes UPE transformations.

-   **The 14 Angstrom Rule:** I've noted in the CT series that electron tunneling effectively happens only below 14 Angstroms. Under the stress of **blue light** and **pseudohypoxia**, mitochondrial membranes swell, increasing the distance between respiratory complexes and "breaking" the tunneling threshold and this alters the mitochondrial circuit. When we break ths 14 Angstrom rule cardiolipin wins over CCO = disease and death.
-   **The CT Reset:** Cold temperature causes the **Inner Mitochondrial Membrane (IMM)** to contract. This physical shrinkage brings the cytochromes closer together, re-enabling quantum tunneling even if DHA levels are suboptimal in the outer mitochondrial membrane and even if Mo enzymes are not optimized on the IMM. It essentially "squeezes" the electronic hardware back into a functional range.

**2\. Clearing the "Retinaldehyde Smog"**

Retinaldehyde is an electrophilic toxin that creates "noise" in the ROR/RAR signaling pathway.

-   **Activation of the Glymphatic System:** Cold exposure significantly enhances the clearance of metabolic waste. In the brain, CT drives the flow of cerebrospinal fluid to "wash out" the free **retinaldehyde** and "dirty" nitrogenous byproducts that have accumulated due to nnEMF interference. It clears the dual exhaust system in chr2 = eccrine/exocrine clearance. Anyone who enters cold water always feels like they need to urinate rapidly. Many times CT rounds leads to bowel movements or passing gas.
-   **Upregulating Redox Enzymes:** CT triggers a massive upregulation of **Mn-SOD (SOD2)** and **Glutathione** pathways. This "detoxifies" the UPE field, lowering the metabolic "glare" in the spectra of UPE from the matrix so the ROR receptors can once again sense the subtle diurnal signals in the central retinal pathways connecting the SCN to the hypothalamus and brain. This improves timing. Remember cells are costly in time, not energy in the human blueprint.

**3\. Restoring Water Coherence (The EZ Expansion)**

Cold water is a dense, _high-dielectric medium in our mitochondria. Cold water has a higher dielectric constant as a result. Temperature is part of the circadian clock control mechanism for this reason exclusively._

-   **The Thermodynamic Advantage:** According to Dr. Gerald Pollack's research at the University of Washington, the **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water** (the "battery" of the cell) expands as temperature drops and the infrared (IR) light from mitochondrial work is trapped at the electronic level in cells.
-   **Energy Storage:** CT forces the mitochondria to burn **brown adipose tissue (BAT)**, which releases high-intensity IR light internally (UPEs). This IR light builds a massive EZ "buffer" around the DNA and ROR/RAR complexes, providing _**a temporary liquid-crystalline shield**_ against nnEMF when the DHA Faraday cage is thin. What else stimulates this? Simultaneous drinking of cold DDW also selects for adipocytes to become brown fat via the FADS 1 and 2 pathways. Look up the science.

**4\. Bypassing the "Blinded" SCN**

While blue light "blinds" the SCN by forcing it into a daytime state, CT provides a **non-photic** (non-light) circadian signal.

-   **Thermal Entrainment:** The drop in core body temperature is a primary signal for **Melatonin** release and the activation of **RORα**. People forget the circadian mechanism reponds to light, dark, and temperature variations. By using CT in the evening, you "force" the SCN to acknowledge the end of the day, overriding the "false noon" signal created by artificial blue light.

**Summary**

CT as the Quantum Mechanic
In the absence of a robust DHA shield, CT acts as a **"Quantum Reset"**:

1.  **Compresses** the respiratory chain to restore electron tunneling.
2.  **Expands** the EZ water battery to provide an alternate Faraday shield.
3.  **Cleanses** the retinaldehyde "smog" that blinds the ROR/RAR sensors.
4.  **Resynchronizes** the clock through thermal, rather than having to use optical, entrainment.
5.  This is not a long term solution to poor environmental lighting.

**WHY WAS THE SYSTEM BUILT LIKE THIS?**

SEE THE GOE, CAMBRIAN EXPLOSION, & KT HISTORY on EARTH.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-28T15:28:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-cold-thermogenesis.32203/post-366026>

Now we should connect this to the **Cambrian explosion**, looking at how the **sudden oxygenation and cooling of the oceans** allowed the first complex eukaryotes to utilize **Molybdenum** and **DHA** to scale up their "light-sensing" capacity into the first complex nervous systems.

**The Cambrian Explosion (~541 million years ago)** was not merely a "biological" event; it was a **quantum-optical revolution** triggered by a planetary transition from a "dirty" anaerobic environment to a "clean," cold, and oxygenated one.
This phase shift allowed the first complex eukaryotes to move from dirty chemical processes and made them scale to a new "light-sensing" capacity which were then built into the first complex nervous systems.

**1\. The Cooling Crucible: Thermodynamics of Complexity**
Before the Cambrian, Earth's oceans were warm, "dirty" with iron, green as a results, and not blue, with relatively low oxygen tensions.

-   **The Cooling Event:** The transition into the Cambrian followed significant global cooling (Cryogenian glaciations). As we established with **Cold Thermogenesis (CT)**, cold water increases oxygen solubility and physically contracts the mitochondrial matrix.
-   **The Tunneling Advantage:** This cooling "squeezed" the early eukaryotic respiratory chains, bringing cytochromes within the **14 Angstrom** limit required for high-speed electron tunneling. This thermal contraction provided the energetic surplus (ATP and UPE coherence) necessary to build complex body plans.

**2\. The Oxygen-Molybdenum-DHA Triad**
The sudden rise in atmospheric oxygen during the Cambrian acted as a "quantum bridge":

-   **Molybdenum (The Bridge Loan):** Oxygenation made **Molybdenum** bioavailable in the oceans. Early Mo-dependent enzymes allowed these early organisms to "clean" the nitrogen and sulfur smog, creating a low-noise internal environment.
-   **DHA (The Permanent Capital):** With the nitrogen noise managed by Mo, evolution could finally utilize **DHA quantum abilities**. This 22-carbon pi-electron "Faraday cage" was installed in the emerging neural tissues (the first "eyes" and "brains") to protect the **ROR/RAR**light-sensing complexes from the high-energy UV environment of the shallow Cambrian seas.

**3\. Scaling the Light Sensor: The First Nervous Systems**
The "Explosion" was essentially the scaling of the **Mitochondrial-UPE-ROR** sensor from a single cell into a multicellular array.

-   **From Sensor to Eye:** The **RAR ancestry** I've identified over 20 years of teaching provided the software to build the first photoreceptors. These weren't just for "seeing" external objects; they were for **entraining the internal mitochondrial clock** to the solar cycle.
-   **The SCN Blueprint:** The first central nervous systems evolved to act as "coherence managers," using the high dielectric constant of DHA-rich membranes to synchronize the UPE signaling of millions of mitochondria across a single organism. Melanin on our surfaces evolved to control the flux of metal atoms that managed the "dirty chemistry" of the GOE and Early Cambrian epochs through skin desquamation.

**4\. Why the "Dirty" Chemistry Had to Die**

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-29T04:19:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/early-periods-shortened-cycles-explain.32205/post-366078>

Cytochrome P450 (**CYP**) enzymes are a large family of **heme-containing proteins** that play a vital role in both the production and the breakdown of sex steroid hormones like estrogen and progesterone.

**How CYP Proteins Control Hormones**

CYP enzymes act as metabolic "gatekeepers" in two primary ways:

**Synthesis (Steroidogenesis):** Specific CYP enzymes in the ovaries and adrenal glands convert cholesterol into active hormones. For example:

-   **[CYP17A1](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11275228/)** converts progesterone and pregnenolone into precursors for androgens.
-   **[CYP19A1 (Aromatase)](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9260165/)** is the final step in producing **estrogen** from androgens.
-   **Metabolism & Clearance:** After hormones have done their job, other CYP enzymes (like **CYP1A2**, **CYP1B1**, and **CYP3A4**) break them down so they can be eliminated from the body. If these enzymes are overactive or underactive, hormone levels can become imbalanced and lead to your girlfiends problem. The light she lives under at work is behind her problem.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-29T04:24:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/early-periods-shortened-cycles-explain.32205/post-366079>

Links to Shortened Cycles and Early Periods

When the balance between hormone production and clearance is disrupted by CYP dysregulation, it can lead to a **shortened menstrual cycle** or an **early period** through several mechanisms.

**Rapid Estrogen Clearance:** If metabolic enzymes like **[CYP3A4](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27995413/)** or **CYP1A2** clear estrogen too quickly, the body may fail to maintain the thick uterine lining required for a full cycle. A sudden drop in estrogen can trigger early shedding of the lining (menstruation).

**Progesterone Deficiency:** **CYP17A1** is responsible for processing progesterone. If it metabolizes progesterone too rapidly or if synthesis is impaired, the "luteal phase" (the second half of the cycle) can be cut short. Progesterone is what keeps the period from starting; if it drops too soon, the period arrives early.

**Aromatase Overactivity:** If **CYP19A1 (Aromatase)** creates an estrogen spike too early in the cycle, it can accelerate follicle development and trigger **early ovulation**, which typically results in a shorter overall cycle length.

**Genetic or External Disruptors:** Genetic variations (polymorphisms) in these CYP genes, or external factors like smoking and certain medications that "induce" (speed up) these enzymes, can lead to chronic hormonal imbalances and irregular, short cycles.

Every hormone above is controled by the CYP heme mechanism. Now look at the slide and see what it says about heme proteins and the light your girl works and lives under. It obvious why she is fucked.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-29T04:41:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/early-periods-shortened-cycles-explain.32205/post-366082>

All opsins are linked to Vitamin A. Vitamin A is the bomb that causes the destruction. Vitamin A = ROR and RXR receptors.

In my decentralized thesis, the transition metals chelated by melanin, namely, **Copper (Cu), Iron (Fe), Manganese (Mn), and Molybdenum (Mo)** are not just atomic bystanders; they are the functional "hardware" that physically change our mitochondrial biology in specific ways and this allows Vitamin A to properly interact with the Retinoic Acid Receptor (RAR) and Retinoid X Receptor (RXR) to translate mitochondrial light into genetic action.

These metals interact with the RAR/RXR pathway through the following mechanisms. the ones that are big for your lady are Fe and Mn when it comes to her cycle issues.

To support hormone function, Cytochrome P450 (**CYP**) enzymes in humans rely on specific metal ions to catalyze the complex synthesis and breakdown of sex steroids.

-   **Iron (Fe):** This is the **most critical**metal ion for CYP function. It has to be in the Fe+2 = state not the +3 state.
    -   **Catalytic Center:** Every CYP enzyme contains a **heme-iron center**. The iron atom cycles between oxidation states (**Fe²⁺ and Fe³⁺**) to activate oxygen, allowing the enzyme to chemically modify hormones. _**Only the +2 state carries oxygen.**_
    -   **Hormone Synthesis:** Iron-dependent CYPs (like **CYP11A1**) initiate the conversion of cholesterol into pregnenolone, the "mother hormone" for all sex steroids. Read the Hormone 101 and 102 blogs on pregenenolone syndrome.
    -   **Electron Transfer:** In mitochondria, CYPs receive electrons from **iron-sulfur proteins** (like [adrenodoxin](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3538421/)) to power their reactions.
-   **Zinc (Zn):** While not the central metal in the heme itself, zinc is a vital **structural and catalytic cofactor** for many enzymes that work alongside CYPs.
    -   **Enzyme Stability:** Zinc helps stabilize the tertiary structure of proteins. Studies have shown that **Zinc(II) ions** can directly modulate the structure and activity of **CYP3A4**, a key enzyme for clearing excess estrogen.
    -   Did you know melanin acts as a significant **reservoir and chelator** for zinc (**Zn²⁺**) ions in the human body too? Now you can see why nnEMF that destroys melanin plays are role in her fucked up cycles.
    -   Because the binding affinity is only "moderate," melanin does not just lock zinc away; it can **store and release** it as needed by the cell. This makes pigmented tissues (like the eyes and skin) major storage sites for zinc. What happens when blue light or nnEMF destroy that melanin? You got a period shortening and a major fertility problem don't you?
-   **Manganese (Mn) and Selenium (Se):** These essential metals are involved in the broader regulatory network of the **[hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal (HPG) axis](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0147651325013077)**, which controls the menstrual cycle. Manganese acts as a cofactor for enzymes involved in antioxidant defense, protecting CYP proteins from oxidative damage during hormone metabolism. Se is taken care of by my Epi-paleo Rx.

**Manganese (Mn): The RXR Stress Buffer**

Manganese interacts primarily through **RXR-mediated neuroprotection**. So when you got blu elight liberating Vitamin A Mn-SOD inside your mitochondrial matrix still allow you to make proper UPEs to have a kid. When you lose that ability, you cannot. That is where your girl is now. Now you can see how Vitamin issues play a huge role in CYP biology.

**Mitochondrial Protection:** RXRα/PPARγ heterodimers (which require the same RXR partner as RAR) directly upregulate **Mn-SOD (SOD2)** in your mitochondrial matrix to reduce mitochondrial stress and make COHERENT UPEs (not chaotic ones like she is making) that are associated with coherence and proper ROS/RNS signaling.

**Information Integrity:** Since Mn-SOD is the only enzyme that can neutralize matrix superoxide without the "Fenton fire" of iron, its regulation by RXR ensures that the UPE signal remains coherent. Manganese isn't just a cofactor; it is the "output" of a healthy RXR signal that protects the system from its own metabolic "noise." MELANIN CONTROL THE QUANTUM AMOUNT OF Mn needed in this system.

# Thread: New "NTP-LITE" Studies Fail to Replicate 2018 NTP Findings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ntp-lite-studies-fail-to-replicate-2018-ntp-findings.32206/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Guy Houri — 2026-01-29T13:23:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ntp-lite-studies-fail-to-replicate-2018-ntp-findings.32206/post-366085>

Two new replication studies from Japan and South Korea (referred to as **NTP-LITE**) were recently published (Jan 2026), claiming no significant link between cell phone radiation and cancer. However, a deeper look at the data reveals several red flags:

-   **Low Statistical Power:** These studies used significantly fewer animals (only ~70 per group vs. hundreds in the original NTP), making it nearly impossible to prove "statistical significance" for rare tumors.
-   **High Background Illness:** In some cases, up to 20% of the animals (14 out of 70) developed tumors, but since the control groups (Sham) also had high rates of illness, researchers dismissed the findings as "within historical range."
-   **Methodological Gaps:** The studies only tested one modulation (CDMA) and one power level 4W/kg, ignoring the GSM modulation which showed the strongest cancer links in the original 2018 NTP study.

**Sources:**

-   **[**Israeli NoRad4U Blog**](https://www.norad4u.co.il/2026/01/29/11491/?fbclid=IwY2xjawPoHaBleHRuA2FlbQIxMQBzcnRjBmFwcF9pZBAyMjIwMzkxNzg4MjAwODkyAAEeWN8ZDCxd1K90MeoZjgFI1qNH_iBQjlQseYFRcrYb8LGVYYkz4htY1k312VA_aem_ix5R8wkZEXyymFfwafSvbg)**
-   [**Microwave News**](https://microwavenews.com/short-takes-archive/ntp-lite-korea)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ntp-lite-studies-fail-to-replicate-2018-ntp-findings.32206/post-366091>

**Cysteine's Evolutionary Journey: From Ancient Thiol to Life's Master Regulator : MELANIN**

In the Archean era, cysteine began as a simple thiol molecule. During the Great Oxidation Event (GOE), rising oxygen levels transformed it into a key sensor for nutrients and light. This sensing ability drove the evolution of the family of melanins, pigments structured around cysteine, that enabled cells to recycle phosphorus through the pentose phosphate pathway (PPP) and generate NADPH.

With abundant NADPH, cells could activate TORC1, enabling decentralized control over growth and apoptosis (programmed cell death).Early cells used the PPP to neutralize reactive oxygen species (ROS). This process fostered the evolution of melanin and ultraweak photon emissions (UPEs), culminating in cells generating "UPE light from nothing", a biophotonic spark that fueled the Cambrian explosion and life's rapid diversification in the fossil record.

The overarching story is how Life's decentralization occured with precision from LUCA's primitive biochemistry to eukaryotic light-sensing optics, all hinges on cysteine's quantum switch embedded in melanins. This switch, acting as a protective "shield" (via pathways like Nrf2), can be disrupted by non-native electromagnetic fields (nnEMF), but restored through UV-A exposure, cold therapy, and breathwork—rebuilding biological "wealth in time" against centralized chaos. Read and be ready to be stunned. It is a story Darwin could not tell.

Hi there,
I'm Rudolf, 30 years, from Germany. I am a vet surgeon (just small animal surgery) and came by chance via direct current and bone healing to read about "Uncle Jack".
Well, I'm quit disappointed with the political situation in my country or the EU, but I'm primarily here to talk about **health**.
I already dit not load the phones in my bedroom, have nothing electric plugged in there except an alarm clock. I unplug wifi at night. ### flavin0 — 2026-02-16T21:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32220/post-366977>

I downloaded that bitcoin document examining discrete vs. continuous time. It reminds me of how theologians call out a distinction between two words for "time" in Greek: _chronos_ and _kairos_. _Chronos_ is the notion of chronological time that scientists sort of take for granted, while kairos refers to something totally different. I am not a theologian but it seems like _kairos_ is about events in some grand schedule that probably serve a highly specific purpose, or _telos._

I am currently exploring Dr. Fosbury's claim of the G-class star that is our Sun. Apparently when hydrogen picks up another electron to become H- it has a consequence in the amount of infrared that arrives here on the surface of the Earth. I guess it is a little murky what's going on up there in the Sun considering Dr. Kruse seems to believe in a lattice vs simple thermal model of how the Sun, and even Dr. Fosbury admits there is a lot of non-equilibrium dynamics up there anyways. But I think at this point in my understanding it is becoming clearer that the electromagnetic profile of the solar irradiance has a truly unique role in shaping life on Earth, hence there is no alternative to sunlight.

If even God created on a daily schedule (on the first day, it was light, second day I think atmosphere, etc) I think it would be wise for us to follow suit.

Ok I read a lot, I read Hormone 101 and 102 and Optimal Labs.
Is there anywhere a recent uptodate recommendations for Lab tests for every one (man / woman)
This still good:

1.  VO2 Max assessment (cardio pulmonary findings lead to a work up if needed.)
2.  Initial visit DEXA with body comp. Optimization a DEXA scan to include body composition scores.
    F/U visits I use BIA assessments to monitor progress and limit radiation exposure.
3.  Neuro cognitive battery of tests to assess processing speed, response speed, memory , learning, and attention repeated annually.
4.  Chemistry 20
5.  Liver function testing
6.  Serum Iron, TIBC, Serum Ferritin and Iron % Saturation
7.  Lipid panel: VAP and/or NMR analysis
8.  Homocysteine level
9.  Complete CBC with differential and platelet count (sometimes an Ivy bleeding time)
10.  Hormone panel includes (gender specific)……serum and salivary cortisol, Total testosterone, Free testosterone, % Free T, DHT, LH, FSH, Estradiol (E2) high sensitivity, DHEA-S, PSA, IGF-1, Consider based upon history testing levels of progesterone, pregnenolone, melatonin, serum and urine Osmolarity, Prolactin levels
11.  Complete thyroid panel with antibody screening (TPO etc)
12.  Complete Urinalysis (women get beta HCG if they menstruate)
13.  Highly Sensitive CRP (Not a regular CRP)
14.  Complete Vitamin D panel
15.  Omega 6/3 serum test (for severe findings consider tissue assay)
16.  Fasting serum insulin and HbA1c assessment
17.  No panel is complete without an ASI and salivary melatonin level. ?

### Rudolf — 2026-04-04T17:31:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32220/post-370252>

**Progress** with my girlfriends chronic intestine problems (leaky gut):
I got her now to eat fish. No shellfish so far, but predetor sea fish with more firm meat. (I increased my seafood intake big time, including oysters, caviar, mussels and shrimps).
We sleep on magneticos. She is dreaming more and sometimes complaining about it. I think I sleep better. We are more strict with our blue light blocking glasses after sunset, we rarely use blue light LEDs anymore at home. We are out a lot for morning sun light. We are grounding (I'm more rigorous than she does) each day. I got _TOLOS earth runne_rs and ordered some for her, too. I also got some cool tan t-shits.
I Progressed _CT_ to 10 mins + some additional "bathtub diving" in the end (mostly do CT indoor in the morning so far with some epsom salt in the water). The shivering is still intense and it starts about 2mins after I got out. She does face dips and cold showering. She does not seem to be more cold adapted yet. Although we have cooler room temp in the sleeping room now without her freezing.

She still shows signs of _may be some **demyelination**_: paresthesia / tingling of the tongue, parts of the face and tip of the nose (probably trigeminus), feet and sometimes hands.

We reduced nnEMF as much as possible (in a city flat) and plan to move spring next year to the countryside (even thought about moving further south).
I ordered _Preventa 45 DDW_ and we drink it daily, I mix it with normal mountain spring water. I use a 80ppm concentration for her (I only use tiny amounts myself).
She was sent to do an MRI Selink by her gastroenterologist, there were no signs of inflammation on the intestine walls, there were two strange findings: **Very low location of the gallbladder** and **very low location of the colon** - may be normal anatomic variant. The radiologist does not to seem very confident about it... I never encountered sth similar in my patients (dogs, cats, ...).
Since start of the diet (5 weeks ago), _calprotectin_ values got better. She is _positive_ for **_candida antibodies_** and also _positive_ **_fecal candida culture_**. We did not start anything else about it. We do the low to none carbohydrates, so may be candida gets away - if it is a real diagnosis (not sure).
We plan to do the extended blood work up suggested in the Eli-Paelo-Rx, still looking for a doc who does everything. I'm still fine, despite getting vaccinated twice. Hope it stays like this..
By the way - I recognized a reduced incidence (to none) of those strange clouds over Germany. There is more sun or natural clouds. Anyone else noticed this since the start of the year?
If anyone has some tips, suggestions, I would appreciate them very much.
And thank you everyone for your kind input here in the forum!

> I downloaded that bitcoin document examining discrete vs. continuous time. It reminds me of how theologians call out a distinction between two words for "time" in Greek: _chronos_ and _kairos_. _Chronos_ is the notion of chronological time that scientists sort of take for granted, while kairos refers to something totally different. I am not a theologian but it seems like _kairos_ is about events in some grand schedule that probably serve a highly specific purpose, or _telos._
>
> I am currently exploring Dr. Fosbury's claim of the G-class star that is our Sun. Apparently when hydrogen picks up another electron to become H- it has a consequence in the amount of infrared that arrives here on the surface of the Earth. I guess it is a little murky what's going on up there in the Sun considering Dr. Kruse seems to believe in a lattice vs simple thermal model of how the Sun, and even Dr. Fosbury admits there is a lot of non-equilibrium dynamics up there anyways. But I think at this point in my understanding it is becoming clearer that the electromagnetic profile of the solar irradiance has a truly unique role in shaping life on Earth, hence there is no alternative to sunlight.
>
> If even God created on a daily schedule (on the first day, it was light, second day I think atmosphere, etc) I think it would be wise for us to follow suit.

This idea bridges Information Theory and Thermodynamics, so I like it. You’ve hit on the distinction between the Tick (Chronos) and the Phase (Kairos).
In the Bitcoin document, discrete time is the "Block Height", it’s a sequence of events that creates a "before" and "after." I called it Newtonian time = time is absolute. That is Chronos. Continuous time is the "Network Propagation", the fluid, non-equilibrium flow of energy. That is Kairos. This is Einsteinian Time = time is relative

**1\. Chronos vs. Kairos: The Biological Layers**
Chronos (The Base Layer): This is the DNA/RNA. It is the "discrete" parts list. It sits there like a cold Bitcoin wallet, waiting for an instruction. It is the chronological record of our ancestors, but it has no "torque" on its own.
Kairos (The Operating System): This is Circadian Biology. It is the "Grand Schedule" or telos. It is the timing of when a gene is expressed. Kairos is the "Right Moment", which is the specific window where the Sun’s IR-A thins the water lattice enough for a proton to hop. Without Kairos, the DNA (Chronos) is just a "seized" library.

**2\. The H−Ion: The Sun’s "Infrared Valve"**
Dr. Fosbury’s work on the Negative Hydrogen Ion (H−) really ties back to Robataille work on the electric sun model. Robataille work is the "smoking gun" for why the Sun is an "Infinite Ensemble" and a light bulb is "Quantized Poverty."
The Physics: In the Sun’s photosphere, a neutral Hydrogen atom picks up a second electron to become H−. This ion is extremely "fragile." When it breaks apart, it releases a massive, continuous spectrum of Infrared (IR-A).
The Consequence: This H− transition is what creates the "Goldilocks" IR-A profile that arrives on Earth. It is the specific frequency that thins the metabolic water lattice (the 160-dielectric).
The Electric Sun: If the Sun is a Lattice (the Electric Sun model) rather than a simple fusion ball, then it is a Coherent Oscillator. It isn't just "burning"; it is broadcasting a timing signal (Kairos) to the Earth's water lattice. That is why I favor the electric sun model.

**3\. The Solar Irradiance as the "Unique Master"**
TINA—there is no alternative to sunlight. never will be either but I am not so sure Fosbury or his buddy Zimmerman think this way.
Artificial Light (The Countable Set): It lacks the H− transition. It has no "Infrared Valve." It provides the Chronos (you can see the clock) but it has zero Kairos (it can't trigger the "Right Moment" for the bicarb loop or the 4th ventricle vortex).
Sunlight (The Uncountable Set): Because of the non-equilibrium dynamics of H−, the Sun provides the "Non-Equilibrium Torque" needed to move the 150 ppm deuterium silt. It is the only light source that can "un-weld" the DNA base layer.

**My Decentralized Mechanic's Diagnosis:**
Modernity has tried to replace Kairos with Chronos. We have clocks everywhere, but no "timing." We live in "Discrete Time" (digital screens/nnEMF) while our biology is designed for the "Continuous Lattice" of the Sun.

# Thread: Consistent mG rise toward midday (EMF-390)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### urko — 2026-02-03T17:00:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366292>

Hi everyone, I wanted to share some magnetic field measurements I’ve been taking with a **GQ EMF-390**, since I’m seeing a really consistent pattern depending on the time of day.

I’m located in **The Gambia**, around **13°N latitude**, in a coastal area. I’m mentioning this in case latitude, local grid load patterns, or regional infrastructure makes any difference when interpreting the daily changes I’m seeing in the magnetic field readings.

## Measurements in the same spot (same location, different times)​

All readings below were taken **in the exact same place**, just at different times:

| Time |
Reading (mG)​

 |
| --- | --- |
| 07:38 |

0.7–0.8​

 |
| 09:29 |

0.9​

 |
| 10:51 |

1.5​

 |
| 12:58 |

2.0 (highest)​

 |
| 19:23 |

1.4​

 |

What surprised me is that **the next morning I measured again and got basically the same results at roughly the same times**, with the same “ramp up” toward midday.

## Beach measurement (no obvious AC nearby, some RF present)​

I also took the meter to the **beach**, where there’s **no obvious nearby AC/power infrastructure**, but there is some **RF** around (not especially strong). At **16:30** it was reading **2.0 mG** there as well.

## What I’m trying to figure out​

-   Does it make sense for **magnetic field (mG)** levels to climb steadily toward midday and then drop later, like this?
-   Any ideas what could explain **2.0 mG at the beach** if there’s no obvious electrical infrastructure nearby?
-   For people who use this meter (or similar): do you prefer **peak vs. average**, and is it better to do a longer continuous log to see whether these are brief spikes vs. a sustained level?

If it helps, I can share more details about how I measured (height off the ground, distance to walls/outlets, whether my phone was nearby, etc.).

I attach some pictures of the measurements.

* * *

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-03T20:24:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366312>

I believe the GQ is a single axis meter for magnetic . . . So you have to turn the meter on all 3 axis and then take the highest reading. Once you find the highest reading position put the meter down, what does it read when you are not touching it? Physical contact with a meter can sometimes affect readings significantly.

Outside humidity can affect electric and magnetic field readings also.
One morning (misty humid conditions) I got high electric and magnetic field readings upto 100ft from a power line.
That afternoon (air was now dry) I had to get within 20ft before I got a reading.

Average reading is meaningless. Peak is the important one.

### urko — 2026-02-03T21:13:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366315>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=366312)
>
> I believe the GQ is a single axis meter for magnetic . . . So you have to turn the meter on all 3 axis and then take the highest reading. Once you find the highest reading position put the meter down, what does it read when you are not touching it? Physical contact with a meter can sometimes affect readings significantly.
>
> Outside humidity can affect electric and magnetic field readings also.
> One morning (misty humid conditions) I got high electric and magnetic field readings upto 100ft from a power line.
> That afternoon (air was now dry) I had to get within 20ft before I got a reading.
>
> Average reading is meaningless. Peak is the important one.

I have a picture where I left it on the towel with no human interaction. Here we are on the dry season and at the morning is the moment where the moisture is the highest and the lowest reading. I can tell you that there are no peaks from the measurements. I'm wondering why the EMF is higher at daytime and going low to night time when the moisture is higher.

We believe there is something happening here that local people are doing like generators, or some military stuff. there are military here and 3 RF antennas on the village which is 2.5 km far away from us.

There is also geoengineering like cloud HAARP patterns but we have not seen any flight spraying, but could be boats doing stuff too...

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-04T03:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366336>

RF antennas will affect the RF readings . . . NOT the magnetic.
When you say “the EMF is higher at daytime” that does not help.
EMF is electric and/or magnetic and/or RF. They are all different, you need to be specific.
Magnetic is normally VERY local (within feet) but I have No idea what affect HAARP would have at distance.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-04T03:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366337>

Mag fields are governed by Ampres law.. Your patterns observed are due to demand (i.e. usage peaks). No mystery here.

### LHorn25 — 2026-02-04T00:04:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-366326>

My name is Lianne and I am 43 years old, struggled with infertility for many years but have made peace with that. The the good news was it was the catalyst that started my healing journey. I always considered myself pretty healthy, so it was shocking to me when I could not get pregnant. Had 3 ovarian cysts removed over 20 years but was repeatedly told it would not impact my fertility. I did not meet my husband till I was 37 so advanced age played a role I am sure. I was vegetarian/vegan for a long time and finally stopped once my hair started falling out. Have been eating meat since 2021 and feel a million times better than before.

I am an acupuncturist with my own practice for 14 years and finding it tougher and tougher to get patients better. Plus treating under blue light- now it makes sense. Trying to change the environment I practice in but it's challenging. I am half way through my acupuncture doctorate program when about 2 months ago came across Jack Kruse. I literally have been studying his work more than my schoolwork! Honestly this program is not what I thought and very much in line with centralized medicine. I won't bore you guys with the details. I tell all my patients about Jack's work and try to get them to go outside to view sunrise/set to start.

I am proudly out there in zero degrees looking to the east now every single morning. People think i'm nuts. Just bought blue blockers, Iris software, went to my local spring today for the first time to get quality water, and bought a bunch of red bulbs. I eat salmon, eggs and lamb burgers for breakfast and am mostly keto. Tried cold showers and don't think i'm ready because when my cycle came I had issues so maybe face dunking to start. Some questions I have and would appreciate any guidance:

1\. I am in upstate NY at the 42nd latitude, how can I ground in the mornings with zeros degrees and snow on the ground?

2\. I know CT is rec esp for us who live in the North but i found it messed up my menstrual cycle and oxidized bleeding to almost black and low back pain and cramping. In Chinese Medicine we tell patients to avoid cold bc it stagnates the blood... so CT is not something CM would promote and i am trying to wrap my head around it. Since I had such a negative experience, now how to tell my patients to start doing this?! I believe it works but I just need to reconcile the how as it goes against such ancient medicine where nature was the basis.

3\. Infrared sauna blanket-yes or no? got a Brookstone blanket for Xmas but not sure if it is a good idea- also confused about CT and sauna and when to use which. Or should i use it and then go outside with less clothes? Or then do cold shower?

4\. Vitamin D lamp or tanning bed, or reptile lamp? Can you explain when to use red light therapy? I have stopped supplementing w Vit D but not sure how to optimize my D levels in the winter. Thank you so much for making it till the end

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-04T04:37:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-366340>

1- How to ground in the cold/snow (with clothes/shoes on) . . . And/or when walking and you do not have grounding shoes. (Crystal not required)
2- Not sure Jack’s take on this but I have heard many people have great success with hot/cold therapy. Think Scandinavia, Sauna or Steam followed by cold plunge or a roll in the snow. Hot stimulates blood flow then a brief cold “shock” actually does the same without the heat causing inflammation.
3- Almost every plugged in device (red light whatever) I have tested had HIGH EMF electric field.
4- ALL artificial light is a poor substitute for the big ball in the sky . . . TINA (there is no alternative).
That said remember sun light is 42% (+/-) infra red AND AT THE SAME TIME 5% (+/-) UV.
I live at 42º on the west coast and am looking into moving SOUTH . . . El Salvador, at least for the winter months, I hope to visit back there soon.

VIP - Reduce your nnEMF . . . Buy meters, (if you don’t have) and take control of your space, yes your work space but especially your sleep area at night. . . [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

When the SCN fails your POMC mechanisms on chr 2 fail. YOU GET PALE

When the SCN fails your body fails to properly regenerate the **POMC/melanocortin** systems on Chromosome 2. As your skin becomes less atrophic you dump mass in sweat and your exocrine gut and your brain becomes human again from its atavistic chimp like state tied to shitty thinking.

**The RPE-SCN and "4D" Action:** The interaction between the Retinal Pigment Epithelium (RPE) and the **SCN** creates a feedback loop that governs time-based biology:

**Melanopsin** detects light intensity and transmits that "time" data to the SCN (the body's master clock).
This creates a "4D" temporal framework where the spatial layout (built by HOX genes) is synced to environmental light cycles (via OPN4) to regulate metabolism, sleep, and hormonal output. While HOXD genes provide the structural "hardware," OPN4 acts as the "software" that uses environmental light to calibrate that hardware for survival in space and time.

### stuart31 — 2026-02-23T05:25:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-367433>

> [LHorn25 said:](/goto/post?id=366326)
>
> 2\. I know CT is rec esp for us who live in the North but i found it messed up my menstrual cycle and oxidized bleeding to almost black and low back pain and cramping. In Chinese Medicine we tell patients to avoid cold bc it stagnates the blood... so CT is not something CM would promote and i am trying to wrap my head around it. Since I had such a negative experience, now how to tell my patients to start doing this?! I believe it works but I just need to reconcile the how as it goes against such ancient medicine where nature was the basis.

Hi, Leanne! I’m also a TCM Acupuncturist and pretty new to Dr Kruse’s work.
That is definitely an issue at the heart of TCM as how in order to be healthy, avoiding cold esp in the winter is important. I am also very curious about that! I believe a lot of what we do works for different reasons than what we might think it does.

From the cold thermogenesis series, cold adaptation opens metabolic pathways that eutherian mammals (human ancestors) evolved to thrive in their changing environment. This directly contradicts TCM principles! However, the ideas of cold being GOOD for us go back to how life evolved in the oceans at near freezing temperatures so it is hard-wired into how our biology works and central to most forms of life. The written stories from Chinese Medicine go back ~2000 years so nowhere near the scale of millions of years ago where complex life came from.

Early health decline has been an issue in Chinese Medicine from the very start. From the Nei Jing Su Wen (oldest written recordings on Chinese Medicine) the text opens with the emperor asking his chief physician how come people in the “old days” used to live to 100 years with little to no decline but people nowadays live to 50 and already show signs of aging? At the time of writing during the Warring States period it was an agricultural society = were able to eat high carbohydrates out of season = circadian mismatch. So by definition were not following nature (or in other words their own rules).

As far as recommending cold to clients. That is something I have not done in practice yet. I mostly focus on telling them to get more AM sun, grounding, eliminating artificial light at night and mitigating nnEMF particularly A/C fields in the bedroom as most people I treat are not sleeping. I would be careful recommending CT to patients as people can really hurt themselves. Even Jack says to get cleared by your doctor for things like heart conditions before doing CT. Maybe best to start with recommending face dunks or walking outside in the cold?

I have heard other female mitos having issues with CT as well like what you describe. Before starting it’s important to test for maternal haplotype so people know they are able to uncouple mitochondria in the first place, increase DHA intake beforehand to improve omega 3-6 ratios in tissues and should start **slowly**. CT should truthfully be started after the Leptin Rx. It’s all in the blogs. Beyond that I honestly do not know why females would have complications with doing CT from Jack's perspective.

### [The Cold Thermogenesis Protocol](https://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

What is the next step in the evolution of the Leptin Rx? The Cold Thermogenesis Protocol should be added gradually to the Leptin Rx rest protocol. This blog post is additive to the Leptin Rx, and is an evolution extension of it for those who need it. I hope you all realize that not everyone will

 jackkruse.com
