Stuart, thanks so much for your thoughtful reply. I have been recommending AM light, seafood, blue blockers, Iris software and grounding (cautiously because many wires in the area are buried under ground) and so far have just 1-2 patients taking my suggestions seriously. This helps me understand why Jack gets so frustrated with even his members, haha, because people seem to want the most convenient fix. This is how we have been conditioned and it is a hard program to breeak. I find the few who go all in do reap the biggest rewards.

Where are you located? Are you finding it is becoming more and more difficult to help patients fully recover from issues? I have been in practice for 14 years and I can't be getting "worse" as a practitioner as i am always trying to learn more and more, but in the last few years I am seeing that patients' results from tx do not seem to hold as long. Could also be that as I gain more knowledge, I have been taking on more difficult cases and things like Long Covid did not exist before 2020. Wonder your thoughts on this?
As for the Nei Jing... I did have the thought after I posted, that it seemed they were already living out of harmony with nature a few thousand years ago. It got me thinking that perhaps the "cold" that infiltrates the body that TCM describes is because we are not adapting to our environment the way we are designed to. That living in warm shelters even back then-plus agriculture, etc has been our demise. But it certainly will be one I keep pondering. i keep saying I am going to read the Nei jing cover to cover to gain more clarity- i am staring at the book right now... BUT between catching up on all Jack's blogs, the forum and all the webinars as I just became a gold member... seems there's not enough time in the day

### stuart31 — 2026-02-24T17:09:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-367574>

I’m in Saskatoon, Canada aka way too far north. I’m in my second year of practice so don’t have the experience you do. I’m still figuring things out so am finding my results are getting more consistent just with finding my bearings..Jack’s work, Mae Wan Ho and Becker’s books have been helpful in understanding what it is I’m actually doing.

For me, patients are generally in 3 categories. Those that don’t get better, those that get better right away and those that see results temporarily..That being said, here in Canada during winter people are mostly grounded 0% of the time, pale as can be, constantly exposed to nnEMF. It seems in clinical practice there’s so many factors that play into people’s improvement that it’s difficult to say what is truly helping. The most problematic variable is people’s environment so for me that variable has to change to know what treatments actually help people heal/reach optimal best.

Ideally we need to treat people who are:
\- cold adapted
\- well melanated
\- not living in a blue lit, microwaved environment
\- grounded

In my limited experience on more complex cases, mitigating the harm from centralized medicine is a huge factor. People are often way overmedicated. I just saw a patient who recovered from severe urticaria who has diabetes, heart problems, hypothyroid. Guess what? The hives were a side effect from her heart meds. She had also previously had an anaphylactic reaction to a CCB she was on. Often SSRIs, statins and hypertension meds are actually causing the complaints people come to see me for!

As for Chinese history, have you heard of the tomb of Xin Zhui from the Han dynasty? "The best preserved mummy in history", was a 2000 year old body of a noblewoman. It was so well preserved she had intact skin and internal organs. They did an autopsy and found blood clots that caused her to have a heart attack that killed her. She had liver disease, gallstones, high blood pressure, arteriosclersis and a degenerated lumbar disc that likely caused her severe pain.

### [Meet The Most Well-Preserved Mummy In Human History](https://allthatsinteresting.com/xin-zhui-lady-dai)

How the Lady of Dai remained so shockingly intact for 2,000 years.

 allthatsinteresting.com

Being disconnected from nature goes back a long time. Just look at how much clothing they wore

I have not heard about the tomb of Xin Zhui but this is really eye opening. Makes so much sense. I read Becker's book a couple years ago while studying under Dr. Jeremy Steiner (he is big in the acu world for effects of electro-acupuncture, not sure if you've heard of him?) some how that led me down the rabbit hole and i found Dr. Jack. When he mentioned The Body Electric book a light bulb went off for me! I think we have a lot to offer patients and I think EA is really beneficial. My issue with Jeremy is he is always trying to sell something and it got to be too much "markety" for me so I'm no longer part of his private group- but have learned a lot. Some of his teachings conflict with Jack (like PEMF devices, for example) but other views are exactly in line and it is wild!

### stuart31 — 2026-02-27T17:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-367739>

Wow I love that you brought Steiner up! I heard about him just recently from [@amocchino1](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/41548/) , another Acupuncturist on here. Jeremy's work does seem to line up with Jack's a lot. I did a bit more digging and it seems he learned a lot of what he's teaching from Jerry Tennant who was big into PEMF biomodulator devices. You can find a lot of Jerry's talks on Youtube and he's written a few books as well. Their business models seems very similar. Get you into the funnel and sell you more and more stuff.

They offer a lot of big promises as well but do seem to get into the realm of pseudoscience so left me skeptical.

What Jerry and subsequently Jeremy say about filling up the acu channels with electrons does seem to make sense but feels too much like a one stop solution. If we truly could use EA to exogenously restore "voltage" in channels akin to the photoelectric effect from sun exposure, eating abundant seafood and grounding, there's a few problems with that.

\- we would need to have hydrated collagen networks and water proton cables in the first place to not overpower the existing "wiring"
or
\- if we have high heteroplasmy

in both cases the treatment would cause massive amounts of inflammation rather than having the desired effects.

I did make a post in the healthcare practitioners forum asking about these concerns over electroacupuncture vs standard. In the last Q&A Jack referred to TENS devices being good for short term use but long term can be problematic. I'd assume the same would hold true for EA. The power even from microcurrent devices is just so much greater than endogenous currents. Becker also talks a bit about concerns with EA in his book Cross Currents. What acu can do though is stimulate UPE's and photorepair as mentioned here:

### [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1AuQyQJwHo/)

 www.facebook.com

Maybe Jack can jump in here if I'm talking out of my ass.

Interestingly a lot of research on acupuncture does use electric stimulation so for sure it has benefits. Jack's recent FB post above references acu studes using EA to increase brown adipose tissue (increasing melanin in fat) and stem cells in blood circulation which blew me away.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-27T19:01:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-367752>

The claims of adding electrons to a acu circuit is pure nonsense.

I am at a crossroads between putting forth all this effort and money into something that might be futile to try and help others, or just leave all together.

# Thread: Decentralized perspective on wisdom teeth?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fouadfo1 — 2026-02-04T04:00:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/post-366338>

Hello everyone, so I have what my dentist says are impacted wisdom teeth on my lower jaw. I have been in off and on pain for the last 2 years trying to remain patient because I do not want to remove them with the hopes that with time they will perhaps ‘settle in’.

However, during this time frame, I noticed that my tinnitus actually coincided with the jaw pain I started to experience 2 years ago. The tinnitus has only increased in intensity, and on my ‘bad days’ when my jaw is tense and painful, the tinnitus seems to be particularly intense.

I’m wondering what the best solution here would be, I’m concerned that removing my wisdom teeth would result in further complications.

Any ideas? Thanks

### Shannon Eavenson — 2026-02-04T05:41:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/post-366344>

Is it better to try to expand your jaw with special devices or exercises? I have held back on getting my daughters pulled. My son took out only one side because one tooth was growing sideways but he kept the other side top and bottom two years ago age 23. I don't know I just know DR> Jack was talking about how it would be better to keep the wisdom teeth.

### S Hudson — 2026-02-04T20:57:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/post-366368>

Not enough information to be of assistance I'm afraid. I'd need radiographs, intra-oral photos and a full clinical history of the symptoms as and when they presented.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-06T17:42:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/post-373221>

How is your Wednesday going? Just came off the beach for AM sunrise and spent 3 hours there. Now I will begin resisting my guild. Decentralized reistance is worth doing in a dystopian fiat world like centralized medicine.

This is a summary of what it is :

The Phiray represents a pinnacle advancement in bio-active plasma field technology, engineered as a phase-conjugate centripetal plasma generator that harnesses the precise fractal recursion of the golden ratio (Phi, φ ≈ 1.618) to facilitate negentropic charge collapse and implosive coherence in biological systems. At its core, the device operates on the principle of longitudinal electromagnetic wave interferometry, where counter-rotating plasma toroids—energized by high-voltage, low-current noble gas discharges (primarily argon and neon mixtures)—are precisely phase-locked to a broadband magnetic cascade array tuned to the Schumann resonance harmonics (approximately 7.83 Hz fundamental, with cascading multiples up to 33 Hz for enhanced alpha-brainwave entrainment).

In thermodynamic terms, the Phiray induces a negentropic reversal of entropy through perfected charge compression: the plasma bulbs, positioned in a conjugate opposition geometry, create a centripetal 'bliss cocoon' field wherein electromagnetic flux lines implode fractally, accelerating charge towards planck-scale symmetry (the quantum vacuum threshold where gravity emerges from charge rotation). This implosion is mathematically governed by the recursive embedding of Phi-ratioed wave nodes, ensuring non-destructive interference that amplifies longitudinal (scalar) wave propagation—waves that penetrate biological tissues far more efficiently than transverse EM fields, as per the equations of phase conjugation optics (e.g., time-reversed four-wave mixing, where ψ\* = φ^{-n} for n recursive iterations).

Functionally, the Phiray restores bio-coherence by realigning cellular and auric field fractality: it phase-conjugates disruptive entropic charge distributions (e.g., from EMF pollution or psycho-emotional dissonance) into negentropic order, thereby enhancing membrane potential, ATP production via mitochondrial implosion, and DNA braid coherence (modeled as a Phi-spiral solenoid for charge embedding). Empirical observations indicate profound effects on psychophysiological states, including induction of peak bliss experiences (correlated with golden ratio EEG harmonics), accelerated tissue regeneration through increased charge radiance (measurable as biophoton emission), and mitigation of phase-dissonant pathologies such as addiction, depression, and inflammatory cascades. Furthermore, at advanced scales, the device's programmable magnetic coil synchronization enables speculative applications in portal physics, where sustained implosive fields may facilitate phase-conjugate access to higher-dimensional charge symmetries, akin to stargate dynamics via recursive embedding.In essence, the Phiray embodies the fractal field science of life force itself: by engineering the precise geometry of implosion, it recreates the conditions under which biology originates—charge acceleration through perfect embedding—thus serving as a therapeutic accelerator for human evolution towards centripetal consciousness.

# Thread: No more Blue Blockers in my house!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-blue-blockers-in-my-house.32234/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TommyM — 2026-02-06T19:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-blue-blockers-in-my-house.32234/post-366445>

A few months ago I converted all of my lights, ceiling lights, bulbs, etc., to Incandescent bulbs from Healthlighting.com. Everyone loves them. I wore blue blockers in the house while using my laptop, phone, or when the TV was on, but I hate wearing blue blockers in my house, and they leak from the sides so I did a little research. It turns out that the theatrical/photography industry makes colored gel films for spotlights and lighting effects. I found a company called ROSCO and they publish the light filtering/blocking specs of their various gel films. After some experimentation I narrowed my selection to the two films below.

I use the Roscolux Medium Amber #20 on my two 65" TV's. I trimmed the film and it clings to the screen naturally through static electric bond. I use tiny amounts of scotch tape at the top. Ideally my goal is to eliminate TV altogether, but this works great and it's easy on the eyes, skin, and brain.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

For my laptop and desk monitor I use Roscolux #21 Golden Amber Gel Film. For the laptop, I cut the film to size and had it laminated at the local UPS store, and use sticky back magnetic tape for attachment. For my large monitor, the film sticks naturally. The real life image below, appears darker than it actually is when backlit by my screens.

For my iPhones I use Roscolux Medium Amber #20. I cut the film and place it over my glass screen and use a two part phone case with a built in screen protector.

The Rosco Roscolux Gel Films are available in various sizes:
20" X 24" - $11.00 -$15.00
48" X 25' Roll - $170

Available at
[Stagelightingstore.com](https://www.stagelightingstore.com/Rosco-E-Colour-020-Medium-Amber-48-x-5-Roll?srsltid=AfmBOoqG8TKJGfWgX66-OYNOZwMuUmoHXqNyhbO2vZvZ6MXTWUFH7Q5L3uo)
[BHPhotovideo.com](https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/163249-REG/Rosco_102300204825_E_Colour_020_Medium_Amber.html)
Fullcompass.com

I suggest you purchase two 20"X 24" rolls of each color and see what works for you.

No more Blue Blockers in the house anymore. Hope this help on your journey to becoming a Mitochondriac

Thanks Dr. Jack for pointing the way!

Reading your blog: DECENTRALIZED MEDICINE #90: MELANIN EVOLUTION 2

_"What do tennis & baseball players avoid in their jobs? They avoid polarized lenses in glasses and sunglasses.
Do you know why?
Polarized lenses decrease depth perception in the ocular system by altering the noise in the leptin melanocortin pathways, making it harder to judge the distance and speed of the tennis or baseball in their sports."_

I discovered this a few years ago after I took up mountain biking. Whenever I wore my sunglasses i felt like they were interfering with my depth perception so I stopped wearing sunglasses while riding and driving my car. I've stopped wearing them completely after understand the effect of polarized light from your writings.

I've stopped watching TV's too.

My goal over the next 60 days is to read all blogs from the beginning.

**Subforum:** The Cave

### DLO — 2026-02-07T05:25:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-soldiers.32218/post-366464>

Listen to this in the dark and picture what the Germans felt.
Somehow images just couldn't capture this.

### DLO — 2026-02-09T18:09:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-soldiers.32218/post-366599>

I dont see any mito connection

> [S Hudson said:](/goto/post?id=366511)

### S Hudson — 2026-02-10T00:38:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-soldiers.32218/post-366616>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=366599)
>
> I dont see any mito connection

Well, at first i got thrown for a tangent because the Mito Regiment was part of the Imperial Japanese Army's 14th Division, serving in the Second Sino-Japanese War before being deployed to the Pacific Theatre. The term Mito also references the Jinrai Butai kamikaze Special Attack Corp. And yet, none of your videos were related to the Japanese. So then I'm thinking, being where we are in this quantum forum, your use of the term **"Mito Soldiers"** referred to the health philosophy cantered on cellular energy, and you are thus using Native American soldiers as a symbolic or literal example of "peak" human biology.

Now let's look at my examples:

**Paddy Mayne**
He was an international rugby player and amateur boxer known for "Herculean" strength, such as allegedly ripping control panels out of aircraft with his bare hands. Mayne had a reputation for incredible night vision and the ability to navigate or "sense" the enemy in total darkness. He spent years living in the harsh desert and European wilderness.

**Carton De Weart**
He was shot in the face, head, stomach, ankle, leg, hip, and ear- suffering 11 injuries in all over numerous wars. When a doctor refused to amputate his shattered fingers in 1915, he reportedly bit them off himself. A person with poorly mitochondria would have died from infection or systemic inflammation after just one of those injuries. He survived two plane crashes, escaped a POW camp by tunnelling out at age 61 (staying on the run for 8 days), and survived a broken back later in life.
He had incredible cellular repair and stress tolerance

**Anders Lassen**
Like the Native American scouts, Lassen was an expert hunter from childhood who preferred the outdoors to school. During his training in the Scottish Highlands, Lassen reportedly stalked and killed a stag using only a commando knife.
Lassen had superior night vision and was famous for his night raids. His Victoria Cross citation describes him single-handedly destroying three machine-gun nests in total darkness and killing 8 enemy soldiers at Lake Comacchio, despite being mortally wounded.

If these 3 men didn't have optimised mitochondria, I don't know who has
All three possessed extreme physical endurance and sensory awareness, which can easily be attributed to high-functioning mitochondria

# Thread: Chris’s Optimal Journal. VA living,wife and two kids.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal-va-living-wife-and-two-kids.32236/>

### Cdavet24 — 2026-02-07T22:52:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal-va-living-wife-and-two-kids.32236/post-366497>

I am starting an optimal health journal after speaking with Dr. Kruse. I’m very thankful for the conversation [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) as it really shifted my perspective and immediately pointed me in the right direction and got me to stop asking questions that don’t matter. I genuinely had never even considered moving, but now that is all I can think about. Upon further investigation and speaking with my wife, we had decided on three states: Florida, Alabama, or Georgia. With me being a contractor, I did further research on the three states, and although Florida was our prime choice, roofing contractors don’t do great there because of crappy insurance policies due to all the storms and apparently a lot of fraud. I actually worked for a roofing company a few years ago that had to close their office in Florida because of the difficulty there. We have decided on Alabama and we are making a plan within the next 2 to 3 years to head down there and I am wondering if near somewhere south of Montgomery Alabama is a safe place to live? We are wanting to do somewhere near Montgomery because of the available businesses and church proximity. I am spending a lot of money on EMF proofing our house as our new baby was born yesterday. I will come back with questions and concerns during this time but one question I had was how much deuterium depleted water should I be giving my family? I purchased 10ppm.

She mentioned Orange Beach AL, Tybee Island GA and Savannah GA. The Texas coast in our opinion is not great. I haven’t researched yet but I’m looking for places like Dr. K describes Destin, FL - low population off season. Rural is better. I haven’t found a better resource yet but antennasearch.com gives you an idea of tower/antenna locations.
As for the DDW I have not heard Dr K put restrictions on drinking for adults or children. Also maybe consider low DW brands like Icelandic or Voss. I think it comes down to cost and none of it is cheap. Hope you find the perfect place.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-glossary-of-biochemical-terms-from-grok.32237/post-366507>

I was trying to read a paper titled Dynamics and Environment of Mitochondrial Water as Detected by 1H NMR, and I'm not really that keen on biochemical studies, so I asked Grok to make me a glossary of the biochemical terms used as well as provide an analogy of the relaxation with coupled oscillators which I am more familiar with. it's attached. It's a bit more than a glossary actually because Grok read the paper and the results of the paper are sprinkled in the glossary.

### flavin0 — 2026-02-08T04:59:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-glossary-of-biochemical-terms-from-grok.32237/post-366527>

Okay, I think I have an improved mechanistic mental model on what Dr. Kruse is really describing every time he mentions something about the mitochondria. I was inspired by the latest interview between Dr. Sara Pugh and Dr. Glen Jeffry.

The mitochondria can be approximated as a circuit with four complexes, connected not by the wires you'd imagine in an electrical circuit but by hydrogen bonding networks in the uniquely confined "nanowater" between complexes. Energy is defined in physics as the capacity for performing useful work. The useful work we get out of the mitochondria is the ability to live, breath, move, think, etc. and the complexes might be exploiting the motion of charge carriers between complexes to supply the resources to do the aforementioned useful work.

Recall from intro electromagnetism class that every conductive material such as a copper wire has an innate baseline resistance to the motion of charge carriers through it. This is sort of hand waved away a little bit in intro E&M but it is because the motion of charge carriers is somewhat messy (maybe turbulent in a nonliving system, but that may be a stretch considering turbulence is a term to denote fluid motion and classical electrodynamics shies away from characterizing simple circuits with fluid dynamics), and it takes the application of an electrical potential gradient to basically ensure a neat, macroscopic, classical vectorial direction of motion of charge carriers.

Anyways, the resistivity in a copper wire is analogous to the viscosity of the confined water molecules. Or, another way to put it is that the baseline relatively higher viscosity of the confined water over regular water molecules floating about in a bulk volume is like the baseline resistivity to the motion of charge carriers (jargon alert: via the "Grotthuss mechanism") of the mitochondria's nanowater hydrogen bonding network.

The analogy between coupled oscillators and the hydrogen bonding network is key: the water molecules are connected like a matrix of masses (atoms) connected by springs (bonds). From modern physics class we know infrared is going to act more wavelike than particle-like as a general rule of thumb. The spectral reason that sunlight is so much better than artificial lighting is that infrared acts as an external factor that improves the motion of charge carriers between complexes by altering the viscosity via a wave-like and not particle-like mechanism, probably affecting the modes of oscillation rather than direct particle collision type phenomena.

Obviously coupled oscillator problems get messy quickly, the most of them avoiding closed-form analytic solutions. And we are talking about a bunch of masses coupled by springs so this is as far as I can go mechanistically at this point. And also I still don't know much about the Grotthuss mechanism.

What do you guys think? Am I getting AI psychosis a bit or am I on the right track

# Thread: Starlink Solution from China
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starlink-solution-from-china.32238/>

**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2026-02-08T20:35:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starlink-solution-from-china.32238/post-366543>

**A new, compact, high-power microwave weapon, the TPG1000Cs, has been developed at a Shanghai Nuclear Technology Institute, which could become one of the most serious threats to the Starlink satellite network.**

The device can deliver 20 gigawatts of energy for up to a full minute, the South China Morning Post reported, cited by [Portfolio](https://www.portfolio.hu/global/20260205/bekovetkezett-az-amitol-rettegtek-az-egyesult-allamokban-pillanatok-alatt-semlegesithetik-amerika-legnagyobb-erosseget-816196).

The TPG1000Cs, the world’s first compact driver for high-power microwave weapons, has been created at the Northwest Institute of Nuclear Technology in Shanghai. The device can deliver 20 gigawatts of power for up to one minute.

At just four meters long and weighing just five tons, the device is small enough to be mounted on trucks, warships, airplanes, or even satellites. Some Chinese experts estimate that a ground-based microwave weapon with a power of over 1 gigawatt could be capable of seriously disrupting or even damaging satellites in low Earth orbit, such as Starlink, being used in the Russian-Ukrainian war.

Previously known similar systems could operate continuously for no more than three seconds and were much larger. The Russian Sinus-7 drive, for example, was operational for about a second, delivered about 100 pulses per shot, and weighed up to 10 tons.

China has repeatedly signaled that Starlink poses a serious threat to its national security. Chinese military researchers are currently developing new “Starlink killer” weapons, including high-powered microwave systems and lasers, that could be used to relatively cheaply combat large constellations of low-orbit satellites if necessary.

SpaceX has lowered the orbital altitude of its Starlink satellites to reduce the risk of collisions. But that makes them much more vulnerable to attacks from ground-based directed energy weapons. If China eventually deploys the TPG1000Cs in space, the invisible strikes could be even more devastating.

I would appreciate if someone could suggest a light that would mimic the sunrise to cover UVA and red light. Is it OK to use a red light indoors after sunset?

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-11T02:26:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/xanthelasma.32244/post-366668>

In the context of my thesis, **xanthelasma** (the yellow cholesterol deposits on the eyelids) is the ultimate **"Red Flag on the CRT Screen of the RPE."**
It is not a "fat" problem; it is a **localized dielectric failure** of the periorbital neuroectodermal system. It represents the body’s desperate attempt to build a **"Lead Shield"** around the eye because the melanin-water-light stoichiometry in that zip code has been obliterated by blue light stress and nnEMF.

# Thread: Trees and leafy friends blocking  5g
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paul T008 — 2026-02-11T16:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/post-366697>

Does any one know why the trees and hedges block 5g signals ,
I know trees reflect Infar red light, from the the leaf itself in scatter pattern from the sun
but also trees are grounded from the root system guessing picking up electrons from the earth.
but then there is it the Chlorophyll within the leaf itself i thinking this may block the signal of 5g
is 5g a blue wavelength maybe that the chlorophyll absorbs this.
has any one any ideas which of any of the above or other thoughts to why trees and hedges block 5g nature
the council in the UK have a habit of felling the trees to improve the 5 g signal

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-12T01:03:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/post-366729>

Trees, hedges, and foliage contain a high amount of water in their leaves, branches, and trunks. Water molecules absorb radio waves, especially at higher frequencies, converting the signal energy into heat and reducing its strength. This is why wet foliage (e.g., after rain) exacerbates the issue.

The dense structure of leaves, needles (in evergreens), and branches scatters the waves in multiple directions, diffusing the signal and preventing it from maintaining a direct path to the receiver. This is more pronounced in leafy seasons; for example, studies show signal loss can drop by several decibels in winter when deciduous trees lose their leaves.

Research from the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) measured average signal losses of around 35.3 dB through evergreens and 22-27 dB through leafy deciduous trees, which can reduce signal power by factors of 1,000 or more in dense areas like forests.

This makes mmWave 5G less reliable in wooded or suburban areas with heavy vegetation, often requiring more base stations or fallback to lower-frequency bands for coverage.

Lower-frequency 5G bands (sub-6 GHz) are less affected by trees and can penetrate better, similar to 4G, but they don't offer the same ultra-high speeds.

### Paul T008 — 2026-02-12T10:56:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/post-366734>

As a Gardner will have to encourage the tribe/customers to plant hedges and trees around there font garden to mess 5g electric and gas smart meter signals will be reduced . many thanks for the information

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-12T18:41:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/post-366750>

Not sure but I believe electric and gas “smart” meters are sub 1Ghz so have no problem penetrating foliage or walls.

5G is a technology not a set frequency so lower frequency 5G as Jack said, can penetrate (AT&T uses 3-4GHz) where higher frequencies cannot. 20 GHz+ 5G can also be blocked by glass. Check the frequencies being used in your area.

So, overall, after a month having my days outside, my baseline is slightly better, my highs higher and lows lower (twice a week I go into an energy crisis and can't walk).

### mrcjatx — 2026-02-13T05:39:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-finally-sleep.32255/post-366809>

I wanted to take a moment to thank Dr. Kruse.

After coming across his content, my world has shifted.

Most notably, a life long sleeping issue has become largely extinguished.
I say largely extinguished because when I do have challenges, it is because I've
strayed from the suggested protocols.

Sleeping better helps one realize just how precious time is. Being able to think with more
clarity is a gift.

Thank you Dr. Jack - I will not be able to repay you for what you've done for me.

# Thread: Gregg's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greggs-journal.32256/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### grclark_w — 2026-02-13T13:19:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greggs-journal.32256/post-366816>

My name is Gregg. I have been here for about 8 months, and it is time to start my journal.

I’m a 53-year-old male, living at 38 ° latitude in Kansas for most of my life. I am a Fitzpatrick Skin-Type II. I am awaiting my haplotype results and will post as soon as I receive them.

I very much resonate with Jack’s work. I like his attitude. I like his approach. I am amazed by his knowledge. I have worked with semiconductors my whole career, so his work naturally aligns with how I think.

**Family History –** I was close to all my family growing up, and have remained close as an adult.

**Maternal

Great-Grandfather**

Died at 103, mostly due to old age

**Grandmother**

Died of cancer in 2006 at 79 years of age

**Grandfather**

Died at 85 in 2008. I would say mostly due to the loss of a lifelong spouse, he just decided he was done.

**Mother**

Diagnosed with Krones disease when she was 35 years old, but still alive and doing well at the age of 75.

**Paternal History

Grandmother**

Passed away at the age of 90, mostly due to old age.

**Grandfather**

Died due to diabetes complications at 88 years of age

**Father**

Alive and kicking and going strong at the age of 76

**Brief History**
I grew up in a rural area in eastern Kansas. As a kid, I spent almost all my time outside. Once I went to college and started my career, I was inside most of the time. I got married when I was 21. We had our first child immediately. We had a second child four years later. Upon graduating from college, we moved to Austin, Texas, for six years. Then we moved back to eastern Kansas and have been here ever since.

In 2001, I started my own business designing reactor protection systems for commercial nuclear power plants. I sold my first business in 2009. Then, in 2010, I had the opportunity to design reactor protection systems for the new small modular reactors. So, I started another company and ultimately sold it in 2021.

**Past 6 years**

We started experimenting with low-carb diets in the 2000s. I think I dove into the low-carb/keto in 2015/2016, then got into intermittent fasting with a 6/18 window. I did that for a couple of years, probably from 2017 to 2020. I also started lifting less and riding a bike more. I got into “gravel grinding”, riding on the gravel roads around Coffey County. I then started riding my bike with a trainer in the winter months, using Zwift. In late 2019 and early 2020, I was riding hard. I would push myself very hard for 60-90 minutes doing ZWIFT races. Then, in February of 2020, I had a sort of catastrophic moment. Here is the write-up I sent to our naturopathic physician. This was the new patient write-up I provided to him.

“I have been having a lot of various ongoing symptoms that I cannot figure out the cause of. It started Sunday, 2/23/2020. I had been awake early and did an intense ride. Afterwards, I had a very bad dizzy, lightheaded spell, we called 911, and by the time the first responders got here, I had a Blood Pressure of 160/120. They took me to the hospital and monitored me. My blood pressure came back down to the 120/70 range after being in the hospital for 4-5 hours. I stayed the night and went home the next morning. I felt pretty good the rest of that week. They performed a nuclear stress test on Friday, 2/28, and all results looked very good. Then, on the following Saturday, 2/29, around 6 PM, I experienced an adrenaline rush that lasted just over an hour. It is worth noting that, in the lead-up to the Nuclear Stress Test, I stopped drinking coffee on Tuesday, 2/25. I had more adrenaline rushes over the next week. I resumed drinking coffee on Wednesday, 3/4, and slept reasonably well by Thursday evening, 3/5. Before that, I would get an adrenaline rush Episode when I was trying to go to sleep. When I had the adrenaline rush episodes, my BP would spike to the 160/120 range and then subside back down to the 130-140/85-80 range. Since coffee resumed, I have not had any adrenaline rushes.

I spent the rest of 2020 and 2021 being very careful with myself. I reduced my training intensity, cut back on biking, and lifted only in short superset sessions to maintain some muscle.

At the same time, from 2017 to 2021, my business was growing rapidly. I would push myself hard to make everything possible for the business.

In 2020/2021, I pretty much went on a fresh-foods diet. Lots of fruit, vegetables, and meat, no alcohol.

In December 2021, I sold my business. This was a big emotional moment: on the one hand, lots of relief; on the other, lots of unknowns about the future. We enjoyed the holidays that year. I was more relaxed than I had been in years. After the holidays, it hit me… I don’t want to be an employee for this company that just bought me out. I realized how much I hate working for someone. Then, at the end of January, my right ear started ringing and kept ringing for the rest of that year. In the spring of 2022, we decided to buy a lovely house in the Texas Hill Country, outside New Braunfels, TX, as a second home. We spent the early part of 2022 looking for a house and bought one in April 2022. We spent most of 2022 down there, but my ear ringing kept getting worse. My overall health fell off a cliff in the fall of 2022, so much so that I was only getting 1-3 hours of poor sleep a night. There were months that I felt like someone had pumped me full of medium-strength adrenaline; I just could not relax and could not sleep. I finally began to extricate myself from this difficult situation in February 2023. I began to improve by the summer of 2023 and enjoyed that summer. However, my right ear still rings significantly to this day. I also got a weird, full-head feeling headache off and on for most of 2022. It was not a sinus-type headache; it was an overall pressure in the head. And to this day, I have a “regular” schedule of 2 days or so of feeling low energy, and ear ringing goes up. Then I will feel good for 3-5 days, with lots of energy and the ringing way down. It’s like something clicks on and off within me on a 4 good/2 bad sort of schedule.

**Past year**

I have been under a lot of stress again this past year. My wife of 32 years was diagnosed with triple-negative breast cancer in April 2025, and I lost her in late September 2025. I put my health journey on hold and focused on her. Knowing what I know now, if we could go back 2 years, she would still be here…

Now it is time for me to focus on myself, essentially for the first time since before I got married and had kids.

### grclark_w — 2026-02-14T16:05:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greggs-journal.32256/post-366881>

I am going to start adding detail to my journal with observations I have made about myself and my "condition," as well as thoughts I have that tie into the multitude of material I have been reading from Dr. Kruse. I will also list the actions I have taken.

**Observations:**

1) When I have my 2 days or so of bad, I experience the increased ringing amplitude first, followed by loss of energy, and eventually, I will get the feeling of inflammation in the right ear area. It is typically in this order. When the feeling of inflammation comes on, I will notice that I get "popping" noises in that area, which seem to be pressure relief events. I can also press on areas around my ear to vary the frequency and amplitude. This leads me to believe that the pressure of the surrounding areas is playing a major role.

2) I bought a darkfield microscope a couple of years ago. I haven't used darkfield much, but I have used normal mode to view my live blood. What I notice on the "bad" days is a rouleaux formation of the blood. I think this implies a low Zeta Potential, or the inability of the RBCs to repel one another.

3) Another tie into this, not surprisingly, is mild sleep apnea. I have noticed that when I fall asleep on the couch, I often open my mouth and breathe deeply. This then causes an almost instant increase in the ringing amplitude.

**Thoughts:**

1) I am somewhat all over the map with my thoughts. I have read a lot of Dr. Kruse's work, and much of it I can relate to directly. I am just trying to distill this information down into a logical collection.

2) Hypoxia is a key to this. Reading Hypoxia #22 reveals many aspects to me. Then I read CPC #49, and I think about Tinnitus as a sensory processing disorder... I just don't think that is all that I have going on. Hypoxia, at large, seems to tie into everything I feel on this persistent, periodic basis.

3) Given the persistence and periodicity of my "condition", there must be a life cycle going on. I say this because I have altered everything I can think of, and the cycle persists. For me, this leads me to my gut and makes me think of a podcast where Dr. Kruse talked about a normal gut turning over every 24-48 hours and a "bad" gut turning over many days. In my case, 7-8 days.

4) Dehydrated melanin? Leads me to thoughts on deuterium. I have been consuming DDW by mixing a 10PPM DDW solution with Icelandic water to achieve 90-100PPM DDW. The cycle still persists, regardless of drinking primarily DDW or not.

**Actions:**

1) I have watched sunrises consistently since I started following Dr. Kruse. My typical routine is to go out to my yard (I live in a rural area with no close neighbors) and watch the sunrise naked, like the Spinx, while doing planks and such.

2) I have increased my consumption of DHA from seafood significantly.

3) I am outside again almost all day. No pressures of making a living for me, so I get to choose what I do with my time.

4) I sleep in the dark; my house has only incandescent lighting. I go to dark mode when the sun sets, with only a little deep red turned on.

5) I use a Daylight tablet now and blue-blocking glasses plus Iris when using my normal computer screen.

6) DDW consumption as mentioned above

7) I raise and consume my own grass finished needle free beef. I can't get a much better sunlight bar code than that

8) Trying to eat seasonal and local as much as possible

9) No sunglasses for several years now

10) Minimize my exposure to nnEMF, no wifi in the home, all hardwired. Minimize my use of the mobile phone, never have it to my head. Absolutely no Bluetooth shit. (Interesting side note: I was one of the leads on a team at Motorola Semiconductor that implemented the first functional Bluetooth radio.) I was mostly involved in the baseband and software stack development, so I was not around all the intense RF side development.)

### Mr.AJ — 2026-02-15T17:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greggs-journal.32256/post-366919>

Welcome, Gregg

I'm on the sleep apnea train as well.

Would just add 2 things:

1) Candles are the absolute best light if you have to use something post sunset. Or a fireplace. Fire is partially unpolarized.

2) Two surprisingly big movers for me with SA have been grounding both AM and PM but especially during sunset before bed. The other being sleeping on the ground floor or lowest floor in the home. Having your "third prong" plugged in (improving coherence) when sleeping is a big mover.

### grclark_w — 2026-03-01T16:16:19+0200

I have been getting lots of sun: sunrise barefoot walks with maximum skin exposure, lots of daytime sun, grounded sunset walks, and sleeping on the lowest level of the house. I have been mixing my DDW to ~85PPM and drinking at least 500 ml first thing in the morning and 500ml before bed, along with more throughout the day.

Overall, sleep is improved significantly. Overall feeling really good on the "good days".

Started doing a look at my live blood in the mornings. Two examples here, yesterday and today. Yesterday was a "good day", but I started to feel a little off in the afternoon. Today I can feel/hear the ringing coming on. Take a look at the live blood!

# Thread: Hearing aids

**Posts:** 1

### Paul Fieber — 2026-02-14T21:02:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hearing-aids.32258/post-366890>

Hi

I went an ENT and he says I need hearing aids. I asked if there was AI involved and he said yes. Jack has always said never to wear anything in the ear canals?
How do I fix a hearing issue, as I would for sure never want AI.

# Thread: Saturn's Return - Mellica Schmidt - Canada
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MellicaSchmidt — 2026-02-14T22:31:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/post-366895>

My name is Mellica Schmidt. I'm 28, from India originally but I've been living in Vancouver, BC Canada for almost 20 years now. **I've put in bold what's most medically relevant for short reading.** In future posts I might cut to the chase with medical info but I'm finding it really helpful to treat this as a proper journal.

I always like bad news first so here goes:

My two biggest mistakes in life are:
**1\. Took birth control for about nine years (pills for most of it, 2-3 years of the Mirena IUD)
2\. Agreed to 2x Moderna jabs**

I've never been one to shy away from consequences and I know what I get will be no less than I deserve, so I try and take these on the chin best I can. When I was introduced to Jack's work eight months ago (I had to do the math because I thought it had been a couple years) the only thing I had to think about was trying to learn what "cytochrome c oxidase" meant (still working on its intricacies), and now it's, "how do we leave Canada, where do want to go, is it too late, where can we afford to go..."etc etc etc.

Medically, I'm facing potential **infertility**.
**AMH levels came back low (9.7)
AFC showed my right ovary's count was 9** (low-average I was told). My **left ovary's count was 4 (low)** and I was floored with the discovery of a **7cm cyst** in there. All I was told was not to get it removed because I'd likely lose eggs during the surgery. **Going for a blood test to help diagnose what kind of cyst it is**. They also want a sonohysterogram to check if my tubes are blocked etc. I know the ultrasound/SIS are radiating my body but I decided it was worth the risk to find out what's going on. I think after I get answers I'll abandon the clinic, since they will likely push IUI/IVF which I don't consider an option. **Birth control ruined my cycle but over the past three years I've been slowly recovering** through the only supplement I still reliably take from my naturopath. It's a mix of chasteberry, ginkgo berry leaf, and vit B6 to help my body make its own progesterone again.

I think **nnEMFS over the years are probably one of the biggest contributers to lack of fecundity**. I've been working as a camera technician for 7-8 years, being bombarded with radiowaves, and subject to really poor light and circadian rhythm (often in dark studios and shooting overnight) for 10-14 hours a day.
I told my boss today that after this next gig, which ends next week, **I'm leaving the industry.** I have no idea what I'll do for income after that. I fantasize (and I know I'm not the first) about opening a retreat focussed on all the things light, water, and magnetism, even though I have neither the capital nor the expertise...yet.

**We also live two blocks from a substation.

Living at the 54th latitude when I was born at the 33rd** is also a big issue. My Vit D was 142nmol/L end of 2024 but I think back then I was taking Vit D oil, I'm getting it tested again soon. **I do own a Sperti D and use a hybrid tanning bed.** The increase in my mood after tanning is nothing short of miraculous, but I can't afford to go as frequently as I'd like. One of the saddest days of the year for me is the summer solstice because the days afterwards get short so rapidly despite the warm temperatures that linger till September.

Other things of note-
\-I might have **tinnitus,** my ears ring when I go to bed and when I wake up in the morning. During the daytime it's not really noticeable. I know there was a recent blog about this that I'm hoping I can get to soon.
\-Food is fine in general but I have **very strong cravings for sugar**, certain processed foods and sometimes carbs.
\-If it isn't obvious by now I'm a highly stressed individual, by nature, lack of enough UV all my life, and by current circumstances. **My cortisol in a DUTCH test last year showed my waking level at 87.7ng/mg**, a whole 37 points higher than the high range, and it **crashes in the early afternoon**. I try meditating and I use an acupressure mat everyday which helps, but I'm pretty sure I was probably constantly in a sympathetic state from around age 12 to a few years ago, for multiple reasons which I'm sure hasn't helped anything.

\-Other potentially relevant facts from my DUTCH test:
**Testosterone 2.33ng/mg - low
Dopamine markers low- HVA 3.2 ug/mg
Norepinephrine/Epinephrine marker low- VMA 2.0 ug/mg**
Aside from these and the cortisol issue everything else was in range.

That's probably enough of the bad. Now the good.

My husband is the best thing in my life. He's 36, a blond-haired blue-eyed Canadian with German/British ancestry, and the only rock in my life. He's my circle of six. He is my good news. Even just writing this much about him makes me more optimistic.

**I do get out at sunrise everyday** (sometimes my work interferes but even then I at least try to ge 2-5min). At home, even though we're near a substation and in a densely populated city, in lieu of a backyard we have a south-facing rooftop patio which has been a lifesaver for sun exposure.

**I sleep really well**, I rarely have trouble falling asleep and sleep through the night (usually get up to pee like two hours before sunrise but that's it). The only time this is disrupted is if I'm not careful about controlling my blue light exposure then sometimes I get up in the middle of the night for a while. Stress will do the same too.

I started painting with watercolors. My hobbies are generally just reading and learning about quantum biology these days so I needed something different.

I'm taking Alexis Cowan's QuantumU course which has been really fun and challenging.

I have taken steps to change our house to be more circadian friendly, with adding some red bulbs, swapping LEDs etc. Thankfully sunrise has come back to right before 7am so we're entering the sweet spot before it gets bright at 4:30. Next will be hardwiring wifi.

We generally eat healthy, I found a new fish market yesterday to up our DHA intake. I eat caviar like a supplement. I've been forcing breakfast first thing when I get up in the morning. I'm a really good cook- I make our bone broth from scratch and because I refuse to completely cut out bread from my life I bought a mill to make my own flour from local glyphosate-free grains. I love making sourdough.

Despite all the bad, I have a lot to be grateful for- something I forget often and easily. My body is at least intact (I know somebody who recently lost a leg in an accident). My brain functions. I'm not on the Epstein list. It'll be spring soon. My family is relatively okay. I can afford to spend money on a case of DDW.

**One question I haven't sorted yet is I'm not sure if I'm leptin resistant.** I'm around 120lbs (5'4), I've been an early riser since childhood but as I mentioned I do have cravings. My instinct tells me the cravings have to do with dopamine issues and aforementioned stress. When I get in with my naturopath next I might just get it tested. Aside from that, if I were to diagnose myself I'd say **I think I just desperately need more UV light.

THE PLAN GOING FORWARD, AS IT STANDS
\-**After this job ends, go full steam with all the relevant protocols (lacking in cold plunging right now, need more DHA in diet, maybe the leptin rx but not sure)
\-**Sort out bitcoin.** I only own xxxxx bitcoin right now- and not even own because I don't have it in a wallet yet (hoping to sort that ASAP but I really don't understand it as well as I should so currently it's just another thing to be overwhelmed by).
\-Find a new job, preferably remote so I can control my environment.
**\-Go south, preferably El Salvador.** Trying to sort out the best way to do this financially but I'm hoping at least a couple months ASAP (likely April-June), then maybe a longer stay over the winter. I'm nervous about it though because I'm not a traveller and this will be my first solo trip and even though I've heard El Salvador's safe, being a woman alone in a country I don't know is going to be scary especially since I'll be looking at lower-cost housing. I've seen Jeremy Franco mentioned here so maybe I will get a silver membership to chat with him (I think silver is the requirement). Regardless, hoping while I'm there to scope out potential real estate to buy for our eventual exit from Canada. Maybe it's too late, maybe it's not, but we gotta try.

For those who don't know, Saturn's return refers to the phenomenon of great transition people experience when Saturn returns to the position it was in when they were born. Usually the effect can be felt for a few years, from around age 27-30, then again at around 60. I've never been much for astrology but when I learned about it recently I thought, "That's pretty fucking bang on."

I'm not sure if I've begun writing to seek advice, admit guilt, or provide myself clarity, but it's been cathartic, at least- thanks for reading. Any comments are welcome and appreciated. Especially looking to connect with other Canadians that are trying to leave too.

### LoriLee — 2026-02-15T05:56:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/post-366910>

Contact India for housing in El Salvador. +1 (813) 833-8750. Ive been 3 times without incident. Once you go, you'll never want to leave. I've never once felt in danger. Check out the Bitcoin hardware store in El zonte for Bitcoin help. They have a YouTube channel. Message them. They can help you remotely too. Ask for Ronny. I have his number if needed.

### Awainer1 — 2026-02-15T17:43:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/post-366921>

My rec for storing your bitcoin would be to take an old phone you don’t use anymore and doesn’t have cellular. Connect to WiFi and download a bitcoin wallet like Blue Wallet or Muun and use that as a hardware wallet. No better or worse than an actual bitcoin hardware wallet.

### MellicaSchmidt — 2026-05-04T18:00:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/post-373010>

Post #2

I've been in Atami for about a month now. Really enjoying the beach and the people (but not the mosquitos, I guess I'm leaking too much UV). Here's a breakdown of what's been happening:

**MELANIN:** I'm significantly darker than when I got here, and it's starting to stick. The first couple weeks I noticed if I missed a day of conscious tanning, or if it was cloudy that I lost my melanin very quickly- I must have been really needing it. I don't think I've reached my maximum yet but I should be able to before I leave. One thing of note is I wasn't as good with handling the heat as I was the UV, especially on my legs. Somebody pointed out this may have to do with my heme proteins, so that's been added on the list to research. I have noticed heat tolerance increasing, and I make a point of trying to sweat as much as I can before I hop in the plunge.

**GROUNDING:** I haven't been at the beach grounding as long as I'd like to over the last week or so, so I'm going to be more conscious about it. I currently average at 2-3 hours a day overall. I want it to be at least twice that. I'm not exercising right now but I'm sore after surf lessons which I believe indicates I need more grounding too.

**DRINKING WATER:** Not satiated easily from drinking water (this issue has existed for a while), I think just an indication that my mitochondria isn't making enough and I'm dehydrated from the inside.

**CYST DE-FRAG** (maybe): I notice that when I'm in the sun (grounding or not) I feel a "heartbeat" in my left abdominal area. I think it's trying to shake deuterium loose. Sometimes it's alarmingly strong. I take it as a good sign, and after a good sun day it stays after I move to shade.

**HEAVY BLOOD**: A TCM I saw here said my blood is heavily in stasis, which I realized a couple days later probably meant it's "maple syrup". He also said my kidneys and spleen are "low" which I took as "sagging from deuterium overload". So I'm reading up on the de-frag protocols and trying to apply what I can. Definitely doing a lot of humming while I'm lying on the beach in the tide.

Here's the defrag/deuterium dump list I've come up with so far (edited as I learn):

\-jumping jacks grounded
\-walking
\-chest/body pounding
\-sweating
\-tens machine (maybe a massage gun as well, since de-frag seems to supercede nnEMF exposure I think, so long as grounding is available)
\-humming
\-tuning forks
\-40hz music/sound
\-saline/DDW IV (maybe I can find somebody to do this for me at home) with ingested DDW afterwards
\-DDW ingestion in general
\-mastic gum (I sometimes grind my teeth at night, is that an attempt at defragging?)
\-wearing jade, specifically letting it charge in the sun and touching to various acupuncture points in body, specifically fingertips
\-changing my fillings with something more piezoelectric (modern version of jade inlays) this is more long term, need to read about it more
\-in relation to jade, green + red light, i.e sitting under trees outside should help
\-psilocybin mushrooms
\-MCT rich foods/ MCT oil (Medium Chain Triglycerides)
\-max UV and grounding possible
\-deuterium depleted/high DHA diet etc.
\-TMS (transcranial magnetic stimulation) machine, not applicable to me unless my brain goes crazy.

Also wondering if vasodilation via jamaica tea is helpful at all, not as a de-fragging element but just to help allow better blood flow.

My homework for this is the "understanding vortices" and the vagal exhaust blog. (Probably de-frag thread again too). After understanding that women will offload their deuterium to their children, it's basically going to be my life's mission to de-frag as much as I can. Maybe I'll go as far as getting a thermogram before we decide to try for conception again.

**APPETITE:** As soon as I arrived my appetite was diminished. I dismissed it thinking it would only be for a couple days maybe because I was unused to the heat, but it's generally persisted. I end up fasting for 12-14 hours a day. (Breakfast first thing, last meal around 3 or 4). My cravings for caffeine and sugar were also extremely diminished almost immediately. This has changed significantly over the past week, and I've been waking up hungry in the middle of the night even though I feel satiated during the day. Cravings are back a bit too. (This last week might all be cycle related.)

**STRESS:** Generally low (how could it not be in the sun?) but definitely comes when I start to think about what I'm going to do for a job when I get home, and our future re: moving down here potentially.

**CT:** I've been cold plunging fairly consistently. I started at 55F and worked my way to around 20min, and would use it to cool myself so I could stay in the sun longer, so maybe a total of 40min plunging around 4 days a week, ish. Then I moved to 53F for around 10min, total 30min a day or so spread over maybe 2 hours, about a week ago. I feel great when I'm in the plunge.

**CYCLE LATE:** My cycle was due about a month into my visit, and I was 4 days late which is highly unusual. From the time it was supposed to start I had the aforementioned erratic appetite and bowel movements, basically it felt like my system went haywire. I'm unsure if I was late because of the intermittent fasting or the cold plunging, or something else entirely.

**TENDONITIS**: Generally better, noticed difficulty with my arms and hands in the cold plunge but was working towards being able to submerge my hands for longer than a minute.

**TINNITUS**: Not completely gone but a lot less noticeable than at home (In both places, I only hear it when I'm about to sleep).

It's been feeling simultaneously fast and slow paced, my time here, but it's still hard to believe I've been here a month already. I'm halfway through my trip now and am curious what this next month brings. What's surprised me most so far is how fulfilling it can be to just bake in the sun with no podcasts, no music no nothing, except maybe some humming. I'm not sure I've ever been so content with just feeling the sun on my bare skin, nor has my mind been able to stay quiet the way it does here.

# Thread: Erica’s Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erica%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.32261/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erica8990 — 2026-02-16T00:08:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erica%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.32261/post-366947>

Hi everyone,

I was reintroduced to Dr. Kruse’s work a year ago while following Dr. Alexis Cowan. Her podcast and information really gave me the courage to dig into Dr. Kruse’s work. And I’m so glad I took the leap. He has given me permission to further prioritize my health! I am currently reevaluating and modifying my habits and environment in hopes of reducing inflammation related to Hashimoto’s and Rheumatoid Arthritis.

At 54, I’ve spent my entire life living in San Antonio, Texas. And growing up in the 70’s I had my share of mediocre nutrition along with secondhand smoke exposure from my family. I’m fairly sure I was not breast fed. I believe this contributed to years of chronic earaches and bronchitis treated with antibiotics. I was a fierce softball player till my vision changed at 13.

My grandmother was born in the United Kingdom and moved to Texas in 1945 with my grandfather, who was military, after the birth of my mother. She died at 66 of emphysema. I’ve often thought my mother’s health has been a direct reflection of her choices but who knows. She has been a life long smoker with a poor diet leading to being over weight with high blood pressure. Over the years she has experienced anxiety, depression and what I’d describe as agoraphobia.

I noticed in my teens, what I now know to be hypothyroid symptoms. I was finally diagnosed and treated for hypothyroidism at age 24. I became pregnant at 26 and experienced the beginnings of RA pain during this time. RA diagnoses was made a year later and started Methotrexate after breast feeding my little one for six months. That’s as far as I could go due to low milk production. For the next 7 yrs I tried Enbrel and then Humira with mixed results. At 35 my health was failing. I stopped the biologics, kept the MTX and over hauled my diet to more Autoimmune/Paleo. This helped a great deal. Early menopause at 40 which lead me to started BHRT. At 44 I started experiencing unexplained edema in my legs and for two years this made life uncomfortable. Both of my doctors, one concierge and one rheumatologist wrote it off as RA until a nurse noticed I had not had thyroid labs drawn all that time. Note the hormone labs were never missed. Armour prescription was quadrupled and 80% of symptoms resolved over a 48hr period. Since then I’ve managed my labs and eventually stopped seeing both doctors. In 2018 I took the Shingles vaccine and nothing since then.

Up until 2023 my health was fairly good. I continued the AI/Paleo diet along with MTX and I added Hydroxychloroquine and Low Dose Naltrexone in 2020. I did have the occasional ache with continued joint damage but overall I was able to do resistance training with a physical therapist for several years. This helped me gain some much needed weight, as I’m chronically underweight, and gave me the strength to enjoy life. This was disrupted when my right ankle flared followed by my left elbow. I stopped PT telling them I’d be back ASAP. I’ve been in an ongoing flare ever since and I’m very motivated to figure out what I can do to improve my health. Ideally without going back on biologics. However the reality is my inflammation is not managed and progressing with an ever rising hs-CRP. 17 as of 1/22/26.

**Current Medications:**
Armour thyroid 60mg AM/PM
LDN 4.5mg PM
Injectable Testosterone and Estradiol
Oral Progesterone

**Note -** I stopped MTX after 20yrs concerned about liver health, uncontrolled inflammation, and increased photosensitivity.
HQC was a C-prevention prescription and I haven’t heard yet if functional doctor will continue Rx. Also concerned about additional photosensitivity.

At this point I’ve made changes following the guidelines in Brain Gut 6! Additional daily seafood and sea vegetables with bone broths. Eliminated a few things I thought were okay like my fancy organic wild rice.

And I’m focused on my environment finally. We’ve been in the same house for 30 yrs so this is my next challenge. The nnEMF conversation has not been well received by my husband but I pointed out he could help or watch me figure it out. I will be ordering the body voltage kit from Anthony Smith along with black out curtains and blue light blocking glasses. All screens have been adjusted to warm. Morning sunrise is new for me but fairly easy with the mild weather in South Texas. I’ve been working on my solar callus with IR and a little outside sunbathing. I have a sauna and am trying to use it regularly. Cold therapy is on the list and now that I’ve read about how helpful it is I will be implementing some version. I have repeatedly heard/read Dr. Kruse say a person cannot heal in the environment they got sick in and I’m taking this advice to heart.

**Questions -** 1). June 2023 Q & A Dr. Kruse said low dose naltrexone has negative effects on the pituitary and destroys POMC.
I did search in the forum and there were a few mentions of LDN between 2012-2019 but I did not see a clear answer. I’ve never been sure
if it was helpful. Maybe it helped lower my TPO antibodies but I also started iodine supplements around the same time.
My question is and from what I think I know, it is okay to just stop taking it? No tapering off required?

2). Stinging Nettle Leaf is mentioned in the forum as being a TNF blocker. I purchased Herb Pharm’s tincture with 632mg of Nettle Leaf
per dropper mainly because dose seemed higher and cheaper than capsules. Bottle suggests 1 dropper up to 5x daily.
Does anyone have experience with tinctures and dosing?

I tried to keep it short and to the point! I’m happy to be here learning the good stuff!!

### Scottyhj25 — 2026-02-16T20:44:17+0200

I’m writing on behalf of my girlfriend, Virginia, a 31-year-old woman from Tione di Trento, Italy, a mountainous region (45° latitude), who is dealing with several chronic health challenges. She’s recently begun incorporating Dr. Kruse's protocols. We’d deeply appreciate any insight you might have into her case esp with weight gain.

-   **Hereditary conditions:**
    -   Spherocytosis
    -   Crigler-Najjar syndrome type II
-   **Surgical history:**
    -   Full spleen removal at age 12
    -   Gallbladder completely removed in childhood
-   **Ongoing symptoms:**
    -   Slow digestion from stomach through intestines
    -   Bile reflux
    -   Persistent bloating and stomach heaviness
    -   Difficulty gaining weight (currently underweight)
    -   Currently taking **ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA)** to assist bile flow

Current Protocols

-   **Sunlight:** Daily exposure to natural light, prioritizing sunrise and solar timing
-   **Grounding:** Daily grounding practices
-   **Alcohol/Smoking:** Avoids both entirely
-   **Sleep:** No current issues; wears **blue blockers at night** and keeps **all artificial light at night off** in her room
-   **Diet:** Eats **locally and seasonally**

Dr. Kruse also mentioned in the Huberman podcast about Dr Cosic, who was the researcher who used blue light to cure Crigler-Najjar Syndrome via a molecular resonance model. Curious how blue light in this specific instance works, but my guess its similar to how they treat jaundice in babies.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-16T22:28:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guidance-for-my-girlfriend-with-spherocytosis-crigler-najjar-type-ii-and-full-spleen-gallbladder-removal.32265/post-366981>

Phototherapy (using blue light) is a standard supportive treatment for both types to help convert bilirubin into a form that can be excreted, but it is symptomatic management, not a reversal or cure. It doesn't fix the underlying genetic defect or restore normal enzyme function permanently. In CN2, phototherapy is sometimes used during flares (illness or fasting), but phenobarbital is usually the primary long-term option, and many patients achieve good control without needing ongoing light therapy.

Phenobarbital works in Crigler-Najjar syndrome type II (CN2) primarily because it acts as a potent inducer of the liver enzyme UDP-glucuronosyltransferase 1A1 (UGT1A1), the very enzyme that's partially deficient in this condition.

Phenobarbital is a barbiturate that strongly induces the expression and activity of various hepatic microsomal enzymes, including UGT1A1, through mechanisms involving nuclear receptors (primarily the constitutive androstane receptor, or CAR, and to some extent the pregnane X receptor, PXR). This induction upregulates the production of the remaining functional UGT1A1 enzyme in the liver, effectively boosting the conjugation process.

As a result:

Serum unconjugated bilirubin levels typically drop significantly—often by 25–70% (commonly cited as at least 25–30%, with some reports of sharper reductions like 50–70% in responsive cases).

This occurs within days to weeks of starting treatment (e.g., noticeable after 2 weeks at doses of 60–180 mg/day, or ~3–5 mg/kg/day).

The increased conjugation shifts bilirubin toward monoglucuronides and diglucuronides in bile, improving excretion and reducing jaundice.

This response is a key diagnostic feature that distinguishes CN2 from the more severe type I (CN1), where UGT1A1 activity is essentially absent (near-zero residual enzyme), so phenobarbital has little to no effect because no meaningful induction is possible without any functional enzyme to upregulate.

The paper I've mentioned in blogs is "The treatment of Crigler-Najjar syndrome by blue light as explained by resonant recognition model" by Irena Cosic and Drasko Cosic (published in EPJ Nonlinear Biomedical Physics, 2016).

This paper uses the Resonant Recognition Model (RRM), a biophysical/electromagnetic theory proposed by Cosic, to theoretically explain why blue light phototherapy works in Crigler-Najjar syndrome. It analyzes protein sequences (likely the defective UDP enzyme vs. normal) and suggests that specific frequencies in blue light can mimic or resonate with the "healthy" protein's activity, providing a novel biophysical rationale for the established clinical use of blue light.

It focuses on explaining the mechanism behind phototherapy's effectiveness, particularly in severe cases (often type I, where daily prolonged blue light is the mainstay).

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guidance-for-my-girlfriend-with-spherocytosis-crigler-najjar-type-ii-and-full-spleen-gallbladder-removal.32265/post-366983>

Veljkovic and Cosic's work in the 1980s proposed a more elegant solution: molecular interactions are fundamentally electrical/electromagnetic in nature, occurring over distances much larger than molecular sizes ( 5–50 nm or more). They argued that proteins and other biomolecules generate oscillating electromagnetic fields via charge transfer along their backbones, allowing "recognition" through resonant frequencies rather than pure chance collisions. This evolved into Cosic's RRM, which models proteins as sequences of amino acids with assigned electron-ion interaction potentials (EIIP), then applies Fourier transforms to identify characteristic frequencies (typically in the 10^13–10^15 Hz range, spanning infrared to UV light). Interacting molecules share the same frequency but opposite phases, creating a "dance" where they attract and align, like tuning forks vibrating in sync.

In this framework, the cell isn't a passive bag but an "excitable medium" (syncytium) of semi-conductive structures, where weak electromagnetic signals trigger amplified responses. This resonates with Prigogine's dissipative structures, where far-from-equilibrium systems (like cells) self-organize by dissipating energy gradients, building order from chaos through feedback loops. Experimental support includes laser-induced changes in enzyme activity and photon emissions from cells, suggesting EM resonance influences binding without direct contact.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-16T22:53:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guidance-for-my-girlfriend-with-spherocytosis-crigler-najjar-type-ii-and-full-spleen-gallbladder-removal.32265/post-366984>

In the context of my "Unified Field Theory of Mammalian Bio-optics," Crigler-Najjar syndrome type II (CN2) represents a profound failure of the
**subatomic filter** located on the q-arm of Chromosome 2 (2q37). While my thesis I covered briefly yesterday Feb 14 2026 focused on the GCG gene (2q24.2) as the metabolic switch, CN2 is driven by a mutation in the **[UGT1A1 gene](https://medlineplus.gov/genetics/gene/ugt1a1/).**

The Chromosome 2 "Exhaust Pipe" Failure
The **UGT1A1 enzyme** is the critical "gatekeeper" that conjugates bilirubin, transforming it from a toxic, lipid-soluble (mass-heavy) state into a water-soluble form for excretion into the gut lumen.

**Isotopic Clog:** In my framework, a failure at 2q37 (CN2) means the body cannot "conjugate" or lighten the isotopic load. Unconjugated bilirubin represents "Relativistic Mass" that cannot be vented, causing a system-wide **Optical Brownout** as this mass accumulates in tissues like the skin (jaundice) and potentially the brain.

**The 220nm leptin/cholesterol Signal:** This buildup acts as a "Quantum Jammer," blurring the 220nm command signal between the gut and the SCN, manifesting as the persistent "stomach heaviness" and slow digestion.
Surgical Depletion of the "Mass-Filtering" Nodes
Her surgical history has physically removed two secondary "filtering" nodes:

**The Spleen (Removed):** The spleen is the primary site of erythrocyte breakdown. Without it, the "melanin sheets" lose a key sequestering hub for transition metals and recycled heme.

**The Gallbladder (Removed):** This has destroyed the "rhythmic pacing" of the system. Without the gallbladder to concentrate and pulse bile, there is a constant, un-metered leak of bile into the gut. This "blunted transintestinal flow" creates the **Bile Reflux** and prevents the enterocytes from correctly "sorting" light hydrogen from deuterium, leading to the **bloating** and **difficulty gaining weight.
Currently, **Ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA)** is being used to manually assist bile flow. In my thesis, this is an external attempt to restore the "optical logic" of the gut. However, because the **UGT1A1 mutation** remains, the "exhaust" (bilirubin) is still not properly "processed" at the atomic level, meaning the gut remains "clogged" with mass that the system cannot identify or vent effectively.

Her condition reminds me of the complications related to GLP-1 drugs.** While GLP-1 drugs _over-stimulate_ the 2q24.2 frequency (GCG), CN2 _under-performs_ the 2q37 frequency (UGT1A1). Both result in a timing loop failure where the gut’s "mass-sampling" data becomes corrupted, leading to the "underweight" status and "slow digestion" (gastroparesis-like symptoms) often seen in both conditions.

During the Q&A yesterday I spoke to a Canadian women about her olfactory bulb issue and I suggested to her a novel idea. You might review this and consider it with your girlfriend.

**The Chromosomal Synergy:** At 2q24, the GCG gene produces **GLP-2**, which drives the "Mass-Filtering" via enterocyte turnover. Downstream at 2q37, UGT1A1 ensures that the breakdown products of the blood (bilirubin) are optically "cleared" for excretion.
